22044702.utility: The risk score was found to be a good predictor of hospital death in older patients with severe, acute heart failure. The odds ratio for serum albumin, the best independent predictor, was 0.82 (0.74-0.90, P<0.001).

22051257.title: Short-term Ventricular Assist Device (VAD) Mortality Risk Calculator
22051257.code:
def compute_risk_score(postcardiotomy_shock_or_graft_failure, female_sex, total_bilirubin):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the given factors.

    Args:
    postcardiotomy_shock_or_graft_failure (bool): True if the patient has a diagnosis of postcardiotomy shock or graft failure, False otherwise.
    female_sex (bool): True if the patient is female, False otherwise.
    total_bilirubin (float): The patient's total bilirubin level on postoperative day 3.

    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if postcardiotomy_shock_or_graft_failure:
        score += 1
    if female_sex:
        score += 1
    if total_bilirubin > 5.2:
        score += 1
    return score
22051257.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of survival after the placement of a short-term Ventricular Assist Device (VAD).
22051257.eligibility: Patients who have undergone Abiomed or CentriMag placement at the institution or transferred to the institution with the device in place are eligible.
22051257.interpretation:
- A score of 0 is associated with a 13% mortality rate.
- A score of 2 or greater is associated with an 86% mortality rate.
22051257.utility: The clinical utility of the risk score is not explicitly mentioned in the article. Further studies are needed to evaluate its performance, such as AUC, F-score, PPV.

22061093.title: Clinical Autoantibody Ratio (CABR) Score Calculator
22061093.code:
def compute_cabr_score(anti_ApoA1_IgG, anti_PC_IgM, NSTEMI_TIMI_score):
    """
    Compute the CABR score.

    Parameters:
    anti_ApoA1_IgG (float): Level of anti-ApoA1 IgG.
    anti_PC_IgM (float): Level of anti-PC IgM.
    NSTEMI_TIMI_score (int): NSTEMI-TIMI score.

    Returns:
    float: CABR score.
    """
    return (anti_ApoA1_IgG / anti_PC_IgM) + NSTEMI_TIMI_score
22061093.purpose: This calculator is used to diagnose non-ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI) and predict subsequent cardiac troponin I (cTnI) elevation in patients with acute chest pain (ACP).
22061093.eligibility: Patients presenting at the emergency department with acute chest pain without ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction are eligible.
22061093.interpretation:
- CABR score <= 3.3: Low risk of NSTEMI diagnosis. Negative predictive value (NPV) is 97%.
- CABR score > 3.3: High risk of NSTEMI diagnosis. The risk of subsequent NSTEMI diagnosis increases 19-fold.
22061093.utility: The CABR score has a high diagnostic accuracy with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.88 (95% confidence interval (CI): 0.82-0.95). For subsequent cTnI positivity, the CABR score displayed adequate predictive accuracies with AUCs of 0.82 (95% CI: 0.70-0.94); the NPVs were 99% (95% CI: 94-100).

22096046.title: Prognostic Scoring Tool (PST) for HIV-associated Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia
22096046.code:
def compute_pst(age, repeat_pcp, comorb, pks, pao2, hb):
    """
    Compute the Prognostic Scoring Tool (PST) for HIV-associated Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    repeat_pcp (bool): Whether the patient has a repeat episode of PCP.
    comorb (bool): Whether the patient has a medical co-morbidity.
    pks (bool): Whether the patient has pulmonary Kaposi sarcoma.
    pao2 (float): Oxygen partial pressure on admission in kPa.
    hb (float): Haemoglobin on admission in g/dL.

    Returns:
    float: The computed PST.
    """
    pst = 25.5 + (age / 10)
    if repeat_pcp:
        pst += 2
    if comorb:
        pst += 3
    if pks:
        pst += 4
    pst -= pao2
    pst -= hb
    return pst
22096046.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the outcome of HIV-associated Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PCP) and to identify patients at high and low risk of in-hospital death from PCP.
22096046.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with HIV-associated Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PCP) are eligible.
22096046.interpretation:
- 0-3.9: Group 1, 0% mortality
- 4-7.9: Group 2, 3% mortality
- 8-10.9: Group 3, 9% mortality
- 11-14.9: Group 4, 29% mortality
- ≥ 15: Group 5, 52% mortality
22096046.utility: The PST facilitates rapid identification of patients early in their hospitalization who have mild or severe HIV-associated PCP and who are at high and low risk of in-hospital death from PCP. It may aid assessment of severity of illness and in directing treatment strategies, but requires validation in patient cohorts from other health-care institutions.

22152955.title: Genetic Risk Score (GRS) Calculator for Coronary Heart Disease in Type 2 Diabetes Patients
22152955.code:
def compute_grs(rs4977574, rs12526453, rs646776, rs2259816, rs11206510):
    """
    Compute the Genetic Risk Score (GRS) for Coronary Heart Disease in Type 2 Diabetes Patients.

    Parameters:
    rs4977574 (int): 1 if risk allele is present, 0 otherwise.
    rs12526453 (int): 1 if risk allele is present, 0 otherwise.
    rs646776 (int): 1 if risk allele is present, 0 otherwise.
    rs2259816 (int): 1 if risk allele is present, 0 otherwise.
    rs11206510 (int): 1 if risk allele is present, 0 otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: Genetic Risk Score (GRS)
    """
    return rs4977574 + rs12526453 + rs646776 + rs2259816 + rs11206510
22152955.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the genetic susceptibility to coronary heart disease (CHD) in patients with type 2 diabetes.
22152955.eligibility: Patients with type 2 diabetes are eligible for this calculator.
22152955.interpretation:
- GRS ≤5: Lower risk of CHD. This group includes 30% of diabetic subjects.
- GRS ≥8: Almost 2-fold increase in CHD risk. This group includes 19% of diabetic subjects.
22152955.utility: The prediction of CHD was significantly improved (p < 0.001) when the GRS was added to a model including clinical predictors in the combined samples.

22153954.title: Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) for Predicting Healthcare-Associated Infections (HCAIs)
22153954.code:
def calculate_gnri(albumin, body_weight, ideal_body_weight):
    """
    Calculate GNRI score.

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/L.
    body_weight (float): Actual body weight in kg.
    ideal_body_weight (float): Ideal body weight in kg.

    Returns:
    float: GNRI score.
    """
    weight_ratio = body_weight / ideal_body_weight if body_weight <= ideal_body_weight else 1
    gnri = 1.489 * albumin + 41.7 * weight_ratio
    return gnri
22153954.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of healthcare-associated infections in hospitalized elderly patients based on their nutritional status.
22153954.eligibility: Patients aged over 65 years who are admitted as emergencies to the medical ward of an acute care hospital.
22153954.interpretation:
- GNRI > 98: Not at risk for nutrition-related complications.
- 92 < GNRI <= 98: Low or medium risk for nutrition-related complications.
- GNRI <= 92: High risk for nutrition-related complications.
22153954.utility: In the study, a higher GNRI score was associated with a lower risk of HCAIs. The hazard ratio per unit increase in GNRI score was 0.97 (95% CI: 0.95-0.99; P = 0.01).

22188413.title: Munich-LTX-Score
22188413.code:
def calculate_munich_ltx_score(acute_rejection, lymphocytic_bronchiolitis, donor_age, hla_mismatch, single_ltx):
    """
    Calculate the Munich-LTX-Score.

    Parameters:
    acute_rejection (bool): True if acute rejection is present, False otherwise.
    lymphocytic_bronchiolitis (bool): True if lymphocytic bronchiolitis is present, False otherwise.
    donor_age (int): Age of the donor.
    hla_mismatch (bool): True if HLA-A ≥ 2-/DR ≥ 2 mismatch is present, False otherwise.
    single_ltx (bool): True if single LTX is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: Munich-LTX-Score
    """
    score = 0
    if acute_rejection:
        score += 1
    if lymphocytic_bronchiolitis:
        score += 1
    if donor_age >= 55:
        score += 1
    if hla_mismatch:
        score += 1
    if single_ltx:
        score += 1
    return score
22188413.purpose: The Munich-LTX-Score is used to predict long-term survival in patients after lung transplantation (LTX) based on post-transplant factors inclusive of donor and recipient characteristics.
22188413.eligibility: Patients who have undergone lung transplantation are eligible for this score calculation.
22188413.interpretation:
The Munich-LTX-Score identifies three discrete groups:
- Score 0-1: Low risk group with an actuarial five-year survival of 91%
- Score 2-3: Moderate risk group with an actuarial five-year survival of 54%
- Score 4-5: High risk group with an actuarial five-year survival of 0%
22188413.utility: The Munich-LTX-Score was able to identify patients with different risk levels for long-term survival after lung transplantation with a significant difference in survival rates (p < 0.001).

22197776.title: Combined Cytokine Score for Mortality Prediction in Severe Sepsis
22197776.code:
def compute_cytokine_score(il6, il8, il10, p75):
    """
    Compute the cytokine score based on the plasma levels of IL-6, IL-8, IL-10.

    Args:
    il6, il8, il10: Plasma levels of IL-6, IL-8, IL-10.
    p75: The 75th percentile of the plasma levels of IL-6, IL-8, IL-10.

    Returns:
    Cytokine score: 1 if at least one interleukin value is higher than p75, 0 otherwise.
    """
    if il6 > p75 or il8 > p75 or il10 > p75:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
22197776.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality risk in patients with severe sepsis or septic shock based on the plasma levels of three interleukins (IL-6, IL-8, IL-10).
22197776.eligibility: Patients with severe sepsis or septic shock admitted to the ICU are eligible.
22197776.interpretation:
- Score 0: Lower risk of mortality.
- Score 1: Higher risk of mortality.
22197776.utility: The combined cytokine score was found to predict mortality with 2-3-fold higher hazard ratios than those obtained with the individual interleukins values.

22220299.title: Knee Osteoarthritis Non-Response Predictor
22220299.code:
def compute_risk_score(patellofemoral_pain, acl_laxity, height):
    """
    Compute the risk score for non-response to therapy in knee OA patients.

    Args:
    patellofemoral_pain (bool): True if the patient has patellofemoral pain, False otherwise.
    acl_laxity (bool): True if the patient has anterior cruciate ligament laxity, False otherwise.
    height (float): The patient's height in meters.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if patellofemoral_pain:
        score += 1
    if acl_laxity:
        score += 1
    if height > 1.71:
        score += 1
    return score
22220299.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of a patient with knee osteoarthritis (OA) not responding to a combination of manual physical therapy and exercise.
22220299.eligibility: Patients with knee osteoarthritis who are about to undergo a combination of manual physical therapy and exercise.
22220299.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of non-response to therapy.
- Score 1: Moderate risk of non-response to therapy.
- Score 2 or 3: High risk of non-response to therapy, with a posttest probability of 88% for non-success with this treatment.
22220299.utility: The overall prognostic accuracy of the clinical prediction rule (CPR) was 96%. The positive likelihood ratio for a score of 2 or 3 was 36.7.

22231649.title: Colorectal Surgery Surgical-Site Infection (SSI) Risk Score
22231649.code:
def compute_risk_score(obesity, contamination_class, ASA_grade, open_surgery):
    """
    Compute the risk score for surgical-site infection after colorectal resection.

    Parameters:
    obesity (bool): True if the patient is obese, False otherwise.
    contamination_class (int): The contamination class of the patient, should be either 3 or 4.
    ASA_grade (int): The American Society of Anesthesiologists grade of the patient, should be either III or IV.
    open_surgery (bool): True if the surgery is open, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    score = 0
    if obesity:
        score += 1
    if contamination_class in [3, 4]:
        score += 1
    if ASA_grade in [3, 4]:
        score += 1
    if open_surgery:
        score += 1
    return score
22231649.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of surgical-site infection (SSI) after resection of the colon or rectum.
22231649.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing resection of the colon or rectum are eligible.
22231649.interpretation:
- Score 0: 5% risk of SSI
- Score 1: 12% risk of SSI
- Score 2: 18.7% risk of SSI
- Score 3: 44% risk of SSI
- Score 4: 68% risk of SSI
22231649.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve for the score was 0.729, indicating a good predictive power.

22296334.title: Prostate-Specific Antigen Velocity (PSAV) Risk Count Calculator
22296334.code:
def calculate_psav_risk_count(psav_measurements):
    """
    Calculate the PSAV risk count based on the PSAV measurements.

    Args:
    psav_measurements (list): A list of two PSAV measurements.

    Returns:
    int: The PSAV risk count (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    return sum(1 for measurement in psav_measurements if measurement > 0.4)
22296334.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of prostate cancer, particularly high-grade disease, based on the number of times PSAV exceeds a specific threshold.
22296334.eligibility: Patients who have undergone two serial PSAV measurements are eligible.
22296334.interpretation:
- Risk count 0: Low risk of prostate cancer.
- Risk count 1: Moderate risk of prostate cancer.
- Risk count 2: 8.2-fold increased risk of prostate cancer and 5.4-fold increased risk of Gleason score 8-10 prostate cancer.
22296334.utility: The addition of the PSAV risk count significantly improved discrimination (area under the ROC curve 0.625 vs 0.725, P= 0.031) and reclassified individuals for the risk of high-grade prostate cancer (net reclassification, P < 0.001).

22331953.title: Prognostic Staging System for Light Chain Amyloidosis
22331953.code:
def compute_risk_score(FLC_diff, cTnT, NT_ProBNP):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with light chain amyloidosis.

    Parameters:
    FLC_diff (float): The difference between involved and uninvolved light chain, in mg/dL.
    cTnT (float): Cardiac biomarker troponin-T, in ng/mL.
    NT_ProBNP (float): N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide, in pg/mL.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if FLC_diff >= 18:
        score += 1
    if cTnT >= 0.025:
        score += 1
    if NT_ProBNP >= 1800:
        score += 1
    return score
22331953.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with newly diagnosed light chain (AL) amyloidosis, incorporating cardiac biomarkers and serum free light chain measurements.
22331953.eligibility: Patients with newly diagnosed light chain (AL) amyloidosis are eligible.
22331953.interpretation:
- Score 0 (Stage I): Median overall survival from diagnosis is 94.1 months.
- Score 1 (Stage II): Median overall survival from diagnosis is 40.3 months.
- Score 2 (Stage III): Median overall survival from diagnosis is 14 months.
- Score 3 (Stage IV): Median overall survival from diagnosis is 5.8 months.
22331953.utility: The classification system was validated in two other datasets: 303 patients undergoing stem-cell transplantation, and 103 patients enrolled onto different clinical trials. The proportions of patients with stages I, II, III and IV disease were 189 (25%), 206 (27%), 186 (25%) and 177 (23%), and their median OS from diagnosis was 94.1, 40.3, 14, and 5.8 months, respectively (P < .001).

22350695.title: Predictive Model for Long-term Liver Dysfunction after TACE
22350695.code:
import numpy as np

def compute_risk_score(npRQ_ratio, preAlb_ratio):
    """
    Compute the risk score for long-term liver dysfunction after TACE.

    Parameters:
    npRQ_ratio (float): The non-protein respiratory quotient 7 days after/before TACE.
    preAlb_ratio (float): The prealbumin ratio 7 days after/before TACE.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return np.exp(-6.383 * npRQ_ratio - 3.038 * preAlb_ratio + 7.755) / (1 + np.exp(-6.383 * npRQ_ratio - 3.038 * preAlb_ratio + 7.755))
22350695.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of long-term liver dysfunction in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) who have undergone Transcatheter arterial chemoembolization (TACE).
22350695.eligibility: Patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) who have undergone Transcatheter arterial chemoembolization (TACE).
22350695.interpretation:
- Risk Score < 0.449: Low risk of long-term liver dysfunction after TACE
- Risk Score >= 0.449: High risk of long-term liver dysfunction after TACE
22350695.utility: The model has an area under the receiver operating curve (AUC) of 0.713 (95% confidence interval [CI] 0.613-0.812), indicating a good predictive performance. The sensitivity and specificity of the model are 57.1% and 79.1%, respectively.

22386575.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Atrial Fibrillation Stroke Risk
22386575.code:
def calculate_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, stroke_TIA_thromboembolism, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Calculate CHA2DS2-VASc score based on the input parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    stroke_TIA_thromboembolism (bool): True if the patient has had a stroke, TIA or thromboembolism, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): 'M' for male, 'F' for female.
    
    Returns:
    int: CHA2DS2-VASc score.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if stroke_TIA_thromboembolism:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'F':
        score += 1
    return score
22386575.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify patients with nonvalvular atrial fibrillation according to the risk of ischemic stroke and cardiovascular events.
22386575.eligibility: Patients with paroxysmal atrial fibrillation not receiving anticoagulant therapy are eligible.
22386575.interpretation:
- Score 0: 0% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 0% annual risk of cardiovascular events
- Score 1: 0.60% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 1.43% annual risk of cardiovascular events
- Score 2: 0.95% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 1.50% annual risk of cardiovascular events
- Score 3: 1.96% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 2.52% annual risk of cardiovascular events
- Score 4: 5.45% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 10.14% annual risk of cardiovascular events
- Score 5: 9.06% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 12.85% annual risk of cardiovascular events
- Score ≥6: 13.7% annual risk of ischemic stroke, 17.13% annual risk of cardiovascular events
22386575.utility: The CHA2DS2-VASc score was associated with symptomatic ischemic stroke (odds ratio 7.051, 95% confidence interval 3.76-13.22, p<0.001) and cardiovascular events (odds ratio 3.448, 95% confidence interval 2.33-5.11, p<0.001).

22394511.title: ReACT Self-Harm Rule
22394511.code:
def compute_risk(recent_self_harm, living_situation, harm_method, psychiatric_treatment):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the ReACT Self-Harm Rule.

    Parameters:
    recent_self_harm (bool): True if the patient has self-harmed in the past year, False otherwise.
    living_situation (bool): True if the patient lives alone or is homeless, False otherwise.
    harm_method (bool): True if the patient used cutting as a method of harm, False otherwise.
    psychiatric_treatment (bool): True if the patient is receiving treatment for a current psychiatric disorder, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, which is the sum of the four factors.
    """
    return recent_self_harm + living_situation + harm_method + psychiatric_treatment
22394511.purpose: This calculator is used to identify patients at higher risk of repeat self-harm, or suicide, within 6 months of an Emergency Department self-harm presentation.
22394511.eligibility: Adult patients aged 16 years and above who have presented to the Emergency Department due to self-harm.
22394511.interpretation:
- 0: Low risk of repeat self-harm or suicide within 6 months.
- 1-4: High risk of repeat self-harm or suicide within 6 months.
22394511.utility: The ReACT Self-Harm Rule performed with 95% sensitivity and 21% specificity. It had a positive predictive value of 30% and a negative predictive value of 91% in the derivation centres. It identified 83 out of 92 of all subsequent suicides.

22395980.title: Gastric Cancer Prognostic Score Calculator
22395980.code:
def compute_score(tumor_location, tumor_differentiation, tumor_type):
    """
    Compute the gastric cancer prognostic score.

    Parameters:
    tumor_location (str): Localization of the tumor in the stomach. Can be 'upper', 'middle', or 'lower'.
    tumor_differentiation (str): Differentiation of the tumor. Can be 'well', 'moderate', or 'poor'.
    tumor_type (str): Type of the tumor according to Laurén classification. Can be 'intestinal', 'diffuse', or 'mixed'.

    Returns:
    int: The prognostic score.
    """
    score = 0
    if tumor_location == 'middle':
        score += 1
    if tumor_differentiation == 'well':
        score += 1
    if tumor_type == 'intestinal':
        score += 1
    return score
22395980.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the response and prognosis of patients with locally advanced gastric cancer who are treated with neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
22395980.eligibility: Patients with locally advanced gastric cancer who are about to undergo neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
22395980.interpretation:
- Score 0: High risk group with a median survival time of 20.5 months and a 5-year survival rate of 21.2%.
- Score 1-2: Intermediate risk group with a median survival time of 39.2 months and a 5-year survival rate of 41.2%.
- Score 3: Low risk group with a median survival time not reached and a 5-year survival rate of 65.3%.
22395980.utility: The score accurately predicts response and prognosis in neoadjuvant treated gastric cancer. The three risk groups had significantly different clinical and histopathologic response rates and survival times.

22414567.title: Liver Stiffness Measurement (LSM) and LSM-Spleen Diameter to Platelet Ratio Score (LSPS) Calculator
22414567.code:
def calculate_lsms_lspm(lsm, spleen_diameter, platelet_count):
    """
    Calculate LSM and LSPS scores.

    Parameters:
    lsm (float): Liver Stiffness Measurement in kPa.
    spleen_diameter (float): Spleen diameter in cm.
    platelet_count (float): Platelet count in 10^9/L.

    Returns:
    float: LSM score.
    float: LSPS score.
    """
    lsps = lsm * spleen_diameter / platelet_count
    return lsm, lsps
22414567.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of hepatic decompensation in patients with B-viral compensated cirrhosis.
22414567.eligibility: Patients with histologically proven B-viral cirrhosis, well-preserved liver function, and no history of decompensation are eligible.
22414567.interpretation:
- For LSM:
  - LSM < 13 kPa: Low risk of hepatic decompensation.
  - LSM 13-18 kPa: Moderate risk of hepatic decompensation.
  - LSM ≥ 18 kPa: High risk of hepatic decompensation.
- For LSPS:
  - LSPS < 1.1: Low risk of hepatic decompensation.
  - LSPS 1.1-2.5: Moderate risk of hepatic decompensation.
  - LSPS ≥ 2.5: High risk of hepatic decompensation.
22414567.utility: The LSM and LSPS scores were found to be useful in risk assessment of hepatic decompensation among complication-naive B-viral cirrhotic patients. The hazard ratios for LSM 13-18 kPa and ≥18 kPa were 4.547 and 12.446 respectively. For LSPS 1.1-2.5 and ≥2.5, the hazard ratios were 5.796 and 13.618 respectively.

22418007.title: Surgical Mortality Probability Model (S-MPM)
22418007.code:
def compute_risk_score(ASA_status, surgery_risk, emergency_status):
    """
    Compute the S-MPM risk score based on ASA physical status, surgery risk class, and emergency status.

    Parameters:
    ASA_status (int): ASA physical status of the patient (I, II, III, IV, V)
    surgery_risk (str): Risk class of the surgery ('intermediate', 'high')
    emergency_status (bool): Whether the surgery is an emergency (True, False)

    Returns:
    int: The computed S-MPM risk score
    """
    ASA_points = {1: 0, 2: 2, 3: 4, 4: 5, 5: 6}
    surgery_points = {'intermediate': 1, 'high': 2}
    emergency_points = {True: 1, False: 0}

    return ASA_points[ASA_status] + surgery_points[surgery_risk] + emergency_points[emergency_status]
22418007.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality risk for patients undergoing noncardiac surgery. It can be used to communicate risk information to patients, guide clinical management at the "point-of-care," and for surgeons and hospitals to internally audit their quality of care.
22418007.eligibility: Patients undergoing noncardiac surgery are eligible for this risk calculation.
22418007.interpretation:
- Patients with risk scores less than 5 have a predicted risk of mortality less than 0.50%.
- Patients with a risk score of 5 to 6 have a risk of mortality between 1.5% and 4.0%.
- Patients with a risk score greater than 6 have a risk of mortality more than 10%.
22418007.utility: The S-MPM exhibited excellent discrimination (C statistic, 0.897) and acceptable calibration (Hosmer-Lemeshow statistic 13.0, P = 0.023) in the validation data set.

22488406.title: MEBE Expression Score Calculator for Myelodysplastic Syndromes (MDS)
22488406.code:
def compute_MEBE_score(MN1, ERG, BAALC, EVI1):
    """
    Compute the MEBE score based on the expression of the four MEBE genes.

    Parameters:
    MN1 (bool): High expression of MN1 gene (True if high, False otherwise)
    ERG (bool): High expression of ERG gene (True if high, False otherwise)
    BAALC (bool): High expression of BAALC gene (True if high, False otherwise)
    EVI1 (bool): High expression of EVI1 gene (True if high, False otherwise)

    Returns:
    int: MEBE score (1 if low, 2 if high)
    """
    high_expression_genes = sum([MN1, ERG, BAALC, EVI1])
    if high_expression_genes >= 2:
        return 2
    else:
        return 1
22488406.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival and time to Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) progression in patients with Myelodysplastic Syndromes (MDS) based on the expression of the MEBE genes (MN1, ERG, BAALC, and EVI1).
22488406.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Myelodysplastic Syndromes (MDS) are eligible.
22488406.interpretation:
- MEBE score 1: Low risk of shorter overall survival and time to AML progression.
- MEBE score 2: High risk of shorter overall survival and time to AML progression.
22488406.utility: The MEBE score was found to be a significant predictor of overall survival and time to AML progression in patients with MDS, independent of karyotype, percentage of bone marrow blasts, transfusion dependence, ASXL1, and IDH1 mutation status.

22491337.title: NTS Vascular Infection (NTSVI) Score Calculator
22491337.code:
def calculate_ntsvi_score(sex, hypertension, coronary_disease, serogroup_c1, malignancy, immunosuppressive_therapy):
    """
    Calculate the NTSVI score based on the presence of risk factors.

    Parameters:
    sex (str): 'male' or 'female'
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise
    coronary_disease (bool): True if the patient has coronary arterial disease, False otherwise
    serogroup_c1 (bool): True if the patient has serogroup C1 infections, False otherwise
    malignancy (bool): True if the patient has malignancy, False otherwise
    immunosuppressive_therapy (bool): True if the patient is undergoing immunosuppressive therapy, False otherwise

    Returns:
    int: The NTSVI score
    """
    score = 0
    if sex == 'male':
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if coronary_disease:
        score += 1
    if serogroup_c1:
        score += 1
    if malignancy:
        score -= 1
    if immunosuppressive_therapy:
        score -= 1
    return score
22491337.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of vascular infections in adults aged ≥ 50 years with Nontyphoid Salmonella (NTS) bacteremia.
22491337.eligibility: Patients aged ≥ 50 years diagnosed with NTS bacteremia are eligible.
22491337.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 0: 2.2% prevalence of vascular infections
- Score 1: 10.6% prevalence of vascular infections
- Score 2: 39.4% prevalence of vascular infections
- Score 3: 55.2% prevalence of vascular infections
- Score 4: 100% prevalence of vascular infections
22491337.utility: The scoring algorithm shows an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.83, indicating good predictive performance. A cutoff value of +1 represents a high sensitivity (95.0%) and an acceptable specificity (45.3%).

22513180.title: DISTAL Scoring System for Predicting Failure of Snuffbox Arteriovenous Fistulas
22513180.code:
def compute_distal_score(diabetes, ihd, stroke, two_procedures, age, vein_size):
    """
    Compute the DISTAL score for predicting failure of snuffbox arteriovenous fistulas.

    Parameters:
    diabetes (bool): Whether the patient has diabetes.
    ihd (bool): Whether the patient has Ischemic Heart Disease.
    stroke (bool): Whether the patient has had a stroke.
    two_procedures (bool): Whether the patient has undergone two snuffbox procedures.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    vein_size (float): The size of the vein in mm.

    Returns:
    int: The DISTAL score.
    """
    score = 0
    if diabetes:
        score += 1
    if ihd:
        score += 1
    if stroke:
        score += 1
    if two_procedures:
        score += 1
    if age > 70:
        score += 1
    if vein_size < 2.0:
        score += 1
    return score
22513180.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of failure of snuffbox arteriovenous fistulas in patients. It aids in decision making for fistula placement.
22513180.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing snuffbox arteriovenous fistula procedures are eligible.
22513180.interpretation:
- Score 0-3: Lower risk of primary patency failure. Performing snuffbox procedures on these patients would result in a 23% reduction in the number of failures within two months.
- Score 4-6: Higher risk of primary patency failure. These patients may not be suitable for snuffbox procedures.
22513180.utility: The DISTAL scoring system showed a clear decrease in primary patency with increasing DISTAL score (log rank χ(2) = 30.3, DF = 5, P < 0.001). It could give large improvements in primary patency for the snuffbox fistula if the results can be validated in other datasets.

22539053.title: Predictive Index for Lymph Node Management in Major Salivary Gland Cancer
22539053.code:
def compute_risk_score(major_nerve_invasion, histologic_type, lv_invasion, extracapsular_invasion):
    """
    Compute the risk score for lymph node involvement in major salivary gland cancer.

    Parameters:
    major_nerve_invasion (bool): True if major nerve invasion is present, False otherwise.
    histologic_type (bool): True if the histologic type is associated with higher risk, False otherwise.
    lv_invasion (bool): True if lymphatic/vascular invasion is present, False otherwise.
    extracapsular_invasion (bool): True if extracapsular invasion is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    return int(major_nerve_invasion) + int(histologic_type) + int(lv_invasion) + int(extracapsular_invasion)
22539053.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of lymph node (LN) involvement in patients with major salivary gland cancer, based on four clinicopathologic factors.
22539053.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with major salivary gland cancer are eligible.
22539053.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk, with 3.8% of patients having LN involvement
- Score 2: Medium risk, with 27.9% of patients having LN involvement
- Score 3: High risk, with 55.9% of patients having LN involvement
- Score 4: Super high risk, with 94.7% of patients having LN involvement
22539053.utility: The predictive index effectively stratifies patients into risk groups for nodal metastasis, which can guide comprehensive management and potentially improve treatment outcomes.

22561964.title: COPD Comorbidity Index (COTE)
22561964.code:
def compute_cote_score(comorbidities):
    """
    Compute the COTE score based on the presence of specific comorbidities.

    Args:
    comorbidities (list of bool): A list of 12 boolean values indicating the presence of each comorbidity.

    Returns:
    int: The COTE score.
    """
    return sum(comorbidities)
22561964.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the mortality risk in patients with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) based on the presence of specific comorbidities.
22561964.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD).
22561964.interpretation:
- COTE score < 4: Standard risk of death
- COTE score >= 4: 2.2-fold increased risk of death
22561964.utility: The COTE index was found to be a significant predictor of mortality in COPD patients, with increases in the COTE index associated with an increased risk of death from both COPD-related and non-COPD-related causes.

22576696.title: Briganti's Risk Stratification Tool for Baseline Staging Bone Scan in Prostate Cancer
22576696.code:
def briganti_risk_stratification(gleason_score, psa, palpable_disease):
    """
    Calculate the risk of bone metastasis in prostate cancer patients.

    Parameters:
    gleason_score (int): The Gleason score from the patient's biopsy.
    psa (float): The patient's prostate-specific antigen level.
    palpable_disease (bool): Whether the patient has palpable disease (cT2/T3).

    Returns:
    bool: Whether a baseline staging bone scan is recommended.
    """
    if gleason_score > 7 or (psa > 10 and palpable_disease):
        return True
    else:
        return False
22576696.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the need for a baseline staging bone scan in patients with newly diagnosed prostate cancer.
22576696.eligibility: Patients with newly diagnosed prostate cancer are eligible.
22576696.interpretation:
- True: A baseline staging bone scan is recommended due to high risk of bone metastasis.
- False: A baseline staging bone scan is not recommended due to low risk of bone metastasis.
22576696.utility: The Briganti's risk stratification tool was found to be significantly more accurate (AUC: 0.75; CI: 0.632-0.859) than the European Association of Urology guideline (AUC: 0.64; CI: 0.52-0.761) for the prediction of bone metastases.

22586007.title: GAP Index and GAP Calculator for Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF)
22586007.purpose: These calculators are used to predict mortality in patients with IPF using commonly measured clinical and physiologic variables.
22586007.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis.
22586007.interpretation:
- GAP score 0-3: Stage I, 1-year mortality of 6%
- GAP score 4-5: Stage II, 1-year mortality of 16%
- GAP score 6-8: Stage III, 1-year mortality of 39%
22586007.utility: The GAP models performed similarly in derivation (c-index of 70.8 and 69.3, respectively) and validation (c-index of 69.1 and 68.7, respectively). The models also performed similarly in pooled follow-up visits (c-index ≥71.9).

22593431.title: Preconception Dietary Risk (PDR) Score Calculator
22593431.code:
def calculate_pdr_score(responses):
    """
    Calculate the Preconception Dietary Risk (PDR) score.

    Args:
    responses (list of bool): A list of boolean values representing the responses to the dietary questionnaire. 
                              A value of True indicates adherence to the dietary recommendation for a particular food group.

    Returns:
    int: The PDR score.
    """
    return sum(responses)
22593431.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the chance of ongoing pregnancy in women undergoing IVF/ICSI treatment based on their adherence to general dietary recommendations.
22593431.eligibility: Women undergoing IVF/ICSI treatment who have completed a preconception dietary questionnaire are eligible.
22593431.interpretation:
- A higher PDR score indicates better adherence to dietary recommendations and a higher chance of ongoing pregnancy. 
- Specifically, a one-point increase in the PDR score is associated with a 65% increased chance of ongoing pregnancy.
22593431.utility: The PDR score showed a significant association with the chance of ongoing pregnancy after IVF/ICSI treatment (odds ratio 1.65, confidence interval: 1.08-2.52; P=0.02).

22596096.title: 90-Day Mortality Risk Score After Surgical Biopsy for Interstitial Lung Disease
22596096.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, icu_admission, immunosuppressive_treatment, open_surgery):
    """
    Compute the 90-day mortality risk score after surgical lung biopsy for interstitial lung disease.

    Parameters:
    age (int): patient's age
    icu_admission (bool): whether the patient was admitted to the ICU preoperatively
    immunosuppressive_treatment (bool): whether the patient is undergoing immunosuppressive treatment
    open_surgery (bool): whether the patient underwent open surgery

    Returns:
    float: risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 67:
        score += 1.5
    if icu_admission:
        score += 2
    if immunosuppressive_treatment:
        score += 1.5
    if open_surgery:
        score += 1
    return score
22596096.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 90-day mortality risk after surgical lung biopsy for patients with interstitial lung disease.
22596096.eligibility: Patients undergoing surgical lung biopsy for interstitial lung disease are eligible.
22596096.interpretation:
- Score 0: 2% risk of death at 90 days (Class A)
- Score 1-2: 12% risk of death at 90 days (Class B)
- Score 2.5-3: 40% risk of death at 90 days (Class C)
- Score >3: 86% risk of death at 90 days (Class D)
22596096.utility: The risk score was able to stratify the risk of surgical lung biopsy candidates and assess the cost-effectiveness of the procedure. The overall 90-day mortality was around 10%.

22651863.title: Long-term Benefit Prediction of Implantable Cardioverter-Defibrillator (ICD) Risk Score
22651863.code:
def compute_risk_score(NYHA_class_III_or_IV, age_over_70, BUN_over_26, QRS_duration_over_0_12, atrial_fibrillation):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on five clinical factors.
    
    Args:
    NYHA_class_III_or_IV (bool): True if the patient's NYHA class is III or IV, False otherwise.
    age_over_70 (bool): True if the patient's age is over 70, False otherwise.
    BUN_over_26 (bool): True if the patient's blood urea nitrogen level is over 26 mg/dl, False otherwise.
    QRS_duration_over_0_12 (bool): True if the patient's QRS duration is over 0.12 s, False otherwise.
    atrial_fibrillation (bool): True if the patient has atrial fibrillation, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    return NYHA_class_III_or_IV + age_over_70 + BUN_over_26 + QRS_duration_over_0_12 + atrial_fibrillation
22651863.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 8-year survival benefit of a nonresynchronization implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) based on a simple risk stratification score.
22651863.eligibility: Patients who are considering primary prevention with an ICD are eligible for this risk score calculation.
22651863.interpretation:
- 0 risk factors: Low risk, with a significantly higher probability of survival at 8-year follow-up when treated by ICD as compared with non-ICD therapy (75% vs. 58%).
- 1 to 2 risk factors: Intermediate risk, with a significantly higher probability of survival at 8-year follow-up when treated by ICD as compared with non-ICD therapy (47% vs. 31%).
- 3 or more risk factors: High risk, with no significant difference in 8-year survival between the ICD and non-ICD subgroups (19% vs. 17%).
22651863.utility: The risk score was associated with a significant long-term survival benefit among low- and intermediate-risk patients (hazard ratio [HR]: 0.52, p < 0.001, and HR: 0.66, p < 0.001, respectively), whereas treatment with an ICD was not associated with a significant benefit among high-risk patients (HR: 0.84, p = 0.25).

22664936.title: TODAI VAD Score (TVAD Score)
22664936.code:
def compute_tvad_score(albumin, bilirubin, diameter, pressure):
    """
    Compute the TVAD score based on preoperative parameters.

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Serum albumin in mg/dl
    bilirubin (float): Serum total bilirubin in mg/dl
    diameter (float): Left ventricular end-diastolic diameter in mm
    pressure (float): Central venous pressure in mmHg

    Returns:
    int: The TVAD score
    """
    score = 0
    if albumin < 3.2:
        score += 8
    if bilirubin > 4.8:
        score += 7
    if diameter < 55:
        score += 6
    if pressure > 11:
        score += 5
    return score
22664936.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 1-year mortality in patients with a left ventricular assist device (LVAD) based on preoperative objective parameters.
22664936.eligibility: Patients who are about to receive or have received a left ventricular assist device (LVAD) implantation.
22664936.interpretation:
The TVAD score is interpreted as follows:

- 0-8 points: Low risk, with a 1-year survival rate of 95%
- 9-17 points: Intermediate risk, with a 1-year survival rate of 54%
- 18-26 points: High risk, with a 1-year survival rate of 14%
22664936.utility: The TVAD score has a significantly larger area under the curve (0.864) than any of the previously known 5 scoring methods, indicating a better prediction of the prognosis after LVAD implantation.

22682012.title: Infrapopliteal Lesion Risk Calculator
22682012.code:
def compute_risk_score(lesion_calcification, target_vessel_diameter, lesion_length, ba_run_off):
    """
    Compute the risk score for major adverse limb events after infrapopliteal angioplasty.

    Parameters:
    lesion_calcification (bool): True if lesion calcification is present, False otherwise.
    target_vessel_diameter (float): The diameter of the target vessel in mm.
    lesion_length (float): The length of the lesion in mm.
    ba_run_off (bool): True if below-the-ankle run-off is absent, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    score = 0
    if lesion_calcification:
        score += 1
    if target_vessel_diameter < 3.0:
        score += 1
    if lesion_length > 300:
        score += 1
    if ba_run_off:
        score += 1
    return score
22682012.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of major adverse limb events (MALE) after infrapopliteal angioplasty in patients with critical limb ischaemia due to isolated infrapopliteal lesions.
22682012.eligibility: Patients with critical limb ischaemia due to isolated infrapopliteal lesions who have undergone infrapopliteal angioplasty.
22682012.interpretation:
- 0 or 1: Low risk, with a 2-year freedom-from-MALE rate of 59%
- 2: Moderate risk, with a 2-year freedom-from-MALE rate of 46%
- 3 or 4: High risk, with a 2-year freedom-from-MALE rate of 29%
22682012.utility: The risk stratification based on these predictors allows estimation of future incidence of MALE in CLI with isolated infrapopliteal lesions.

22740446.title: International Prognostic Score for Essential Thrombocythemia (IPSET)
22740446.code:
def compute_ipset_score(age, leukocyte_count, history_of_thrombosis):
    """
    Compute the IPSET score for a patient with Essential Thrombocythemia.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient in years.
    leukocyte_count (float): The leukocyte count of the patient in 10(9)/L.
    history_of_thrombosis (bool): Whether the patient has a history of thrombosis.

    Returns:
    int: The IPSET score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 60:
        score += 2
    if leukocyte_count >= 11:
        score += 1
    if history_of_thrombosis:
        score += 1
    return score
22740446.purpose: This calculator is used to predict survival at diagnosis in patients with World Health Organization (WHO)-defined Essential Thrombocythemia (ET).
22740446.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with WHO-defined Essential Thrombocythemia.
22740446.interpretation:
The IPSET score is interpreted as follows:

- Score = 0: Low risk, median survival not reached
- Score = 1-2: Intermediate risk, median survival 24.5 years
- Score = 3-4: High risk, median survival 13.8 years
22740446.utility: The IPSET model was validated in two independent cohorts and was able to predict the occurrence of thrombosis. However, it was not able to predict post-ET myelofibrosis.

22740447.title: Biologic Prognostic Model for Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma
22740447.code:
def compute_risk_score(nongerminal_center, sparc, microvascular_density):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on three biologic markers.

    Parameters:
    nongerminal_center (bool): True if the patient has nongerminal center B cell-like subtype, False otherwise.
    sparc (float): The percentage of SPARC. It should be less than 5% to be considered as an adverse prognostic factor.
    microvascular_density (int): The quartile of microvascular density. It should be 4 to be considered as an adverse prognostic factor.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if nongerminal_center:
        score += 1
    if sparc < 5:
        score += 1
    if microvascular_density == 4:
        score += 1
    return score
22740447.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the survival of patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma based on biologic factors.
22740447.eligibility: Patients with de novo diffuse large B-cell lymphoma treated with rituximab and CHOP (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, and prednisone) or CHOP-like therapies are eligible.
22740447.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low biologic score, indicating good survival.
- Score 2-3: High biologic score, indicating poor survival.
22740447.utility: The model using these 3 biologic markers was highly predictive of overall survival and event-free survival in multivariate analysis after adjusting for the International Prognostic Index in both the training and validation sets.

22748912.title: PRELANE Score for Late Recurrence of Renal Cell Carcinoma
22748912.code:
def compute_prelane_score(LVI, Fuhrman_grade, pT_stage):
    """
    Compute the PRELANE score for late recurrence of renal cell carcinoma.

    Parameters:
    LVI (bool): Lymphovascular invasion (True if present, False otherwise)
    Fuhrman_grade (int): Fuhrman grade (3 or 4)
    pT_stage (int): pT stage (>1)

    Returns:
    int: PRELANE score
    """
    score = 0
    if LVI:
        score += 2
    if Fuhrman_grade in [3, 4]:
        score += 1
    if pT_stage > 1:
        score += 2
    return score
22748912.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of late recurrence (beyond 5 years) in patients who have undergone nephrectomy or nephron-sparing surgery for renal cell carcinoma.
22748912.eligibility: Patients who have undergone nephrectomy or nephron-sparing surgery for renal cell carcinoma and have a minimum recurrence-free survival of 60 months.
22748912.interpretation:
- 0 points: late recurrence risk 3.1%
- 1-3 points: late recurrence risk 8.4%
- 4-5 points: late recurrence risk 22.1%
22748912.utility: The area under the curve value for the model is 70% (95% confidence interval, 67-73), indicating a good predictive ability.

22776742.title: QTc and MELD Score Risk Calculator for Cirrhotic Patients with Acute Gastrointestinal Bleeding
22776742.code:
def calculate_risk(QTc, MELD):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on QTc interval and MELD score.

    Parameters:
    QTc (float): QTc interval in ms.
    MELD (float): Model of End-Stage Liver Disease score.

    Returns:
    int: Risk score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    risk_score = 0
    if QTc >= 460:
        risk_score += 1
    if MELD >= 20:
        risk_score += 1
    return risk_score
22776742.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 6-week mortality risk in cirrhotic patients with acute gastrointestinal bleeding based on QTc interval and MELD score.
22776742.eligibility: Patients with cirrhosis who have experienced acute gastrointestinal bleeding are eligible for this risk calculator.
22776742.interpretation:
- 0 risk factors: 0% 6-week mortality
- 1 risk factor: 32.1% 6-week mortality
- 2 risk factors: 70.6% 6-week mortality
22776742.utility: The areas under the ROC curve for MELD and QTc were 0.88 (CI 95% 0.78-0.95) and 0.75 (CI 95% 0.63-0.85) respectively, indicating good predictive power.

22783052.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) Preoperative Survival Score Calculator
22783052.code:
def compute_score(prealbumin, alkaline_phosphatase, alpha_fetoprotein, tumor_size, platelet_count, gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase):
    """
    Compute the HCC preoperative survival score based on six preoperative factors.
    
    Args:
    prealbumin (float): Prealbumin level in mg/L.
    alkaline_phosphatase (float): Alkaline phosphatase level in U/L.
    alpha_fetoprotein (float): α fetoprotein level in μg/L.
    tumor_size (float): Total tumor size in cm.
    platelet_count (float): Platelet count in 10⁹/L.
    gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase (float): γ-glutamyl transpeptidase level in U/L.
    
    Returns:
    int: The HCC preoperative survival score.
    """
    score = 0
    if prealbumin < 170: score += 1
    if alkaline_phosphatase > 129: score += 1
    if alpha_fetoprotein > 20: score += 1
    if tumor_size > 8: score += 1
    if platelet_count < 100: score += 1
    if gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase > 64: score += 1
    return score
22783052.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the short-term survival outcome after resection for multinodular hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) based on preoperative factors.
22783052.eligibility: Patients with multinodular HCC and Child-Pugh A liver function who are considering surgical resection.
22783052.interpretation:
- Score < 5: Lower risk, with 1-year mortality of 5% and 1-year recurrence rate of 33%.
- Score ≥ 5: Higher risk, with 1-year mortality of 62% and 1-year recurrence rate of 86%. This score indicates a contraindication for surgery.
22783052.utility: The scoring model had a strong ability to predict 1-year survival with an area under the receiver operating characteristics (ROC) curve of 0.925.

22788674.title: Comorbidity-Polypharmacy Score (CPS) Calculator
22788674.code:
def compute_cps(medications, comorbidities):
    """
    Compute the Comorbidity-Polypharmacy Score (CPS).
    
    Parameters:
    medications (int): The number of preinjury medications.
    comorbidities (int): The number of comorbidities.
    
    Returns:
    int: The computed CPS.
    """
    return medications + comorbidities
22788674.purpose: This calculator is used to predict clinical outcomes in older trauma patients by combining the number of preinjury medications and comorbidities.
22788674.interpretation:
- CPS < 15: Lower risk of poor clinical outcomes.
- CPS >= 15: Higher risk of poor clinical outcomes, including greater mortality, complications, longer hospital and intensive care unit stay, and need for discharge to a facility.
22788674.utility: The CPS was found to be significantly associated with 90-day survival (Mantel-Cox, P < .001). Mortality was independently associated with older age (odds ratio (OR) = 1.06 per year), higher injury severity score (OR = 1.19 per point), and higher CPS (OR = 1.11 per point) in multivariate analysis (all P < .01).

22789110.title: Mediastinal Lymph Node Malignancy Risk Calculator
22789110.code:
def compute_risk_score(short_axis, heterogeneous, round_shape, distinct_margin, absence_hilar_structure, high_blood_flow):
    """
    Compute the risk score for mediastinal lymph node malignancy.

    Parameters:
    short_axis (bool): True if short axis >1 cm, False otherwise.
    heterogeneous (bool): True if heterogeneous pattern, False otherwise.
    round_shape (bool): True if round shape, False otherwise.
    distinct_margin (bool): True if distinct margin, False otherwise.
    absence_hilar_structure (bool): True if absence of a central hilar structure, False otherwise.
    high_blood_flow (bool): True if high blood flow in a LN, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 6.
    """
    return short_axis + heterogeneous + round_shape + distinct_margin + absence_hilar_structure + high_blood_flow
22789110.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of malignancy in mediastinal lymph nodes using endobronchial ultrasound (EBUS) criteria.
22789110.eligibility: Patients with mediastinal lymphadenopathy irrespective of the underlying disease are eligible.
22789110.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Very low chance of malignancy
- Score 3-6: High risk of malignancy
22789110.utility: The sum score resulted in an odds ratio of 15.5 if more than two criteria were positive (P < 0.00001). The positive predictive value was best for heterogeneity (73%), with a negative predictive value of more than 80%.

22815214.title: Advanced NSCLC Prognostic Score Calculator
22815214.code:
def compute_risk_score(ECOG, metastases, treatment, CA125, CYFRA, leukocytes):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    ECOG (int): ECOG Performance Status, >1 is considered as APF
    metastases (bool): Presence of metastases, True is considered as APF
    treatment (bool): Anti-neoplastic treatment, False is considered as APF
    CA125 (float): CA 125 level, >35 U/mL is considered as APF
    CYFRA (float): CYFRA 21-1 level, >3.3 ng/mL is considered as APF
    leukocytes (int): Leukocytes count, >10,000/µL is considered as APF
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if ECOG > 1: score += 1
    if metastases: score += 1
    if not treatment: score += 1
    if CA125 > 35: score += 1
    if CYFRA > 3.3: score += 1
    if leukocytes > 10000: score += 1
    return score
22815214.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the prognostic value of clinical variables and biomarkers in patients with advanced stages of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC).
22815214.eligibility: Patients with advanced stages of NSCLC are eligible for this calculator.
22815214.interpretation:
- 0-1 APF: Reference group
- 2-3 APF: Hazard Ratio (HR) = 2.7 (95% CI: 1.5-4.6)
- 4-5 APF: HR = 8.8 (95% CI: 4.6-16.8)
22815214.utility: The score maintained the differences between risk groups both in patients who received antineoplastic treatment and in those who did not, indicating its clinical utility in prognostic classification of NSCLC patients.

22829196.title: Freiburger Comorbidity Index (FCI) for Multiple Myeloma
22829196.code:
def compute_fci(pulmonary_disease, renal_impairment, kps):
    """
    Compute the Freiburger Comorbidity Index (FCI) for a patient with multiple myeloma.

    Parameters:
    pulmonary_disease (bool): True if the patient has severe pulmonary disease, False otherwise.
    renal_impairment (bool): True if the patient's eGFR is ≤30 ml/min/1.73m², False otherwise.
    kps (int): Karnofsky Performance Status, ≤70 if the patient has decreased performance status.

    Returns:
    int: The FCI score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    fci = 0
    if pulmonary_disease:
        fci += 1
    if renal_impairment:
        fci += 1
    if kps <= 70:
        fci += 1
    return fci
22829196.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the impact of comorbidities on the progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) in patients with multiple myeloma.
22829196.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with multiple myeloma are eligible.
22829196.interpretation:
- Score 0: Median overall survival rate of 118 months
- Score 1: Median overall survival rate of 53 months
- Score 2 or 3: Median overall survival rate of 25 months
22829196.utility: The FCI was found to significantly differentiate between different median overall survival rates (P<0.005), making it a valuable tool for treatment decision-making and prognosis prediction in multiple myeloma.

22846680.title: Postdischarge Nausea and Vomiting (PDNV) Risk Score Calculator
22846680.code:
def compute_risk_score(gender, age, history_nausea_vomiting, opioid_administration, nausea_postanesthesia):
    """
    Compute the PDNV risk score.

    Parameters:
    gender (str): 'male' or 'female'
    age (int): patient's age
    history_nausea_vomiting (bool): whether the patient has a history of nausea and/or vomiting after previous anesthesia
    opioid_administration (bool): whether opioids were administered in the postanesthesia care unit
    nausea_postanesthesia (bool): whether the patient experienced nausea in the postanesthesia care unit

    Returns:
    int: PDNV risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if gender == 'female':
        score += 1
    if age < 50:
        score += 1
    if history_nausea_vomiting:
        score += 1
    if opioid_administration:
        score += 1
    if nausea_postanesthesia:
        score += 1
    return score
22846680.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of postdischarge nausea and vomiting (PDNV) in patients undergoing ambulatory surgery.
22846680.eligibility: Patients who are adults undergoing general anesthesia at ambulatory surgery centers are eligible.
22846680.interpretation:
- Score 0: 7% incidence of PDNV
- Score 1: 20% incidence of PDNV
- Score 2: 28% incidence of PDNV
- Score 3: 53% incidence of PDNV
- Score 4: 60% incidence of PDNV
- Score 5: 89% incidence of PDNV
22846680.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of this risk score is 0.72, indicating a good predictive performance.

22868013.title: MELD Score Calculator for Non-Acetaminophen Induced Acute Liver Failure (NAI-ALF)
22868013.code:
import math

def calculate_meld_score(bilirubin, inr, creatinine):
    """
    Calculate MELD score for a patient with non-acetaminophen induced acute liver failure.

    Parameters:
    bilirubin (float): Serum bilirubin level in mg/dL.
    inr (float): International Normalized Ratio.
    creatinine (float): Serum creatinine level in mg/dL.

    Returns:
    float: MELD score.
    """
    meld_score = 3.78 * math.log(bilirubin) + 11.2 * math.log(inr) + 9.57 * math.log(creatinine) + 6.43
    return meld_score
22868013.purpose: This calculator is used to predict mortality in patients with non-acetaminophen induced acute liver failure.
22868013.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with non-acetaminophen induced acute liver failure are eligible.
22868013.interpretation:
- MELD Score < 32: Lower risk of mortality.
- MELD Score >= 32: Higher risk of mortality. This score was found to be the best predictor of mortality with a sensitivity of 79% and specificity of 71%.
22868013.utility: The MELD score has an excellent utility for predicting mortality in NAI-ALF. It has a sensitivity of 79% and specificity of 71%. The positive predictive value (PPV) is 82% and the negative predictive value (NPV) is 67%.

22873681.title: Sepsis Risk Assessment Calculator
22873681.code:
def calculate_risk(APACHE_II, suPAR):
    """
    Calculate the risk of mortality in sepsis patients based on APACHE II score and serum suPAR levels.

    Parameters:
    APACHE_II (int): APACHE II score of the patient.
    suPAR (float): Serum suPAR level of the patient in ng/ml.

    Returns:
    str: Risk category based on APACHE II score and suPAR level.
    """

    if APACHE_II < 17 and suPAR < 12:
        return "Low risk"
    elif APACHE_II < 17 and suPAR >= 12:
        return "Moderate risk"
    elif APACHE_II >= 17 and suPAR < 12:
        return "High risk"
    else:
        return "Very high risk"
22873681.purpose: This calculator is used for early risk assessment in patients with sepsis, aiming to improve prognostication by combining APACHE II score and serum suPAR levels.
22873681.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with sepsis, including those with severe sepsis/septic shock.
22873681.interpretation:
- Low risk: APACHE II < 17 and suPAR < 12 ng/ml, with mortality 5.5%
- Moderate risk: APACHE II < 17 and suPAR ≥ 12 ng/ml, with mortality 17.4%
- High risk: APACHE II ≥ 17 and suPAR < 12 ng/ml, with mortality 37.4%
- Very high risk: APACHE II ≥ 17 and suPAR ≥ 12 ng/ml, with mortality 51.7%
22873681.utility: The prediction rule was validated in a double-blind fashion by an independent confirmation cohort of 196 sepsis patients, predominantly severe sepsis/septic shock patients, from Sweden.

22888271.title: VRE Carriage Risk Calculator at ICU Admission
22888271.code:
def compute_vre_risk_score(icu_readmission, copd, recent_antibiotics, recent_vancomycin):
    """
    Compute the risk score for VRE carriage at ICU admission.

    Parameters:
    icu_readmission (bool): Whether the patient has been readmitted to the ICU during hospitalization.
    copd (bool): Whether the patient has chronic obstructive lung disease.
    recent_antibiotics (bool): Whether the patient has had recent antibiotic treatment.
    recent_vancomycin (bool): Whether the patient has had recent vancomycin use.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for VRE carriage at ICU admission.
    """
    score = 0
    if icu_readmission:
        score += 1
    if copd:
        score += 2
    if recent_antibiotics:
        score += 3
    if recent_vancomycin:
        score += 2
    return score
22888271.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of vancomycin-resistant enterococci (VRE) carriage at the time of admission to the intensive care unit (ICU) in a low VRE prevalence setting.
22888271.eligibility: Patients who are being admitted to the ICU in a hospital setting with low VRE prevalence.
22888271.interpretation:
- Risk score < 3: Low risk of VRE carriage at ICU admission.
- Risk score ≥ 3: High risk of VRE carriage at ICU admission.
22888271.utility: The sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, and negative predictive value of the prediction rule, based on risk scores ≥3 points, were 84.2%, 82.5%, 15.2% and 99.3%, respectively.

22910320.title: CHAID Model for Early Death Prediction in Phase I Trials
22910320.code:
def compute_risk_score(albumin, platelet_count):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on albumin and platelet count.

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/l.
    platelet_count (float): Platelet count in mm(-3).

    Returns:
    int: Risk score (1 for high risk, 0 for low risk).
    """
    if albumin < 33 or (albumin >= 33 and platelet_count >= 400000):
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
22910320.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 90-day mortality rate among patients enrolled in Phase I oncology trials. It helps in selecting patients with 'sufficient life expectancy' for these trials.
22910320.eligibility: Patients who are enrolled in Phase I oncology trials are eligible for this calculator.
22910320.interpretation:
- Risk score 1: High risk of 90-day mortality.
- Risk score 0: Low risk of 90-day mortality.
22910320.utility: The CHAID model has a higher rate of correctly classified patients (0.79) compared to the RMS (0.67). The negative predictive values (NPV) were similar for both models.

22950428.title: Adverse Clinical Events (ACEs) Risk Score Calculator
22950428.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, delirium, pressure_sore, bladder_catheter, malnutrition, acute_infection):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient based on six predictors.
    
    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    delirium (bool): Whether the patient has delirium.
    pressure_sore (bool): Whether the patient has a pressure sore.
    bladder_catheter (bool): Whether the patient has an indwelling bladder catheter.
    malnutrition (bool): Whether the patient is malnourished.
    acute_infection (bool): Whether the patient has an acute infection on admission.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score for the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 87:
        score += 1
    if delirium:
        score += 1
    if pressure_sore:
        score += 1
    if bladder_catheter:
        score += 1
    if malnutrition:
        score += 1
    if acute_infection:
        score += 1
    return score
22950428.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of elderly patients developing adverse clinical events (ACEs) after being transferred from acute hospital wards to post-acute care (PAC) facilities.
22950428.eligibility: Elderly patients who are being transferred from acute hospital wards to post-acute care (PAC) facilities.
22950428.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of ACEs (Relative Risk = 1)
- Score 1: Medium-low risk of ACEs (Relative Risk = 1.5, 95% CI 1.1-1.9)
- Score 2-3: Medium-high risk of ACEs (Relative Risk = 1.6, 95% CI 1.3-2.1)
- Score ≥4: High risk of ACEs (Relative Risk = 1.8, 95% CI 1.4-2.3)
22950428.utility: The risk score has been validated in a study with 502 patients. The relative risk of developing ACEs increased progressively from low to high risk score. The risk score can also be used to predict the likelihood of a patient not being discharged to home.

22972419.title: Preoperative Bilirubin and Creatinine Risk Score Calculator
22972419.code:
def compute_tb_score(age, preoperative_tb):
    """
    Compute the TB score.
    
    Parameters:
    age (int): The patient's age in years.
    preoperative_tb (float): The preoperative levels of total bilirubin in mg/dl.
    
    Returns:
    float: The TB score.
    """
    return 0.15 * age + 1.1 * preoperative_tb

def compute_cre_score(age, preoperative_cre):
    """
    Compute the Cre score.
    
    Parameters:
    age (int): The patient's age in years.
    preoperative_cre (float): The preoperative levels of creatinine in mg/dl.
    
    Returns:
    float: The Cre score.
    """
    return 0.2 * age + 3.6 * preoperative_cre
22972419.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the reversibility of liver or renal function after left ventricular assist device (LVAD) implantation in patients with stage D heart failure.
22972419.eligibility: Patients with stage D heart failure who are considering LVAD implantation are eligible.
22972419.interpretation:
- TB score > 11.0 points: High risk of persistent hepatic dysfunction
- Cre score > 14.1 points: High risk of persistent renal dysfunction
22972419.utility: The TB score and Cre score showed good predictabilities for persistent end-organ dysfunction with area under curve (AUC) of 0.794 and 0.839 respectively.

22977476.title: Modified Glasgow Prognostic Score (mGPS) for Colorectal Cancer
22977476.code:
def compute_mgps(crp, alb):
    """
    Compute the Modified Glasgow Prognostic Score (mGPS)

    Parameters:
    crp (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/dl
    alb (float): Albumin level in mg/dl

    Returns:
    int: mGPS score (0, 1, or 2)
    """
    if crp > 0.5 and alb < 3.5:
        return 2
    elif crp > 0.5 or alb < 3.5:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
22977476.purpose: This calculator is used to predict postoperative mortality in patients with stage II and/or III colorectal cancer.
22977476.eligibility: Patients with stage II and/or III colorectal cancer are eligible.
22977476.interpretation:
- mGPS 0: Low risk of postoperative mortality
- mGPS 1: Moderate risk of postoperative mortality
- mGPS 2: High risk of postoperative mortality
22977476.utility: The mGPS was found to be the most sensitive predictor of postoperative mortality in stage II/III or stage II colorectal cancer patients, independently of the CEA test or TNM system.

22981899.title: Acute Cardiac Events Risk Calculator for Community-Acquired Pneumonia Patients
22981899.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, chronic_heart_disease, chronic_kidney_disease, tachycardia, septic_shock, multilobar_pneumonia, hypoalbuminemia, pneumococcal_pneumonia):
    """
    Compute the risk score for acute cardiac events in CAP patients.

    Parameters:
    age (bool): True if patient is over 65 years old, False otherwise.
    chronic_heart_disease (bool): True if patient has chronic heart disease, False otherwise.
    chronic_kidney_disease (bool): True if patient has chronic kidney disease, False otherwise.
    tachycardia (bool): True if patient has tachycardia, False otherwise.
    septic_shock (bool): True if patient has septic shock, False otherwise.
    multilobar_pneumonia (bool): True if patient has multilobar pneumonia, False otherwise.
    hypoalbuminemia (bool): True if patient has hypoalbuminemia, False otherwise.
    pneumococcal_pneumonia (bool): True if patient has pneumococcal pneumonia, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    return age + chronic_heart_disease + chronic_kidney_disease + tachycardia + septic_shock + multilobar_pneumonia + hypoalbuminemia + pneumococcal_pneumonia
22981899.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of acute cardiac events in hospitalized adults with community-acquired pneumonia (CAP).
22981899.eligibility: Hospitalized adults diagnosed with community-acquired pneumonia are eligible.
22981899.interpretation:
- Score ≤3: Low risk (2.8% probability of acute cardiac events)
- Score 4-5: Intermediate risk (9.7% probability of acute cardiac events)
- Score ≥6: High risk (21.2% probability of acute cardiac events)
22981899.utility: The area under the ROC curve for this risk score is 0.73 (95% CI 0.70-0.76), indicating a moderate predictive ability.

23018151.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score Calculator
23018151.code:
def calculate_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, stroke_TIA_thromboembolism, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Calculate the CHA2DS2-VASc score based on the patient's risk factors.

    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): The patient's age.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    stroke_TIA_thromboembolism (bool): True if the patient has had a stroke, TIA, or thromboembolism, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): The patient's sex, either 'male' or 'female'.

    Returns:
    int: The CHA2DS2-VASc score.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if stroke_TIA_thromboembolism:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'female':
        score += 1
    return score
23018151.purpose: This calculator is used to predict stroke risk in patients with atrial fibrillation (AF), particularly those with a CHADS2 score of 1, for whom recommendations on antithrombotic treatment are uncertain.
23018151.eligibility: Patients with atrial fibrillation and a CHADS2 score of 1 are eligible for this calculator.
23018151.interpretation:
- CHA2DS2-VASc score of 1: The annual risk of ischaemic or unspecified stroke or systemic embolus (SSE) is 0.9%.
- CHA2DS2-VASc score of 2 or more: The annual risk of SSE is 2.1%.
23018151.utility: The c-statistic of the CHA2DS2-VASc score was 0.587 (95% CI: 0.550-0.624). Age 65 to <75 years was the strongest of the three new risk factors in the CHA2DS2-VASc score.

23021334.title: MARKED-Risk Score Calculator
23021334.code:
def compute_marked_risk_score(age, systolic_bp, history_of_heart_failure, dyspnea_class_IV, hs_cTnT, hs_CRP, Cys_C):
    """
    Compute the MARKED-risk score based on the patient's clinical and biomarker data.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Patient's age in years.
    systolic_bp (float): Patient's systolic blood pressure in mm Hg.
    history_of_heart_failure (bool): Whether the patient has a history of heart failure.
    dyspnea_class_IV (bool): Whether the patient has dyspnea New York Heart Association functional class IV.
    hs_cTnT (float): High-sensitivity cardiac troponin T level in μg/l.
    hs_CRP (float): High-sensitivity C-reactive protein level in mg/l.
    Cys_C (float): Cystatin-C level in mg/l.

    Returns:
    int: The MARKED-risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 75:
        score += 1
    if systolic_bp < 110:
        score += 1
    if history_of_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if dyspnea_class_IV:
        score += 1
    if hs_cTnT >= 0.04:
        score += 1
    if hs_CRP >= 25:
        score += 1
    if Cys_C >= 1.125:
        score += 1
    return score
23021334.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the short-term risk assessment in patients presenting to the emergency department (ED) with dyspnea.
23021334.eligibility: Patients presenting to the ED with dyspnea as the primary complaint are eligible.
23021334.interpretation:
- Score <3: Very low risk of 90-day mortality (2%)
- Score ≥3 and <5: Intermediate risk of 90-day mortality (14%)
- Score ≥5: High risk of 90-day mortality (44%)
23021334.utility: The MARKED-risk score had excellent prognostic performance with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.85 (95% CI: 0.81 to 0.89). It was robust in internal validation analyses and could accurately identify patients with very low, intermediate, and high risk.

23021708.title: Erectile Dysfunction Risk Calculator Following Radiation Therapy for Prostate Cancer
23021708.code:
def compute_risk_score(num_of_risk_alleles):
    """
    Compute the risk score for developing ED after radiation therapy for prostate cancer.

    Args:
    num_of_risk_alleles (int): The number of risk alleles a patient has.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score for developing ED.
    """
    return num_of_risk_alleles * 2.2
23021708.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing erectile dysfunction (ED) in prostate cancer patients undergoing radiation therapy based on the presence of certain single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs).
23021708.eligibility: Patients who are diagnosed with prostate cancer and are planned to undergo radiation therapy are eligible.
23021708.interpretation: The risk score indicates the odds of developing ED. A higher score indicates a higher risk. The exact risk interpretation depends on the individual's risk score. For example, a risk score of 2.2 indicates a 2.2 times higher odds of developing ED compared to someone without any risk alleles.
23021708.utility: The cumulative SNP score model had a sensitivity of 84% and specificity of 75% for prediction of developing ED at the radiation therapy planning stage.

23033268.title: International Prognostic Score of Thrombosis in Essential Thrombocythemia (IPSET-thrombosis)
23033268.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, thrombosis_history, cardiovascular_risk, JAK2V617F):
    """
    Compute the risk score for thrombosis in Essential Thrombocythemia (ET) patients.

    Parameters:
    age (bool): True if the patient's age is over 60, False otherwise.
    thrombosis_history (bool): True if the patient has a history of thrombosis, False otherwise.
    cardiovascular_risk (bool): True if the patient has cardiovascular risk factors, False otherwise.
    JAK2V617F (bool): True if the patient has the JAK2V617F mutation, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age:
        score += 1
    if thrombosis_history:
        score += 2
    if cardiovascular_risk:
        score += 1
    if JAK2V617F:
        score += 2
    return score
23033268.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of thrombosis in patients with Essential Thrombocythemia (ET).
23033268.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with World Health Organization (WHO)-defined Essential Thrombocythemia (ET).
23033268.interpretation:
- Score < 2: Low-risk, with a thrombosis risk of 1.03% per year.
- Score = 2: Intermediate-risk, with a thrombosis risk of 2.35% per year.
- Score > 2: High-risk, with a thrombosis risk of 3.56% per year.
23033268.utility: The 3-tiered new prognostic model outperformed the conventional 2-tiered risk stratification in predicting future vascular events.

23036917.title: Wells Score for Pulmonary Embolism Risk
23036917.code:
def wells_score(dvt, alt_diag, hr, immob, prev_pe_dvt, hemop, malign):
    """
    Calculate the Wells Score for Pulmonary Embolism Risk.

    Parameters:
    dvt (bool): Clinical signs and symptoms of DVT.
    alt_diag (bool): An alternative diagnosis is less likely than a PE.
    hr (bool): Heart rate over 100 beats per minute.
    immob (bool): Immobilization or surgery in the previous four weeks.
    prev_pe_dvt (bool): Previous objectively diagnosed PE or DVT.
    hemop (bool): Hemoptysis.
    malign (bool): Malignancy with treatment within 6 months, or palliative.

    Returns:
    float: The Wells Score for Pulmonary Embolism Risk.
    """
    score = 0
    if dvt:
        score += 3
    if alt_diag:
        score += 3
    if hr:
        score += 1.5
    if immob:
        score += 1.5
    if prev_pe_dvt:
        score += 1.5
    if hemop:
        score += 1
    if malign:
        score += 1
    return score
23036917.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the probability of pulmonary embolism in patients presenting with symptoms suggestive of this condition.
23036917.eligibility: Patients who are suspected of having pulmonary embolism.
23036917.interpretation:
- Wells Score ≤ 4 and a negative D-dimer test: Low risk of pulmonary embolism.
- Wells Score > 4 or a positive D-dimer test: High risk of pulmonary embolism.
23036917.utility: The sensitivity and specificity of this combined diagnostic approach was 94.5% (86.6% to 98.5%) and 51.0% (46.7% to 55.4%), respectively.

23059959.title: PABSI Risk Score Calculator
23059959.code:
def compute_pabsi_score(neutropenia, tnp, steroid_use, invasive_procedures, preoperative_antibiotics, postoperative_antibiotics):
    """
    Compute the PABSI risk score based on the presence of six factors.
    
    Parameters:
    neutropenia (bool): True if the patient has neutropenia, False otherwise.
    tnp (bool): True if the patient is on total parenteral nutrition, False otherwise.
    steroid_use (bool): True if the patient is on chronic steroid use, False otherwise.
    invasive_procedures (bool): True if the patient has undergone invasive procedures, False otherwise.
    preoperative_antibiotics (bool): True if the patient has taken preoperative antibiotics, False otherwise.
    postoperative_antibiotics (bool): True if the patient has taken postoperative antibiotics, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
