- Score 4: 12.3 per 100 patient-years mortality rate
- Score 5: 14.8 per 100 patient-years mortality rate
29759336.utility: The C statistic testing of the score was observed to be 0.685 for patients with ischemic cardiomyopathy, 0.658 for nonischemic cardiomyopathy, and 0.678 for those receiving cardiac resynchronization therapy.

29762340.title: Adult Spinal Deformity Frailty Index (ASD-FI)
29762340.code:
def compute_ASD_FI(frailty_level):
    """
    Compute the Adult Spinal Deformity Frailty Index (ASD-FI) score.

    Args:
    frailty_level (float): The frailty level of the patient.

    Returns:
    str: The ASD-FI category of the patient.
    """
    if frailty_level < 0.3:
        return 'Not Frail'
    elif 0.3 <= frailty_level <= 0.5:
        return 'Frail'
    else:
        return 'Severely Frail'
29762340.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the odds of major complications and length of hospital stay for patients with severe preoperative deformity undergoing invasive Adult Spinal Deformity (ASD) surgery.
29762340.eligibility: Patients with severe preoperative deformity who are undergoing invasive Adult Spinal Deformity (ASD) surgery.
29762340.interpretation:
- Not Frail (NF) (<0.3): Lower odds of developing a major complication compared to frail and severely frail patients. 
- Frail (0.3-0.5): Similar odds of developing a major complication or having increased length of stay as not frail patients.
- Severely Frail (SF) (>0.5): Higher odds of developing a major complication (odds ratio = 4.4; 95% CI 2.0, 9.9) compared with not frail patients. Length of hospital stay for severely frail patients increased by 19% (95% CI 1.4%, 39%) compared with not frail patients.
29762340.utility: The ASD-FI score is associated with a longer hospital stay and greater risk of major complications among patients who have severe preoperative deformity and undergo invasive surgical procedures.

29789335.title: CREDO-Kyoto Thrombotic and Bleeding Risk Scores
29789335.code:
def calculate_thrombotic_risk_score(severe_ckd, af, pvd, anemia, age, hf, dm, cto):
    """
    Calculate the thrombotic risk score based on the given parameters.
    Each parameter should be a boolean value (True if the condition is present, False otherwise).
    """
    score = 0
    if severe_ckd: score += 2
    if af: score += 2
    if pvd: score += 2
    if anemia: score += 2
    if age >= 75: score += 1
    if hf: score += 1
    if dm: score += 1
    if cto: score += 1
    return score

def calculate_bleeding_risk_score(thrombocytopenia, severe_ckd, pvd, hf, prior_mi, malignancy, af):
    """
    Calculate the bleeding risk score based on the given parameters.
    Each parameter should be a boolean value (True if the condition is present, False otherwise).
    """
    score = 0
    if thrombocytopenia: score += 2
    if severe_ckd: score += 2
    if pvd: score += 2
    if hf: score += 2
    if prior_mi: score += 1
    if malignancy: score += 1
    if af: score += 1
    return score
29789335.purpose: These calculators are used to predict the risk of thrombotic and bleeding events after percutaneous coronary intervention.
29789335.eligibility: Patients who have undergone percutaneous coronary intervention are eligible.
29789335.interpretation:
- Thrombotic risk score: 
  - 0-1: Low risk (2.4% 3-year incidence of thrombotic events)
  - 2-3: Intermediate risk (3.7% 3-year incidence of thrombotic events)
  - ≥4: High risk (7.6% 3-year incidence of thrombotic events)
- Bleeding risk score: 
  - 0: Low risk (2.3% incidence of bleeding)
  - 1-2: Intermediate risk (4.1% incidence of bleeding)
  - ≥3: High risk (8.8% incidence of bleeding)
29789335.utility: The area under the curve was 0.68 and 0.64, respectively, for thrombosis and 0.66 and 0.66, respectively, for bleeding.

29800321.title: Lymphoma-Associated Hemophagocytic Syndrome (LAHS) Diagnostic Index
29800321.code:
def compute_diagnostic_index(spleen_thickness, IL6_level, IL10_level):
    """
    Compute the diagnostic index for LAHS.

    Parameters:
    spleen_thickness (float): Thickness of spleen in cm.
    IL6_level (float): IL-6 level in pg/ml.
    IL10_level (float): IL-10 level in pg/ml.

    Returns:
    int: Diagnostic index for LAHS.
    """
    score = 0
    if spleen_thickness >= 5:
        score += 1
    if IL6_level >= 55.1:
        score += 1
    if IL10_level >= 425.9:
        score += 3
    return score
29800321.purpose: This calculator is used for early identification of LAHS in patients with hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH), based on laboratory findings.
29800321.eligibility: Patients with HLH who have undergone laboratory tests for spleen thickness, IL-6 level, and IL-10 level are eligible.
29800321.interpretation:
- Diagnostic index < 2: Low risk of LAHS. Further investigation for lymphoma may not be necessary.
- Diagnostic index ≥ 2: High risk of LAHS. Actively looking for evidence of lymphoma and effective chemotherapy may be necessary.
29800321.utility: The diagnostic index has an AUC value of 0.889, with 84.2% sensitivity and 93.7% specificity for predicting LAHS.

29856127.title: gDLBCL Prognostic (gDLP) Model
29856127.code:
def compute_gdlp_score(multiple_gastric_lesions, eber_positivity, b_symptoms):
    """
    Compute the gDLBCL Prognostic (gDLP) score.

    Parameters:
    multiple_gastric_lesions (bool): True if the patient has multiple gastric lesions, False otherwise.
    eber_positivity (bool): True if the patient is EBER positive, False otherwise.
    b_symptoms (bool): True if the patient has B symptoms, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The gDLP score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(multiple_gastric_lesions) + int(eber_positivity) + int(b_symptoms)
29856127.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the clinical outcome of patients with primary gastric diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (gDLBCL) based on three independent adverse prognostic factors.
29856127.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with primary gastric diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (gDLBCL).
29856127.interpretation:
- Score 0: Good risk group with a 5-year overall survival rate of 100%.
- Score 1: Intermediate risk group with a 5-year overall survival rate of 81%.
- Score 2 or 3: Poor risk group with a 5-year overall survival rate of 39%.
29856127.utility: The gDLP model significantly stratified the patients into three distinct risk groups (P < .0001), indicating a high clinical utility of the risk score.

29885826.title: ACEF Score Calculator for Acute Coronary Syndromes
29885826.code:
def calculate_acef_score(age, ejection_fraction, creatinine):
    """
    Calculate the ACEF score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    ejection_fraction (float): The left ventricular ejection fraction of the patient.
    creatinine (float): The creatinine level of the patient in μmol/L.

    Returns:
    float: The ACEF score.
    """
    acef_score = age / ejection_fraction
    if creatinine > 176:
        acef_score += 1
    return acef_score
29885826.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of all-cause mortality, major adverse cardiac and cerebrovascular events (MACCE), and transient ischemic attack/stroke in patients with acute coronary syndromes (ACS) undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention.
29885826.eligibility: Patients with acute coronary syndromes (ACS) who are referred for coronary revascularization are eligible.
29885826.interpretation:
- Low-risk: ACEF score ≤1.45
- Intermediate-risk: ACEF score >1.45 and ≤2.0
- High-risk: ACEF score >2.0

The one-year rates of all-cause death increase across ACEF score groups (1.6% for low-risk, 5.6% for intermediate-risk, and 23.0% for high-risk).
29885826.utility: In multivariate analysis, the ACEF score was related with an increased risk of all-cause mortality (adjusted HR 3.53, 95% CI 2.90-4.31, p < 0.001), MACCE (adjusted HR 2.23, 95% CI 1.88-2.65, p < 0.001), and transient ischemic attack/stroke (adjusted HR 2.58, 95% CI 1.71-3.89, p < 0.001) at 1 year.

29889814.title: qSOFA Score for Predicting Outcomes in Glyphosate Surfactant Herbicide Poisoning
29889814.code:
def compute_qsofa_score(respiratory_rate, systolic_bp, altered_mental_status):
    """
    Compute the qSOFA score for a patient with Glyphosate Surfactant Herbicide poisoning.

    Parameters:
    respiratory_rate (int): The patient's respiratory rate in breaths per minute.
    systolic_bp (int): The patient's systolic blood pressure in mm Hg.
    altered_mental_status (bool): Whether the patient has an altered mental status.

    Returns:
    int: The qSOFA score.
    """
    score = 0
    if respiratory_rate >= 22:
        score += 1
    if systolic_bp < 100:
        score += 1
    if altered_mental_status:
        score += 1
    return score
29889814.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the outcome of patients with Glyphosate Surfactant Herbicide (GlySH) poisoning immediately upon arrival at the emergency department.
29889814.eligibility: Adult patients with confirmed Glyphosate Surfactant Herbicide (GlySH) poisoning.
29889814.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of in-hospital mortality and life-threatening complications including organ injury.
- Score 1: Moderate risk of in-hospital mortality and life-threatening complications including organ injury.
- Score 2-3: High risk of in-hospital mortality and life-threatening complications including organ injury.
29889814.utility: The qSOFA score showed good prognostic performance with an area under curve (AUC) value of 0.841. It was independently associated with in-hospital mortality (odds ratio [OR], 2.73; 95% confidence interval [CI], 1.41-5.76).

29926549.title: TDL on Mechanical Ventilation (TDL-Vent) Score and Modified TDL-Vent Score
29926549.code:
def calculate_tdl_vent_score(age, vasopressor_use, pao2_fio2_ratio):
    """
    Calculate the TDL-Vent score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    vasopressor_use (bool): Whether the patient is using vasopressor.
    pao2_fio2_ratio (float): The ratio of arterial partial pressure of oxygen to fraction of inspired oxygen.

    Returns:
    int: The TDL-Vent score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if vasopressor_use:
        score += 1
    if pao2_fio2_ratio < 180:
        score += 1
    return score
29926549.purpose: These calculators are used to predict the mortality risk in patients with Tuberculous Destroyed Lung (TDL) who require mechanical ventilation.
29926549.eligibility: Patients with Tuberculous Destroyed Lung (TDL) who are receiving mechanical ventilation are eligible.
29926549.interpretation:
- TDL-Vent Score 0: 60-day mortality rate is 11%
- TDL-Vent Score 1: 60-day mortality rate is 27%
- TDL-Vent Score 2: 60-day mortality rate is 30%
- TDL-Vent Score 3: 60-day mortality rate is 77%

For the Modified TDL-Vent Score:
- Score 0: 60-day mortality rate is 0%
- Score 1: 60-day mortality rate is 21%
- Score 2: 60-day mortality rate is 33%
- Score 3 or more: 60-day mortality rate is 57%
29926549.utility: For both the TDL-Vent score and the modified TDL-Vent score, the areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve were larger than that of other illness severity scores.

29948130.title: Combined Objective Nutritional Score (CONS)
29948130.code:
def compute_CONS(CONUT, GNRI, PNI):
    """
    Compute the Combined Objective Nutritional Score (CONS)

    Parameters:
    CONUT (int): Controlling Nutritional Status score, range from 0 to 12.
    GNRI (float): Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index, range from 0 to 100.
    PNI (float): Prognostic Nutritional Index, range from 0 to 70.

    Returns:
    int: Combined Objective Nutritional Score (CONS), range from 0 to 3.
    """
    CONS = 0
    if CONUT >= 3:
        CONS += 1
    if GNRI < 98:
        CONS += 1
    if PNI < 45:
        CONS += 1
    return CONS
29948130.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the nutritional status of patients with coronary artery disease (CAD) who have undergone percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), and to predict their long-term cardiovascular outcomes.
29948130.eligibility: Patients with coronary artery disease who have undergone percutaneous coronary intervention.
29948130.interpretation:
- 0 points: Normal nutritional status
- 1-2 points: Mild-to-moderate malnutrition
- 3 points: Severe malnutrition

Patients with a CONS of 3 showed 2.91-fold and 2.16-fold increases in risk of mortality and cardiac mortality respectively compared with patients with a CONS of 0.
29948130.utility: The CONS was significantly associated with worse long-term cardiovascular outcomes among CAD patients who underwent PCI. The Kaplan-Meier curves showed ongoing divergence in rates of death among nutritional statuses determined by the CONS (log rank test, p < 0.0001).

29948391.title: S. aureus Bloodstream Infection Mortality Risk Calculator for Cancer Patients
29948391.code:
def compute_risk_score(abdominal_infection, hematologic_malignancy, MRSA, glucose_level, endocarditis, catheter_removal, treatment_initiation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for 30-day mortality in cancer patients with S. aureus bloodstream infections.

    Parameters:
    abdominal_infection (bool): Whether the patient has an abdominal source of infection.
    hematologic_malignancy (bool): Whether the patient has a hematologic malignancy.
    MRSA (bool): Whether the patient has MRSA.
    glucose_level (bool): Whether the patient's glucose level is > 140 mg/dL.
    endocarditis (bool): Whether the patient has infectious endocarditis.
    catheter_removal (bool): Whether the patient's catheter has been removed.
    treatment_initiation (bool): Whether the patient has initiated adequate treatment within 48 h of positive blood culture.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = abdominal_infection + hematologic_malignancy + MRSA + glucose_level + endocarditis - catheter_removal - treatment_initiation
    return score
29948391.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality risk in cancer patients with Staphylococcus aureus bloodstream infections (SABIs).
29948391.eligibility: Cancer patients with a positive blood culture for S. aureus are eligible.
29948391.interpretation: - Score > 3: 70.23% (95%CI 47.2-85.3%) probability of infection-related death at 30 days.
29948391.utility: The clinical utility of this risk score is not provided in the article.

29949088.title: Candidemia Risk Prediction Calculator
29949088.code:
def compute_risk_score(total_parenteral_nutrition, central_venous_catheter, peripherally_inserted_central_catheter, antibiotic_treatment_prior, antibiotic_treatment_during, neurological_disability, previous_hospitalization):
    """
    Compute the risk score for candidemia.

    Parameters:
    total_parenteral_nutrition (bool): True if the patient is on total parenteral nutrition, False otherwise.
    central_venous_catheter (bool): True if the patient has a central venous catheter, False otherwise.
    peripherally_inserted_central_catheter (bool): True if the patient has a peripherally inserted central catheter, False otherwise.
    antibiotic_treatment_prior (bool): True if the patient had antibiotic treatment prior to hospitalization, False otherwise.
    antibiotic_treatment_during (bool): True if the patient had antibiotic treatment during hospitalization, False otherwise.
    neurological_disability (bool): True if the patient has a neurological disability, False otherwise.
    previous_hospitalization (bool): True if the patient was hospitalized within the last 3 months, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for candidemia.
    """
    score = 0
    if total_parenteral_nutrition:
        score += 1
    if central_venous_catheter:
        score += 1
    if peripherally_inserted_central_catheter:
        score += 3
    if antibiotic_treatment_prior:
        score += 1
    if antibiotic_treatment_during:
        score += 1
    if neurological_disability:
        score += 1
    if previous_hospitalization:
        score += 1
    return score
29949088.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of candidemia in non-neutropenic patients admitted to Internal Medicine wards (IMWs).
29949088.eligibility: Non-neutropenic patients admitted to Internal Medicine wards are eligible for this calculator.
29949088.interpretation:
- Score < 4: Low risk of candidemia
- Score ≥ 4: High risk of candidemia
29949088.utility: The calculator showed 84% sensitivity, 76% specificity, and 80% accuracy in predicting the risk of candidemia.

29956048.title: Postoperative Complication Risk Score for Elderly Lung Cancer Patients
29956048.code:
def compute_risk_score(performance_status_2, coronary_artery_disease, history_of_cva, restrictive_ventilatory_impairment, male_sex, interstitial_pneumonia):
    """
    Compute the risk score for postoperative complications in elderly lung cancer patients.

    Args:
    performance_status_2 (bool): True if the patient has a performance status of 2, False otherwise.
    coronary_artery_disease (bool): True if the patient has coronary artery disease, False otherwise.
    history_of_cva (bool): True if the patient has a history of cerebrovascular accident, False otherwise.
    restrictive_ventilatory_impairment (bool): True if the patient has restrictive ventilatory impairment, False otherwise.
    male_sex (bool): True if the patient is male, False otherwise.
    interstitial_pneumonia (bool): True if the patient has interstitial pneumonia, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    return 7*performance_status_2 + 6*coronary_artery_disease + 3*history_of_cva + 2*restrictive_ventilatory_impairment + 1*male_sex + 1*interstitial_pneumonia
29956048.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of postoperative complications for elderly patients with lung cancer who underwent lobectomy.
29956048.eligibility: Patients aged 75 years or older who are undergoing lobectomy for lung cancer.
29956048.interpretation:
- Risk score 0: 19% risk of postoperative complications
- Risk score 1-2: 29% risk of postoperative complications
- Risk score 3-5: 56% risk of postoperative complications
- Risk score 6-8: 68% risk of postoperative complications
- Risk score 9-14: 90% risk of postoperative complications
29956048.utility: The proposed risk score was able to predict the incidence of postoperative complications. The risk score can be used to identify high-risk patients and to select proper treatment strategies.

29958849.title: The NAG Scale: Noble Predictive Scale for Hematoma Expansion in Intracerebral Hemorrhage
29958849.code:
def compute_nag_score(nihss, anticoagulant_use, plasma_glucose):
    """
    Compute the NAG score based on NIHSS, anticoagulant use, and plasma glucose level.

    Parameters:
    nihss (int): Baseline NIHSS.
    anticoagulant_use (bool): Whether the patient uses anticoagulants.
    plasma_glucose (float): Plasma glucose level in mg/dL.

    Returns:
    int: The NAG score.
    """
    score = 0
    if nihss >= 10:
        score += 1
    if anticoagulant_use:
        score += 1
    if plasma_glucose >= 133:
        score += 1
    return score
29958849.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of hematoma expansion (HE) in patients with acute intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH).
29958849.eligibility: Patients with primary ICH who received an initial non-contrast computed tomography (CT) scan within 24 hours from symptom onset.
29958849.interpretation:
- Score 0: 4% risk of HE
- Score 1: 25% risk of HE
- Score 2: 60% risk of HE
- Score 3: 100% risk of HE
29958849.utility: The NAG scale was able to predict HE in the study population with varying degrees of accuracy depending on the score. The higher the score, the higher the risk of HE.

29963263.title: Glioblastoma Gene Amplification Risk Stratification Calculator
29963263.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_amplification_profile):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the gene amplification profile.

    Parameters:
    gene_amplification_profile (dict): A dictionary where the key is the chromosomal region and the value is a boolean indicating whether there is gene amplification in that region.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    if not any(gene_amplification_profile.values()):
        return 0
    elif gene_amplification_profile.get('7p/EGFR', False):
        return 1
    else:
        return 2
29963263.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of patients with primary glioblastoma multiforme based on their tumor gene amplification profiles. It can potentially contribute to improved patient management.
29963263.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with primary glioblastoma multiforme are eligible.
29963263.interpretation:
- Score 0: Tumors with no gene amplification (55% of cases)
- Score 1: Tumors with chromosome 7p/EGFR gene amplification (with or without amplification of other chromosomal regions) (38% of cases)
- Score 2: Glioblastoma multiforme with a single (11%) or multiple (6%) amplified DNA sequences in chromosomal regions other than chromosome 7p.
29963263.utility: The gene amplification profiles showed a significant impact on overall survival of glioblastoma multiforme patients (p>0.001).

29966590.title: Acute Type A Aortic Dissection Operative Mortality Risk Score
29966590.code:
def compute_risk_score(lactic_acid, creatinine, liver_malperfusion):
    """
    Compute the risk score for operative mortality in acute type A aortic dissection.

    Parameters:
    lactic_acid (float): Lactic acid level in mmol/L.
    creatinine (float): Creatinine level in mg/dL.
    liver_malperfusion (bool): Whether liver malperfusion is present.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return 5.5 * lactic_acid + 8 * creatinine + (8 if liver_malperfusion else -8)
29966590.purpose: This calculator is used to predict operative mortality in patients presenting with acute type A aortic dissection using admission characteristics.
29966590.eligibility: Patients who are presenting with acute type A aortic dissection and are about to undergo type A repair surgery.
29966590.interpretation:
- Risk score < 7: Operative mortality rate is 4%
- Risk score 7 to 20: Operative mortality rate is 14%
- Risk score ≥ 20: Operative mortality rate is 37%
29966590.utility: The model generated a c-statistic of 0.75 in receiver-operating characteristic curve analysis, indicating a good predictive accuracy.

29976388.title: Hand Surgery Postoperative Complications Risk Score
29976388.code:
def compute_risk_score(hypoalbuminemia, congestive_heart_failure, anemia, elevated_creatinine, male_sex, tobacco_abuse, hyponatremia):
    """
    Compute the risk score for postoperative complications in hand surgery patients.

    Parameters:
    hypoalbuminemia (bool): True if the patient has hypoalbuminemia, False otherwise.
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    anemia (bool): True if the patient has anemia, False otherwise.
    elevated_creatinine (bool): True if the patient has elevated creatinine, False otherwise.
    male_sex (bool): True if the patient is male, False otherwise.
    tobacco_abuse (bool): True if the patient abuses tobacco, False otherwise.
    hyponatremia (bool): True if the patient has hyponatremia, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if hypoalbuminemia:
        score += 5
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 2
    if anemia:
        score += 2
    if elevated_creatinine:
        score += 2
    if male_sex:
        score += 1
    if tobacco_abuse:
        score += 1
    if hyponatremia:
        score += 1
    return score
29976388.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of general postoperative complications in patients undergoing hand surgery.
29976388.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing hand surgery procedures.
29976388.interpretation:
- 0-3: Low risk, with a 2.4% rate of 30-day complications
- 4-7: Medium risk, with a 10.4% complication rate
- 8 and above: High risk, with a 28.9% complication rate
29976388.utility: The risk score has been validated on a subset of patients from the database who had undergone hand surgery. The relative risk for medium risk patients compared to low risk patients is 4.3 (95% confidence interval, 3.1-5.9), and for high risk patients compared to low risk patients is 11.9 (95% confidence interval, 9.0-15.7).

29976504.title: Radiation Pneumonitis Risk Calculator for NSCLC Patients
29976504.code:
def calculate_risk(V10, V20):
    """
    Calculate the risk group of radiation pneumonitis based on lung V10 and V20 values.

    Parameters:
    V10 (float): The percentage of the lung receiving a dose of 10 Gy.
    V20 (float): The percentage of the lung receiving a dose of 20 Gy.

    Returns:
    string: The risk group ("High", "Intermediate", "Low").
    """
    if V10 > 30 and V20 > 20:
        return "High"
    elif V10 > 30 and V20 <= 20 or V10 <= 30 and V20 > 20:
        return "Intermediate"
    else:
        return "Low"
29976504.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of grade ≥2 radiation pneumonitis (RP) for patients undergoing postoperative radiation therapy (PORT) for non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).
29976504.eligibility: Patients who have undergone postoperative radiation therapy for non-small cell lung cancer are eligible.
29976504.interpretation:
- High risk: 33% chance of developing grade ≥2 RP
- Intermediate risk: 23% chance of developing grade ≥2 RP
- Low risk: 6% chance of developing grade ≥2 RP
29976504.utility: The risk calculator was able to stratify patients into high, intermediate, and low risk groups with significant differences in the incidence of grade ≥2 RP (P < .0001).

30005037.title: Modified Cervical Deformity Frailty Index (mCD-FI)
30005037.code:
def calculate_mcd_fi(deficits):
    """
    Calculate the Modified Cervical Deformity Frailty Index (mCD-FI).

    Args:
    deficits (list of int): A list of 15 health deficits, each represented as 0 (absent) or 1 (present).

    Returns:
    float: The mCD-FI score, ranging from 0 to 1.
    """
    return sum(deficits) / len(deficits)
30005037.purpose: This calculator is used for preoperative risk stratification in cervical deformity (CD) patients.
30005037.eligibility: Patients over 18 years old with cervical deformity (C2-C7 Cobb>10°, CL>10°, cSVA>4 cm, or CBVA>25°).
30005037.interpretation:
- Not Frail (NF): mCD-FI < 0.3
- Frail: 0.3 <= mCD-FI <= 0.5
- Severely Frail (SF): mCD-FI > 0.5
30005037.utility: Increased frailty, assessed by mCD-FI, correlated with increased length of stay, neck pain, and decreased health-related quality of life. Frail patients were at greater risk for infection, and severely frail patients had greater odds of mortality.

30026252.title: Kawasaki Disease IVIG Resistance Predictive Tool
30026252.code:
def compute_risk_score(crp, neutrophil_percentage, sodium_ion_concentration, albumin, total_bilirubin):
    """
    Compute the risk score for IVIG resistance in Kawasaki disease.

    Parameters:
    crp (float): C reactive protein level in mg/L.
    neutrophil_percentage (float): Neutrophil percentage.
    sodium_ion_concentration (float): Sodium ion concentration in mmol/L.
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/L.
    total_bilirubin (float): Total bilirubin level in μmol/L.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if crp >= 90:
        score += 3
    if neutrophil_percentage >= 70:
        score += 2.5
    if sodium_ion_concentration < 135:
        score += 3
    if albumin < 35:
        score += 2.5
    if total_bilirubin > 20:
        score += 5
    return score
30026252.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the efficacy of intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) therapy in children with Kawasaki disease (KD) in Beijing, China.
30026252.eligibility: Children diagnosed with Kawasaki disease are eligible for this calculator.
30026252.interpretation:
- A total score of less than 6 points is considered low-risk for IVIG resistance.
- A total score of 6 points or more is considered high-risk for IVIG resistance.
30026252.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve was 0.77 for the internal validation data set, and 0.69 and 0.63 for two external validation data sets, respectively. If a total of ≥6 points were considered high-risk for IVIG resistance, sensitivity and specificity were 56% and 79% in the internal verification, and the predictive ability was similar in the external validation.

30027102.title: Fibrinogen/Albumin Ratio Prognostic Score for Hepatocellular Carcinoma
30027102.code:
def compute_ratio(fibrinogen_level, albumin_level):
    """
    Compute the fibrinogen/albumin ratio.

    Args:
    fibrinogen_level (float): The fibrinogen level in the blood.
    albumin_level (float): The albumin level in the blood.

    Returns:
    float: The fibrinogen/albumin ratio.
    """
    return fibrinogen_level / albumin_level
30027102.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) who have undergone curative resection, based on their preoperative fibrinogen/albumin ratio.
30027102.eligibility: Patients who have been diagnosed with hepatocellular carcinoma and have undergone curative resection are eligible.
30027102.interpretation:
- Fibrinogen/Albumin Ratio ≤ 0.062: Lower risk of recurrence and higher survival rates. 1-, 3-, and 5-year overall survival rates are 81.9%, 69.3%, and 56.1% respectively. 1-, 3-, and 5-year recurrence rates are 49.5%, 69.1%, and 77.1% respectively.
- Fibrinogen/Albumin Ratio > 0.062: Higher risk of recurrence and lower survival rates. 1-, 3-, and 5-year overall survival rates are 66.0%, 41.8%, and 28.2% respectively. 1-, 3-, and 5-year recurrence rates are 60.9%, 79.2%, and 90.5% respectively.
30027102.utility: The fibrinogen/albumin ratio was found to be an independent predictor for overall survival (P = 0.003) and time to recurrence (P = 0.035). Its prognostic ability was comparable to other prognostic scores (NLR, PLR, and ALBI score) by AUC analysis.

30042224.title: IgA Nephropathy Progression Risk Calculator
30042224.code:
def calculate_risk_score(rs11150612, rs7634389, rs2412971, rs2856717):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on the presence of four SNPs.
    
    Args:
    rs11150612 (bool): Presence of SNP rs11150612
    rs7634389 (bool): Presence of SNP rs7634389
    rs2412971 (bool): Presence of SNP rs2412971
    rs2856717 (bool): Presence of SNP rs2856717
    
    Returns:
    int: The total risk score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    return rs11150612 + rs7634389 + rs2412971 + rs2856717
30042224.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of disease progression in patients with IgA nephropathy based on four genetic markers.
30042224.eligibility: Adult patients diagnosed with IgA nephropathy are eligible for this calculator.
30042224.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low genetic risk
- Score 1-2: Middle genetic risk, 2.12-fold increase of progression risk
- Score 3-4: High genetic risk, 3.61-fold increase of progression risk
30042224.utility: The genetic risk score was independently associated with IgA nephropathy progression and could enhance the performance of clinical and clinical-pathologic risk models. The c-statistic increased from 0.83 to 0.86 for the clinical model and from 0.82 to 0.85 for the clinical-pathologic model when incorporating the genetic risk score.

30044045.title: Anticholinergic Risk Scale Score Calculator for Hip Fracture in Geriatric Patients
30044045.code:
def calculate_risk(anticholinergic_score):
    """
    Calculate the risk of hip fracture based on the Anticholinergic Risk Scale score.

    Parameters:
    anticholinergic_score (int): The Anticholinergic Risk Scale score of the patient.

    Returns:
    float: The risk of hip fracture.
    """
    if anticholinergic_score == 2:
        return 2.86
    elif anticholinergic_score >= 3:
        return 4.21
    else:
        return 1
30044045.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of hip fracture in geriatric patients based on their Anticholinergic Risk Scale score.
30044045.eligibility: Geriatric patients who are admitted and discharged from the convalescent rehabilitation ward and are on anticholinergic drugs.
30044045.interpretation:
- An Anticholinergic Risk Scale score of 2 corresponds to a 2.86-fold greater risk for hip fracture.
- An Anticholinergic Risk Scale score of ≥3 corresponds to a 4.21-fold greater risk for hip fracture.
30044045.utility: The study found a statistically significant correlation between anticholinergic drug use and hip fracture, indicating that the Anticholinergic Risk Scale score can be a useful predictor of hip fracture risk in geriatric patients.

30050733.title: Venous Thromboembolism Risk Score in Glioblastoma Patients
30050733.code:
def compute_vte_risk_score(KPS, age, smoking, hypertension):
    """
    Compute the VTE risk score for a patient with Glioblastoma.

    Parameters:
    KPS (int): Karnofsky performance status
    age (int): Age of the patient
    smoking (bool): Whether the patient is a current smoker
    hypertension (bool): Whether the patient has hypertension

    Returns:
    int: The VTE risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if KPS == 70:
        score += 1
    elif KPS < 70:
        score += 2

    if 45 <= age <= 60:
        score += 1
    elif 61 <= age <= 70:
        score += 2

    if smoking:
        score += 1

    if hypertension:
        score += 1

    return score
30050733.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of developing Venous Thromboembolism (VTE) in patients with Glioblastoma (GB) undergoing first-line chemoradiotherapy.
30050733.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Glioblastoma and are undergoing first-line chemoradiotherapy.
30050733.interpretation:
- Score <= 3: The patient is at normal risk of developing VTE.
- Score > 3: The patient is 5 times more likely to develop a VTE.
30050733.utility: The scoring system allows the identification of patients with GB receiving first-line therapy, who are at the highest risk of VTE. The clinical utility of the risk score is yet to be validated in an independent series.

30056546.title: Nodal Peripheral T-cell Lymphomas Risk Stratification Model
30056546.code:
def calculate_risk(deauville_score, nccn_ipi):
    """
    Calculate the risk group based on Deauville score and NCCN-IPI.

    Parameters:
    deauville_score (int): Posttreatment Deauville score (1-5)
    nccn_ipi (str): Pretreatment NCCN-IPI ('low', 'low-intermediate', 'high-intermediate', 'high')

    Returns:
    str: Risk group ('low-risk', 'high-risk', 'treatment failure')
    """
    if (nccn_ipi in ['low', 'low-intermediate'] and deauville_score in [1, 2]) or (nccn_ipi == 'low' and deauville_score == 3):
        return 'low-risk'
    elif (nccn_ipi in ['high', 'high-intermediate'] and deauville_score in [1, 2, 3]) or (nccn_ipi == 'low-intermediate' and deauville_score == 3):
        return 'high-risk'
    elif deauville_score in [4, 5]:
        return 'treatment failure'
30056546.purpose: This calculator is used to predict treatment failure in patients with nodal Peripheral T-cell Lymphomas (PTCLs) by integrating posttreatment Deauville score and pretreatment National Comprehensive Cancer Network-International Prognostic Index (NCCN-IPI).
30056546.eligibility: Patients with newly diagnosed nodal PTCLs who have both baseline and posttreatment PET/CT data are eligible.
30056546.interpretation:
- 'low-risk': 5-year progression-free survival rate of 70.3% and overall survival rate of 82.1%
- 'high-risk': 5-year progression-free survival rate of 31.4% and overall survival rate of 45.5%
- 'treatment failure': 5-year progression-free survival rate of 4.7% and overall survival rate of 14.7%
30056546.utility: The risk-stratification model was significantly associated with progression-free survival (P < 0.001) and overall survival (P < 0.001). Similar associations were also observed in the independent validation cohort.

30064156.title: Visceral Adiposity Index (VAI) Calculator for Cardiometabolic Disease Risk in Elderly Women
30064156.code:
def calculate_vai(waist_circumference, bmi, triglycerides, hdl):
    """
    Calculate the Visceral Adiposity Index (VAI).
    
    Parameters:
    waist_circumference (float): Waist circumference in cm
    bmi (float): Body Mass Index
    triglycerides (float): Triglycerides level in mmol/L
    hdl (float): High-Density Lipoprotein level in mmol/L

    Returns:
    float: VAI score
    """
    vai = (waist_circumference / (39.68 + (1.88 * bmi))) * (triglycerides / 1.03) * (1.31 / hdl)
    return vai
30064156.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of cardiometabolic diseases in elderly women using the Visceral Adiposity Index (VAI).
30064156.eligibility: Elderly women aged between 65 to 74 years are eligible for this calculator.
30064156.interpretation:
- VAI < 2.71: Low risk of cardiometabolic diseases
- VAI >= 2.71: High risk of cardiometabolic diseases. Routine screening for cardiovascular disease, abnormal glucose metabolism, and impaired kidney function is recommended.
30064156.utility: The VAI demonstrated a better correlation with cardiometabolic disease endpoints than BMI or waist circumference. It was significantly correlated with a history of myocardial infarction, higher carotid intima-media thickness, diabetes, prediabetes, and impaired kidney function.

30065497.title: Ultrasound Scoring System for Preoperative Prediction of Difficult Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
30065497.code:
def compute_score(gb_wall_thickness, distended_gb, impacted_stones, cbd_diameter, pericholecystic_collection, multiple_stones, liver_size):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on ultrasound parameters.

    Parameters:
    gb_wall_thickness (bool): True if GB wall thickness is present, False otherwise.
    distended_gb (bool): True if distended GB is present, False otherwise.
    impacted_stones (bool): True if impacted stones are present, False otherwise.
    cbd_diameter (bool): True if CBD diameter is present, False otherwise.
    pericholecystic_collection (bool): True if pericholecystic collection is present, False otherwise.
    multiple_stones (bool): True if multiple stones are present, False otherwise.
    liver_size (bool): True if liver size is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if gb_wall_thickness:
        score += 2
    if distended_gb:
        score += 2
    if impacted_stones:
        score += 2
    if cbd_diameter:
        score += 2
    if pericholecystic_collection:
        score += 1
    if multiple_stones:
        score += 1
    if liver_size:
        score += 1
    return score
30065497.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the difficulty of Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy (LC) and the potential need for conversion to Open Cholecystectomy (OC).
30065497.eligibility: Patients who are scheduled to undergo Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy for cholelithiasis.
30065497.interpretation:
- Score <= 5: The LC is predicted to be easy.
- Score > 5: The LC is predicted to be difficult and there is a high risk of conversion to OC.
30065497.utility: The scoring system has a sensitivity of 80.7% and specificity of 91.7% for correctly identifying difficult LC. The prediction came true in 78.8% of difficult cases and 92.6% of easy cases.

30094644.title: HUS Severity Score Calculator
30094644.code:
def calculate_hus_severity_score(hb, scr):
    """
    Calculate the HUS Severity Score.

    Parameters:
    hb (float): Hemoglobin level at presentation in g/dL.
    scr (float): Serum creatinine level at presentation in mg/dL.

    Returns:
    float: HUS Severity Score.
    """
    return hb + (scr * 2)
30094644.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the short- and long-term outcomes in patients with Shigatoxin Escherichia coli-related hemolytic uremic syndrome (eHUS) at onset.
30094644.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Shigatoxin Escherichia coli-related hemolytic uremic syndrome (eHUS) are eligible.
30094644.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 13: Good outcome, no patients in this range died or entered a permanent vegetative state.
- Score > 13: High risk of worse short- and long-term outcome. 8% of patients in this range died or entered a permanent vegetative state.
30094644.utility: The HUS Severity Score showed a good predictive ability with an AUC of 0.75. It was strongly associated and predictive of all complications and health care needs.

30103018.title: Graft-versus-Host Disease-Free, Relapse-Free Survival (GRFS) Risk Calculator
30103018.code:
def compute_risk_score(circulating_blast, cytogenetic_risk, comorbidity_index, conditioning, ATG_dose):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on five factors.
    
    Parameters:
    circulating_blast (bool): True if circulating blast > 3%, False otherwise.
    cytogenetic_risk (bool): True if high cytogenetic risk, False otherwise.
    comorbidity_index (bool): True if high comorbidity index, False otherwise.
    conditioning (bool): True if myeloablative conditioning (MAC), False otherwise.
    ATG_dose (float): The dose of antithymocyte globulin (ATG).
    
    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if circulating_blast:
        score += 1
    if cytogenetic_risk:
        score += 1
    if comorbidity_index:
        score += 1
    if conditioning:
        score += 1
    if ATG_dose < 7.5:
        score += 1
    return score
30103018.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 3-year GRFS rate after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) for patients with myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) or secondary acute myeloid leukemia evolved from MDS.
30103018.eligibility: Patients who have received HSCT with fludarabine and busulfan-based conditioning for MDS or secondary acute myeloid leukemia evolved from MDS.
30103018.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk, with a 3-year GRFS rate of 64.9%
- Score 2: Intermediate risk, with a 3-year GRFS rate of 33.6%
- Score 3-5: High risk, with a 3-year GRFS rate of 6.6%
30103018.utility: The risk score was found to be significantly associated with the 3-year GRFS rate (P < .001).

30114222.title: Pulmonary Hypertension Outcome Risk Score (PHORS)
30114222.code:
def compute_PHORS(functional_status, ASA_class, surgical_approach, procedure_duration, systemic_hypertension):
    """
    Compute the Pulmonary Hypertension Outcome Risk Score (PHORS).
    
    Parameters:
    functional_status (float): <4 METs or not
    ASA_class (int): ASA class of the patient
    surgical_approach (str): 'open' or 'minimally invasive'
    procedure_duration (float): duration of the procedure in hours
    systemic_hypertension (bool): presence of systemic hypertension
    
    Returns:
    int: PHORS
    """
    PHORS = 0
    if functional_status < 4:
        PHORS += 1
    if ASA_class > 2:
        PHORS += 1
    if surgical_approach == 'open':
        PHORS += 1
    if procedure_duration > 2:
        PHORS += 1
    if not systemic_hypertension:
        PHORS += 1
    return PHORS
30114222.purpose: This calculator is used to predict post-procedure outcomes in non-cardiac surgery patients with pulmonary hypertension.
30114222.eligibility: Patients with pulmonary hypertension (WHO Class I-V) undergoing non-cardiac, non-obstetric anesthesia and surgery.
30114222.interpretation:
- PHORS Score <2: Lower risk of post-procedure complications
- PHORS Score ≥2: Higher risk of post-procedure complications, with a 30-day major complication rate of 28.7% and ICU admission rate of 8.6%.
30114222.utility: The PHORS model predicts increased length of hospital stay with fair accuracy. However, it does not significantly predict hospital readmissions rate or mortality.

30116261.title: Preoperative Systemic Immune Score (SIS) Calculator for Stage I Gastric Cancer Patients
30116261.code:
def compute_sis(hypoalbuminemia, elevated_dNLR):
    """
    Compute the Systemic Immune Score (SIS) for a patient.

    Parameters:
    hypoalbuminemia (bool): True if the patient has pretreatment hypoalbuminemia, False otherwise.
    elevated_dNLR (bool): True if the patient has elevated derived neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio (dNLR), False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The SIS score, ranging from 0 to 2.
    """
    return int(hypoalbuminemia) + int(elevated_dNLR)
30116261.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the benefits of adjuvant chemotherapy (AC) for stage I gastric cancer (GC) patients.
30116261.eligibility: Patients with pathologically confirmed stage I gastric cancer who underwent R0 gastrectomy are eligible.
30116261.interpretation:
- SIS = 0 or 1: These patients are considered low-risk and may not significantly benefit from adjuvant chemotherapy.
- SIS = 2: These patients are considered high-risk and may significantly benefit from adjuvant chemotherapy.
30116261.utility: The SIS served as an independent prognostic factor for reduced overall survival (OS). In the high-risk group (SIS = 2), patients with AC had a significantly better OS than those undergoing surgery alone.

30120668.title: CR-PF Risk Score Calculator
30120668.code:
def compute_risk_score(VFA, blood_loss, drainage_type):
    """
    Compute the risk score for CR-PF following DP.

    Parameters:
    VFA (float): Visceral Fat Area in cm2.
    blood_loss (float): Blood loss in ml.
    drainage_type (str): Type of drainage used. Can be 'open' or 'closed'.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if VFA >= 92:
        score += 1
    if blood_loss >= 225:
        score += 1
    if drainage_type == 'open':
        score += 1
    return score
30120668.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of clinically relevant pancreatic fistula (CR-PF) following distal pancreatectomy (DP).
30120668.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing distal pancreatectomy are eligible.
30120668.interpretation:
- 0 points: 0% risk of CR-PF
- 1-3 points: Risk of CR-PF increases with each point, up to a 39% risk when all 3 risk factors are present.
30120668.utility: The calculator was developed based on a retrospective cohort analysis of a single-institution database of patients undergoing DP. The predictive accuracy of the calculator was not provided in the article.

30122419.title: Takayasu Arteritis Mortality Risk Calculator
30122419.code:
def compute_risk_score(progressive_course, thoracic_aorta_involvement, retinopathy):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with Takayasu Arteritis.

    Parameters:
    progressive_course (bool): True if the patient has a progressive clinical course, False otherwise.
    thoracic_aorta_involvement (bool): True if the patient has thoracic aorta involvement, False otherwise.
    retinopathy (bool): True if the patient has retinopathy, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for the patient.
    """
    return int(progressive_course) + int(thoracic_aorta_involvement) + int(retinopathy)
30122419.purpose: This calculator is used to identify Takayasu Arteritis (TA) patients at highest risk for death and vascular complications.
30122419.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Takayasu Arteritis are eligible.
30122419.interpretation:
- Score 0 or 1: Low risk group. The 5-year incidence of death and vascular complication is 21.6%.
- Score 2 or 3: High risk group. The 5-year incidence of death and vascular complication is 48.5%.
30122419.utility: The risk score was able to distinguish between high and low risk patients with a significant difference in 5-year incidence of death and vascular complications (p = 0.001).

30138917.title: Micro-Vascular Occlusion Risk Calculator
30138917.code:
def compute_risk_score(second_peak_troponin, crp_level):
    """
    Compute the risk score for MVO after MI.

    Parameters:
    second_peak_troponin (bool): Whether a second peak of hs-T-Troponin is observed.
    crp_level (float): The CRP level at day 2.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score. 2 if both criteria are met, 1 if only one criterion is met, 0 otherwise.
    """
    return int(second_peak_troponin) + int(crp_level > 40)
30138917.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of Micro-Vascular Occlusion (MVO) after a myocardial infarction (MI).
30138917.eligibility: Patients who have been hospitalized for MI, had Thrombolysis In Myocardial Infarction flow 0 at coronary angiography, reperfusion within 12 h from the onset of chest pain, cardiac MRI within the first month, and a 5-days' biological follow-up with at least hs-T-Troponin and C-reactive protein (CRP).
30138917.interpretation:
- Score 0: MVO is absent in 78% of the cases.
- Score 1: The risk of MVO is uncertain.
- Score 2: MVO is present in 95% of the cases.
30138917.utility: The risk score was associated with a higher rate of hospitalization at 2 years. Further evaluation of the clinical utility of the risk score is needed.

30143376.title: Lymph Node Metastasis Risk Stratification System for Superficial Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma
30143376.code:
def compute_risk_score(tumor_size, depth_of_invasion, lymphovascular_invasion):
    """
    Compute the risk score for lymph node metastasis in patients with superficial esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.

    Parameters:
    tumor_size (int): Size of the tumor in millimeters. Score 0 if <=15mm, Score 2 if >15mm.
    depth_of_invasion (str): Depth of tumor invasion. Score 0 if mucosal invasion, Score 2 if submucosal invasion.
    lymphovascular_invasion (bool): Presence of lymphovascular invasion. Score 0 if No, Score 2 if Yes.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score, ranging from 0 to 6.
    """
    score = 0
    if tumor_size > 15:
        score += 2
    if depth_of_invasion == 'submucosal':
        score += 2
    if lymphovascular_invasion:
        score += 2
    return score
30143376.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of lymph node metastasis in patients with superficial esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
30143376.eligibility: Patients who have undergone endoscopic resection or surgery for superficial esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
30143376.interpretation:
- Score 0: No risk of lymph node metastasis.
- Score <3: Low risk of lymph node metastasis (1.6%).
- Score >=3: High risk of lymph node metastasis (26.5%).
30143376.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve for predicting lymph node metastasis was 0.869 (95% confidence interval, 0.813-0.926). The sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, negative predictive value, and accuracy of the risk stratification system were 94.7%, 55.4%, 26.5%, 98.4%, and 61.1%, respectively.

30151937.title: FLASH Score for Gastrointestinal Events in Lymphoma Patients
30151937.code:
def compute_flash_score(focal_large, aggressive_small_bowel, high_fdg_uptake):
    """
    Compute the FLASH score for a patient with gastrointestinal lymphoma.

    Args:
    focal_large (bool): True if the patient has a focal appearance and large size (≥ 40 mm), False otherwise.
    aggressive_small_bowel (bool): True if the patient has aggressive lymphoma of the small bowel, False otherwise.
    high_fdg_uptake (bool): True if the patient has high (18)F-fluorodeoxyglucose positron emission tomography uptake, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The FLASH score for the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if focal_large:
        score += 1
    if aggressive_small_bowel:
        score += 2
    if high_fdg_uptake:
        score += 1
    return score
30151937.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of severe gastrointestinal (GI) events requiring surgery in patients with gastrointestinal lymphoma.
30151937.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with gastrointestinal lymphoma are eligible for this risk score calculation.
30151937.interpretation:
- 0 points: Low risk group with a 0% rate of severe GI events.
- 1-2 points: Intermediate risk group with a 9% rate of severe GI events.
- 3-4 points: High risk group with a 61% rate of severe GI events.
30151937.utility: The FLASH score predicted severe GI events with an AUC value of 0.91 (internal validation; AUC, 0.86). The risk was classified into three categories with an AUC of 0.89.

30153377.title: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) Risk Score in Alcoholic Hepatitis (AH) Patients
30153377.code:
def compute_aki_risk_score(hepatic_encephalopathy, sirs, meld_score):
    """
    Compute the AKI risk score for a patient with alcoholic hepatitis.

    Parameters:
    hepatic_encephalopathy (bool): True if the patient has hepatic encephalopathy, False otherwise.
    sirs (bool): True if the patient has systemic inflammatory response syndrome, False otherwise.
    meld_score (int): The patient's Model for End-Stage Liver Disease score at admission.

    Returns:
    float: The patient's AKI risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if hepatic_encephalopathy:
        score += 3.86
    if sirs:
        score += 2.24
    score += 1.14 * meld_score
    return score
30153377.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of inpatient AKI in patients admitted with severe alcoholic hepatitis (AH).
30153377.eligibility: Patients admitted with severe alcoholic hepatitis (AH) are eligible for this calculator.
30153377.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk of inpatient AKI
- Score 3-4: Moderate risk of inpatient AKI
- Score > 4: High risk of inpatient AKI
30153377.utility: The AKI risk score was found to be a significant predictor of inpatient AKI in a validation cohort. Patients with a score of >4 had significantly lower 90-day survival compared to those with lower scores.

30157792.title: SPAN-100 Index and SPAN65-100 Index
30157792.code:
def compute_span_index(age, nihss_score):
    """
    Compute the SPAN index.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    nihss_score (int): The baseline NIHSS-score of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The SPAN index.
    """
    return age + nihss_score
30157792.purpose: These calculators are used to predict clinical outcomes after acute ischemic stroke (AIS) in patients receiving intravenous thrombolysis (IVT).
30157792.eligibility: Patients who have suffered an acute ischemic stroke and are receiving intravenous thrombolysis. For the SPAN65-100 Index, patients must be 65 years or older.
30157792.interpretation:
- SPAN-100-positive (index ≥100): 9-fold increased risk for unfavorable outcome compared to SPAN-negative patients. The odds ratio for mortality is 7.48.
- SPAN65-100-positive (index ≥100): 8-fold increased risk for unfavorable outcome compared to SPAN-negative patients.
30157792.utility: The SPAN-100 Index and SPAN65-100 Index can be a fast, easy method to predict clinical outcome of IVT-patients in everyday practice. However, it should not be used to determine the risk of symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage (sICH) after IVT.

30158383.title: RVAD Requirement Prediction Score
30158383.code:
def calculate_risk_score(PVR, RAP, PAWP):
    """
    Calculate the risk score for RVAD requirement.

    Parameters:
    PVR (float): Pulmonary Vascular Resistance in Wood units.
    RAP (float): Mean Right Atrial Pressure in mmHg.
    PAWP (float): Mean Pulmonary Artery Wedge Pressure in mmHg.

    Returns:
    int: Risk score for RVAD requirement.
    """
    score = 0
    if PVR > 4.5:
        score += 1
    if RAP/PAWP > 0.8:
        score += 2
    return score
30158383.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the requirement of a Right Ventricular Assist Device (RVAD) in patients who have undergone paracorporeal Ventricular Assist Device (VAD) implantation.
30158383.eligibility: Patients who have undergone paracorporeal VAD implantation are eligible for this calculator.
30158383.interpretation:
- Score 0: 4.4% incidence of RVAD implantation
- Score 1: 28.6% incidence of RVAD implantation
- Score 2: 41.7% incidence of RVAD implantation
- Score 3: 83.3% incidence of RVAD implantation
30158383.utility: The scoring system adequately stratified the incidence of RVAD implantation. However, the article does not provide specific evaluation results such as AUC, F-score, or PPV.

30170439.title: Ultrasonic Scoring System for Placenta Accreta Prognosis
30170439.code:
def compute_risk_group(score):
    """
    Compute the risk group based on the ultrasonic score.

    Args:
    score (int): The ultrasonic score.

    Returns:
    str: The risk group (N1, N2, or N3).
    """
    if score <= 5:
        return 'N1'
    elif 6 <= score <= 9:
        return 'N2'
    else:
        return 'N3'
30170439.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of placenta accreta, including the associated risk of bleeding and the possibility of hysterectomy.
30170439.eligibility: Patients who are suspiciously diagnosed with placenta accreta before delivery are eligible.
30170439.interpretation:
- N1 group (score ≤5): Lower risk of hemorrhage during operation (median quantities of 400 mL) and 0.0% uterine resection rate.
- N2 group (score between 6 and 9): Moderate risk of hemorrhage during operation (median quantities of 1200 mL) and 11.1% uterine resection rate.
- N3 group (score ≥10): Higher risk of hemorrhage during operation (median quantities of 4000 mL) and 39.3% uterine resection rate.
30170439.utility: The prediction accuracy rates were 87.6% and 92.0%, respectively in groups 1 and 3. The Kappa value was 0.75\0.77 for the prediction accuracy rate.

30172349.title: Tibial Nonunion Risk Prediction Calculator
30172349.code:
def calculate_nonunion_risk(infection, rust, nurd):
    """
    Calculate the risk of nonunion for tibial shaft fracture.

    Parameters:
    infection (bool): Whether the patient had an infection within 6 weeks of operation.
    rust (int): The Radiographic Union Score for Tibial fractures (RUST). It ranges from 0 to 12.
    nurd (int): The Nonunion Risk Determination (NURD) score. It ranges from 0 to 10.

    Returns:
    risk (str): The risk of nonunion.
    """
    if rust >= 10:
        return 'Low risk'
    elif 6 <= rust < 10 and nurd >= 7:
        return 'Medium risk'
    elif rust < 6 or infection and nurd >= 7:
        return 'High risk'
    else:
        return 'Low risk'
30172349.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of tibial shaft fracture nonunion 6 weeks after reamed intramedullary (IM) nail fixation.
30172349.eligibility: Patients with tibial shaft fractures treated with reamed intramedullary (IM) nail fixation are eligible. Patients with fracture gaps ≥3 mm are excluded.
30172349.interpretation:
- Low risk: Patients in the high RUST group (RUST ≥ 10), achieved union regardless of NURD score.
- Medium risk: In the medium RUST group (RUST 6-9), 25% of patients with a NURD score ≥7 experienced nonunion.
- High risk: In the low RUST group (RUST <6 or infection within 6 weeks), 69% of patients with a NURD score ≥7 experienced nonunion.
30172349.utility: The calculator has a statistically significant association with nonunion (odds ratio > or < 1.0; p < 0.01). The NURD score was increasingly predictive of nonunion with decreasing RUST.

30174132.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Stroke Prediction after Surgical Aortic Valve Replacement
30174132.code:
def compute_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, prior_stroke_tia_thromboembolism, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Compute the CHA2DS2-VASc score based on the input parameters.

    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    prior_stroke_tia_thromboembolism (bool): True if the patient has a history of stroke, transient ischemic attack, or thromboembolism, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): The sex of the patient. 'M' for male, 'F' for female.

    Returns:
    int: The CHA2DS2-VASc score.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if prior_stroke_tia_thromboembolism:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'F':
        score += 1
    return score
30174132.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of stroke after surgical aortic valve replacement.
30174132.eligibility: Patients undergoing isolated surgical aortic valve replacement with a bioprosthesis are eligible.
30174132.interpretation:
- Scores 0-1: Low risk of stroke. Incidences of stroke or transient ischemic attack at 1 year are 2.6%, at 5 years are 5.2%, and at 10 years are 5.2%.
- Scores 2-4: High risk of stroke. Incidences of stroke or transient ischemic attack at 1 year are 4.8%, at 5 years are 14.0%, and at 10 years are 20.7%.
- Scores 5-9: Very high risk of stroke. Incidences of stroke or transient ischemic attack at 1 year are 10.7%, at 5 years are 21.9%, and at 10 years are 37.9%.
30174132.utility: Patients with a CHA2DS2-VASc score of 5 through 9 had a significantly increased risk of stroke or transient ischemic attack (hazard ratio, 4.75; 95% confidence interval, 1.09-20.6; P = .037) irrespective of preoperative or new-onset in-hospital atrial fibrillation compared with low-risk patients.

30176527.title: qSOFA Score Calculator for Mortality and Prolonged ICU Stay
30176527.code:
def calculate_qsofa(mental_status: bool, systolic_bp: int, respiratory_rate: int) -> int:
    """
    Calculate the qSOFA score based on the patient's mental status, systolic blood pressure, and respiratory rate.

    Parameters:
    mental_status (bool): Altered mental status, True if present, False if absent.
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure, ≤100 mmHg scores 1, >100 mmHg scores 0.
    respiratory_rate (int): Respiratory rate, ≥22/min scores 1, <22/min scores 0.

    Returns:
    int: The total qSOFA score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if mental_status:
        score += 1
    if systolic_bp <= 100:
        score += 1
    if respiratory_rate >= 22:
        score += 1
    return score
30176527.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of in-hospital mortality or prolonged ICU stay (≥3 days) in Emergency Department (ED) patients with suspected infection.
30176527.eligibility: Patients in the Emergency Department with suspected infection are eligible for this calculator.
30176527.interpretation:
- qSOFA score 0-1: Low risk of in-hospital mortality or prolonged ICU stay. In-hospital mortality rate is 2.2%, and the rate of in-hospital mortality or ICU stay ≥3 days is 4.2%.
- qSOFA score 2-3: High risk of in-hospital mortality or prolonged ICU stay. In-hospital mortality rate is 12.8%, and the rate of in-hospital mortality or ICU stay ≥3 days is 17.2%.
30176527.utility: For the prediction of in-hospital mortality, a positive qSOFA (score 2-3) had a positive predictive value (PPV) of 13% and a negative predictive value (NPV) of 98%. For the prediction of in-hospital mortality or ICU stay ≥3 days, the PPV and NPV of a positive qSOFA were 17% and 96%, respectively.

30177115.title: Strain Rejection Score (SRS) Calculator
30177115.code:
def compute_srs(rv_fw, ch_ls):
    """
    Compute the Strain Rejection Score (SRS)

    Parameters:
    rv_fw (float): RV free wall longitudinal strain
    ch_ls (float): 4-chamber longitudinal strain

    Returns:
    int: Strain Rejection Score (SRS)
    """
    srs = 0
    if rv_fw <= 16.8:
        srs += 1
    if ch_ls <= 13.8:
        srs += 1
    return srs
30177115.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of acute cellular rejection (ACR) in heart transplant patients using speckle-tracking derived parameters.
30177115.eligibility: Patients who have undergone heart transplantation and are in their first year of follow-up are eligible.
30177115.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of ACR
- Score 1: Moderate risk of ACR
- Score 2: High risk of ACR, indicating the presence of ACR requiring treatment
30177115.utility: The SRS calculator showed good specificity and strong negative predictive value in identifying patients at risk of ACR ≥ 2R. It also had the highest area under the curve in the study.

30180211.title: ACEF Score and Urinary NGAL Test for Predicting Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) After Cardiac Surgery
30180211.code:
def calculate_acef_score(age, ef, creatinine):
    """
    Calculate the ACEF score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    ef (float): The ejection fraction of the patient.
    creatinine (float): The creatinine level of the patient.

    Returns:
    float: The ACEF score.
    """
    acef_score = age / ef
    if creatinine > 2.0:
        acef_score += 1
    return acef_score
30180211.purpose: This calculator should be used to predict the risk of acute kidney injury (AKI) in patients undergoing cardiac surgery.
30180211.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing cardiac surgery are eligible for this risk assessment.
30180211.interpretation:
- If the ACEF score is less than 1.1, the patient is at lower risk of AKI.
- If the ACEF score is greater than or equal to 1.1, the patient is at higher risk of AKI. In this case, the urinary NGAL test can be used for further risk stratification.
30180211.utility: The combination of the ACEF score and the urinary NGAL test has an AUROC of 0.873 (95% confidence interval, 0.784-0.961; P < .001), indicating a high level of accuracy in predicting AKI after cardiac surgery.

30194336.title: Early Postoperative CONUT Score Risk Calculator
30194336.code:
def compute_risk(conut_score):
    """
    Compute the risk of postoperative complications based on CONUT score.

    Args:
    conut_score (int): The early postoperative CONUT score.

    Returns:
    str: 'High' if the CONUT score is greater than or equal to 8, 'Low' otherwise.
    """
    if conut_score >= 8:
        return 'High'
    else:
        return 'Low'
30194336.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of postoperative complications (III-V) in patients who have undergone hepatectomy for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).
30194336.eligibility: Patients who have undergone hepatectomy for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) are eligible.
30194336.interpretation:
- 'High': High risk of postoperative complications (III-V). This group had higher incidence of various complications and longer ICU stay and hospitalization period.
- 'Low': Low risk of postoperative complications (III-V).
30194336.utility: The AUROC of the early postoperative CONUT score for predicting postoperative complications (III-V) was 0.653, indicating a good prediction ability.

30206045.title: Post-Transplant Prognostic Scoring System for Myelodysplastic Syndrome (MDS)
30206045.code:
def compute_risk_score(relapse, platelet_recovery, gvhd_grade):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on relapse, platelet recovery, and GVHD grade.

    Parameters:
    relapse (bool): True if relapse occurred, False otherwise.
    platelet_recovery (bool): True if platelet recovery occurred, False otherwise.
    gvhd_grade (int): The grade of acute GVHD, can be 0, II, or III/IV.

    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if relapse:
        score += 4
    if not platelet_recovery:
        score += 2
    if gvhd_grade == 'II':
        score += 1
    elif gvhd_grade == 'III/IV':
        score += 2
    return score
30206045.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the prognosis of MDS patients who have survived more than 100 days after allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation (allo-HCT).
30206045.eligibility: Patients with myelodysplastic syndrome who have survived more than 100 days after undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation.
30206045.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk, with a 3-year overall survival rate of 70%
- Score 1-3: Intermediate risk, with a 3-year overall survival rate of 46%
- Score 4 or more: High risk, with a 3-year overall survival rate of 6%
30206045.utility: The model performed consistently in both the derivation and validation cohorts, with good calibration.

30231057.title: Leprosy Risk Prediction Calculator
30231057.code:
def calculate_grs(gwas_variants):
    """
    Calculate the genetic risk score (GRS) based on GWAS variants.

    Args:
    gwas_variants (dict): A dictionary where keys are the GWAS variants and values are the number of risk alleles (0, 1, or 2).

    Returns:
    float: The calculated GRS.
    """
    grs = 0
    for variant, risk_alleles in gwas_variants.items():
        weight = variant_weights[variant]  # variant_weights is a predefined dictionary of weights
        grs += risk_alleles * weight
    return grs
30231057.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing leprosy in the Chinese population based on a weighted genetic risk score (GRS).
30231057.eligibility: This calculator is applicable to individuals in the Chinese population who have come into contact with leprosy.
30231057.interpretation:
- Low-risk group: GRS ≤ 18.17. These individuals have a significantly lower risk of developing leprosy.
- High-risk group: GRS > 28.06. These individuals have a 24.65 higher odds ratio for developing leprosy relative to those in the low-risk group.
30231057.utility: The calculator showed good discrimination capabilities with an AUC of 0.743 in the discovery set and an AUC of 0.707 in the validation set.

30233849.title: NoSAS Score for Screening Sleep-Disordered Breathing
30233849.code:
def calculate_nosas_score(neck_circumference, bmi, snoring, age, sex):
    """
    Calculate the NoSAS score for screening Sleep-Disordered Breathing.

    Parameters:
    neck_circumference (int): Neck circumference in cm.
    bmi (float): Body Mass Index.
    snoring (bool): Whether the patient snores or not.
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    sex (str): Sex of the patient. Can be 'male' or 'female'.

    Returns:
    int: The NoSAS score.
    """
    score = 0
    if neck_circumference > 40:
        score += 4
    if bmi > 30:
        score += 3
    if snoring:
        score += 2
    if age > 55:
        score += 4
    if sex == 'male':
        score += 2
    return score
30233849.purpose: This calculator is used to identify subjects at high risk of Sleep-Disordered Breathing (SDB).
30233849.eligibility: Patients aged between 18 to 80 years old who are suspected of having Sleep-Disordered Breathing (SDB) are eligible.
30233849.interpretation:
- NoSAS score <8: Low risk of SDB
- NoSAS score ≥8: High risk of SDB
30233849.utility: The NoSAS score has a good predictive value for SDB patients with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.734. The sensitivity and specificity of NoSAS ≥8 to predict AHI ≥5 events/h, AHI ≥15 events/h and AHI >30 events/h were 0.590 and 0.707, 0.649 and 0.626, and 0.644 and 0.562, respectively.

30236390.title: VT-LVAD Score Calculator
30236390.code:
def compute_vt_lvad_score(va_pre_lvad, af_pre_lvad, idiopathic_etiology, hf_duration, early_va, no_ace_inhibitors):
    """
    Compute the VT-LVAD score based on the six predictors.
    
    Args:
    va_pre_lvad (bool): Whether the patient had VAs before LVAD implantation.
    af_pre_lvad (bool): Whether the patient had atrial fibrillation before LVAD implantation.
    idiopathic_etiology (bool): Whether the patient has idiopathic etiology of the cardiomyopathy.
    hf_duration (bool): Whether the patient had heart failure duration >12 months.
    early_va (bool): Whether the patient had early VAs (<30 days post-LVAD).
    no_ace_inhibitors (bool): Whether the patient did not take angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors during follow-up.
    
    Returns:
    int: The VT-LVAD score.
    """
    return va_pre_lvad + af_pre_lvad + idiopathic_etiology + hf_duration + early_va + no_ace_inhibitors
30236390.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of late ventricular arrhythmias (VAs) in patients with left ventricular assist device (LVAD).
30236390.eligibility: Patients who have received a left ventricular assist device (LVAD) are eligible.
30236390.interpretation:
- Score 0 to 1: Low risk group, with a 0.0% rate of VAs at 1 year.
- Score 2 to 4: Intermediate risk group, with an 8.0% rate of VAs at 1 year.
- Score 5 to 6: High risk group, with a 31.0% rate of VAs at 1 year.
- Score 7 to 10: Very high risk group, with a 55.0% rate of VAs at 1 year.
30236390.utility: The VT-LVAD score can help to identify patients at risk of late VAs and guide ICD indications in previously nonimplanted patients.

30238513.title: Korean Cancer Study-Prostate Risk Classification Calculator
30238513.code:
def compute_risk_score(initial_psa, gleason_score, clinical_n1, non_regional_lymph_node, bone_metastasis, visceral_metastasis):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the Korean Cancer Study-Prostate Risk Classification.

    Parameters:
    initial_psa (float): Initial prostate-specific antigen level.
    gleason_score (int): Biopsy Gleason score.
    clinical_n1 (bool): Whether clinical N1 is present.
    non_regional_lymph_node (bool): Whether non-regional lymph node metastasis is present.
    bone_metastasis (bool): Whether bone metastasis is present.
    visceral_metastasis (bool): Whether visceral metastasis is present.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if initial_psa > 40:
        score += 1
    if gleason_score >= 9:
        score += 1
    if clinical_n1:
        score += 1
    if non_regional_lymph_node:
        score += 1
    if bone_metastasis:
        score += 1
    if visceral_metastasis:
        score += 1
    return score
30238513.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of progression to castration-resistant prostate cancer in patients treated with primary androgen deprivation therapy.
30238513.eligibility: Patients who have been diagnosed with prostate cancer and are receiving primary androgen deprivation therapy.
30238513.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk, with 5-year estimated castration-resistant prostate cancer/cancer-specific death rates of 10.3%/6.3%
- Score 1-2: Intermediate risk, with 5-year estimated castration-resistant prostate cancer/cancer-specific death rates of 48.4%/22.2%
- Score ≥3: High risk, with 5-year estimated castration-resistant prostate cancer/cancer-specific death rates of 81.7%/53.1%
30238513.utility: The Korean Cancer Study-prostate model predicted castration-resistant prostate cancer with a c-index of 0.7242, cancer-specific survival with a c-index of 0.7036 and overall survival with a c-index of 0.5890.

30267028.title: MoCA-CFS Hepatic Encephalopathy Risk Calculator
30267028.code:
def compute_moca_cfs(moca, cfs):
    """
    Compute the MoCA-CFS score.

    Parameters:
    moca (int): Montreal Cognitive Assessment score (0-30)
    cfs (int): Clinical Frailty Scale score (1-9)

    Returns:
    int: MoCA-CFS score (0-2)
    """
    if moca > 24 and cfs <= 4:
        return 0
    elif moca <= 24 or cfs > 4:
        return 1
    else:
        return 2
30267028.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of Hepatic Encephalopathy-related hospitalizations within 6 months in patients with cirrhosis.
30267028.eligibility: Adult patients with cirrhosis who do not have overt Hepatic Encephalopathy and dementia.
30267028.interpretation:
- MoCA-CFS score of 0: Low risk of HE-related hospitalization within 6 months.
- MoCA-CFS score of 1: Moderate risk of HE-related hospitalization within 6 months, with odds increased by 3.3 times.
- MoCA-CFS score of 2: High risk of HE-related hospitalization within 6 months, with odds increased by 5.7 times.
30267028.utility: The MoCA-CFS score independently predicted HE hospitalization within 6 months. The odds of hospitalization increased by 3.3 times for a MoCA-CFS score of 1 and by 5.7 times for a MoCA-CFS score of 2.

30273388.title: eCura System for Gastric Cancer Residue Risk
30273388.code:
def calculate_eCura_score(lymphatic_invasion, tumor_size, positive_vertical_margin, venous_invasion, submucosal_invasion):
    """
    Calculate the eCura score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    lymphatic_invasion (bool): True if lymphatic invasion is present, False otherwise.
    tumor_size (float): Size of the tumor in mm.
    positive_vertical_margin (bool): True if positive vertical margin is present, False otherwise.
    venous_invasion (bool): True if venous invasion is present, False otherwise.
    submucosal_invasion (float): Depth of submucosal invasion in μm.

    Returns:
    int: eCura score.
    """
    score = 0
    if lymphatic_invasion:
        score += 3
    if tumor_size > 30:
        score += 1
    if positive_vertical_margin:
        score += 1
    if venous_invasion:
        score += 1
    if submucosal_invasion >= 500:
        score += 1
    return score
30273388.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk of cancer residue (CR) after non-curative Endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD) for gastric cancer. It helps in selecting appropriate cases for salvage surgery.
30273388.eligibility: Patients who have undergone non-curative ESD for gastric cancer are eligible.
30273388.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk group, no cancer residue detected.
- Score 2 and above: Higher risk of cancer residue, the higher the score, the greater the CR positivity.
30273388.utility: The eCura scoring system was the only significant factor for CR status in the study (p = 0.0128). All patients in the low-risk group (score = 0-1 point) had no CR.

30289624.title: Surgical Pressure Ulcer Risk Score (SPURS)
30289624.code:
def calculate_spurs(age, gender, asa_score, bmi, braden_score, anaemia, respiratory_disease, hypertension):
    """
    Calculate the Surgical Pressure Ulcer Risk Score (SPURS).
    
    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    gender (str): Gender of the patient. Can be 'male' or 'female'.
    asa_score (int): American Society of Anesthesiologists score.
    bmi (float): Body Mass Index of the patient.
    braden_score (int): Preoperative Braden score.
    anaemia (bool): Whether the patient has anaemia.
    respiratory_disease (bool): Whether the patient has a respiratory disease.
    hypertension (bool): Whether the patient has hypertension.
    
    Returns:
    int: The SPURS.
    """
    spurs = 0
    if age >= 75:
        spurs += 1
    if gender == 'female':
        spurs += 1
    if asa_score >= 3:
        spurs += 1
    if bmi < 23:
        spurs += 1
    if braden_score <= 14:
        spurs += 1
    if anaemia:
        spurs += 1
    if respiratory_disease:
        spurs += 1
    if hypertension:
        spurs += 1
    return spurs
30289624.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of hospital-acquired pressure ulcers (HAPU) in surgical patients using preoperative data.
30289624.eligibility: Surgical patients who are prone to developing hospital-acquired pressure ulcers (HAPU).
30289624.interpretation:
- SPURS < 6: Low risk of developing HAPU.
- SPURS ≥ 6: High risk of developing HAPU, with a positive predictive value of 73.2% and a negative predictive value of 80.7%.
30289624.utility: The SPURS model had a bootstrap-corrected c-statistic of 0.78, indicating good discrimination. A cut-off score of ≥6 is strongly predictive, with a positive predictive value of 73.2% (confidence interval [CI]: 59.7%-84.2%) and a negative predictive value of 80.7% (CI: 74.3%-86.1%).

30317861.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Predicting Ischemic Events in PCI Patients
30317861.code:
