32962426.eligibility: Patients presenting to the emergency room who underwent upper urinary tract decompression due to concern for infection in the setting of obstructing urolithiasis.
32962426.interpretation:
- Risk score 0: 8% chance of confirmed UTI
- Risk score 1: 11% chance of confirmed UTI
- Risk score 2: 68% chance of confirmed UTI
- Risk score 3 to 4: 100% chance of confirmed UTI
32962426.utility: The risk score has an area under curve (AUC) of 0.91 to predict UTI, which is significantly higher than the standard model of serum white blood cells >15 or temperature >38°C (AUC of 0.67).

32964117.title: De Novo Stage IV Breast Cancer Risk Score Calculator
32964117.code:
def compute_risk_score(grade, hormone_receptor_status, her2_status):
    """
    Compute the risk score for de novo stage IV breast cancer.

    Parameters:
    grade (int): Histological grade of the tumor. Grade 3 is assigned 1 point, otherwise 0.
    hormone_receptor_status (str): Status of hormone receptors (ER and PR). 'Negative' is assigned 1 point, otherwise 0.
    her2_status (str): Status of HER2. 'Negative' is assigned 1 point, otherwise 0.

    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if grade == 3:
        score += 1
    if hormone_receptor_status.lower() == 'negative':
        score += 1
    if her2_status.lower() == 'negative':
        score += 1
    return score
32964117.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the 3-year breast cancer-specific survival (BCSS) rate for patients with de novo stage IV breast cancer, based on biological factors including histological grade, estrogen receptor (ER), progesterone receptor (PR), and human epidermal growth factor receptor-2 (HER2) status.
32964117.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with de novo stage IV breast cancer are eligible.
32964117.interpretation:
The risk score ranges from 0 to 3, with higher scores indicating lower BCSS. The 3-year BCSS rates for each score are as follows:
- Score 0: 76.3%
- Score 1: 64.5%
- Score 2: 48.5%
- Score 3: 23.7%
32964117.utility: The risk score staging system was found to be an independent prognostic factor associated with BCSS. Patients with a higher risk score had a lower BCSS.

32988897.title: Combined Platelet Distribution Width and C-Reactive Protein (CPC) Score Calculator
32988897.code:
def calculate_cpc_score(pdw, crp):
    """
    Calculate the CPC score based on PDW and CRP levels.

    Parameters:
    pdw (float): Pretherapeutic Platelet Distribution Width (PDW) level.
    crp (float): Postoperative serum C-Reactive Protein (CRP) level.

    Returns:
    int: CPC score (0, 1, or 2)
    """
    if pdw <= 12.4 and crp >= 0.5:
        return 2
    elif pdw > 12.4 or crp < 0.5:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
32988897.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the prognostic significance of combined PDW and serum CRP levels in esophageal cancer patients.
32988897.eligibility: Patients with esophageal cancer who underwent neoadjuvant therapy prior to esophagectomy.
32988897.interpretation:
- CPC Score 0: Low risk of poor prognosis.
- CPC Score 1: Moderate risk of poor prognosis.
- CPC Score 2: High risk of poor prognosis.
32988897.utility: The CPC score was found to be a significant risk factor for overall (p=0.006) and recurrence-free (p=0.004) survival in esophageal cancer patients.

32991776.title: Contrast-Induced Nephropathy (CIN) Risk Score for Diabetic Patients
32991776.code:
def compute_risk_score(LVEF, ACS, eGFR, contrast_volume):
    """
    Compute the risk score for Contrast-Induced Nephropathy (CIN) in diabetic patients.

    Parameters:
    LVEF (bool): True if Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction < 40%, False otherwise.
    ACS (bool): True if patient has Acute Coronary Syndrome, False otherwise.
    eGFR (bool): True if Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate < 60, False otherwise.
    contrast_volume (bool): True if contrast volume > 300 mL, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    return int(LVEF) + int(ACS) + int(eGFR) + int(contrast_volume)
32991776.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of contrast-induced nephropathy (CIN) and major adverse cardiac events (MACEs) after percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) in patients with diabetes.
32991776.eligibility: Patients with diabetes who are undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with contrast exposure.
32991776.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk, with an incidence of CIN of 5.9%
- Score 1-2: Moderate risk, with an incidence of CIN of 32.9%
- Score 3-4: High risk, with an incidence of CIN of 60.0%
32991776.utility: The risk assessment model exhibited moderate discrimination ability for predicting CIN, with an AUC of 0.759 [95% CI 0.668-0.852, P = .001] in the validation cohort. It also had similar prognostic values for one-year follow-up MACE (C-statistic: 0.705 and 0.606 for new risk score and Mehran score, respectively).

32991860.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for COVID-19 Mortality Risk Assessment
32991860.code:
def compute_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(C, H, A2, D, S2, V, A, Sc):
    """
    Compute the CHA2DS2-VASc score.

    Parameters:
    C (bool): Congestive heart failure
    H (bool): Hypertension
    A2 (bool): Age ≥ 75 years
    D (bool): Diabetes Mellitus
    S2 (bool): Stroke/TIA/Thromboembolism
    V (bool): Vascular disease
    A (bool): Age 65-74 years
    Sc (bool): Sex category (1 if female)

    Returns:
    int: CHA2DS2-VASc score
    """
    return C + H + 2*A2 + D + 2*S2 + V + A + Sc
32991860.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of complications and death in patients hospitalized with COVID-19 infection.
32991860.eligibility: Patients hospitalized with COVID-19 infection are eligible for this risk assessment.
32991860.interpretation:
- Score ≤1 (T1): 8.1% risk of death, 18.6% risk of death or invasive ventilation
- Score 2 to 3 (T2): 24.3% risk of death, 31.9% risk of death or invasive ventilation
- Score ≥4 (T3): 33.3% risk of death, 43.5% risk of death or invasive ventilation
32991860.utility: The odds ratios for mortality and the composite end point for T2 patients versus T1 CHA2DS2-VASc score were 3.62 and 2.04, respectively. Similarly, the odds ratios for mortality and the composite end point for T3 patients versus T1 were 5.65 and 3.36, respectively.

33000361.title: AML Patient Survival Predictive Scoring System
33000361.code:
def calculate_score(hsct_type, standardized_regimen, blast_cells_reduction):
    """
    Calculate the predictive score for AML patient survival.

    Parameters:
    hsct_type (str): Type of HSCT, either 'ISD' or 'HID'
    standardized_regimen (bool): Whether a standardized conditioning regimen is used
    blast_cells_reduction (float): Proportional reduction of blast cells in the bone marrow

    Returns:
    int: Predictive score
    """
    score = 0
    if hsct_type == 'ISD':
        score += 1
    if standardized_regimen:
        score += 1
    if blast_cells_reduction < 0.5:
        score += 1
    return score
33000361.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 5-year overall survival rate for refractory/relapsed acute myeloid leukemia (R/R AML) patients not in remission (NR) status undergoing hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT).
33000361.eligibility: Patients with refractory/relapsed acute myeloid leukemia (R/R AML) not in remission (NR) status who are undergoing hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT).
33000361.interpretation:
- Score 0: 66.6% ± 4.5% 5-year overall survival rate
- Score 1: 44.1% ± 3.6% 5-year overall survival rate
- Score ≥ 2: 4.4 ± 0.2% 5-year overall survival rate
33000361.utility: The predictive scoring system was developed based on a retrospective analysis of 151 R/R AML patients. It was found to be effective in predicting patient survival, with different scores correlating with significantly different 5-year overall survival rates.

33002202.title: Preoperative Risk Score for CRS/HIPEC with Liver Resection
33002202.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, histology, liver_tumors):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on preoperative factors.

    Parameters:
    age (bool): True if age is less than 60 years, False otherwise.
    histology (str): 'colorectal' if the histology is colorectal, 'other' otherwise.
    liver_tumors (bool): True if there are multiple liver tumors, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if age:
        score += 1
    if histology == 'colorectal':
        score += 1
    if liver_tumors:
        score += 1
    return score
33002202.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify patients considered for cytoreductive surgery (CRS) and heated intraperitoneal chemotherapy (HIPEC) with liver resection (LR), based on preoperative factors.
33002202.eligibility: Patients with invasive appendiceal or colorectal adenocarcinoma undergoing primary, curative intent CRS/HIPEC with CC0-1 resection are eligible.
33002202.interpretation:
- Score 0: 100% 2-year survival rate
- Score 1: 91% 2-year survival rate
- Score 2: 58% 2-year survival rate
- Score 3: 0% 2-year survival rate
33002202.utility: The risk score has been shown to have an incremental decrease in 2-year survival as the risk score increased from 0 to 3.

33002587.title: FBEVAR Risk Calculator
33002587.code:
def calculate_risk(ASA_score, LPMA):
    """
    Calculate the risk category based on ASA score and LPMA.

    Parameters:
    ASA_score (int): ASA score of the patient.
    LPMA (float): Lean Psoas Muscle Area of the patient in cm2HU.

    Returns:
    str: Risk category ('low', 'medium', 'high')
    """
    if ASA_score == 2 or LPMA > 350:
        return 'low'
    elif ASA_score == 3 and LPMA <= 350:
        return 'medium'
    elif ASA_score == 4 and LPMA <= 350:
        return 'high'
33002587.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of complications and mortality after fenestrated and branched endovascular aortic repair (FBEVAR) based on the patient's ASA score and Lean Psoas Muscle Area (LPMA).
33002587.eligibility: Patients who have undergone elective FBEVAR for pararenal or thoracoabdominal aortic aneurysms.
33002587.interpretation:
- Low risk: 90-day mortality and MAE rates are 1.7% and 16% respectively.
- Medium risk: 90-day mortality and MAE rates are 7.2% and 24% respectively.
- High risk: 90-day mortality and MAE rates are 30% and 33% respectively. If LPMA < 200 cm2HU, indicating sarcopenia, the risk of death within 90 days is 43%.
33002587.utility: The risk calculator was found to be a strong predictor of outcomes and the only independent predictor of both mortality and MAEs after FBEVAR. A high muscle mass was protective against complications, regardless of the ASA score.

33014783.title: Fibrinogen-to-Albumin Ratio Index (FARI) Prognosis Predictor for Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GISTs)
33014783.code:
def calculate_FARI(fibrinogen_level, albumin_level):
    """
    Calculate the Fibrinogen-to-Albumin Ratio Index (FARI).

    Parameters:
    fibrinogen_level (float): The fibrinogen level in g/L.
    albumin_level (float): The albumin level in g/L.

    Returns:
    float: The FARI.
    """
    return fibrinogen_level / albumin_level
33014783.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the recurrence-free survival (RFS) in patients after surgical resection of GISTs.
33014783.eligibility: Patients who have been diagnosed with GISTs and have undergone curative resection are eligible.
33014783.interpretation:
- FARI-high (≥0.08): Associated with worse outcomes concerning RFS in GIST patients.
- FARI-low (<0.08): Associated with a more prolonged RFS in GIST patients.
33014783.utility: The nomogram incorporating FARI had a concordance probability of 0.802 (SE 0.025), indicating a good predictive performance. The concordance probabilities of the nomogram were better than the National Institute of Health (NIH) risk classification system [0.802 [0.025] vs. 0.737 [0.024], p < 0.01].

33026937.title: Four-Factor Prognostic Model for Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia
33026937.code:
def compute_risk_score(TP53_aberration, prior_treatment, beta_2_microglobulin, lactate_dehydrogenase):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia treated with Ibrutinib.

    Parameters:
    TP53_aberration (bool): Whether the patient has TP53 aberration.
    prior_treatment (bool): Whether the patient has received prior treatment.
    beta_2_microglobulin (float): The patient's β-2 microglobulin level in mg/L.
    lactate_dehydrogenase (float): The patient's lactate dehydrogenase level in U/L.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if TP53_aberration:
        score += 1
    if prior_treatment:
        score += 1
    if beta_2_microglobulin >= 5:
        score += 1
    if lactate_dehydrogenase > 250:
        score += 1
    return score
33026937.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the outcome in patients with Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia treated with Ibrutinib.
33026937.eligibility: Patients with Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia who are being treated with Ibrutinib.
33026937.interpretation:
The risk score stratifies patients into three risk groups:

- High risk: 3 to 4 points
- Intermediate risk: 2 points
- Low risk: 0 to 1 point

The 3-year PFS rates for all patients combined were 47%, 74%, and 87% for the high-, intermediate-, and low-risk group, respectively. The 3-year OS rates were 63%, 83%, and 93%, respectively.
33026937.utility: The model remained significant when applied to treatment-naïve and relapsed/refractory cohorts individually. For 84 patients in the external cohort, BTK and PLCG2 mutations were tested cross-sectionally and at progression. The cumulative incidences of mutations were strongly correlated with the model.

33028177.title: Adjusted Global Antiphospholipid Syndrome Score (aGAPSS)
33028177.code:
def calculate_aGAPSS(hypertension, dyslipidemia, LA_B2GPI, aCL):
    """
    Calculate the Adjusted Global Antiphospholipid Syndrome Score (aGAPSS)

    Parameters:
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise
    dyslipidemia (bool): True if the patient has dyslipidemia, False otherwise
    LA_B2GPI (bool): True if the patient has LA and B2GPI antibodies, False otherwise
    aCL (bool): True if the patient has aCL antibodies, False otherwise

    Returns:
    int: aGAPSS score
    """
    score = 0
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if dyslipidemia:
        score += 3
    if LA_B2GPI:
        score += 4
    if aCL:
        score += 5
    return score
33028177.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of thrombosis and pregnancy morbidity in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) patients with anti-phospholipid antibodies (aPL).
33028177.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) are eligible for this calculator.
33028177.interpretation:
- aGAPSS < 4: Lower risk of thrombosis and/or pregnancy complications.
- aGAPSS ≥ 4: Higher risk of thrombosis and/or pregnancy complications.
33028177.utility: The discriminative ability of the aGAPSS was calculated by measuring the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC). The best cut off point for the diagnosis of thrombosis and/or pregnancy complications was aGAPSS ≥4.

33038202.title: Spine Oncology Morbidity Assessment (SOMA) Score
33038202.code:
def compute_SOMA_score(age, hypoalbuminemia, karnofsky, frankel_grade, multilevel_disease):
    """
    Compute the SOMA score based on the input parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    hypoalbuminemia (bool): Whether the patient has hypoalbuminemia.
    karnofsky (int): Karnofsky score of the patient.
    frankel_grade (str): Frankel grade of the patient.
    multilevel_disease (int): Number of continuous vertebral bodies affected.

    Returns:
    int: The computed SOMA score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 70:
        score += 1
    if hypoalbuminemia:
        score += 1
    if karnofsky <= 40:
        score += 1
    if frankel_grade in ['A', 'B', 'C']:
        score += 1
    if multilevel_disease >= 2:
        score += 1
    return score
33038202.purpose: This calculator is used to predict perioperative morbidity for patients undergoing metastatic spinal tumor surgery (MSTS).
33038202.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing metastatic spinal tumor surgery are eligible.
33038202.interpretation:
- Score 0: The perioperative morbidity is 4.6%
- Score 1: The perioperative morbidity is 30.0%
- Score 2: The perioperative morbidity is 53.9%
- Score ≥3: The perioperative morbidity is 64.7%
33038202.utility: The model's accuracy was estimated at 0.75 (AUC).

33043285.title: ROX-6 Score Calculator for COVID-19 Patients
33043285.code:
def compute_rox_6_score(oxygen_saturation, fio2, respiratory_rate):
    """
    Compute the ROX-6 score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    oxygen_saturation (float): The patient's oxygen saturation level.
    fio2 (float): The fraction of inspired oxygen.
    respiratory_rate (int): The patient's respiratory rate.

    Returns:
    float: The ROX-6 score.
    """
    return (oxygen_saturation / fio2) / respiratory_rate
33043285.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the success of weaning from high-flow nasal oxygen (HFNO) in patients with severe COVID-19-related hypoxaemic respiratory failure (HRF).
33043285.eligibility: Patients with severe COVID-19-related HRF treated with HFNO are eligible.
33043285.interpretation:
- A ROX-6 score of ≥3.7 is 80% predictive of successful weaning from HFNO.
- A ROX-6 score of ≤2.2 is 74% predictive of failure to wean from HFNO.
33043285.utility: The ROX-6 score was found to be associated with HFNO success (AHR 0.43, 0.31-0.60).

33044089.title: Systemic Inflammation Response Index (SIRI) Prognostic Calculator for Aneurismal Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (aSAH)
33044089.code:
def compute_siri(neutrophil_count, monocyte_count, lymphocyte_count):
    """
    Compute the Systemic Inflammation Response Index (SIRI)

    Args:
    neutrophil_count (float): The neutrophil count in the blood.
    monocyte_count (float): The monocyte count in the blood.
    lymphocyte_count (float): The lymphocyte count in the blood.

    Returns:
    float: The computed SIRI.
    """
    return neutrophil_count * monocyte_count / lymphocyte_count
33044089.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with aneurismal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH) based on the Systemic Inflammation Response Index (SIRI).
33044089.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with aneurismal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH) are eligible.
33044089.interpretation:
- SIRI < 4.105 × 10^9/L: Lower risk of unfavorable outcome.
- SIRI >= 4.105 × 10^9/L: Higher risk of unfavorable outcome.
33044089.utility: The ROC analysis showed that a nomogram model combining the SIRI and other conventional factors showed more favorable predictive ability than the model without the SIRI.

33046365.title: Systemic Immune-Inflammation Index (SII) Risk Calculator
33046365.code:
def calculate_sii(neutrophil_count, platelet_count, lymphocyte_count):
    """
    Calculate the Systemic Immune-Inflammation Index (SII).

    Parameters:
    neutrophil_count (float): The neutrophil count in 10^3/mm^3.
    platelet_count (float): The platelet count in 10^3/mm^3.
    lymphocyte_count (float): The lymphocyte count in 10^3/mm^3.

    Returns:
    float: The calculated SII.
    """
    return neutrophil_count * platelet_count / lymphocyte_count
33046365.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the short-term outcomes after off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting (OPCABG) based on preoperative hematologic indices.
33046365.eligibility: Patients undergoing elective OPCABG are eligible for this calculator.
33046365.interpretation:
- SII < 878.06 × 10^3/mm^3: Lower risk of poor outcome after OPCABG.
- SII ≥ 878.06 × 10^3/mm^3: Higher risk of poor outcome after OPCABG, with a higher incidence of complications such as atrial fibrillation, intra-aortic balloon pump requirement, vasoactive-ionotropic score >20 for >6 hours, and other infections.
33046365.utility: The SII cutoff of 878.06 × 10^3/mm^3 predicted poor outcome with 97.6% sensitivity and 91% specificity. The area under the curve (AUC) was 0.984, indicating excellent predictive power.

33054496.title: Small Artery Disease (SAD) and Medial Arterial Calcification (MAC) Risk Score Calculator
33054496.code:
def compute_SAD_MAC_score(angiography_result, radiography_result):
    """
    Compute the SAD and MAC scores based on angiography and radiography results.

    Parameters:
    angiography_result (str): Result of the angiography, can be 'absent', 'moderate', or 'severe'.
    radiography_result (str): Result of the radiography, can be 'absent', 'moderate', or 'severe'.

    Returns:
    tuple: A tuple containing the SAD and MAC scores (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    score_dict = {'absent': 0, 'moderate': 1, 'severe': 2}
    SAD_score = score_dict[angiography_result]
    MAC_score = score_dict[radiography_result]
    return SAD_score, MAC_score
33054496.purpose: This calculator is used to quantify the severity of Small Artery Disease (SAD) and Medial Arterial Calcification (MAC) in patients with chronic limb-threatening ischemia (CLTI), and to predict the risk of major adverse limb events.
33054496.eligibility: Patients with chronic limb-threatening ischemia (CLTI) who have undergone an angiographic foot vessel study, foot radiography, and at least 6 months of follow-up after intervention.
33054496.interpretation:
- Score 0: Absence of SAD or MAC, lower risk of major adverse limb events.
- Score 1: Moderate SAD or MAC, moderate risk of major adverse limb events.
- Score 2: Severe SAD or MAC, high risk of major adverse limb events.
33054496.utility: The MAC score demonstrated high interobserver reproducibility (correlation coefficient 0.96). The sensitivity and specificity of the MAC score in detecting SAD were 100% and 98.1% respectively in SAD groups 0 and 2, and 99.1% and 92.7% respectively for SAD group 1.

33055057.title: Fitness-tracker assisted Frailty-Assessment Score (FIFA score)
33055057.code:
def compute_FIFA_score(step_count, heart_rate, preprocedural_stress, step_count_threshold, heart_rate_threshold, stress_threshold):
    """
    Compute the FIFA score based on step count, heart rate, and preprocedural stress.

    Parameters:
    step_count (int): The number of steps taken by the patient in a day.
    heart_rate (int): The patient's heart rate.
    preprocedural_stress (int): The patient's stress level before the procedure.
    step_count_threshold (int): The threshold for step count.
    heart_rate_threshold (int): The threshold for heart rate.
    stress_threshold (int): The threshold for preprocedural stress.

    Returns:
    int: The FIFA score.
    """
    score = 0
    if step_count > step_count_threshold:
        score += 1
    if heart_rate > heart_rate_threshold:
        score += 1
    if preprocedural_stress > stress_threshold:
        score += 1
    return score
33055057.purpose: This calculator is used for preprocedural frailty assessment in patients undergoing transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR).
33055057.eligibility: Patients who are scheduled for either transfemoral or transapical TAVR and can wear a wrist-worn health-monitoring device for 1 week prior to the procedure.
33055057.interpretation:
- 0: No frailty
- 1: Borderline frailty
- 2: Moderate frailty
- 3: Severe frailty
33055057.utility: The FIFA score demonstrated the greatest predictive power for hospital mortality (FIFA area under the curve [AUC] 0.844, CI 0.656-1.000; P=.048).

33057996.title: PI-DLBCL Prognostic Stratification Calculator
33057996.code:
def compute_risk_score(TLG, non_GCB, NCCN_IPI):
    """
    Compute the risk score for PI-DLBCL prognosis.

    Parameters:
    TLG (bool): True if TLG is high, False otherwise.
    non_GCB (bool): True if the cell-of-origin is non-GCB, False otherwise.
    NCCN_IPI (bool): True if NCCN-IPI is high, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(TLG) + int(non_GCB) + int(NCCN_IPI)
33057996.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the prognosis of patients with primary intestinal diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (PI-DLBCL) treated with an R-CHOP-like regimen.
33057996.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with primary intestinal diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (PI-DLBCL) and treated with an R-CHOP-like regimen are eligible.
33057996.interpretation:
- 0: Low-risk group
- 1: Low-intermediate risk group
- 2: High-intermediate risk group
- 3: High-risk group
33057996.utility: The grading system was found to be significantly associated with progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) in PI-DLBCL patients (PFS: χ2 = 33.858, P < 0.001; OS: χ2 = 29.435, P < 0.001).

33061470.title: CRKP BSI Risk Score Calculator for Hematologic Malignancies
33061470.code:
def compute_risk_score(crkp_colonization, severe_neutropenia, recent_imv):
    """
    Compute the risk score for CRKP BSI in patients with hematologic malignancies.

    Parameters:
    crkp_colonization (bool): Whether the patient has CRKP rectal colonization.
    severe_neutropenia (bool): Whether the patient has severe neutropenia.
    recent_imv (bool): Whether the patient has had invasive mechanical ventilation within the previous 30 days.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if crkp_colonization:
        score += 3
    if severe_neutropenia:
        score += 1
    if recent_imv:
        score += 4
    return score
33061470.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of bloodstream infection (BSI) due to carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae (CRKP) in patients with hematologic malignancies.
33061470.eligibility: Patients with hematologic malignancies are eligible for this calculator.
33061470.interpretation:
- A total risk score of less than 5 indicates that the probability of CRKP BSI occurrence is less than 48%.
- A total risk score of 5 or more indicates that the probability of CRKP BSI occurrence is 48% or more.
33061470.utility: The risk score calculator has been validated in a single-center retrospective case-control study with 734 patients with hematologic malignancies. The study found that the risk score calculator could help clinicians start prompt effective anti-infective therapy in patients with suspicion of CRKP BSI and improve outcomes.

33063583.title: Vascular Calcification (VC) Score Calculator for Mortality Risk in Hemodialysis Patients
33063583.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality risk in maintenance hemodialysis (MHD) patients based on the number of calcified vascular beds.
33063583.eligibility: Patients undergoing maintenance hemodialysis are eligible for this calculator.
33063583.interpretation:
- VC score = 0: No increased risk of mortality.
- VC score = 1: 2.67 times higher risk of mortality.
- VC score = 2: 2.89 times higher risk of mortality.
- VC score = 3: 3.27 times higher risk of mortality.
33063583.utility: The VC score was found to be monotonically and independently associated with mortality in a population of African descent MHD patients. The hazard ratios for VC scores of 1, 2, and 3 were 2.67, 2.89, and 3.27 respectively, indicating a higher risk of mortality with increasing VC score.

33065167.title: Open-Angle Glaucoma Risk Calculator
33065167.code:
def compute_risk_score(quadrant, age, pulse_rate):
    """
    Compute the risk score for open-angle glaucoma.

    Parameters:
    quadrant (str): The quadrant of the eye, either 'superior', 'temporal', or 'inferior'.
    age (int): The patient's age in years.
    pulse_rate (int): The patient's pulse rate in beats per minute.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if quadrant in ['superior', 'temporal']:
        score += 1
    if age > 61:
        score += 1
    if pulse_rate > 74:
        score += 1
    return score
33065167.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk of a primary reduction in optic nerve head (ONH) tissue blood flow, which precedes circumpapillary retinal nerve fiber layer thickness (cpRNFLT) decrease, in patients with open-angle glaucoma (OAG).
33065167.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with open-angle glaucoma (OAG) are eligible.
33065167.interpretation:
- 0 risk points: Primary cpRNFLT decrease.
- 1 risk point: Simultaneous cpRNFLT and MT decrease.
- 2 or 3 risk points: Primary MT decrease.
33065167.utility: The risk score was found to be significantly associated with the time-course changes in optic nerve head blood flow and retinal nerve fiber layer thickness in eyes with open-angle glaucoma. However, the clinical utility of the risk score, such as AUC, F-score, PPV, was not reported in the article.

33067019.title: High-Risk Varices Prediction Calculator
33067019.code:
def compute_risk_score(SS, LS, SSZ):
    """
    Compute the risk score for high-risk varices.

    Parameters:
    SS (float): Spleen stiffness in kPa.
    LS (float): Liver stiffness in kPa.
    SSZ (float): Spleen size in cm.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return 0.053 * SS + 0.054 * LS + 0.059 * SSZ - 1.84
33067019.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the presence of high-risk varices in patients with compensated liver cirrhosis using spleen and liver stiffness measurements and spleen size.
33067019.eligibility: Patients with compensated liver cirrhosis who have undergone abdominal ultrasound spleen and liver stiffness measurements using a 2-D shear wave elastography technique are eligible.
33067019.interpretation:
- Risk Score <= 0.34: Low risk of high-risk varices.
- Risk Score > 0.34: High risk of high-risk varices.
33067019.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) for this risk score is 0.93, indicating excellent predictive performance. The score including spleen size, spleen stiffness measurement, and liver stiffness measurement performed better than each independent factor for predicting high-risk varices.

33068099.title: COVID-19 Mortality Risk Calculator using Modified PRO-AGE and NEWS Scores
33068099.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 60-day mortality risk in hospitalised COVID-19 patients aged 50 years and above.
33068099.eligibility: Hospitalised COVID-19 patients aged 50 years and above.
33068099.interpretation:
- Modified PRO-AGE scores 0-1: Lowest quartile, baseline risk
- Modified PRO-AGE scores 2-3: 1.4 times the baseline risk
- Modified PRO-AGE score 4: 2.0 times the baseline risk
- Modified PRO-AGE scores 5-7: 2.8 times the baseline risk
33068099.utility: The modified PRO-AGE predicted different mortality risk levels within each stratum of NEWS and improved the discrimination of mortality prediction models.

33077580.title: DEFUSE 3 Mortality Risk Score Calculator
33077580.code:
def calculate_defuse3_score(age, nihss, wake_up_stroke, diabetes):
    """
    Calculate DEFUSE 3 Mortality Risk Score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Patient's age.
    nihss (int): Baseline NIHSS score.
    wake_up_stroke (bool): Whether the patient had a wake-up stroke.
    diabetes (bool): Whether the patient has a history of diabetes.

    Returns:
    int: DEFUSE 3 Mortality Risk Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 75:
        score += 1
    if nihss >= 20:
        score += 1
    if wake_up_stroke:
        score += 1
    if diabetes:
        score += 1
    return score
33077580.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of death within 90 days after endovascular thrombectomy (EVT) in stroke patients during the period 6-16 hours after last normal.
33077580.eligibility: Patients who have undergone endovascular thrombectomy (EVT) for ischemic stroke during the period 6-16 hours after last normal.
33077580.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of death (3.9%)
- Score 2: Moderate risk of death (13.0%)
- Score 3-4: High risk of death (52.9%)
33077580.utility: The DEFUSE 3 Mortality Risk Score was found to be statistically significant in predicting death within 90 days after EVT (P<0.001).

33078280.title: Pediatric Respiratory Rate-Oxygenation Index (p-ROXI) and Variation (p-ROXV) Calculator
33078280.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of high-flow nasal cannula (HFNC) failure in pediatric patients with acute respiratory failure.
33078280.eligibility: Pediatric patients who have received HFNC therapy in the general pediatrics ward, pediatric intensive care unit, or the pediatric emergency department.
33078280.interpretation:
- 'Low risk': The risk of HFNC failure is low. At 24 hours, the failure rate is 1.9%. At 48 hours, the failure rate is 0.0%.
- 'High risk': The risk of HFNC failure is high. At 24 hours, the failure rate is 40.6%. At 48 hours, the failure rate is 100%.
33078280.utility: The study found that these parameters can be used as good markers in pediatric clinics to predict the risk of HFNC failure in patients with acute respiratory failure.

33103225.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Predicting Clinical Outcomes in Carotid Artery Stenting
33103225.code:
def calculate_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, stroke_tia_te, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Calculate CHA2DS2-VASc score based on the input parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    stroke_tia_te (bool): True if the patient has had a stroke, TIA or TE, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): 'M' for male, 'F' for female.
    
    Returns:
    int: CHA2DS2-VASc score.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if stroke_tia_te:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'F':
        score += 1
    return score
33103225.purpose: This calculator is used to predict in-hospital and long-term outcomes in patients undergoing carotid artery stenting (CAS).
33103225.eligibility: Patients undergoing carotid artery stenting are eligible for this calculator.
33103225.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 2: Low risk of in-hospital and 3-year stroke and mortality.
- Score 3-5: Moderate risk of in-hospital and 3-year stroke and mortality.
- Score 6-8: High risk of in-hospital and 3-year stroke and mortality.
33103225.utility: The CHA2DS2-VASc score was found to be an independent prognostic factor for mortality and major stroke in patients undergoing CAS. Higher scores predicted increased risk of in-hospital and 3-year stroke and mortality.

33106001.title: 5-Item Modified Frailty Index (mFI-5) for Below-Knee Amputation (BKA) Outcomes
33106001.code:
def compute_mfi_5(diabetes, copd_or_pneumonia, heart_failure, hypertension, nonindependent_status):
    """
    Compute the mFI-5 score.

    Parameters:
    diabetes (bool): True if the patient has a history of diabetes, False otherwise.
    copd_or_pneumonia (bool): True if the patient has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease or active pneumonia, False otherwise.
    heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    nonindependent_status (bool): True if the patient has a nonindependent functional status, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The mFI-5 score.
    """
    return diabetes + copd_or_pneumonia + heart_failure + hypertension + nonindependent_status
33106001.purpose: This calculator is used to predict post-operative complications, 30-day mortality, post-op myocardial infarction (MI), and failure of long-term prosthetic use in patients undergoing below-knee amputation (BKA).
33106001.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing below-knee amputation (BKA).
33106001.interpretation:
- Every one-point increase in mFI-5 is associated with:
  - An increased risk of 22% for combined complications
  - 60% for 30-day mortality
  - Nearly 80% for post-op MI
  - 17% for failure of prosthetic use in BKA patients
33106001.utility: The mFI-5 frailty index showed a significant association with higher risk of combined complications (OR 1.22, CI 1.07-1.38, P < .05), 30-day mortality (OR 1.60, CI 1.19-2.16, P < .05), post-op MI (OR 1.79, CI 1.30-2.45, P < .05), and failure of long-term prosthetic use (OR 1.17, CI 1.03-1.32, P < .05).

33118057.title: Modified qSOFA (MqSOFA) Calculator
33118057.code:
def calculate_mqsofa(mentation, systolic_bp, respiratory_rate, spo2_fio2_ratio):
    """
    Calculate the MqSOFA score.

    Parameters:
    mentation (bool): Altered mentation, True if present, False if not.
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure.
    respiratory_rate (int): Respiratory rate.
    spo2_fio2_ratio (int): SpO2/FiO2 ratio.

    Returns:
    int: MqSOFA score.
    """
    qsofa_score = int(mentation) + int(systolic_bp <= 100) + int(respiratory_rate >= 22)
    spo2_fio2_score = int(spo2_fio2_ratio <= 315)
    mqsofa_score = qsofa_score + spo2_fio2_score
    return mqsofa_score
33118057.purpose: This calculator is used for the early prognostic assessment of patients with sepsis or septic shock. It helps to predict in-hospital mortality.
33118057.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with sepsis or septic shock are eligible.
33118057.interpretation:
- MqSOFA score < 2: Low risk of in-hospital mortality.
- MqSOFA score ≥ 2: High risk of in-hospital mortality.
33118057.utility: The MqSOFA score has a greater ability to detect in-hospital mortality than the qSOFA score (AUC 0.805 vs. 0.712). It reclassified 8.5% of patients as high-risk, resulting in an improvement of sensitivity with a minor reduction in specificity.

33121304.title: HA2T2 COVID-19 Mortality Risk Score
33121304.code:
def compute_HA2T2_score(hypoxia, age, troponin_I):
    """
    Compute the HA2T2 score.

    Parameters:
    hypoxia (bool): True if the patient has hypoxia on presentation, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    troponin_I (float): The level of troponin-I in ng/mL.

    Returns:
    int: The HA2T2 score.
    """
    score = 0
    if hypoxia:
        score += 1
    if age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if troponin_I >= 0.34:
        score += 1
    return score
33121304.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day in-hospital mortality risk for patients hospitalized with COVID-19 based on hypoxia on presentation, age, and troponin-I elevation.
33121304.eligibility: Patients hospitalized with COVID-19 who have had measurements of the following biomarkers: troponin-I, B-type natriuretic peptide, C-reactive protein, ferritin, and d-dimer.
33121304.interpretation:
- Score <3: Low risk, with a 30-day mortality of 5.9%
- Score ≥3: High risk, with a 30-day mortality of 43.7%
33121304.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of the HA2T2 score was 0.834 for the derivation cohort and 0.784 for the validation cohort, indicating good predictive performance.

33128186.title: Spleen Volume-to-Platelet Ratio Index for High-Risk Varix Detection
33128186.code:
def compute_spleen_volume_to_platelet_ratio(spleen_volume, platelet_count):
    """
    Compute the spleen volume-to-platelet ratio.

    Args:
    spleen_volume (float): The volume of the spleen measured from CT images.
    platelet_count (float): The patient's platelet count.

    Returns:
    float: The spleen volume-to-platelet ratio.
    """
    return spleen_volume / platelet_count
33128186.purpose: This calculator is used to detect high-risk varices and assess the risk of varix bleeding in patients with B-viral compensated cirrhosis using spleen volume-to-platelet ratio.
33128186.eligibility: Patients with B-viral compensated cirrhosis who have undergone endoscopy and CT are eligible.
33128186.interpretation:
- Low risk: Index value ≤ 1.63; cumulative 5-year incidences of varix bleeding is 0%
- Intermediate risk: Index value > 1.63 and ≤ 3.78; cumulative 5-year incidences of varix bleeding is 1.0%
- High risk: Index value > 3.78; cumulative 5-year incidences of varix bleeding is 12.0%
33128186.utility: The cutoff index value for balanced sensitivity and specificity (> 3.78) resulted in the sensitivity of 69.4% and the specificity of 78.5% for detecting high-risk varix. The cutoff index value for high sensitivity (> 1.63) detected all high-risk varices.

33131341.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma Prognosis Calculator
33131341.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expressions, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with hepatocellular carcinoma.

    Parameters:
    gene_expressions (list of float): The expression levels of the six genes.
    coefficients (list of float): The coefficients of the six genes.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score of the patient.
    """
    risk_score = sum([a*b for a, b in zip(gene_expressions, coefficients)])
    return risk_score
33131341.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) based on the expression of six specific genes.
33131341.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with hepatocellular carcinoma are eligible for this calculator.
33131341.interpretation:
- Low risk: Patients with a lower risk score have a significantly better prognosis.
- High risk: Patients with a higher risk score have a poor prognosis.

The exact thresholds for "low" and "high" risk scores need to be determined in further studies.
33131341.utility: The clinical utility of this risk score has been validated in a cohort of 374 HCC patients. The risk score was able to accurately predict patient prognosis. However, the exact performance metrics such as AUC, F-score, PPV are not provided in the article.

33132561.title: PGI Score: A Simplified Three-point Prognostic Score for Acute Aluminum Phosphide Poisoning
33132561.code:
def compute_pgi_score(pH, GCS, SBP):
    """
    Compute the PGI score based on pH, GCS, and SBP.

    Args:
    pH (float): The pH value of the patient.
    GCS (int): The Glasgow Coma Scale score of the patient.
    SBP (int): The Systolic Blood Pressure of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The PGI score.
    """
    score = 0
    if pH < 7.25:
        score += 1
    if GCS < 13:
        score += 1
    if SBP < 87:
        score += 1
    return score
33132561.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality risk in patients with acute Aluminum Phosphide (AlP) poisoning.
33132561.eligibility: Patients aged 15 years and over with acute Aluminum Phosphide poisoning.
33132561.interpretation:
- A total score of 3: High risk of mortality (98.2% specificity and a positive predictive value of 96.4%)
- A total score ≤ 1: Low risk of mortality (100% sensitivity and 100% negative predictive value)
33132561.utility: The PGI score has shown high specificity and positive predictive value when the score is 3, and high sensitivity and negative predictive value when the score is less than or equal to 1.

33145663.title: Lung Ultrasound and Wells Score for Diagnosing Pulmonary Embolism in COVID-19 Patients
33145663.code:
def calculate_risk(large_consolidations, wells_score):
    """
    Calculate the risk of pulmonary embolism in COVID-19 patients.

    Parameters:
    large_consolidations (bool): True if large subpleural consolidations (≥ 1 cm) are present in lung ultrasound, False otherwise.
    wells_score (float): The Wells score of the patient.

    Returns:
    risk (str): The risk of pulmonary embolism.
    """
    if large_consolidations and wells_score >= 2:
        return 'High risk'
    else:
        return 'Low risk'
33145663.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the probability of pulmonary embolism (PE) in critically ill COVID-19 patients.
33145663.eligibility: Critically ill COVID-19 patients with ARDS who have undergone lung ultrasound and computed tomography pulmonary angiography (CTPA).
33145663.interpretation:
- 'High risk': The patient is considered to have a high risk of pulmonary embolism. This is based on the presence of large subpleural consolidations in lung ultrasound and a Wells score ≥ 2.
- 'Low risk': The patient is considered to have a low risk of pulmonary embolism. This is based on the absence of large subpleural consolidations in lung ultrasound or a Wells score < 2.
33145663.utility: The combination of lung ultrasound and Wells score predicted PE with a sensitivity of 100% and a specificity of 80%.

33169527.title: Stroke Risk Score in Heart Failure Patients with Sinus Rhythm
33169527.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, previous_is, ischaemic_heart_disease, diabetes, hypertension, kidney_dysfunction, nyha_class):
    """
    Compute the risk score for ischemic stroke in heart failure patients with sinus rhythm.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    previous_is (bool): Whether the patient has a history of ischemic stroke.
    ischaemic_heart_disease (bool): Whether the patient has ischaemic heart disease.
    diabetes (bool): Whether the patient has diabetes.
    hypertension (bool): Whether the patient has hypertension.
    kidney_dysfunction (bool): Whether the patient has kidney dysfunction.
    nyha_class (int): New York Heart Association class (III or IV).

    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if 65 <= age < 75:
        score += 1
    elif 75 <= age < 85:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 85:
        score += 3
    if previous_is:
        score += 2
    if ischaemic_heart_disease:
        score += 1
    if diabetes:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if kidney_dysfunction:
        score += 1
    if nyha_class in [3, 4]:
        score += 1
    return score
33169527.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of ischemic stroke/transient ischemic attack (IS) in patients with heart failure who are in sinus rhythm (HF-SR).
33169527.eligibility: Patients with heart failure who are in sinus rhythm.
33169527.interpretation:
- Score 0: 2.2 per 1000 person-years
- Score 1: 5.3 per 1000 person-years
- Score 2: 8.9 per 1000 person-years
- Score 3: 13.2 per 1000 person-years
- Score 4: 15.7 per 1000 person-years
- Score 5: 20.4 per 1000 person-years
- Score 6: 26.4 per 1000 person-years
- Score ≥7: 33.0 per 1000 person-years
33169527.utility: The risk score performed modestly with an area under the curve (AUC) of 63.7%.

33178755.title: Fibrosis Index (FI) Risk Calculator for Esophageal Variceal Bleeding (EVB)
33178755.code:
def calculate_risk(FI):
    """
    Calculate the risk of EVB based on the Fibrosis Index (FI).
    
    Args:
    FI (float): The Fibrosis Index of the patient.

    Returns:
    str: The risk level of the patient.
    """
    if FI < 3.95:
        return "Low risk"
    else:
        return "High risk"
33178755.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of subsequent EVB within 1 and 2 years in patients with compensated liver cirrhosis (CLC), initial small EV without red-color sign (RCS), without use of non-selective beta-blockers (NSBB) and endoscopic variceal ligation (EVL).
33178755.eligibility: Patients with compensated liver cirrhosis (CLC), initial small esophageal varices (EV) without red-color sign (RCS), and not using non-selective beta-blockers (NSBB) or endoscopic variceal ligation (EVL).
33178755.interpretation:
- Low risk: FI < 3.95. These patients have a low risk of EVB within 1 and 2 years.
- High risk: FI >= 3.95. These patients have a high risk of EVB within 1 and 2 years.
33178755.utility: The Fibrosis Index (FI) with a cut-off value of 3.95 showed a negative predictive value (NPV) of 94.3% and an area under receiver operating characteristic (AUROC) of 62.95% for predicting subsequent EVB within 1 year.

33178828.title: GDF15 Level Based Risk Calculator for Cirrhotic Primary Biliary Cholangitis
33178828.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the disease severity, prognosis, nutrition, and response to treatment of cirrhotic Primary Biliary Cholangitis (PBC) patients based on circulating Growth Differentiation Factor 15 (GDF15) levels.
33178828.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with cirrhotic Primary Biliary Cholangitis are eligible.
33178828.interpretation:
- GDF15 level > 4073.30 pg/mL: High risk of decompensated cirrhosis
- GDF15 level > 3646.55 pg/mL: High risk of cirrhosis
- GDF15 level > 3479.20 pg/mL: High risk of cirrhotic biochemical non-responders
- GDF15 level <= 3479.20 pg/mL: Low risk
33178828.utility: The overall diagnostic accuracies of GDF15 were as follows: cirrhosis = 0.725, decompensated cirrhosis = 0.956, and cirrhotic biochemical non-responders = 0.835.

33179472.title: Prognostic Index for Transformed Waldenström Macroglobulinemia
33179472.code:
def compute_risk_score(ldh_elevated, platelet_count, previous_treatment):
    """
    Compute the risk score for transformed Waldenström macroglobulinemia.

    Parameters:
    ldh_elevated (bool): Whether the patient has elevated serum LDH.
    platelet_count (float): The patient's platelet count (in 10^9/L).
    previous_treatment (bool): Whether the patient has received any previous treatment for WM.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if ldh_elevated:
        score += 2
    if platelet_count < 100:
        score += 1
    if previous_treatment:
        score += 1
    return score
33179472.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 2-year survival rate in patients with transformed Waldenström macroglobulinemia (WM).
33179472.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with transformed Waldenström macroglobulinemia are eligible.
33179472.interpretation:
The risk score is interpreted as follows:

- 0-1 point: Low-risk group, with a 2-year survival rate of 81%
- 2-3 points: Intermediate-risk group, with a 2-year survival rate of 47%
- 4 points: High-risk group, with a 2-year survival rate of 21%
33179472.utility: The model was validated in an independent cohort and was found to be a better discriminant than the International Prognostic Index (IPI) and the revised IPI (R-IPI).

33195327.title: Mortality and Cerebrovascular and Cardiovascular Events Risk Calculator for Hemodialysis Patients
33195327.code:
def calculate_risk(PWV, galectin_3):
    """
    Calculate the risk of mortality and cerebrovascular and cardiovascular events.

    Parameters:
    PWV (float): Pulse Wave Velocity in m/s.
    galectin_3 (float): Galectin-3 levels in ng/ml.

    Returns:
    string: Risk group.
    """
    if PWV <= 7.9 and galectin_3 <= 30.5:
        return 'low PWV-low galectin-3'
    elif PWV > 7.9 and galectin_3 <= 30.5:
        return 'high PWV-low galectin-3'
    elif PWV <= 7.9 and galectin_3 > 30.5:
        return 'low PWV-high galectin-3'
    else:
        return 'high PWV-high galectin-3'
33195327.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality and cerebrovascular and cardiovascular events in hemodialysis patients.
33195327.eligibility: End-stage renal disease patients who are undergoing stable hemodialysis and do not have preexisting cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases.
33195327.interpretation:
- 'low PWV-low galectin-3': Lowest risk of mortality and cerebrovascular and cardiovascular events.
- 'low PWV-high galectin-3': Increased risk of mortality and cerebrovascular and cardiovascular events.
- 'high PWV-low galectin-3': Increased risk of mortality and cerebrovascular and cardiovascular events.
- 'high PWV-high galectin-3': Highest risk of mortality and cerebrovascular and cardiovascular events, with a 3-fold increased risk compared to the low PWV-low galectin-3 group.
33195327.utility: The combination of PWV and galectin-3 was associated with improved model discrimination, calibration, and reclassification.

33195462.title: qSOFA Score for Heart Failure Severity
33195462.code:
def compute_qsofa(systolic_bp, respiratory_rate, gcs):
    """
    Compute the qSOFA score based on systolic blood pressure, respiratory rate, and Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS).
    
    Args:
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure in mmHg.
    respiratory_rate (int): Respiratory rate in breaths per minute.
    gcs (int): Glasgow Coma Scale score.
    
    Returns:
    int: The qSOFA score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if systolic_bp <= 100:
        score += 1
    if respiratory_rate >= 22:
        score += 1
    if gcs < 15:
        score += 1
    return score
33195462.purpose: This calculator is used to assess disease severity in patients with acute decompensated heart failure (ADHF) and predict their outcome.
33195462.eligibility: Patients admitted to a Heart Failure Unit (HFU) with acute decompensated heart failure are eligible.
33195462.interpretation:
- qSOFA score of 0-1: Lower risk of mortality or readmission due to worsening of heart failure symptoms.
- qSOFA score of 2-3: Higher risk of mortality or readmission due to worsening of heart failure symptoms.
33195462.utility: Patients with a qSOFA score of 2 or more reached the primary endpoint (all-cause mortality or readmission to hospital due to worsening of heart failure symptoms) more frequently (48% vs. 19%).

33196587.title: Adverse Discharge in Older Patients after Lower Extremity Surgery (ADELES) Risk Score
33196587.code:
def calculate_adeles_score(age, surgery_type, fracture_management, dementia, marital_status, insurance, income):
    """
    Calculate the ADELES risk score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient. 10 points if age > 90, else 0.
    surgery_type (str): Type of surgery. 7 points for hip surgery, 8 points for knee surgery, else 0.
    fracture_management (bool): Whether fracture management was involved. 6 points if True, else 0.
    dementia (bool): Whether the patient has dementia. 5 points if True, else 0.
    marital_status (str): Marital status of the patient. 3 points if unmarried, else 0.
    insurance (str): Type of insurance. 2 points if federally provided, else 0.
    income (str): Estimated household income. 1 point if low, else 0.

    Returns:
    int: The calculated ADELES risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 90:
        score += 10
    if surgery_type == 'hip':
        score += 7
    elif surgery_type == 'knee':
        score += 8
    if fracture_management:
        score += 6
    if dementia:
        score += 5
    if marital_status == 'unmarried':
        score += 3
    if insurance == 'federal':
        score += 2
    if income == 'low':
        score += 1
    return score
33196587.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of adverse discharge disposition in patients older than 60 years after lower-extremity surgery.
33196587.eligibility: Patients who are 60 years or older and are undergoing lower-extremity orthopedic surgery.
33196587.interpretation:
- Score < 23: Lower risk of adverse discharge disposition
- Score ≥ 23: Higher risk of adverse discharge disposition
33196587.utility: The ADELES score has an ROC-AUC of 0.85 in the development cohort and 0.72 in the independent validation cohort, indicating excellent and good discriminative ability. It outperforms other prediction scores considering frailty, comorbidities, or procedural risk alone.

33214165.title: MRE combined with FIB-4 (MEFIB) Index Calculator
33214165.code:
def compute_mefib_index(mre, fib4):
    """
    Compute the MEFIB index based on MRE and FIB-4 values.

    Args:
    mre (float): The MRE value in kPa.
    fib4 (float): The FIB-4 score.

    Returns:
    bool: True if MRE ≥3.3 kPa and FIB-4 ≥1.6, False otherwise.
    """
    return mre >= 3.3 and fib4 >= 1.6
33214165.purpose: This calculator is used for non-invasive identification of candidates for pharmacological therapy among patients with non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) who have ≥stage 2 fibrosis.
33214165.eligibility: Patients with non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) who have undergone MR elastography (MRE) and have a fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) score.
33214165.interpretation:
- If the MEFIB index is True (MRE ≥3.3 kPa and FIB-4 ≥1.6), the patient is a candidate for pharmacological therapy for NAFLD with ≥stage 2 fibrosis.
- If the MEFIB index is False, the patient is not a candidate for pharmacological therapy for NAFLD with ≥stage 2 fibrosis.
33214165.utility: The MEFIB index demonstrated a clinically significant diagnostic accuracy for the detection of ≥stage 2 fibrosis with an area under the ROC curve (AUROC) of 0.90 (95% CI 0.85 to 0.95) in the UCSD-NAFLD cohort and remained significant at AUROC of 0.84 (95% CI 0.78 to 0.89) in the Japan-NAFLD Cohort. The positive predictive value (PPV) was 97.1% in the UCSD-NAFLD cohort and 91.0% in the Japan-NAFLD Cohort.

33215084.title: The PRESSURE Score Calculator
33215084.code:
def compute_pressure_score(parenchymal, rapid_vasospasm, early_infarction, aneurysm_sac, surgery, age, hunt_hess_grade, external_ventricular_drain):
    """
    Compute the PRESSURE score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    parenchymal (bool): True if the patient has parenchymal haemorrhage, False otherwise.
    rapid_vasospasm (bool): True if the patient has rapid vasospasm on angiography, False otherwise.
    early_infarction (bool): True if the patient has early cerebral infarction, False otherwise.
    aneurysm_sac (bool): True if the patient has aneurysm sac > 5mm, False otherwise.
    surgery (bool): True if the patient has undergone surgery (clipping), False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    hunt_hess_grade (int): The Hunt and Hess grade of the patient.
    external_ventricular_drain (bool): True if the patient has an external ventricular drain, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The PRESSURE score.
    """
    score = 0
    if parenchymal:
        score += 1
    if rapid_vasospasm:
        score += 1
    if early_infarction:
        score += 1
    if aneurysm_sac:
        score += 1
    if surgery:
        score += 1
    if age < 55:
        score += 2
    if hunt_hess_grade >= 4:
        score += 1
    if external_ventricular_drain:
        score += 1
    return score
33215084.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the need for decompressive craniectomy after aneurysmal subarachnoid haemorrhage.
33215084.eligibility: Patients with aneurysmal subarachnoid haemorrhage are eligible for this calculator.
33215084.interpretation:
- PRESSURE score < 6: Low risk of requiring decompressive craniectomy (12%)
- PRESSURE score ≥ 6: High risk of requiring decompressive craniectomy (63.7%)
33215084.utility: The PRESSURE score showed high diagnostic accuracy for the prediction of primary and secondary decompressive craniectomy in the test (AUC = 0.842/0.818) and validation cohorts (AUC = 0.903/0.823).

33217456.title: 4Kscore Test for Prostate Cancer Risk
33217456.code:
def compute_4kscore_risk(kscore):
    """
    Compute the risk of aggressive prostate cancer based on 4Kscore.

    Args:
    kscore (float): The 4Kscore test result.

    Returns:
    str: The risk level of aggressive prostate cancer.
    """
    if kscore <= 7.5:
        return 'Low risk'
    else:
        return 'High risk'
33217456.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of clinically significant prostate cancer, helping to decide whether a prostate biopsy is necessary.
33217456.eligibility: Men aged 45-75 years with a total prostate specific antigen between 3.0 and 10.0 ng/mL.
33217456.interpretation:
- 'Low risk': The patient has a low risk of aggressive prostate cancer. The sensitivity for detecting Grade group (GG) ≥2 cancer is 94%, and the negative predictive value is 95%. No GG ≥4 cancers were found with a low risk 4Kscore.
- 'High risk': The patient has a high risk of aggressive prostate cancer and should consider a prostate biopsy.
33217456.utility: The 4Kscore test cut-off of 7.5% allowed a significant biopsy reduction, while maintaining high sensitivity and NPV for detecting and ruling out aggressive prostate cancer.

33236252.title: Sarcoma Lung Metastasis Score
33236252.code:
def compute_score(disease_free_interval, tumor_diameter, num_metastases):
    """
    Compute the Sarcoma Lung Metastasis Score.

    Parameters:
    disease_free_interval (int): The disease-free interval in years.
    tumor_diameter (float): The maximum tumor diameter in mm.
    num_metastases (int): The number of pulmonary metastases.

    Returns:
    int: The Sarcoma Lung Metastasis Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if disease_free_interval < 2:
        score += 1
    if tumor_diameter >= 15:
        score += 1
    if num_metastases >= 6:
        score += 1
    return score
33236252.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 3-year survival rate of sarcoma patients with pulmonary metastases.
33236252.eligibility: Patients with sarcoma who have pulmonary metastases and are considering undergoing a first pulmonary metastasectomy.
33236252.interpretation:
- Score 0: 89.5% 3-year survival rate
- Score 1: 63.2% 3-year survival rate
- Score 2: 39.0% 3-year survival rate
- Score 3: 10.5% 3-year survival rate
33236252.utility: The Sarcoma Lung Metastasis Score reliably predicted 3-year survival in the study cohort.

33243159.title: Surgical Site Infection (SSI) Risk Calculator for HIV-Positive Patients Following Orthopedic Surgery
33243159.code:
def compute_risk_score(CD4, ESR, PCT):
    """
    Compute the risk score for surgical site infection (SSI) in HIV-positive patients following orthopedic surgery.

    Parameters:
    CD4 (float): CD4 cell count
    ESR (float): Erythrocyte sedimentation rate
    PCT (float): Procalcitonin level

    Returns:
    float: Risk score for SSI
    """
    CD4_factor = 1 if CD4 < 430.75 else 0
    ESR_factor = 1 if ESR < 17.46 else 0
    PCT_factor = 1 if PCT < 0.22 else 0

    risk_score = -2.63589 - 0.00314*CD4_factor + 0.04695*ESR_factor + 2.93694*PCT_factor

    return risk_score
33243159.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of surgical site infection (SSI) in HIV-positive patients following orthopedic surgery.
33243159.eligibility: HIV-positive patients who have undergone orthopedic surgery.
33243159.interpretation:
- If the risk score is less than -2.1026, the patient has a low risk of SSI.
- If the risk score is greater than or equal to -2.1026, the patient has a high risk of SSI.
33243159.utility: The area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve was 0.946, indicating a high level of accuracy. The cutoff score was -2.1026 with a sensitivity of 93.33% and a specificity of 84.88%.

33246179.title: Colloid Cyst Risk Score (CCRS) Calculator
33246179.code:
def compute_ccrs(age, diameter, headache):
    """
    Compute the Colloid Cyst Risk Score (CCRS).

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    diameter (float): Diameter of the cyst in mm.
    headache (bool): Whether the patient has a headache at presentation.

    Returns:
    int: The computed CCRS.
    """
    score = 0
    if age < 65:
        score += 1
    if diameter >= 7:
        score += 1
    if headache:
        score += 1
    return score
33246179.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of patients with colloid cysts (CCs) becoming symptomatic or developing obstructive hydrocephalus.
33246179.eligibility: Patients with diagnosed colloid cysts are eligible.
33246179.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of becoming symptomatic or developing obstructive hydrocephalus.
- Score 1-2: Moderate risk of becoming symptomatic or developing obstructive hydrocephalus.
- Score 3: High risk of becoming symptomatic or developing obstructive hydrocephalus. All patients with cyst growth and symptomatic progression had an initial CCRS of ≥3.
33246179.utility: The CCRS was highly effective at identifying symptomatic patients and those with obstructive hydrocephalus in the study of 103 patients.

33266264.title: Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) Calculator
33266264.code:
def calculate_gnri(albumin, present_body_weight, ideal_body_weight):
    """
    Calculate the Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI)

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Serum albumin level in g/L
    present_body_weight (float): Present body weight in kg
    ideal_body_weight (float): Ideal body weight in kg

    Returns:
    float: GNRI score
    """
    weight_ratio = present_body_weight / ideal_body_weight if present_body_weight <= ideal_body_weight else 1
    gnri = (1.489 * albumin) + (41.7 * weight_ratio)
    return gnri
33266264.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the nutritional status of elderly patients with femoral fractures and predict their in-hospital mortality risk.
33266264.eligibility: Elderly patients with femoral fractures are eligible for this calculator.
33266264.interpretation:
- GNRI < 82: Major nutritional risk group with a significantly higher mortality rate (17.3%)
- GNRI 82 - <92: Moderate nutritional risk group with a mortality rate of 6.7%
- GNRI 92 - 98: Low nutritional risk group with a mortality rate of 2.4%
- GNRI > 98: No nutritional risk group with a mortality rate of 3.6%
33266264.utility: The GNRI calculator has been shown to have a significant prognostic value in predicting in-hospital mortality among elderly patients with femoral fractures. Patients in the major risk group had significantly higher odds of mortality than those in the no-risk group (odds ratio, 6.3; 95% confidence interval, 1.34-29.37; p = 0.009).

33276383.title: Ischemic Stroke Prognostic Metrics Calculator
33276383.code:
def compute_risk_score(mmp9, s100a8_a9, hscrp, gdf15):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the levels of four biomarkers.
    
    Parameters:
    mmp9 (float): Level of MMP-9.
    s100a8_a9 (float): Level of S100A8/A9.
    hscrp (float): Level of hsCRP.
    gdf15 (float): Level of GDF-15.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score (0-4).
    """
    mmp9_high = mmp9 > mmp9_median
    s100a8_a9_high = s100a8_a9 > s100a8_a9_median
    hscrp_high = hscrp > hscrp_median
    gdf15_high = gdf15 > gdf15_median
    
    return mmp9_high + s100a8_a9_high + hscrp_high + gdf15_high
33276383.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis at 3 months after ischemic stroke based on four prognostic metrics: Matrix metalloproteinase-9 (MMP-9), S100A8/A9, high-sensitivity C-reactive protein (hsCRP), and growth differentiation factor-15 (GDF-15).
33276383.eligibility: Patients who have suffered an ischemic stroke are eligible for this calculator.
33276383.interpretation:
- 0-1 high-level biomarkers: Low risk of adverse clinical outcomes
- 2-3 high-level biomarkers: Moderate risk of adverse clinical outcomes
- 4 high-level biomarkers: High risk of adverse clinical outcomes (OR: 3.84, 95%CI: 2.67-5.51; PAF: 37.4%, 95%CI: 19.5%-52.9%)
33276383.utility: The set of prognostic metrics was independently associated with poor prognosis of ischemic stroke. The population-attributable fractions (PAFs) related to the risk of clinical outcomes were estimated to be 37.4% (95%CI: 19.5%-52.9%) for patients with 4 high-level prognostic metrics.

33277744.title: R-CHA2 DS2 -VASc Score Calculator
33277744.code:
def compute_r_cha2_ds2_vasc_score(cha2_ds2_vasc_score, renal_dysfunction):
    """
    Compute the R-CHA2 DS2 -VASc score.

    Parameters:
    cha2_ds2_vasc_score (int): The original CHA2 DS2 -VASc score.
    renal_dysfunction (bool): Whether the patient has advanced renal dysfunction.

    Returns:
    int: The R-CHA2 DS2 -VASc score.
    """
    return cha2_ds2_vasc_score + int(renal_dysfunction)
33277744.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk of all-cause mortality and poor functional outcome in patients with embolic stroke of undetermined source (ESUS), by incorporating advanced renal dysfunction into the CHA2 DS2 -VASc score.
33277744.eligibility: Patients with embolic stroke of undetermined source (ESUS) are eligible.
33277744.interpretation:
- R-CHA2 DS2 -VASc score ≥2: The risk of all-cause mortality increases by 1.94-fold.
- R-CHA2 DS2 -VASc score ≥4: The risk of poor functional outcome at third-month post-stroke increases by 1.62-fold, and at sixth-month post-stroke increases by 1.81-fold.
33277744.utility: Incorporating advanced renal dysfunction significantly improved risk discrimination over the original CHA2 DS2 -VASc score, as shown by the integrated discrimination improvement (IDI) and category-free net reclassification index (NRI).

33283324.title: Lewis Score and Prognostic Nutritional Index Calculator for Crohn's Disease
33283324.code:
def calculate_risk(LS, PNI):
    """
    Calculate the risk of Crohn's disease-related emergency hospitalization.

    Parameters:
    LS (int): Lewis Score from capsule endoscopy.
    PNI (int): Prognostic Nutritional Index.

    Returns:
    str: Risk category.
    """
    if LS >= 270 and PNI < 45:
        return 'High risk'
    elif LS >= 270 or PNI < 45:
        return 'Moderate risk'
    else:
        return 'Low risk'
33283324.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of Crohn's disease-related emergency hospitalization based on the Lewis Score (LS) and Prognostic Nutritional Index (PNI).
33283324.eligibility: Patients with Crohn's disease without gastrointestinal stenosis who have undergone capsule endoscopy are eligible.
33283324.interpretation:
- 'High risk': Patients with LS ≥ 270 and PNI < 45 are at high risk for Crohn's disease-related emergency hospitalization.
- 'Moderate risk': Patients with either LS ≥ 270 or PNI < 45 are at moderate risk for Crohn's disease-related emergency hospitalization.
- 'Low risk': Patients with LS < 270 and PNI ≥ 45 are at low risk for Crohn's disease-related emergency hospitalization.
33283324.utility: The study identified LS ≥ 270 and PNI < 45 as independent predictors of Crohn's disease-related emergency hospitalization with hazard ratios of 9.48 and 3.01, respectively.

33283484.title: Octogenarian Risk Score for Percutaneous Coronary Intervention
33283484.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, angina_type, creatinine, ventilation, ejection_fraction, shock):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient
    angina_type (str): Type of angina - 'NSTEMI' or 'STEMI'
    creatinine (float): Creatinine level in mmol/L
    ventilation (bool): Whether the patient is on preprocedural ventilation
    ejection_fraction (float): Left ventricular ejection fraction in percentage
    shock (bool): Whether the patient is in cardiogenic shock
    
    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if 80 <= age < 90:
        score += 1
    elif age > 90:
        score += 2
    if angina_type == 'NSTEMI':
        score += 1
    elif angina_type == 'STEMI':
        score += 2
    if creatinine > 200:
        score += 1
    if ventilation:
        score += 1
    if ejection_fraction < 30:
        score += 1
    if shock:
        score += 2
    return score
33283484.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality risk for octogenarians undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI).
33283484.eligibility: Patients aged 80 and above who are presenting for PCI are eligible.
33283484.interpretation:
- Scores of 1-4: Associated with good survival
- Scores ≥5: Associated with an estimated likelihood of death within 30 days of ≥40%
33283484.utility: The scoring system generated an AUC of 0.83, suggesting high sensitivity and specificity.

33298030.title: Creatinine-Albumin Ratio (Cr/Alb) Prognostic Score for Acute Pancreatitis
33298030.code:
def compute_cr_alb_ratio(creatinine, albumin):
    """
    Compute the Creatinine-Albumin (Cr/Alb) ratio.

    Parameters:
    creatinine (float): The serum creatinine level.
    albumin (float): The serum albumin level.

    Returns:
    float: The Cr/Alb ratio.
    """
    return creatinine / albumin
33298030.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of re-operation and mortality in patients with acute pancreatitis who underwent debridement.
33298030.eligibility: Patients with acute pancreatitis who are undergoing or have undergone debridement.
33298030.interpretation:
- Cr/Alb ratio < 3.43: Lower risk of re-operation and mortality.
- Cr/Alb ratio >= 3.43: Higher risk of re-operation and mortality, indicative of a worse clinical state, including impaired laboratory test values, APACHE II scores, rates of post-operative complications and re-operation, and mortality.
33298030.utility: The Cr/Alb ratio at admission could predict the risk of AP-related re-operation and mortality with sensitivities, specificities and areas under the curve of 86.3%, 61.7% and 0.824, and 73.4%, 81.3% and 0.794, respectively.

33308976.title: Pathomolecular Risk Score Calculator for Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer (MIBC)
33308976.code:
def compute_risk_score(resection_margin, mixed_histology, LVI, pN, Ki67):
    """
    Compute the pathomolecular risk score for a patient with MIBC.

    Args:
    resection_margin (bool): True if resection margin is positive, False otherwise.
    mixed_histology (bool): True if mixed histology is present, False otherwise.
    LVI (bool): True if lymphovascular invasion is positive, False otherwise.
    pN (bool): True if pN status is positive, False otherwise.
    Ki67 (bool): True if Ki67 index is high (>17%), False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The pathomolecular risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if resection_margin:
        score += 1
    if mixed_histology:
        score += 1
    if LVI:
        score += 1
    if pN:
        score += 1
    if Ki67:
        score += 1
    return score
33308976.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis and pathological factors in patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC) after radical cystectomy (RC).
33308976.eligibility: Patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer who have undergone radical cystectomy.
33308976.interpretation:
- Score <= 1: Lower risk of recurrence and longer recurrence-free survival (RFS) and overall survival.
- Score > 1: Higher risk of recurrence and shorter RFS and overall survival.
33308976.utility: The pathomolecular risk score was significantly associated with recurrence-free survival (RFS) and overall survival in the study. Patients with higher risk scores (>1) had significantly shorter RFS and overall survival than those with lower risk scores (≤1).

33318493.title: Breast Cancer Polygenic Risk Score (PRS) Calculator
33318493.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the lifetime risk of breast cancer based on the presence of high-impact frameshift risk variants and the individual's polygenic risk score (PRS).
33318493.eligibility: This calculator is applicable to women who carry the c.1592delT variant in PALB2 or c.1100delC in CHEK2, and have a known PRS.
33318493.interpretation:
- For carriers of the c.1592delT variant in PALB2:
  - High PRS (>90th percentile): 84% lifetime risk of breast cancer
  - Average PRS (10-90th percentile): 55% lifetime risk
  - Low PRS (<10th percentile): 49% lifetime risk
- For carriers of the c.1100delC variant in CHEK2:
  - High PRS (>90th percentile): 59% lifetime risk
  - Average PRS (10-90th percentile): 29% lifetime risk
  - Low PRS (<10th percentile): 9% lifetime risk
33318493.utility: The PRS refines the risk assessment of women with first-degree relatives diagnosed with breast cancer, particularly among women with a positive family history of early-onset breast cancer.

33321867.title: Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) Calculator
33321867.code:
def calculate_gnri(albumin, current_body_weight, ideal_body_weight):
    """
    Calculate GNRI based on albumin level and body weight ratio.

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Serum albumin level in g/L.
    current_body_weight (float): Current body weight in kg.
    ideal_body_weight (float): Ideal body weight in kg.

    Returns:
    float: GNRI score.
    """
    weight_ratio = current_body_weight / ideal_body_weight if current_body_weight <= ideal_body_weight else 1
    gnri = 1.489 * albumin + 41.7 * weight_ratio
    return gnri
33321867.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the impact of malnutrition risk on mortality in adult patients with polytrauma.
33321867.eligibility: Adult patients with polytrauma are eligible for this calculator.
33321867.interpretation:
- High-risk group: GNRI < 82, mortality rate is 34.5%
- Moderate-risk group: GNRI 82 to <92, mortality rate is 20.1%
- Low-risk group: GNRI 92-98, mortality rate is 8.5%
- No-risk group: GNRI > 98, mortality rate is 12.1%
33321867.utility: The GNRI was found to be a significant independent risk factor for mortality in adult patients with polytrauma. The odds ratio was 0.94 (95% CI, 0.91-0.97; p < 0.001).

33350037.title: Genetic Risk Score (GRS) Calculator for Cardiovascular Disease Risk in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
33350037.code:
def compute_grs(genetic_variants):
    """
    Compute the Genetic Risk Score (GRS) for a patient.

    Args:
    genetic_variants (dict): A dictionary where keys are the names of the 35 genetic variants and values are the number of risk alleles (0, 1, or 2) for each variant.

    Returns:
    float: The computed GRS for the patient.
    """
    grs = 0
    for variant, alleles in genetic_variants.items():
        grs += alleles * log_odds_ratios[variant]  # log_odds_ratios is a predefined dictionary of log ORs for each variant
    return grs
33350037.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of cardiovascular disease in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus based on 35 genetic variants.
33350037.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus are eligible for this calculator.
33350037.interpretation:
- Patients with a weighted GRS < 10.39 have a baseline risk of cardiovascular disease.
- Patients with a weighted GRS ≥ 12.63 have an approximately 15-fold increase in cardiovascular disease risk compared to patients with a weighted GRS < 10.39.
33350037.utility: The addition of the weighted GRS to conventional risk factors improved the area under the receiver-operating characteristic curve from 0.719 (95% CI = 0.689-0.750) to 0.888 (95% CI = 0.866-0.910).

33364550.title: Mantle Cell Lymphoma Prognostic Risk Score Calculator
33364550.code:
def compute_risk_score(mcl_index, cell_proliferation, blastoid_morphology, tp53_mutations):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with Mantle Cell Lymphoma.

    Parameters:
    mcl_index (bool): True if the MCL international prognostic index is high, False otherwise.
    cell_proliferation (bool): True if the cell proliferation marker is high, False otherwise.
    blastoid_morphology (bool): True if blastoid morphology is present, False otherwise.
    tp53_mutations (bool): True if TP53 mutations are present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    return int(mcl_index) + int(cell_proliferation) + int(blastoid_morphology) + int(tp53_mutations)
33364550.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 5-year overall survival rate of patients with Mantle Cell Lymphoma (MCL) who have relapsed after initial treatment.
33364550.eligibility: Patients who have been diagnosed with Mantle Cell Lymphoma and have relapsed after initial treatment are eligible.
33364550.interpretation:
- Risk Score 0: 5-year overall survival rate of 62%
- Risk Score 1: 5-year overall survival rate of 39%
- Risk Score 2-3: 5-year overall survival rate of 31%
