- Risk Score 4-5: 5-year overall survival rate of 0%
33364550.utility: The risk score showed independent prognostic value irrespective of progression of disease before 24 months (POD24). The clinical utility of the risk score in terms of AUC, F-score, PPV was not provided in the article.

33370365.title: EGFR-Mutated Lung Adenocarcinoma Survival Prognostic Calculator
33370365.code:
def compute_score(suv_entropy, pleural_effusion):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on SUV entropy and presence of pleural effusion.

    Args:
    suv_entropy (float): The SUV entropy of the primary tumor.
    pleural_effusion (bool): Whether the patient has pleural effusion.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score (0-2).
    """
    score = 0
    if suv_entropy > 5.36:
        score += 1
    if pleural_effusion:
        score += 1
    return score
33370365.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the survival outcomes of patients with stage III-IV lung adenocarcinoma and actionable EGFR mutation who received first-line TKI treatment.
33370365.eligibility: Patients with stage III-IV lung adenocarcinoma and actionable EGFR mutation who received first-line TKI treatment are eligible.
33370365.interpretation:
- Score 0: Lower risk of disease progression and death.
- Score 1: Medium risk of disease progression (HR for PFS: 2.2) and death (HR for OS: 3.6).
- Score 2: High risk of disease progression (HR for PFS: 8.8) and death (HR for OS: 21.8).
33370365.utility: The scoring system surpassed the TNM staging system (c-index = 0.691 versus 0.574, p = 0.013 for OS, and c-index = 0.649 versus 0.517, p = 0.004 for PFS).

33384274.title: REMARCC Risk Score Calculator for Metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma (mRCC)
33384274.code:
def calculate_REMARCC_score(obesity, bone_metastases, liver_metastases, lung_metastases, performance_status, num_metastases):
    """
    Calculate REMARCC Risk Score for mRCC patients.

    Parameters:
    obesity (bool): True if the patient is obese, False otherwise.
    bone_metastases (bool): True if the patient has bone metastases, False otherwise.
    liver_metastases (bool): True if the patient has liver metastases, False otherwise.
    lung_metastases (bool): True if the patient has lung metastases, False otherwise.
    performance_status (int): Performance status of the patient, should be less than 80 for higher OM rates.
    num_metastases (int): Number of metastases, should be more than 3 for higher OM rates.

    Returns:
    int: REMARCC Risk Score
    """
    score = 0
    if not obesity:
        score += 1
    if bone_metastases or liver_metastases or lung_metastases:
        score += 1
    if performance_status < 80:
        score += 1
    if num_metastases > 3:
        score += 1
    return score
33384274.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall mortality (OM) in patients with metastatic renal cell carcinoma (mRCC) undergoing cytoreductive nephrectomy (CN).
33384274.eligibility: Patients with synchronous mRCC undergoing CN are eligible.
33384274.interpretation:
- Score 0: Favorable prognosis (no risk factors)
- Score 1-2: Intermediate prognosis (one or two risk factors)
- Score >2: Poor prognosis (more than two risk factors)
33384274.utility: The concordance index (C-index) of the REMARCC model was higher than that of the MSKCC and IMDC models (66.4% vs 60.4% vs 60.3%).

33390431.title: COVID-19 Critical Risk Prediction Score
33390431.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, sex, hypertension, cancer, fever, dyspnea):
    """
    Compute the COVID-19 Critical Risk Prediction Score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient
    sex (str): Sex of the patient, 'male' or 'female'
    hypertension (bool): Whether the patient has hypertension
    cancer (bool): Whether the patient has cancer
    fever (bool): Whether the patient has fever on admission
    dyspnea (bool): Whether the patient has dyspnea on admission

    Returns:
    int: The risk prediction score
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 45:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'male':
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if cancer:
        score += 1
    if fever:
        score += 1
    if dyspnea:
        score += 1
    return score
33390431.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of a patient with COVID-19 developing a critical infection.
33390431.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with COVID-19 are eligible for this risk prediction score.
33390431.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 2: No critical events noted
- Score ≥ 4: More likely to have critical COVID-19 infection
33390431.utility: The clinical utility of the risk score is not provided in the article.

33394305.title: Osteoporosis Risk Model in Patients with Rheumatoid Arthritis
33394305.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, course_of_disease, DAS28, CCP, bone_erosion):
    """
    Compute the osteoporosis risk score for a patient with rheumatoid arthritis.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The patient's age in years.
    course_of_disease (int): The course of the disease in years.
    DAS28 (float): The Disease Activity Score using 28 joint counts.
    CCP (float): The anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide antibody level.
    bone_erosion (float): The 7-joint ultrasonic bone erosion score.

    Returns:
    float: The computed osteoporosis risk score.
    """
    return -12.647 + 0.133*age + 0.011*course_of_disease + 0.754*DAS28 + 0.001*CCP + 0.605*bone_erosion
33394305.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of osteoporosis in patients with rheumatoid arthritis.
33394305.eligibility: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis who have undergone bone mineral density measurement are eligible.
33394305.interpretation:
- If the computed risk score is greater than 0.483, the patient is likely to have osteoporosis. The sensitivity and specificity of this cutoff value are 90.24% and 87.76%, respectively.
- If the computed risk score is less than or equal to 0.483, the patient is unlikely to have osteoporosis.
33394305.utility: The model has good differentiation with a C-index of 0.947 in the internal verification and 0.946 in the external verification. The calibration plot of the model showed excellent consistency between the prediction probability and actual probability.

33399326.title: Risk Score (RS) and Aspartate Aminotransferase-to-Platelet Ratio Index (APRi) Calculator for High-Risk Varices in Pediatric Cirrhotic Patients
33399326.code:
def calculate_risk_score(RS, APRi):
    """
    Calculate the risk of high-risk varices (HRV) in pediatric patients with cirrhosis.

    Args:
    RS (float): Risk Score as described by Park et al.
    APRi (float): Aspartate Aminotransferase-to-Platelet Ratio Index.

    Returns:
    float: The risk of HRV.
    """
    if RS > -1.2 or APRi > 1.4:
        return 'High risk of HRV'
    else:
        return 'Low risk of HRV'
33399326.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the presence of high-risk varices (HRV) in pediatric patients with cirrhosis, thus helping to prevent unnecessary endoscopy.
33399326.eligibility: Pediatric patients with cirrhosis who have not had a history of upper gastrointestinal bleeding are eligible.
33399326.interpretation:
- If RS > -1.2 or APRi > 1.4, the patient is at high risk of HRV.
- If RS <= -1.2 and APRi <= 1.4, the patient is at low risk of HRV.
33399326.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) for the RS was 0.764, indicating a good predictive ability. The sensitivity of the RS was 90% and specificity was 53% when using a cut-off point of -1.2. The use of RS or APRi correctly identified 96% of children with HRV.

33421279.title: STEMI Shock Index (STEMI SI)
33421279.code:
def compute_stemi_si(age, hr, cpp):
    """
    Compute the STEMI Shock Index (STEMI SI).

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    hr (int): Heart rate of the patient in beats per minute.
    cpp (float): Coronary perfusion pressure of the patient in mmHg.

    Returns:
    float: The computed STEMI SI.
    """
    return age * hr / cpp
33421279.purpose: This calculator is used to predict short-term mortality in patients with ST-elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI).
33421279.eligibility: Patients who have undergone primary percutaneous coronary intervention for STEMI are eligible.
33421279.interpretation:
- STEMI SI < 182: Lower risk of in-hospital death, cardiac arrest in the catheterization laboratory, need for mechanical circulatory support, and treatment with vasopressors.
- STEMI SI ≥ 182: Higher risk of in-hospital death, cardiac arrest in the catheterization laboratory, need for mechanical circulatory support, and treatment with vasopressors. These patients are 10 times more likely to die during hospitalization, 10.1 times more likely to die at 30 days, and 4.8 times more likely to die at 5 years.
33421279.utility: The C statistic of STEMI SI was 0.870, similar to GRACE score (AUC = 0.902; p = .29) and TIMI STEMI score (AUC = 0.895; p = .36).

33425951.title: High-Flow Nasal Cannula (HFNC) Failure Risk Calculator for COVID-19 Patients
33425951.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, rox, platelets, il6):
    """
    Compute the risk score for HFNC failure in COVID-19 patients.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Patient's age in years.
    rox (float): Respiratory rate-oxygenation index within the first 4 hours of HFNC initiation.
    platelets (float): Platelet count in 10^9/L.
    il6 (float): Interleukin 6 level in pg/mL at HFNC initiation.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 60:
        score += 2
    if rox < 5.31:
        score += 5
    if platelets < 125:
        score += 3
    if il6 > 7.0:
        score += 3
    return score
33425951.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of HFNC therapy failure in COVID-19 patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory failure.
33425951.eligibility: COVID-19 patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory failure who are treated with HFNC therapy are eligible.
33425951.interpretation:
- Score <= 6: Low risk of HFNC failure, with a success probability of 84.2%
- Score > 6: High risk of HFNC failure, with a success probability of 34.8%
33425951.utility: The risk-stratification model showed a sensitivity of 80.3%, specificity of 71.2%, and a better predictive ability than the ROX index alone (AUC = 0.807 vs. 0.779, p < 0.001).

33427313.title: Antibodies Contributing to Focal Epilepsy Signs and Symptoms (ACES) Score Calculator
33427313.code:
def compute_ACES_score(temporal_MRI_hyperintensities, autoimmune_diseases, behavioral_changes, autonomic_symptoms, cognitive_symptoms, speech_problems):
    """
    Compute the ACES score based on the presence of six factors.

    Parameters:
    temporal_MRI_hyperintensities (bool): True if present, False otherwise.
    autoimmune_diseases (bool): True if present, False otherwise.
    behavioral_changes (bool): True if present, False otherwise.
    autonomic_symptoms (bool): True if present, False otherwise.
    cognitive_symptoms (bool): True if present, False otherwise.
    speech_problems (bool): True if present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The ACES score.
    """
    return int(temporal_MRI_hyperintensities) + int(autoimmune_diseases) + int(behavioral_changes) + int(autonomic_symptoms) + int(cognitive_symptoms) + int(speech_problems)
33427313.purpose: This calculator is used to preselect patients with focal epilepsy of unknown etiology who require antibody testing for autoimmune etiology of seizures (AES).
33427313.eligibility: Adult patients with focal epilepsy of unknown etiology, without recognized autoimmune encephalitis (AIE), are eligible.
33427313.interpretation:
- ACES score < 2: Low risk of AES, antibody testing may not be necessary.
- ACES score ≥ 2: High risk of AES, antibody testing is recommended.
33427313.utility: The internally validated Concordance (C) statistic was 0.95, and 0.92 in the validation cohort. The ACES score ≥ 2 had a sensitivity of 100% to detect AES, and a specificity of 84.9%.

33437790.title: ALBI-APRI Postoperative Morbidity Calculator
33437790.code:
def calculate_risk(ALBI, APRI):
    """
    Calculate the risk of postoperative morbidity based on ALBI and APRI scores.

    Parameters:
    ALBI (float): Albumin-Bilirubin score of the patient.
    APRI (float): Aspartate transaminase-to-platelet ratio index of the patient.

    Returns:
    string: Risk level of postoperative morbidity.
    """
    if ALBI > -2.6 and APRI > 1.5:
        return "High risk"
    else:
        return "Low risk"
33437790.purpose: This calculator is used to predict postoperative morbidity following hepatectomy for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) based on preoperative albumin-bilirubin (ALBI) and aspartate transaminase-to-platelet ratio index (APRI) scores.
33437790.eligibility: Patients undergoing hepatectomy for hepatocellular carcinoma are eligible.
33437790.interpretation:
- High risk: Patients with ALBI > -2.6 and APRI > 1.5 are at high risk of postoperative morbidity.
- Low risk: Patients with ALBI <= -2.6 or APRI <= 1.5 are at low risk of postoperative morbidity.
33437790.utility: The AUCs of the preoperative and postoperative models were 0.728 and 0.742 to predict overall morbidity, and 0.739 and 0.713 to predict major morbidity, respectively.

33441105.title: Mycoplasma Pneumoniae Pneumonia (MPP) Mucus Plug Risk Calculator
33441105.code:
def compute_risk_score(PA, corticosteroid_days, CRP, LDH, mucus_formation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for MPP mucus plug formation.

    Parameters:
    PA (float): Prealbumin levels in mg/L. Risk factor if PA ≤ 144.5.
    corticosteroid_days (int): Number of days corticosteroids were used during the illness. Risk factor if ≥ 4.5 days.
    CRP (float): C-reactive protein levels in mg/L. Risk factor if CRP ≥ 12.27.
    LDH (float): Lactate dehydrogenase levels in U/L. Risk factor if LDH ≥ 462.65.
    mucus_formation (bool): Presence of intra-airway mucus formation.

    Returns:
    risk_score (int): The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    if PA <= 144.5:
        risk_score += 1
    if corticosteroid_days >= 4.5:
        risk_score += 1
    if CRP >= 12.27:
        risk_score += 1
    if LDH >= 462.65:
        risk_score += 1
    if mucus_formation:
        risk_score += 1

    return risk_score
33441105.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of mucus plug formation in children with Mycoplasma pneumoniae pneumonia (MPP).
33441105.eligibility: Children diagnosed with Mycoplasma pneumoniae pneumonia (MPP) are eligible.
33441105.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk group, 11% chance of mucus plug formation.
- Score 2-3: Middle risk group, 34.3% chance of mucus plug formation.
- Score 4-5: High risk group, 83.02% chance of mucus plug formation.
33441105.utility: The utility of this risk score is evaluated by the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve analysis. The exact AUC, F-score, PPV are not provided in the article.

33444080.title: Cancer and Aging Research Group-Breast Cancer (CARG-BC) Score
33444080.code:
def calculate_CARG_BC_score(anthracycline_use, stage, treatment_duration, liver_function, hemoglobin, falls, walking, social_support):
    """
    Calculate the CARG-BC score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    anthracycline_use (bool): Whether the patient is using anthracycline.
    stage (int): The stage of the breast cancer (1, 2, or 3).
    treatment_duration (int): The planned treatment duration in months.
    liver_function (bool): Whether the patient has abnormal liver function.
    hemoglobin (bool): Whether the patient has low hemoglobin.
    falls (bool): Whether the patient has experienced falls.
    walking (bool): Whether the patient has limited walking ability.
    social_support (bool): Whether the patient lacks social support.

    Returns:
    int: The CARG-BC score.
    """
    score = 0
    if anthracycline_use:
        score += 1
    if stage in [2, 3]:
        score += 3
    if treatment_duration > 3:
        score += 4
    if liver_function:
        score += 3
    if hemoglobin:
        score += 3
    if falls:
        score += 4
    if walking:
        score += 3
    if social_support:
        score += 3
    return score
33444080.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of grade 3-5 chemotherapy toxicity in older adults with early-stage breast cancer.
33444080.eligibility: Patients aged 65 years or older with stage I-III breast cancer who are treated with neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy.
33444080.interpretation:
- 0-5 points: Low risk group, with a 19-27% rate of grade 3-5 chemotherapy toxicity.
- 6-11 points: Intermediate risk group, with a 45-54% rate of grade 3-5 chemotherapy toxicity.
- 12 or more points: High risk group, with a 76-87% rate of grade 3-5 chemotherapy toxicity.
33444080.utility: The AUC was 0.75 (95% CI, 0.70 to 0.81) in the development cohort and 0.69 (95% CI, 0.62 to 0.77) in the validation cohort. Risk groups were also associated with hospitalizations and reduced dose intensity (P < .01).

33447407.title: CT-Based Anastomotic Leakage Risk Calculator
33447407.code:
def compute_risk_score(extraluminal_contrast, air_collection, fluid_collection, pneumothorax, loculated_effusion):
    """
    Compute the risk score for anastomotic leakage following Ivor Lewis esophagectomy.

    Args:
    extraluminal_contrast (bool): Presence of extraluminal oral contrast in CT scan.
    air_collection (bool): Presence of air collection at the anastomotic site in CT scan.
    fluid_collection (bool): Presence of fluid collection at the anastomotic site in CT scan.
    pneumothorax (bool): Presence of pneumothorax in CT scan.
    loculated_effusion (bool): Presence of loculated pleural effusion in CT scan.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    return sum([extraluminal_contrast, air_collection, fluid_collection, pneumothorax, loculated_effusion])
33447407.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of anastomotic leakage following Ivor Lewis esophagectomy using CT scan findings.
33447407.eligibility: Patients who have undergone Ivor Lewis esophagectomy and have had a CT scan due to clinical suspicion of postoperative anastomotic leakage.
33447407.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk for anastomotic leakage (negative predictive value: 84.4%)
- Score ≥ 3: High risk for anastomotic leakage (positive predictive value: 83.3%)
33447407.utility: The scoring system showed a superior diagnostic performance compared to the original CT report and blinded interpretation of two radiologists.

33454543.title: Low-Grade Glioma Prognostic Calculator Based on Immune-Related lncRNA
33454543.code:
def compute_risk_score(expression_levels, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression levels of lncRNAs and their coefficients.

    Parameters:
    expression_levels (dict): A dictionary where the key is the lncRNA name and the value is its expression level.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the key is the lncRNA name and the value is its coefficient.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    for lncRNA in expression_levels:
        risk_score += expression_levels[lncRNA] * coefficients[lncRNA]
    return risk_score
33454543.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with low-grade glioma based on the expression of nine immune-related long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs).
33454543.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with low-grade glioma are eligible for this calculator.
33454543.interpretation:
- High risk: A higher risk score indicates a higher risk of poor prognosis, with a 10-year survival rate of 22.68%.
- Low risk: A lower risk score indicates a lower risk of poor prognosis, with a 10-year survival rate of 54%.
33454543.utility: The hazard ratio (HR) of the risk score from univariate Cox regression analysis was 1.081 (95% CI: 1.060-1.102, p < 0.001), indicating that the risk score is a significant prognostic factor for patients with low-grade glioma.

33465514.title: PREVENTION-ACHD Risk Score Model
33465514.code:
def calculate_risk_score(coronary_artery_disease, heart_failure, supraventricular_tachycardia, systemic_ejection_fraction, subpulmonary_ejection_fraction, QRS_duration, QT_dispersion):
    """
    Calculate the PREVENTION-ACHD risk score based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    coronary_artery_disease (bool): True if the patient has coronary artery disease, False otherwise.
    heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has New York Heart Association class II/III heart failure, False otherwise.
    supraventricular_tachycardia (bool): True if the patient has supraventricular tachycardia, False otherwise.
    systemic_ejection_fraction (float): The patient's systemic ejection fraction.
    subpulmonary_ejection_fraction (float): The patient's subpulmonary ejection fraction.
    QRS_duration (float): The patient's QRS duration.
    QT_dispersion (float): The patient's QT dispersion.
    
    Returns:
    int: The calculated risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if coronary_artery_disease:
        score += 1
    if heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if supraventricular_tachycardia:
        score += 1
    if systemic_ejection_fraction < 40:
        score += 1
    if subpulmonary_ejection_fraction < 40:
        score += 1
    if QRS_duration >= 120:
        score += 1
    if QT_dispersion >= 70:
        score += 1
    return score
33465514.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of Sudden Cardiac Death (SCD) or sustained Ventricular Tachycardia/Ventricular Fibrillation (VT/VF) in patients with Adult Congenital Heart Disease (ACHD).
33465514.eligibility: Patients with Adult Congenital Heart Disease (ACHD) are eligible for this risk score model.
33465514.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk of SCD or VT/VF. The Kaplan-Meier estimates were 0.6% (95% CI 0.0%-1.1%) in the low-risk group.
- Score ≥ 3: High risk of SCD or VT/VF. The Kaplan-Meier estimates were 7% (95% CI 0.1%-13.3%) in the high-risk group.
33465514.utility: The risk score model's sensitivity was 0.5 and specificity 0.93, resulting in a C-statistic of 0.75 (95% CI 0.57-0.90). The hazard ratio for SCD was 12.4 (95% CI 1.8-88.1) (P = .01); the sensitivity and specificity were 0.5 and 0.92, and the C-statistic was 0.81 (95% CI 0.67-0.95).

33469933.title: Electronic Frailty Index (eFI) Calculator
33469933.code:
def calculate_efi(deficits):
    """
    Calculate the Electronic Frailty Index (eFI).
    
    Args:
    deficits (list): A list of 54 health deficits, each represented as a boolean (True if present, False if not).
    
    Returns:
    float: The eFI score, ranging from 0 to 1.
    """
    return sum(deficits) / len(deficits)
33469933.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the frailty of patients aged 65 years or older undergoing nonemergency surgery, to predict post-operative outcomes such as inpatient length of stay, requirements for post-acute care, 30-day readmission, and 6-month all-cause mortality.
33469933.eligibility: Patients who are 65 years or older and are undergoing nonemergency surgery with an inpatient stay of 24 hours or more.
33469933.interpretation:
- eFI ≤ 0.10: Fit, lower risk of post-operative adverse outcomes.
- 0.10 < eFI ≤ 0.21: Pre-frail, moderate risk of post-operative adverse outcomes.
- eFI > 0.21: Frail, high risk of post-operative adverse outcomes.
33469933.utility: Patients identified as frail based on the eFI had greater needs for post-acute care (odds ratio (OR) = 1.68; 95% confidence interval (CI) = 1.36-2.08), higher rates of 30-day readmission (hazard ratio (HR) = 2.46; 95%CI = 1.72-3.52) and higher all-cause mortality (HR = 2.86; 95%CI = 1.84-4.44) over 6 months' follow-up.

33479052.title: M10LS20 Risk Calculator for Advanced Chronic Liver Disease (ACLD)
33479052.code:
def calculate_risk(L_SWE, MELD):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on L-SWE and MELD score.

    Parameters:
    L_SWE (float): Liver Shear Wave Elastography result in kPa.
    MELD (int): Model for End Stage Liver Disease score.

    Returns:
    int: Risk score (0 for low risk, 1 for high risk).
    """
    if L_SWE >= 20 and MELD >= 10:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
33479052.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of mortality and first/further decompensation in patients with advanced chronic liver disease (ACLD).
33479052.eligibility: Patients with advanced chronic liver disease (ACLD) who have valid two-dimensional shear wave elastography (2D-SWE) results and a model for end stage liver disease (MELD) score.
33479052.interpretation:
- 0: Low risk. The 2-year mortality and decompensation rates are 1.1% and 3.5%, respectively.
- 1: High risk. The 2-year mortality and decompensation rates are 36.9% and 61.8%, respectively.
33479052.utility: The M10LS20 algorithm has an AUC of 0.8, indicating a good predictive ability for mortality in ACLD patients.

33480111.title: Glasgow Prognostic Score (GPS) for Predicting Chemotherapy-Triggered Acute Exacerbation-Interstitial Lung Disease in NSCLC Patients
33480111.code:
def compute_gps(c_reactive_protein, albumin):
    """
    Compute Glasgow Prognostic Score (GPS)

    Parameters:
    c_reactive_protein (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/L
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/L

    Returns:
    int: Glasgow Prognostic Score (0-2)
    """
    if c_reactive_protein <= 10 and albumin >= 35:
        return 0
    elif c_reactive_protein > 10 or albumin < 35:
        return 1
    elif c_reactive_protein > 10 and albumin < 35:
        return 2
33480111.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of chemotherapy-triggered acute exacerbation-interstitial lung disease (AE-ILD) and overall survival (OS) in patients with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and pre-existing ILD.
33480111.eligibility: Patients with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and pre-existing interstitial lung disease (ILD) who are receiving chemotherapy.
33480111.interpretation:
- GPS 0: Low risk of chemotherapy-triggered AE-ILD (9.6% incidence within the first year of chemotherapy) and longer median overall survival time (16.9 months).
- GPS 1: Moderate risk of chemotherapy-triggered AE-ILD (25.0% incidence within the first year of chemotherapy) and shorter median overall survival time (9.8 months).
- GPS 2: High risk of chemotherapy-triggered AE-ILD (55.5% incidence within the first year of chemotherapy) and shortest median overall survival time (7.6 months).
33480111.utility: The GPS was found to be a significant predictor for chemotherapy-triggered AE-ILD and overall survival in patients with NSCLC and pre-existing ILD (P < 0.05).

33498444.title: hPG80 Prognostic Calculator for Metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma (mRCC)
33498444.code:
def compute_hpg80_score(hpg80_level):
    """
    Compute the hPG80 score based on the plasma hPG80 level.

    Args:
    hpg80_level (float): The plasma hPG80 level in pM.

    Returns:
    int: The hPG80 score (1 if hpg80_level > 4.5, 0 otherwise).
    """
    if hpg80_level > 4.5:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
33498444.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival (OS) in patients with metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma (mRCC) based on plasma hPG80 levels.
33498444.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma (mRCC).
33498444.interpretation:
- Score 0: Patients with hPG80 levels <= 4.5 pM have a higher overall survival (OS) rate (31.2 months).
- Score 1: Patients with hPG80 levels > 4.5 pM have a lower overall survival (OS) rate (12 months).
33498444.utility: The hPG80 score showed high predictive accuracy with an AUC of 0.93 and 0.84 when compared to 18-25 year old controls and 50-80 year old controls, respectively.

33501908.title: STOP-BANG Risk Assessment Tool
33501908.code:
def compute_stop_bang_score(snoring, tired, observed, pressure, bmi, age, neck_circumference, gender):
    """
    Compute the STOP-BANG score.

    Parameters:
    snoring (bool): True if the patient snores loudly, False otherwise.
    tired (bool): True if the patient often feels tired, fatigued, or sleepy during the daytime, False otherwise.
    observed (bool): True if anyone has observed the patient stop breathing or choking/gasping during sleep, False otherwise.
    pressure (bool): True if the patient has or is being treated for high blood pressure, False otherwise.
    bmi (bool): True if the patient's Body Mass Index is more than 35 kg/m2, False otherwise.
    age (bool): True if the patient is older than 50 years old, False otherwise.
    neck_circumference (bool): True if the patient's neck circumference is greater than 40 cm, False otherwise.
    gender (bool): True if the patient is a male, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The STOP-BANG score, ranging from 0 to 8.
    """
    return snoring + tired + observed + pressure + bmi + age + neck_circumference + gender
33501908.purpose: This calculator is used to identify patients at high risk of Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA) and evaluate their risk of intraprocedure airway maneuvers and adverse events in a Gastrointestinal (GI) laboratory.
33501908.eligibility: Patients presenting for elective procedures in a GI lab are eligible.
33501908.interpretation:
- Score < 5: Low risk of OSA and lower occurrence of intraprocedure airway maneuvers and adverse events.
- Score >= 5: High risk of OSA with an increased need for airway maneuvers and higher occurrence of adverse events.
33501908.utility: Patients with high STOP-BANG scores had an increased need for airway maneuvers and higher occurrence of adverse events (P=.05). This supports the use of STOP-BANG as a pre-procedure risk assessment tool.

33503489.title: The SURFASA Score Calculator
33503489.code:
def compute_surfasa_score(D0_INR, D3_INR, D0_bilirubin, D3_bilirubin):
    """
    Compute the SURFASA score for a patient with acute severe autoimmune hepatitis.

    Parameters:
    D0_INR (float): INR at the initiation of corticosteroids.
    D3_INR (float): INR at day 3 after the initiation of corticosteroids.
    D0_bilirubin (float): Bilirubin at the initiation of corticosteroids (in μmol/L).
    D3_bilirubin (float): Bilirubin at day 3 after the initiation of corticosteroids (in μmol/L).

    Returns:
    float: The SURFASA score.
    """
    delta_percent_3_INR = (D3_INR - D0_INR) / D0_INR
    delta_percent_3_bilirubin = (D3_bilirubin - D0_bilirubin) / D0_bilirubin
    return -6.80 + 1.92 * D0_INR + 1.94 * delta_percent_3_INR + 1.64 * delta_percent_3_bilirubin
33503489.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the need for liver transplantation in patients with acute severe autoimmune hepatitis (AS-AIH) who are non-responsive to corticosteroids.
33503489.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with AS-AIH based on the simplified IAIHG score, with an international normalized ratio (INR) ≥1.5 and/or bilirubin >200 μmol/L, no previous history of AIH, and histologically proven AIH are eligible.
33503489.interpretation:
- If the SURFASA score is less than -0.9, the chance of responding to corticosteroids is 75%.
- If the SURFASA score is higher than 1.75, the risk of dying or needing a liver transplant is between 85% and 100%.
33503489.utility: The SURFASA score has an AUC of 0.93, indicating a high predictive accuracy. It has a specificity of 88% and a sensitivity of 84%.

33509119.title: ED-SAS Score for Acute Pancreatitis Mortality Risk
33509119.code:
def compute_ED_SAS(sirs, age, spo2):
    """
    Compute the ED-SAS score for acute pancreatitis mortality risk.

    Parameters:
    sirs (bool): True if the patient has ≥2 SIRS criteria, False otherwise.
    age (bool): True if the patient's age is > 60 years, False otherwise.
    spo2 (bool): True if the patient's SpO2 is < 96%, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The ED-SAS score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(sirs) + int(age) + int(spo2)
33509119.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality risk in patients with acute pancreatitis presenting to the emergency department.
33509119.eligibility: Adult patients who present to the emergency department, require hospital admission, and have a confirmed diagnosis of acute pancreatitis are eligible. Patients with chronic or recurrent episodes of pancreatitis are excluded.
33509119.interpretation:
- Score 0: No increased risk of 30-day mortality.
- Score 1: 4.4 times increased odds of 30-day mortality (95% CI 1.8-10.8).
- Score 2: 12.0 times increased odds of 30-day mortality (95% CI 4.9-29.4).
- Score 3: 41.7 times increased odds of 30-day mortality (95% CI 15.8-110.1).
33509119.utility: The ED-SAS score demonstrated good predictive ability with a c-statistic of 0.77.

33515095.title: COVID-19 Pneumonia Severity Prediction Calculator
33515095.code:
def calculate_risk(hbp, il6):
    """
    Calculate the risk of severe respiratory failure and 28-day mortality.

    Parameters:
    hbp (float): Heparin-Binding Protein level at admission (ng/ml).
    il6 (float): Interleukin-6 level at admission (pg/ml).

    Returns:
    tuple: Risk of SRF and 28-day mortality (boolean values).
    """
    srf_risk = hbp > 18 and il6 > 30
    mortality_risk = hbp > 35 and il6 > 30

    return srf_risk, mortality_risk
33515095.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the severity of respiratory failure and 28-day mortality in patients with pneumonia caused by SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) based on admission levels of Heparin-Binding Protein (HBP) and Interleukin-6 (IL-6).
33515095.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with pneumonia caused by SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) are eligible.
33515095.interpretation:
- If both HBP > 18 ng/ml and IL-6 > 30 pg/ml, the patient is at risk of developing severe respiratory failure.
- If both HBP > 35 ng/ml and IL-6 > 30 pg/ml, the patient is at risk of 28-day mortality.
33515095.utility: The integration of HBP and IL-6 prognosticated SRF with a sensitivity of 59.1%, specificity of 96.3%, positive predictive value of 83.9%, and negative predictive value of 87.8%. For 28-day mortality, the sensitivity was 69.2%, specificity 92.7%, positive predictive value 42.9%, and negative predictive value 97.5%.

33530248.title: Lactic Dehydrogenase-Lymphocyte Ratio (LLR) Calculator for Severe COVID-19 Prognosis
33530248.code:
def compute_LLR(lactic_dehydrogenase, lymphocyte_count):
    """
    Compute the Lactic Dehydrogenase-Lymphocyte Ratio (LLR) score.

    Parameters:
    lactic_dehydrogenase (float): Patient's lactic dehydrogenase level.
    lymphocyte_count (float): Patient's lymphocyte count.

    Returns:
    float: LLR score.
    """
    return lactic_dehydrogenase / lymphocyte_count
33530248.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with severe COVID-19.
33530248.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with severe COVID-19 are eligible.
33530248.interpretation:
- LLR <= 345: Lower risk of poor prognosis.
- LLR > 345: Higher risk of poor prognosis, associated with liver injury, renal injury, and higher disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) score on admission.
33530248.utility: The LLR score has been shown to be an accurate predictive score for poor prognosis of severe COVID-19 patients, with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.857.

33542159.title: CHIP Score for Predicting One-Year Mortality in Patients Undergoing Complex High-Risk Indicated Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (CHIP-PCI)
33542159.code:
def compute_CHIP_score(age, dialysis, ejection_fraction, num_lesions):
    """
    Compute the CHIP score based on the patient's age, dialysis status, ejection fraction, and number of lesions treated.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The patient's age.
    dialysis (bool): Whether the patient is on dialysis.
    ejection_fraction (float): The patient's ejection fraction.
    num_lesions (int): The number of lesions treated.

    Returns:
    int: The computed CHIP score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 80:
        score += 3
    if dialysis:
        score += 6
    if ejection_fraction < 30:
        score += 2
    if num_lesions > 2:
        score += 2
    return score
33542159.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the one-year mortality risk in patients undergoing complex high-risk indicated percutaneous coronary intervention (CHIP-PCI).
33542159.eligibility: Patients undergoing complex high-risk indicated percutaneous coronary intervention (CHIP-PCI) are eligible.
33542159.interpretation:
- Score of 0: Low-risk, with a 1-year mortality rate of 1.24%
- Score of 2-3: Intermediate-risk, with a 1-year mortality rate of 2.47%
- Score of 4-13: High-risk, with a 1-year mortality rate of 10.86%
33542159.utility: The CHIP score was found to be an independent predictor of one-year mortality in patients undergoing CHIP-PCI, with a significant difference in mortality rates among low, intermediate, and high-risk groups (P<.001).

33548341.title: SRS Prognostic Score for Grade 2 Meningiomas
33548341.code:
def compute_srs_score(age, treatment_volume, prior_treatment_history):
    """
    Compute the SRS Prognostic Score for a patient with grade 2 meningioma.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient in years.
    treatment_volume (float): The volume of the tumor treated with SRS in cm3.
    prior_treatment_history (bool): Whether the patient has undergone prior radiation therapy or multiple surgeries.

    Returns:
    int: The SRS Prognostic Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 50:
        score += 1
    if treatment_volume > 11.5:
        score += 1
    if prior_treatment_history:
        score += 1
    return score
33548341.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the progression-free survival (PFS) of patients with World Health Organization grade 2 meningiomas treated with stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS).
33548341.eligibility: Patients with World Health Organization grade 2 meningiomas who are being considered for or have undergone stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS).
33548341.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Good-prognostic group with improved progression-free survival (PFS) and time to local failure.
- Score 2-3: Poor-prognostic group with reduced PFS and time to local failure.
33548341.utility: The SRS Prognostic Score was found to be significantly associated with PFS and time to local failure in a study of 233 patients with grade 2 meningiomas treated with SRS.

33550903.title: 10-Gene Signature Prognostic Calculator for Low Grade Glioma
33550903.code:
def compute_risk_score(expression_levels, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression levels of 10 genes and their corresponding coefficients.

    Args:
    expression_levels (list): A list of 10 gene expression levels.
    coefficients (list): A list of 10 coefficients corresponding to the genes.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = sum([expression_levels[i] * coefficients[i] for i in range(10)])
    return risk_score
33550903.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with Low Grade Glioma (LGG) based on the expression levels of 10 specific genes.
33550903.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Low Grade Glioma are eligible for this calculator.
33550903.interpretation:
The interpretation of the risk score is as follows:
- Low risk: Risk score less than the median risk score. These patients have a better prognosis.
- High risk: Risk score greater than or equal to the median risk score. These patients have a poorer prognosis and are more likely to have relapse.
33550903.utility: The risk score was found to be predictive of poor overall survival and relapse-free survival in LGG patients. However, the exact clinical utility metrics such as AUC, F-score, PPV are not provided in the article.

33572844.title: DEI Score for Prediction of Early Readmission after a First Episode of Acute Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction
33572844.code:
import math

def compute_dei_score(LVEDD, E_e_ratio, LVOT_VTI):
    """
    Compute the DEI Score for predicting heart failure readmission.

    Parameters:
    LVEDD (float): Left ventricle end-diastolic diameter in mm.
    E_e_ratio (float): E/e' ratio.
    LVOT_VTI (float): Left ventricle outflow tract velocity-time integral in cm.

    Returns:
    float: The computed DEI Score.
    """
    dei_score = - 28.763 + 4.558 * math.log(LVEDD) + 1.961 * math.log(E_e_ratio) + 1.759 * math.log(LVOT_VTI)
    return dei_score
33572844.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of heart failure readmission (HFR) following an initial acute hospitalization for heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF).
33572844.eligibility: Patients who have been admitted for the first time due to an acute episode of HFpEF are eligible.
33572844.interpretation: - DEI Score > -0.747: The model predicts a relative amount of 20.50% of HFR during the first 6 months after the first acute hospitalization within the general population with HFpEF.
33572844.utility: The prognostic DEI score demonstrated good accuracy in predicting HFR following an initial acute HFpEF hospitalization.

33582195.title: Cardiac Tumor Risk Score Calculator
33582195.code:
def compute_risk_score(invasiveness, irregular_border, heterogenous_lge):
    """
    Compute the risk score for cardiac tumors based on CMR parameters.

    Parameters:
    invasiveness (bool): True if the tumor is invasive, False otherwise.
    irregular_border (bool): True if the tumor has an irregular border, False otherwise.
    heterogenous_lge (bool): True if the tumor has heterogenous LGE, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if invasiveness:
        score += 2
    if irregular_border:
        score += 1
    if heterogenous_lge:
        score += 2
    return score
33582195.purpose: This calculator is used to differentiate between benign and malignant cardiac tumors and predict patient outcomes based on cardiovascular magnetic resonance (CMR) parameters.
33582195.eligibility: Patients referred for CMR for suspected cardiac tumors are eligible.
33582195.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 4: The tumor is likely benign.
- Score = 5: The tumor is likely malignant and the patient has a significantly higher risk of mortality.
33582195.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) of the scoring algorithm was 0.912, indicating a high ability to differentiate between benign and malignant tumors. The scoring algorithm also showed significantly higher AUCs than individual variables (all P < 0.05).

33586122.title: PILE Score Calculator for Cancer Patients Treated with Immunotherapy
33586122.code:
def calculate_pile_score(piv, ldh, ecog_ps, piv_median, ldh_normal):
    """
    Calculate PILE score.

    Parameters:
    piv (float): Patient's Pan-Immune-Inflammation Value.
    ldh (float): Patient's lactate dehydrogenase levels.
    ecog_ps (int): Patient's Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status.
    piv_median (float): Median PIV in the patient population.
    ldh_normal (float): Normal LDH level.

    Returns:
    int: PILE score.
    """
    piv_score = 1 if piv >= piv_median else 0
    ldh_score = 1 if ldh > ldh_normal else 0
    ecog_ps_score = 1 if ecog_ps >= 1 else 0

    return piv_score + ldh_score + ecog_ps_score
33586122.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival and progression-free survival in cancer patients treated with immunotherapy.
33586122.eligibility: Patients with advanced cancer of any type who are being treated with anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 inhibitors.
33586122.interpretation:
- PILE score 0-1: Low-risk group. These patients have longer overall survival and progression-free survival.
- PILE score 2-3: High-risk group. These patients have shorter overall survival and progression-free survival.
33586122.utility: The Harrell C-Index values were 0.65 for overall survival prediction and 0.61 for progression-free survival prediction.

33598084.title: Hepatic Steatosis Index (HSI) Calculator
33598084.code:
def compute_hsi(alt, ast, bmi, is_diabetic, is_female):
    """
    Compute the Hepatic Steatosis Index (HSI).

    Parameters:
    alt (float): Alanine aminotransferase level.
    ast (float): Aspartate aminotransferase level.
    bmi (float): Body Mass Index.
    is_diabetic (bool): Whether the patient has type 2 diabetes.
    is_female (bool): Whether the patient is female.

    Returns:
    float: The computed HSI.
    """
    hsi = 8 * (alt / ast) + bmi
    if is_diabetic:
        hsi += 2
    if is_female:
        hsi += 2
    return hsi
33598084.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the presence of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) in patients with type 2 diabetes.
33598084.eligibility: Patients with type 2 diabetes are eligible for this calculator.
33598084.interpretation: - HSI score > 36: Predicts the presence of fatty liver.
33598084.utility: The sensitivity of HSI was 89.55%, negative predictive value (NPV) was 90.91%, specificity was 95.24%, and positive predictive value (PPV) was 94.49%. The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) for HSI was at 0.979 (95% CI, 0.962-0.997).

33601427.title: COVID-19 Pneumonia Lung Ultrasound (LUS) Risk Score Calculator
33601427.code:
def compute_lus_score(lus_findings):
    """
    Compute the LUS score based on the findings in each of the 12 areas of the chest.

    Parameters:
    lus_findings (list of int): A list of 12 integers representing the LUS findings in each area of the chest.

    Returns:
    int: The total LUS score.
    """
    return sum(lus_findings)
33601427.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with COVID-19 pneumonia based on Lung Ultrasound (LUS) findings.
33601427.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with COVID-19 pneumonia and have undergone a Lung Ultrasound (LUS) are eligible.
33601427.interpretation:
- LUS score > 20: High risk of endotracheal intubation, no active further management, or death.
- LUS score < 10: Low risk, safe for discharge from the emergency room.
33601427.utility: The LUS score demonstrated good predictive performance with an Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) Curve (AUC) of 0.837 for scores > 20 and an AUC of 0.967 for scores < 10.

33602220.title: Seven-IRGs Risk Signature Calculator for Male Gastric Cancer Patients
33602220.code:
def compute_risk_score(LCN12, CCL21, RNASE2, CGB5, NRG4, AGTR1, NPR3, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression levels of seven immune-related genes and their coefficients.
    
    Parameters:
    LCN12, CCL21, RNASE2, CGB5, NRG4, AGTR1, NPR3 (float): Expression levels of the seven genes.
    coefficients (list): Cox regression coefficients of the seven genes.
    
    Returns:
    risk_score (float): The computed risk score.
    """
    gene_expressions = [LCN12, CCL21, RNASE2, CGB5, NRG4, AGTR1, NPR3]
    risk_score = sum([gene * coef for gene, coef in zip(gene_expressions, coefficients)])
    return risk_score
33602220.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival of male patients with gastric cancer based on a signature of seven immune-related genes.
33602220.eligibility: Male patients diagnosed with gastric cancer are eligible for this calculator.
33602220.interpretation:
- High-risk score: Patients with a high-risk score have a poor clinical outcome and lower overall survival.
- Low-risk score: Patients with a low-risk score have a better clinical outcome and higher overall survival.

The exact cut-off between high and low risk scores is determined by the median risk score of the population.
33602220.utility: The prognostic model has excellent predictive performance in both the TCGA and validated cohorts. The risk score was found to be an independent prognostic factor.

33602617.title: Liver Fibrosis Severity and NASH Risk Calculator
33602617.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the severity of liver fibrosis and the risk of non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH) in patients with type 2 diabetes.
33602617.eligibility: Patients with type 2 diabetes are eligible for this calculator.
33602617.interpretation:
- LSM Score:
  - <8.0 kPa: No significant fibrosis
  - 8.0 to <9.7 kPa: Significant fibrosis
  - 9.7 to <13.0 kPa: Advanced fibrosis
  - ≥13.0 kPa: Liver cirrhosis
- FAST Score:
  - <0.35: Low risk of NASH with fibrosis
  - 0.35 to <0.67: Intermediate risk of NASH with fibrosis
  - ≥0.67: High risk of NASH with fibrosis
33602617.utility: The study found that serum sDPP-4 was strongly associated with severity of liver fibrosis evaluated by LSM and the FAST score. It was markedly elevated in diabetic patients with LSM ≥13.0 kPa indicating probable cirrhosis. However, the clinical utility of the risk score such as AUC, F-score, PPV was not provided in the article.

33602645.title: Prognostic Model for Unresectable Undifferentiated Carcinoma of the Pancreas
33602645.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, ECOG_PS, CRP):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with unresectable undifferentiated carcinoma of the pancreas.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    ECOG_PS (int): The Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status of the patient.
    CRP (float): The C-reactive protein level of the patient in mg/L.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 65:
        score += 3
    if ECOG_PS >= 2:
        score += 6
    if CRP > 10:
        score += 2
    return score
33602645.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with unresectable undifferentiated carcinoma of the pancreas.
33602645.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with unresectable undifferentiated carcinoma of the pancreas are eligible.
33602645.interpretation:
- Score 0-3: Low-risk group, with a median overall survival of 8.19 months.
- Score ≥4: High-risk group, with a median overall survival of 1.45 months.
33602645.utility: The derived prognostic model stratified patients into high-risk and low-risk groups, with significant differences in overall survival (p < 0.001).

33607205.title: Japanese-BTK (J-BTK) CTO Score Calculator
33607205.code:
def compute_jbtk_score(no_outflow, cto_length, vessel_diameter, calcification, blunt_type):
    """
    Compute the J-BTK CTO score.

    Parameters:
    no_outflow (bool): True if there is no outflow of the target vessel, False otherwise.
    cto_length (float): The length of the CTO in mm.
    vessel_diameter (float): The diameter of the reference vessel in mm.
    calcification (bool): True if there is calcification at the proximal entry point, False otherwise.
    blunt_type (bool): True if there is a blunt type at the proximal entry point, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The J-BTK CTO score.
    """
    score = 0
    if no_outflow:
        score += 2
    if cto_length >= 200:
        score += 1
    if vessel_diameter < 2.0:
        score += 1
    if calcification:
        score += 1
    if blunt_type:
        score += 1
    return score
33607205.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the probability of a successful guidewire crossing (S-GC) of below-the-knee (BTK) chronic total occlusions (CTOs) in angiographic evaluation.
33607205.eligibility: Patients with below-the-knee chronic total occlusions (BTK CTOs) who are being considered for endovascular therapy.
33607205.interpretation:
- Score of 0 and 1 (Grade A): 97.3% chance of successful guidewire crossing
- Score of 2 and 3 (Grade B): 76.8% chance of successful guidewire crossing
- Score of 4 and 5 (Grade C): 19.3% chance of successful guidewire crossing
- Score of 6 (Grade D): 0% chance of successful guidewire crossing
33607205.utility: The J-BTK CTO score was validated using the Hosmer-Lemeshow test (P = .99), indicating a good fit of the model.

33613456.title: Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD) Prediction Rule
33613456.code:
def predict_GHD(pituitary_dysgenesis, anterior_pituitary_deficiencies, neonatal_hypoglycemia, hypogenitalism, diabetes_insipidus, midline_abnormalities, suprasellar_tumor_surgery, cranial_radiotherapy):
    """
    Function to predict Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD) in children.
    Parameters:
    pituitary_dysgenesis (bool): True if the patient has pituitary dysgenesis, False otherwise.
    anterior_pituitary_deficiencies (int): Number of anterior pituitary deficiencies.
    neonatal_hypoglycemia (bool): True if the patient has neonatal hypoglycemia, False otherwise.
    hypogenitalism (bool): True if the patient has hypogenitalism, False otherwise.
    diabetes_insipidus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes insipidus, False otherwise.
    midline_abnormalities (bool): True if the patient has midline abnormalities, False otherwise.
    suprasellar_tumor_surgery (bool): True if the patient has (supra)sellar tumor/surgery, False otherwise.
    cranial_radiotherapy (float): The dose of cranial radiotherapy in Gy. If no radiotherapy, enter 0.
    Returns:
    bool: True if the patient is predicted to have GHD, False otherwise.
    """
    if pituitary_dysgenesis:
        return True
    elif anterior_pituitary_deficiencies >= 2:
        return True
    elif anterior_pituitary_deficiencies == 1 and (neonatal_hypoglycemia or hypogenitalism or diabetes_insipidus or midline_abnormalities or suprasellar_tumor_surgery or cranial_radiotherapy >= 18):
        return True
    else:
        return False
33613456.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the existence of Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD) in children with growth failure and clinically identifiable risk factors, without the need for growth hormone stimulation tests (GHST).
33613456.eligibility: Children with growth failure and clinically identifiable risk factors such as pituitary dysgenesis, midline abnormalities, (supra)sellar tumor/surgery, CNS infection, traumatic brain injury, cranial radiotherapy, chemotherapy, genetic GHD, pituitary hormone deficiencies, and neonatal hypoglycemia, cholestasis, or hypogenitalism.
33613456.interpretation:
- True: The patient is predicted to have Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD).
- False: The patient is not predicted to have Growth Hormone Deficiency (GHD).
33613456.utility: The specificity of the prediction rule was 99.2% (95% CI: 95.6-100%).

33620435.title: PESI-Echo Score Calculator
33620435.code:
def compute_pesi_echo(pesi, pasp, tapse):
    """
    Compute the PESI-Echo score.

    Parameters:
    pesi (int): Pulmonary Embolism Severity Index score.
    pasp (float): Pulmonary Artery Systolic Pressure measured from echocardiogram.
    tapse (float): Tricuspid Annular Plane Systolic Excursion measured from echocardiogram.

    Returns:
    int: PESI-Echo score.
    """
    return pesi + pasp - tapse
33620435.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the mortality risk in patients with acute pulmonary embolism (PE) by combining the Pulmonary Embolism Severity Index (PESI) score with echocardiographic parameters.
33620435.eligibility: Patients admitted with acute pulmonary embolism who have had an echocardiogram at admission with simultaneous measurement of Tricuspid Annular Plane Systolic Excursion (TAPSE) and Pulmonary Artery Systolic Pressure (PASP) are eligible.
33620435.interpretation:
- PESI-Echo score < 128: Lower risk of hospital mortality.
- PESI-Echo score ≥ 128: Higher risk of hospital mortality, with a sensitivity of 82% and specificity of 69%.
33620435.utility: The PESI-Echo score showed an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.82, indicating a good predictive ability for hospital mortality. The global net reclassification improvement was 9.9%.

33624088.title: Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF) Risk Score Calculator
33624088.code:
def compute_risk_score(delta_b_lines, peak_oxygen_consumption, ve_vco2_slope, peak_systolic_pap, resting_ntprobnp):
    """
    Compute the risk score for HFpEF based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    delta_b_lines (int): The difference in B-lines before and after stress.
    peak_oxygen_consumption (float): The peak oxygen consumption in mL/kg/min.
    ve_vco2_slope (float): The slope of the minute ventilation/carbon dioxide production.
    peak_systolic_pap (float): The peak systolic pulmonary artery pressure in mmHg.
    resting_ntprobnp (float): The resting N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide in pg/mL.

    Returns:
    risk_score (int): The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    if delta_b_lines > 10:
        risk_score += 3
    if peak_oxygen_consumption < 16:
        risk_score += 2
    if ve_vco2_slope >= 36:
        risk_score += 2
    if peak_systolic_pap >= 50:
        risk_score += 1
    if resting_ntprobnp > 900:
        risk_score += 1
    return risk_score
33624088.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the transition to and progression of heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) in patients.
33624088.eligibility: Patients at risk of developing heart failure (HF Stages A and B) and patients with HFpEF (Stage C) are eligible for this calculator.
33624088.interpretation:
- Low risk (<3 points): 93% event-free survival probability
- Intermediate risk (3-6 points): 52% event-free survival probability
- High risk (>6 points): 20% event-free survival probability
33624088.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for the scoring system to predict events was 0.92 (95% CI 0.88-0.96), with an accuracy significantly higher than the individual components of the score (all P < 0.01 vs. individual AUCs).

33625028.title: Modified Frailty Index (mFI) Score for Risk Stratification in Breast Reconstruction Surgery
33625028.code:
def compute_mfi_score(diabetes, heart_failure, hypertension, copd_pneumonia, nonindependent_status):
    """
    Compute the Modified Frailty Index (mFI) score.

    Parameters:
    diabetes (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    copd_pneumonia (bool): True if the patient has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease/pneumonia, False otherwise.
    nonindependent_status (bool): True if the patient has nonindependent functional status, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The mFI score, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    return diabetes + heart_failure + hypertension + copd_pneumonia + nonindependent_status
33625028.purpose: This calculator is used to predict and minimize complications in patients undergoing breast reconstruction by stratifying them based on their mFI score.
33625028.eligibility: Patients undergoing primary breast reconstruction are eligible for this calculator.
33625028.interpretation:
- mFI score of 0: No comorbidities, lower risk of postoperative complications.
- mFI score of 1: One comorbidity, moderate risk of postoperative complications.
- mFI score of 2 or more: Two or more comorbidities, higher risk of postoperative complications.
33625028.utility: Patients with higher mFI scores are more likely to develop postoperative complications. The mFI score can be used to predict the risk of complications and guide the timing and type of breast reconstruction surgery.

33627243.title: Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) Calculator for Cardiovascular Events in CTO Patients
33627243.code:
def calculate_gnri_score(gnri):
    """
    Calculate GNRI risk category based on GNRI score.

    Args:
    gnri (float): GNRI score of the patient.

    Returns:
    str: Risk category ('Moderate to Severe', 'Low', 'Absence of Risk').
    """
    if gnri < 92:
        return 'Moderate to Severe'
    elif 92 <= gnri < 98:
        return 'Low'
    else:
        return 'Absence of Risk'
33627243.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of malnutrition and predict the likelihood of adverse cardiovascular events in patients with coronary chronic total occlusion (CTO) after percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI).
33627243.eligibility: Patients with coronary chronic total occlusion (CTO) who have undergone percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) are eligible.
33627243.interpretation:
- Moderate to Severe Risk: Significantly increased risk for all-cause death, cardiovascular death, and major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE).
- Low Risk: Increased risk for all-cause death, cardiovascular death, and MACE, but less than 'Moderate to Severe' risk.
- Absence of Risk: Lowest risk for all-cause death, cardiovascular death, and MACE.
33627243.utility: The GNRI score significantly raised the predictive value for all-cause death, cardiovascular death, and MACE compared to traditional factors. The GNRI score was an independent predictor for adverse cardiovascular events in CTO patients after PCI.

33634092.title: RBP-Signature Glioma Prognosis Calculator
33634092.code:
def calculate_risk_score(rbp_expression_levels, lasso_coefficients):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on the expression levels of RBP genes and the Lasso coefficients.

    Parameters:
    rbp_expression_levels (list): A list of expression levels of the 14 RBP genes.
    lasso_coefficients (list): A list of Lasso coefficients for the 14 RBP genes.

    Returns:
    float: The calculated risk score.
    """
    risk_score = sum([expression_level * coefficient for expression_level, coefficient in zip(rbp_expression_levels, lasso_coefficients)])
    return risk_score
33634092.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival prognosis of glioma patients based on a risk signature constructed by RNA-Binding Protein (RBP) coding genes.
33634092.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with glioma are eligible for this calculator.
33634092.interpretation:
- Risk scores lower than the median risk score in the cohort are considered low-risk gliomas.
- Risk scores higher than the median risk score in the cohort are considered high-risk gliomas.
33634092.utility: The RBP-signature showed robust and stable prognostic value in the TCGA training cohort and in three independent validation cohorts. It was found to be an independent prognostic factor for overall survival with a Hazard Ratio of 1.594 (95% CI: 1.244-2.043, p < 0.001).

33639233.title: POPSAVEIT Score
33639233.code:
def compute_popsaveit_score(systolic_bp, orthopedic_injury, pedal_doppler):
    """
    Compute the POPSAVEIT score for a patient with traumatic popliteal artery injury.

    Parameters:
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure of the patient.
    orthopedic_injury (bool): Whether the patient has an associated orthopedic injury.
    pedal_doppler (bool): Whether the patient has preoperative pedal Doppler signals.

    Returns:
    int: The POPSAVEIT score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if systolic_bp < 90:
        score += 1
    if orthopedic_injury:
        score += 2
    if not pedal_doppler:
        score += 2
    return score
33639233.purpose: The POPSAVEIT score is used to preoperatively stratify patients with traumatic popliteal vascular injuries into low- and high-risk major amputation categories.
33639233.eligibility: Patients with traumatic popliteal artery injuries are eligible for this score.
33639233.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk of amputation
- Score ≥ 3: High risk of amputation
33639233.utility: The POPSAVEIT score has been validated with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.750. It has a sensitivity of 85% and a specificity of 49% for predicting a high risk of amputation.

33653913.title: COVID-19 NIOS Failure Risk Calculator
33653913.code:
def compute_risk_score(SAPS_II_score, LDH_level):
    """
    Compute the risk score for NIOS failure in COVID-19 patients.

    Parameters:
    SAPS_II_score (int): Simplified Acute Physiology II score of the patient.
    LDH_level (int): Serum lactate dehydrogenase level at ICU admission.

    Returns:
    int: Risk score for NIOS failure.
    """
    if SAPS_II_score >= 33 and LDH_level >= 405:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
33653913.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of noninvasive oxygenation strategies (NIOS) failure in critically ill patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory failure due to COVID-19.
33653913.eligibility: Patients receiving first-line treatment NIOS for hypoxemic respiratory failure due to COVID-19 in the ICU.
33653913.interpretation:
- Score 0: Lower risk of NIOS failure.
- Score 1: Higher risk of NIOS failure, with 91% specificity in predicting the need for endotracheal intubation.
33653913.utility: The risk score has a specificity of 91% in predicting the need for endotracheal intubation.

33656803.title: MEDIA Echo Score for Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF)
33656803.code:
def compute_media_echo_score(pasp, rvivcd, ee, lmas):
    """
    Compute the MEDIA Echo Score for HFpEF.

    Parameters:
    pasp (float): Pulmonary arterial systolic pressure
    rvivcd (float): Respiratory variation in inferior vena cava diameter
    ee (float): E/e'
    lmas (float): Lateral mitral annular s'

    Returns:
    int: MEDIA Echo Score
    """
    score = 0
    if pasp > 40:
        score += 1
    if rvivcd > 0.5:
        score += 1
    if ee > 9:
        score += 1
    if lmas < 7:
        score += 1
    return score
33656803.purpose: This calculator is used to predict cardiovascular events in patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) using four simple echocardiographic parameters.
33656803.eligibility: Patients with heart failure with preserved left ventricular (LV) ejection fraction (>50%).
33656803.interpretation:
- Score 0: <10% 1-year risk of death or cardiovascular hospitalization
- Score 1-2: Intermediate risk
- Score 3-4: >35% 1-year risk of death or cardiovascular hospitalization
33656803.utility: Adding these four echocardiographic variables on top of clinical variables and NT-proBNP yielded significant net reclassification improvement (33.8%, P < 0.0001) and increase in C-index (5.3%, a change from 72.2% to 77.5%, P = 0.015).

33667526.title: Renal Stone Recurrence Risk Calculator
33667526.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, history_of_surgery, stone_burden, residual_fragment):
    """
    Compute the risk score for renal stone recurrence.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    history_of_surgery (bool): Whether the patient has a history of stone surgery.
    stone_burden (float): Preoperative stone burden in mm.
    residual_fragment (float): Size of the residual fragment in mm.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age <= 60:
        score += 1
    if history_of_surgery:
        score += 1
    if stone_burden >= 20:
        score += 1
    if residual_fragment >= 4:
        score += 1
    return score
33667526.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of repeat surgery and stone-related events after flexible Ureteroscopy (fURS) for renal stones.
33667526.eligibility: Patients who have undergone flexible Ureteroscopy (fURS) for renal stones with or without concomitant ureteral stones.
33667526.interpretation:
The risk score can be interpreted as follows:

- Score 0-1: Low risk. The estimated 2-year intervention-free survival rate is 96.2%.
- Score 2: Intermediate risk. The estimated 2-year intervention-free survival rate is 86.4%.
- Score 3-4: High risk. The estimated 2-year intervention-free survival rate is 71.3%.
33667526.utility: The calculator was able to stratify patients into low, intermediate, and high-risk groups with different 2-year intervention-free survival rates. The exact AUC, F-score, PPV were not provided in the article.

33674669.title: Albumin Platelet Product (APP) Calculator
33674669.code:
def calculate_APP(albumin, platelet):
    """
    Calculate the Albumin Platelet Product (APP).

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): The patient's albumin level in g/dL.
    platelet (float): The patient's platelet count in 10^9/L.

    Returns:
    float: The calculated APP.
    """
    APP = (albumin * platelet) / 1000
    return APP
33674669.purpose: This calculator is used to differentiate the four stages of liver fibrosis and predict prognosis independent from hepatitis activity grade and age.
33674669.eligibility: Patients with chronic liver disease are eligible for this calculator.
33674669.interpretation:
- APP < 4.349: The patient is diagnosed with cirrhosis. The smaller the APP, the higher the risk of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) prevalence and overall mortality.
- APP >= 4.349: The patient is not diagnosed with cirrhosis.
33674669.utility: The APP calculator has an area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve of more than 0.8, indicating a good performance in diagnosing cirrhosis.

33675416.title: Prostate Cancer Prognosis Calculator
33675416.code:
def compute_risk_score(cfDNA, AR_amp):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with CRPC based on cfDNA level and AR-amp.

    Args:
    cfDNA (float): The total cfDNA level in pg/μL.
    AR_amp (float): The AR-amp in copies.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score (0-2).
    """
    cfDNA_score = 1 if cfDNA > 600 else 0
    AR_amp_score = 1 if AR_amp > 1.26 else 0
    return cfDNA_score + AR_amp_score
33675416.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) based on total cell-free DNA (cfDNA) level and androgen receptor amplification (AR-amp).
33675416.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC).
33675416.interpretation:
- Score 0: Lower risk of poor prognosis.
- Score 1-2: Higher risk of poor prognosis.
33675416.utility: The risk score was significantly associated with overall survival in patients with CRPC. Patients with a score of 1-2 had worse overall survival than those with a score of 0.

33678592.title: Leukemia Pre-transplant Risk Score
33678592.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, diagnosis, donor, cardiovascular_comorbidity, diabetes, arrhythmia):
    """
    Compute the leukemia pre-transplant risk score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): patient's age
    diagnosis (str): type of leukemia ('AML' for acute myeloid leukemia, 'ALL' for acute lymphocytic leukemia)
    donor (str): type of donor ('matched' for matched related or unrelated donor, 'mismatch' for mismatch unrelated or haploidentical donor)
    cardiovascular_comorbidity (bool): presence of cardiovascular comorbidity
    diabetes (bool): presence of pre-transplant diabetes
    arrhythmia (bool): presence of arrhythmia

    Returns:
    risk_score (int): computed risk score
    """
    risk_score = 0
    if age >= 60:
        risk_score += 1
    if diagnosis == 'ALL':
        risk_score += 1
    if donor == 'mismatch':
        risk_score += 1
    if cardiovascular_comorbidity:
        risk_score += 1
    if diabetes:
        risk_score += 1
    if arrhythmia:
        risk_score += 2
    return risk_score
33678592.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the outcomes of allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation (HCT) in patients with acute leukemia.
33678592.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia or acute lymphocytic leukemia who are undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation.
33678592.interpretation:
- 0 points: Low risk
- 1 to 2 points: Medium risk
- ≥ 3 points: High risk
33678592.utility: The risk score demonstrated improved prognostic capability compared with other scores (P = .0001; c-statistic, 0.61).

33679575.title: Poor-Grade Aneurysmal Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (aSAH) Prognosis Scoring Model
33679575.code:
def compute_risk_score(modified_fisher_grade, age, conservative_treatment, WFNS_grade, delayed_cerebral_ischemia, shunt_dependent_hydrocephalus, cerebral_herniation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for poor-grade aSAH prognosis.

    Parameters:
    modified_fisher_grade (bool): True if >2, False otherwise.
    age (int): Patient's age.
    conservative_treatment (bool): True if the patient is under conservative treatment, False otherwise.
    WFNS_grade (int): WFNS grade of the patient.
    delayed_cerebral_ischemia (bool): True if the patient has delayed cerebral ischemia, False otherwise.
    shunt_dependent_hydrocephalus (bool): True if the patient has shunt-dependent hydrocephalus, False otherwise.
    cerebral_herniation (bool): True if the patient has cerebral herniation, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if modified_fisher_grade: score += 1
    if age >= 65: score += 1
    if conservative_treatment: score += 2
    if WFNS_grade == 5: score += 1
    if delayed_cerebral_ischemia: score += 1
    if shunt_dependent_hydrocephalus: score += 1
    if cerebral_herniation: score += 3
    return score
33679575.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the functional outcome in patients with poor-grade aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH).
33679575.eligibility: Patients with poor-grade aSAH, defined as World Federation of Neurosurgical Societies (WFNS) grades IV-V.
33679575.interpretation:
- Low risk (0-1 points): Predicted risk of poor prognosis is 11%
- Intermediate risk (2-3 points): Predicted risk of poor prognosis is 52%
- High risk (4-9 points): Predicted risk of poor prognosis is 87%
33679575.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) in the derivation cohort was 0.844 (95% CI, 0.778-0.909). The AUC in the validation cohort was 0.831 (95% CI, 0.732-0.929).

33680818.title: Modified Frailty Index (mFI) for Forearm Fracture Surgery
33680818.code:
def compute_mfi(comorbid_conditions):
    """
    Compute the Modified Frailty Index (mFI) based on the comorbid conditions.

    Args:
    comorbid_conditions (list): A list of boolean values representing the presence (True) or absence (False) of each comorbid condition.

    Returns:
    int: The mFI score.
    """
    return sum(comorbid_conditions)
33680818.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of complications, including unplanned repeat operations and major post-operative complications, in patients undergoing open fixation of forearm fractures.
33680818.eligibility: Patients undergoing open fixation of forearm fractures are eligible for this risk assessment.
33680818.interpretation:
- mFI score < 2: Lower risk of complications and unplanned repeat operations.
- mFI score ≥ 2: Higher risk of complications, unplanned repeat operations, and discharge to a destination other than home.
33680818.utility: The mFI score was found to be positively associated with unplanned repeat operations and major post-operative complications in the general population. In the elderly population, an mFI score ≥2 was associated with a discharge destination other than the patient's home.

33683539.title: COVID-19 In-Hospital Mortality Risk Calculator
33683539.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, crp, pf_ratio, brixia_score):
    """
    Compute the risk score for in-hospital mortality in COVID-19 patients.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    crp (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/L.
    pf_ratio (float): PaO2/FiO2 ratio.
    brixia_score (int): CXR "Brixia score".

    Returns:
    int: Risk score (0-4).
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 75:
        score += 1
    if crp > 60:
        score += 1
    if pf_ratio < 250:
        score += 1
    if brixia_score > 7:
        score += 1
    return score
33683539.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk of in-hospital mortality for patients with COVID-19 based on age, C-reactive protein (CRP) levels, PaO2/FiO2 ratio (P/F), and chest x-ray (CXR) "Brixia score".
33683539.eligibility: Patients admitted to the Emergency Room with clinical suspicion of COVID-19 confirmed by reverse transcriptase-polymerase reaction chain test (RT-PCR), and have had a CXR performed within 24 hours.
33683539.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of in-hospital mortality (6%)
- Score 2-4: High risk of in-hospital mortality (58%)
33683539.utility: The risk score has a relative risk of 7.6 (4.4-13) for patients with at least two predictors, indicating a strong predictive power for in-hospital mortality.

33685575.title: Ultrasonic Scoring System for Intraoperative Blood Loss in Placenta Accreta Spectrum Disorders
33685575.code:
def compute_risk_group(ultrasonic_score):
    """
    Compute the risk group based on the ultrasonic score.

    Args:
    ultrasonic_score (int): The ultrasonic score of the patient.

    Returns:
    str: The risk group of the patient.
    """
    if ultrasonic_score <= 6:
        return 'low'
    elif 7 <= ultrasonic_score <= 9:
        return 'median'
    else:
        return 'high'
33685575.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of intraoperative blood loss (IBL) equal to or greater than 1,500 mL in patients with placenta accreta spectrum (PAS) disorders based on ultrasonic scores.
33685575.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with placenta accreta spectrum disorders are eligible.
33685575.interpretation:
- Low score group (≤ 6 points): Lower risk of IBL ≥ 1,500 mL.
- Median score group (7-9 points): Moderate risk of IBL ≥ 1,500 mL.
- High score group (≥ 10 points): Higher risk of IBL ≥ 1,500 mL, with an odds ratio of 15.09 (95% confidence interval (3.85, 59.19)).
33685575.utility: The calculator showed a significant positive correlation between ultrasonic score and blood loss, indicating its potential utility in predicting the risk of IBL in PAS disorders.

33687061.title: Ulcerative Colitis Neoplasia Risk Calculator
33687061.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of developing colorectal neoplasia in patients with Ulcerative Colitis (UC) based on the mean Robarts Histopathologic Index (RHI) and Nancy Histopathologic Index (NHI) during a 5-year colonoscopic surveillance period.
33687061.eligibility: Patients with Ulcerative Colitis undergoing colonoscopic surveillance are eligible.
33687061.interpretation:
- If the mean RHI is >= 8, the patient has a 7.53-fold increased risk of developing colorectal neoplasia.
- If the mean NHI is >= 1.9, the patient has a 5.89-fold increased risk of developing colorectal neoplasia.
- If neither condition is met, the patient has a baseline risk.
33687061.utility: The risk score was significantly associated with colorectal neoplasia development in a multivariable analysis (p < 0.001 for both RHI and NHI).

33687565.title: Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) for Esophageal Cancer Patients
33687565.code:
def calculate_gnri(albumin, body_weight, ideal_body_weight):
    """
    Calculate the Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI)

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Albumin levels in g/L
    body_weight (float): Patient's body weight in kg
    ideal_body_weight (float): Patient's ideal body weight in kg

    Returns:
    float: GNRI score
    """
    weight_ratio = body_weight / ideal_body_weight if body_weight <= ideal_body_weight else 1
    gnri = 1.489 * albumin + 41.7 * weight_ratio
    return gnri
33687565.purpose: This calculator is used to quantify nutritional risk in esophageal cancer patients and guide their perioperative management.
33687565.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with esophageal cancer who are undergoing perioperative management.
33687565.interpretation:
- GNRI < 92: High nutritional risk, consistent with malnutrition diagnosis by ESPEN 2015.
- GNRI >= 92: Low nutritional risk.
33687565.utility: The GNRI showed a high sensitivity (72.0%), specificity (78.9%), and consistency (AUC 0.754, 95% CI 0.672-0.836) with malnutrition diagnosed by ESPEN 2015. It also showed good property in predicting major complications, infectious complications, overall complications, and delayed hospital discharge (all P < 0.01).

33706084.title: TRS2P and LDL-C Risk Calculator
33706084.code:
def calculate_risk_group(trs2p, ldl_c, median_trs2p, median_ldl_c):
    """
