    Function to calculate risk group based on TRS2P and LDL-C levels.
    
    Parameters:
    trs2p (float): TRS2P score of the patient.
    ldl_c (float): LDL-C level of the patient.
    median_trs2p (float): Median TRS2P score for the population.
    median_ldl_c (float): Median LDL-C level for the population.
    
    Returns:
    int: Risk group (1-4)
    """
    if trs2p < median_trs2p and ldl_c < median_ldl_c:
        return 1
    elif trs2p < median_trs2p and ldl_c >= median_ldl_c:
        return 2
    elif trs2p >= median_trs2p and ldl_c < median_ldl_c:
        return 3
    else:
        return 4
33706084.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the absolute benefits from additional LDL-C lowering in patients undergoing statin therapy.
33706084.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing statin therapy and have their TRS2P and LDL-C levels measured are eligible.
33706084.interpretation:
- Group 1: Lowest risk of cardiovascular events (21.5%)
- Group 2: Moderate risk of cardiovascular events (28.1%)
- Group 3: High risk of cardiovascular events (36.3%)
- Group 4: Highest risk of cardiovascular events (40.5%)
33706084.utility: The calculator was able to identify patients who remain at high risk despite statins with the combination identifying those who derive the greatest benefits from even modest additional LDL-C lowering.

33709171.title: DIstal Cholangiocarcinoma Early Recurrence Score (DICERS)
33709171.code:
def compute_dicers(nlr, t_bil, mvr):
    """
    Compute the DIstal Cholangiocarcinoma Early Recurrence Score (DICERS).
    
    Parameters:
    nlr (float): Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio.
    t_bil (float): Peak total bilirubin in mg/dL.
    mvr (bool): Whether major vascular resection was performed (True or False).
    
    Returns:
    int: The DICERS, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    score = 0
    if nlr > 9.0:
        score += 2
    if t_bil > 1.5:
        score += 1
    if mvr:
        score += 2
    return score
33709171.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of early recurrence (ER) within 12 months following surgery for patients with distal cholangiocarcinoma (DCC).
33709171.eligibility: Patients who have undergone resection for distal cholangiocarcinoma (DCC).
33709171.interpretation:
- 0 points: Low risk, with a cumulative ER rate of 10.6% in the training dataset and 3.4% in the validation dataset.
- 1-2 points: Intermediate risk, with a cumulative ER rate of 26.8% in the training dataset and 32.7% in the validation dataset.
- 3-5 points: High risk, with a cumulative ER rate of 57.6% in the training dataset and 55.6% in the validation dataset.
33709171.utility: The DICERS was found to be significantly associated with the risk of ER in both the training and validation datasets (p < 0.001).

33727855.title: C-Reactive Protein/Albumin Ratio (CAR) Postoperative Complication Predictor
33727855.code:
def compute_car(crp, albumin):
    """
    Compute the C-Reactive Protein/Albumin Ratio (CAR).

    Parameters:
    crp (float): C-Reactive Protein level on the third postoperative day.
    albumin (float): Albumin level on the third postoperative day.

    Returns:
    float: The C-Reactive Protein/Albumin Ratio (CAR).
    """
    return crp / albumin
33727855.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the probability of early postoperative complications in patients who have undergone Pancreaticoduodenectomy (PD), based on the C-Reactive Protein/Albumin Ratio (CAR) on the third postoperative day (POD3).
33727855.eligibility: Patients who have undergone Pancreaticoduodenectomy (PD) are eligible for this calculator.
33727855.interpretation:
- CAR < 4.86: Lower probability of complications (14.6%)
- CAR ≥ 4.86: Higher probability of complications (85.5%), especially Clinically Relevant Postoperative Pancreatic Fistulas (CR-POPF) (33.3%) and intra-abdominal infection with a positive drainage fluid culture (36.2%)
33727855.utility: The CAR on POD3 showed the largest area under the curve (AUC=0.883), indicating a high predictive accuracy for postoperative complications.

33734137.title: B-ALL Relapse Risk Score Calculator
33734137.code:
def compute_risk_score(MRD_status, CR_status, cGVHD_status):
    """
    Compute the risk score for B-ALL relapse after allo-SCT.

    Parameters:
    MRD_status (bool): True if post-transplantation MRD is positive, False otherwise.
    CR_status (bool): True if transplanted beyond the first complete remission (≥CR2), False if transplanted in CR1.
    cGVHD_status (bool): True if without cGVHD, False if with cGVHD.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(MRD_status) + int(CR_status) + int(cGVHD_status)
33734137.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of relapse in patients with B cell acute lymphocytic leukemia (B-ALL) who underwent allogeneic stem cell transplantation (allo-SCT).
33734137.eligibility: Patients with B cell acute lymphocytic leukemia (B-ALL) who have undergone allogeneic stem cell transplantation (allo-SCT).
33734137.interpretation:
- Score 0: 5-year relapse rate is 6.3%
- Score 1: 5-year relapse rate is 16.6%
- Score 2: 5-year relapse rate is 55.9%
- Score 3: 5-year relapse rate is 81.8%
33734137.utility: The risk score system stratifies patients with different risks of relapse, which could guide treatment. The 5-year leukemia-free survival (LFS) and overall survival (OS) values decrease with increasing risk score.

33741206.title: Forearm Skin Tear Risk Score for Japanese Older Adults
33741206.code:
def compute_risk_score(senile_purpura, pseudoscar, contracture, dry_skin):
    """
    Compute the risk score for forearm skin tears in Japanese older adults.

    Args:
    senile_purpura (bool): True if the patient has senile purpura, False otherwise.
    pseudoscar (bool): True if the patient has pseudoscar, False otherwise.
    contracture (bool): True if the patient has contracture, False otherwise.
    dry_skin (bool): True if the patient has dry skin, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if senile_purpura:
        score += 6
    if pseudoscar:
        score += 4
    if contracture:
        score += 5
    if dry_skin:
        score += 6
    return score
33741206.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of forearm skin tears in Japanese older adults.
33741206.eligibility: Japanese older adults who are inpatients in long-term care hospitals.
33741206.interpretation:
- Score < 12: Low risk of forearm skin tears
- Score >= 12: High risk of forearm skin tears
33741206.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of the total score was 0.806. At a cut-off score of 12, the sensitivity was 0.86, and the specificity was 0.67.

33745754.title: NTM-LD Risk Calculator
33745754.code:
def compute_risk_score(sTIM3, BMI, radiographic_score):
    """
    Compute the risk score for NTM-LD.

    Args:
    sTIM3 (float): sTIM-3 levels in pg/mL.
    BMI (float): Body Mass Index.
    radiographic_score (int): Radiographic score.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if sTIM3 <= 530:
        score += 1
    if BMI <= 22.5:
        score += 1
    if radiographic_score >= 5:
        score += 1
    return score
33745754.purpose: This calculator is used to differentiate between nontuberculous mycobacterial lung disease (NTM-LD) and pulmonary NTM colonization (NTM-Col) based on sTIM-3 levels and other risk factors.
33745754.eligibility: Patients with NTM-positive respiratory samples are eligible for this calculator.
33745754.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of NTM-LD.
- Score 1: Moderate risk of NTM-LD.
- Score 2 or 3: High risk of NTM-LD. Patients with a score of 2 or 3 are 13 times more likely to exhibit NTM-LD than those with a score of 0.
33745754.utility: The adjusted odds ratio for patients with a score of 2 or 3 is 13.234 (95% CI, 2.983-58.709), indicating a strong association between the risk score and NTM-LD.

33775699.title: Glycolysis-Related Risk Signature Calculator for Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma Survival Prediction
33775699.code:
def compute_risk_score(KIF20A_expr, CHST2_expr, MET_expr, KIF20A_coef, CHST2_coef, MET_coef):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression levels of three genes and their coefficients.

    Parameters:
    KIF20A_expr (float): Expression level of KIF20A.
    CHST2_expr (float): Expression level of CHST2.
    MET_expr (float): Expression level of MET.
    KIF20A_coef (float): Coefficient of KIF20A obtained from multivariate Cox regression analysis.
    CHST2_coef (float): Coefficient of CHST2 obtained from multivariate Cox regression analysis.
    MET_coef (float): Coefficient of MET obtained from multivariate Cox regression analysis.

    Returns:
    float: Risk score.
    """
    return KIF20A_expr * KIF20A_coef + CHST2_expr * CHST2_coef + MET_expr * MET_coef
33775699.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the survival of patients with pancreatic adenocarcinoma based on the expression levels of three genes: KIF20A, CHST2, and MET.
33775699.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with pancreatic adenocarcinoma are eligible.
33775699.interpretation:
Patients are classified into high-risk and low-risk groups using the median risk score as the cut-off value. 

- Risk Score < Median Risk Score: Low risk of mortality
- Risk Score >= Median Risk Score: High risk of mortality
33775699.utility: The three-gene signature was validated using the Kaplan-Meier method and showed significant association with overall survival. It was also validated in the GSE28735 dataset from the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) database. Multivariate Cox regression analysis revealed that the three-gene signature had an independent prognostic value.

33778502.title: IPF Survival Prediction Calculator
33778502.purpose: This calculator is used to predict survival in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) by incorporating a semiquantitative fibrotic score from thin-section CT to the Gender, Age, and Physiology (GAP) score.
33778502.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF).
33778502.interpretation:
- 'A1': Lowest risk group. Survival is significantly better than in the other three groups.
- 'B1': Medium-low risk group. Has four times the increased risk of death or transplantation compared to 'A1'.
- 'A2': Medium-high risk group. Has similar risk to 'B1'.
- 'B2': Highest risk group. Has the highest risk of death or transplantation.
33778502.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) of the prediction model is 0.654 (95% confidence interval [CI]: 0.569, 0.74). The hazard ratios for respective groups are as follows: B1 is 4.03 (95% CI: 2.02, 8.07), A2 is 4.10 (95% CI: 1.89, 8.87), and B2 is 5.62 (95% CI: 2.86, 11.06).

33787930.title: Prehospital Assessment of Blood Consumption (PhABC) Score Calculator
33787930.code:
def compute_PhABC_score(penetrating_trauma, heart_rate, systolic_bp, positive_FAST):
    """
    Compute the Prehospital Assessment of Blood Consumption (PhABC) Score.

    Parameters:
    penetrating_trauma (bool): True if penetrating trauma is present, False otherwise.
    heart_rate (int): Heart rate in beats per minute.
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure in mm Hg.
    positive_FAST (bool): True if abdominal FAST is positive, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The PhABC Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if penetrating_trauma:
        score += 1
    if heart_rate > 120:
        score += 1
    if systolic_bp < 90:
        score += 1
    if positive_FAST:
        score += 1
    return score
33787930.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the need for massive transfusion (MT) and emergent laparotomy in trauma patients during aeromedical transport.
33787930.eligibility: Trauma patients who undergo an in-flight Focused Assessment with Sonography for Trauma (FAST) during aeromedical transport are eligible.
33787930.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of requiring MT or emergent laparotomy. Negative predictive value of 95% for emergent laparotomy and 94% for MT.
- Score 2-4: High risk of requiring MT or emergent laparotomy. Positive predictive value of 48% for emergent laparotomy and 28% for MT.
33787930.utility: The PhABC Score has an AUROC curve of 0.83 for predicting emergent laparotomy and 0.77 for predicting MT. It has a sensitivity of 46% and specificity of 96% for predicting emergent laparotomy, and a sensitivity of 33% and specificity of 93% for predicting MT.

33796393.title: COVID-19 Disease Progression Risk Score
33796393.code:
def compute_risk_score(lymphocyte_count, NLR, PLR):
    """
    Compute the risk score for COVID-19 disease progression.

    Parameters:
    lymphocyte_count (float): The patient's lymphocyte count in mcl.
    NLR (float): The patient's neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio.
    PLR (float): The patient's platelet to lymphocyte ratio.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if lymphocyte_count < 620:
        score += 1
    if NLR > 6:
        score += 1
    if PLR > 350:
        score += 1
    return score
33796393.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of disease progression, need for anti-cytokine treatment, intubation, and death in COVID-19 patients.
33796393.eligibility: Patients who have tested positive for COVID-19 and are presenting with moderate to severe pneumonia are eligible.
33796393.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Lower risk of disease progression, need for anti-cytokine treatment, intubation, and death. The discharge rate is 89%.
- Score 2-3: Higher risk of disease progression, need for anti-cytokine treatment, intubation, and death. The discharge rate is 73.6%.
33796393.utility: The risk score was able to predict disease progression, and the likelihood of anti-cytokine treatment, intubation, and death. The discharge rate was significantly lower for patients with a score of 2-3 compared to those with a score of 0-1 (P=0.009).

33806240.title: GERtality Score Calculator
33806240.code:
def compute_GERtality_score(age, PRBC_transfusion, ASA_score, GCS, AIS):
    """
    Compute the GERtality score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    PRBC_transfusion (bool): Whether the patient needed PRBC transfusion prior to ICU.
    ASA_score (int): American Society of Anesthesiologists score.
    GCS (int): Glasgow Coma Scale score.
    AIS (int): Abbreviated Injury Scale score in any body region.

    Returns:
    int: The GERtality score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 80:
        score += 1
    if PRBC_transfusion:
        score += 1
    if ASA_score >= 3:
        score += 1
    if GCS <= 13:
        score += 1
    if AIS >= 4:
        score += 1
    return score
33806240.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the probability of in-hospital mortality in severely injured geriatric trauma patients.
33806240.eligibility: Geriatric patients (≥65 years) who have been severely injured and admitted to the hospital are eligible.
33806240.interpretation:
- GERtality score of 5: Mortality rate of 72.4%
- GERtality score of 4: Mortality rate of 65.1%
- GERtality score of 3: Mortality rate of 47.5%
- GERtality score of 2 or less: Not specified in the article.
33806240.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) of the GERtality score was 0.803, indicating a good predictive ability.

33809852.title: Unplanned Hospitalization Risk Calculator for Elderly Cancer Patients on Chemotherapy
33809852.code:
def compute_risk_score(combination_chemo, max2_index, charlson_score, albumin_level, falls, weight_loss):
    """
    Compute the risk score for unplanned hospitalization.
    
    Parameters:
    combination_chemo (bool): True if the patient is on combination chemotherapy at standard doses, False otherwise.
    max2_index (bool): True if the patient's MAX2 index is >=1, False otherwise.
    charlson_score (bool): True if the patient's Charlson comorbidity score is >=2, False otherwise.
    albumin_level (bool): True if the patient's albumin level is <3.5 g/dL, False otherwise.
    falls (bool): True if the patient had >=1 falls in the past 6 months, False otherwise.
    weight_loss (bool): True if the patient had >5% weight loss, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score for unplanned hospitalization.
    """
    return combination_chemo + max2_index + charlson_score + albumin_level + falls + weight_loss
33809852.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of unplanned hospitalization in elderly patients with cancer who are about to start chemotherapy.
33809852.eligibility: Patients over 70 years old who are starting new chemotherapy regimens.
33809852.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: 17.5% risk of unplanned hospitalization
- Score 2: 34% risk of unplanned hospitalization
- Score 3-7: 57% risk of unplanned hospitalization
33809852.utility: The area under the receiver operation characteristic (ROC) curve was 0.72 (95% CI: 0.67-0.77), indicating a good predictive performance of the risk score.

33816531.title: PREdictor of MAlnutrition in Systemic Sclerosis (PREMASS) Score
33816531.code:
def compute_PREMASS(A, L, Scl70):
    """
    Compute the PREMASS Score.

    Parameters:
    A (float): Serum adiponectin level.
    L (float): Serum leptin level.
    Scl70 (float): Scl70 autoantibody level.

    Returns:
    float: The PREMASS Score.
    """
    A_L_ratio = A / L
    PREMASS = -2.13 + (A_L_ratio * 0.45) + (Scl70 * 0.28)
    return PREMASS
33816531.purpose: The PREMASS Score is used to predict the onset of malnutrition at 12 months in patients with Systemic Sclerosis (SSc).
33816531.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Systemic Sclerosis are eligible for this calculator.
33816531.interpretation:
- PREMASS < -1.46: Low risk of malnutrition at 12 months. Negative predictive value > 97%.
- PREMASS >= -1.46: High risk of malnutrition at 12 months. Positive predictive value > 62%.
33816531.utility: The PREMASS Score has shown very good performance in two independent cohorts for predicting malnutrition at 12 months in SSc, with an AUC of 0.96 (95% CI 0.93, 0.99).

33819676.title: Obstetric Warning Score System
33819676.code:
def compute_obstetric_warning_score(heart_rate, temperature, c_reactive_protein, respiratory_rate, dyspnea, chest_xray):
    """
    Compute the Obstetric Warning Score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    heart_rate (int): Heart rate in beats per minute
    temperature (float): Temperature in Fahrenheit
    c_reactive_protein (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/dL
    respiratory_rate (int): Respiratory rate in breaths per minute
    dyspnea (bool): Whether the patient has complaints of dyspnea
    chest_xray (bool): Whether the chest x-ray is positive

    Returns:
    int: The Obstetric Warning Score
    """
    score = 0
    if heart_rate > 100:
        score += 1
    if temperature > 99.0:
        score += 1
    if c_reactive_protein > 2.0:
        score += 1
    if 20 <= respiratory_rate <= 24:
        score += 1
    if respiratory_rate > 24:
        score += 2
    if dyspnea:
        score += 1
    if chest_xray:
        score += 1
    return score
33819676.purpose: This calculator is used to identify pregnant patients diagnosed with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) infection who are at risk for clinical deterioration and requiring advanced respiratory support.
33819676.eligibility: Pregnant patients diagnosed with severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) infection are eligible.
33819676.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk of requiring advanced respiratory support
- Score ≥ 3: High risk of requiring advanced respiratory support
33819676.utility: The Obstetric Warning Score system has an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.97, indicating excellent predictive power. It has a sensitivity of 100%, specificity of 64%, and a positive predictive value of 36%.

33823539.title: Cardiac Tamponade Prediction Score
33823539.code:
def compute_score(systolic_bp, effusion_diameter, rv_collapse, mitral_inflow_variation):
    """
    Compute the Cardiac Tamponade Prediction Score.

    Parameters:
    systolic_bp (float): Systolic blood pressure in mmHg.
    effusion_diameter (float): Diameter of the effusion in cm.
    rv_collapse (bool): Whether there is right ventricular diastolic collapse.
    mitral_inflow_variation (float): Mitral inflow velocity variation in %.

    Returns:
    float: The Cardiac Tamponade Prediction Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if systolic_bp < 100:
        score += 1.5
    if 1 < effusion_diameter <= 2:
        score += 0
    elif 2 < effusion_diameter <= 3:
        score += 1.5
    elif effusion_diameter > 3:
        score += 2
    if rv_collapse:
        score += 2
    if mitral_inflow_variation > 25:
        score += 1
    return score
33823539.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the need for urgent pericardial drainage in patients with pericardial effusion.
33823539.eligibility: Adult patients admitted through the emergency department with pericardial effusion.
33823539.interpretation:
- Score < 2: Low risk, corresponding to a risk of 8.1% [95% CI 3.0-16.8%] for the need for pericardial drainage within 24 hours.
- Score 2-4: Intermediate risk, corresponding to a risk of 63.8% [95% CI 50.1-76.0%].
- Score >= 4: High risk, corresponding to a risk of 93.7% [95% CI 84.5-98.2%].
33823539.utility: The area under the curve of the simplified score was 0.94 for the derivation and 0.91 for the validation cohort.

33827291.title: Systemic Immune Inflammation Index (SII) Risk Calculator for Contrast-Induced Nephropathy (CIN)
33827291.code:
def calculate_sii(platelet_count, neutrophil_count, lymphocyte_count):
    """
    Calculate the Systemic Immune Inflammation Index (SII)

    Parameters:
    platelet_count (float): Patient's platelet count
    neutrophil_count (float): Patient's neutrophil count
    lymphocyte_count (float): Patient's lymphocyte count

    Returns:
    float: Systemic Immune Inflammation Index (SII)
    """
    return platelet_count * neutrophil_count / lymphocyte_count
33827291.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing contrast-induced nephropathy (CIN) in patients with non-ST segment elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI) who underwent percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI).
33827291.eligibility: Patients with non-ST segment elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI) who are undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) are eligible.
33827291.interpretation:
- SII score < 933.2: Low risk of developing CIN
- SII score >= 933.2: High risk of developing CIN
33827291.utility: The receiver operating characteristic curve analysis showed that at a cutoff of 933.2, the value of SII exhibited 77.6% sensitivity and 69.2% specificity for detecting CIN.

33827660.title: Clinical Prediction Model of Intestinal Necrosis (CPMIN)
33827660.code:
import numpy as np

def calculate_cpm_score(BUN, WBC, D_Dimer):
    """
    Calculate the CPMIN score for predicting intestinal necrosis in AMI patients.

    Parameters:
    BUN (float): Blood Urea Nitrogen level in mmol/L
    WBC (float): White Blood Cell count in 10^9/L
    D_Dimer (float): D-Dimer level in ug/L

    Returns:
    float: CPMIN score
    """
    return 0.349 * BUN + 0.109 * WBC + 0.394 * np.log(D_Dimer) - 7.883
33827660.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of intestinal necrosis in patients with Acute Mesenteric Ischemia (AMI).
33827660.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Acute Mesenteric Ischemia (AMI).
33827660.interpretation:
- CPMIN Score ≥ -0.1992: High risk of intestinal necrosis (82.7% of patients in this group developed intestinal necrosis)
- CPMIN Score < -0.1992: Low risk of intestinal necrosis (15.0% of patients in this group developed intestinal necrosis)
33827660.utility: The CPMIN model has an area under the receiver operating characteristics (ROC) curve of 0.889 (95% confidence interval: 0.833-0.944). The accuracy, specificity, and sensitivity of the model were 82.6%, 78.2%, and 88.3%, respectively.

33833552.title: NLR*CURB-65 Score Calculator for Predicting CAP-related In-hospital Mortality in the Elderly
33833552.code:
def calculate_nlr_curb65(nlr, curb65):
    """
    Calculate the NLR*CURB-65 score.

    Parameters:
    nlr (float): Neutrophil-to-Lymphocyte Ratio
    curb65 (int): CURB-65 score

    Returns:
    float: NLR*CURB-65 score
    """
    return nlr * curb65
33833552.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of in-hospital mortality in elderly patients with community-acquired pneumonia (CAP).
33833552.eligibility: Patients who are 65 years or older and have been diagnosed with community-acquired pneumonia (CAP).
33833552.interpretation:
- NLR*CURB-65 score ≤9.06: Lower risk of in-hospital mortality
- NLR*CURB-65 score >9.06: Higher risk of in-hospital mortality
33833552.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve for the NLR*CURB-65 score in predicting CAP-related in-hospital mortality in the elderly was 0.755, indicating a good predictive accuracy.

33849471.title: Geriatric-QuickSOFA (qSOFA) Score Calculator
33849471.code:
def compute_geriatric_qsofa_score(low_blood_pressure, high_respiratory_rate, delirium):
    """
    Compute the Geriatric-qSOFA score.

    Parameters:
    low_blood_pressure (bool): True if the patient has low arterial blood pressure, False otherwise.
    high_respiratory_rate (bool): True if the patient has a high respiratory rate, False otherwise.
    delirium (bool): True if the patient has delirium, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The Geriatric-qSOFA score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(low_blood_pressure) + int(high_respiratory_rate) + int(delirium)
33849471.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality risk in older patients with sepsis.
33849471.eligibility: Older patients diagnosed with sepsis or septic shock are eligible for this calculator.
33849471.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of 30-day mortality (13.3% in-hospital, 30.0% 30-day mortality)
- Score 2-3: High risk of 30-day mortality (51.5% in-hospital, 84.3% 30-day mortality)
33849471.utility: The Geriatric-qSOFA score was significantly associated with both in-hospital and 30-day mortality in the study population.

33859298.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma Recurrence Risk Calculator
33859298.code:
def compute_risk_score(ctc, afp, dcp, diameter, multiple_tumors):
    """
    Compute the risk score for tumor recurrence after RFA in HCC patients.

    Parameters:
    ctc (float): Circulating tumor cells (in 3.2 mL of blood)
    afp (float): Alpha-fetoprotein level (in ng/mL)
    dcp (float): Des-γ-carboxyprothrombin level (in mAU/mL)
    diameter (float): Maximum tumor diameter (in mm)
    multiple_tumors (bool): Whether there are multiple tumors

    Returns:
    int: The risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if ctc > 2: score += 1
    if afp > 20: score += 1
    if dcp > 40: score += 1
    if diameter > 20: score += 1
    if multiple_tumors: score += 1
    return score
33859298.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of tumor recurrence after percutaneous radiofrequency ablation (RFA) in patients with early hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) of 3 cm or less.
33859298.eligibility: Patients with hepatocellular carcinoma of 3 cm or less who are planning to undergo or have undergone percutaneous radiofrequency ablation.
33859298.interpretation:
- 0-1 score: Low-risk group. The cumulative tumor recurrence rates in 1 year, 2 years, and 3 years are 19.4%, 27.5%, and 30.9% respectively.
- 2-3 scores: Medium-risk group. The cumulative tumor recurrence rates in 1 year, 2 years, and 3 years are 37.0%, 63.2%, and 79.9% respectively.
- 4-5 scores: High-risk group. The cumulative tumor recurrence rates in 1 year, 2 years, and 3 years are 68.2%, 100%, and 100% respectively.
33859298.utility: The clinical scoring system has been validated and shows significant differences in tumor recurrence rates among the low-risk, medium-risk, and high-risk groups (P < 0.001).

33865312.title: 30-Day Unplanned Readmission Risk Score for Older Patients
33865312.code:
def compute_risk_score(malnutrition, depression, malignancy, dependent_functional_status):
    """
    Compute the 30-day unplanned readmission risk score for older patients.

    Parameters:
    malnutrition (bool): Whether the patient is malnourished.
    depression (bool): Whether the patient is depressed.
    malignancy (bool): Whether the patient has a malignancy.
    dependent_functional_status (bool): Whether the patient has a dependent functional status.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if malnutrition:
        score += 2
    if depression:
        score += 2
    if malignancy:
        score += 2
    if dependent_functional_status:
        score += 1
    return score
33865312.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of 30-day unplanned readmission for older patients based on malnutrition, depression, malignancy, and functional status.
33865312.eligibility: Patients aged 60 years and older who have been admitted to a tertiary referral centre are eligible.
33865312.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low risk of 30-day unplanned readmission
- Score 3-7: High risk of 30-day unplanned readmission, with a probability of 82%
33865312.utility: The scoring system has moderate accuracy and strong calibration in predicting 30-day unplanned readmission for older patients, with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.694 (p = 0.001).

33883935.title: Preoperative Fibrinogen-Albumin Ratio (FAR) Prognostic Calculator for Bladder Cancer Patients
33883935.code:
def compute_FAR(fibrinogen, albumin):
    """
    Compute the Fibrinogen-Albumin Ratio (FAR).

    Parameters:
    fibrinogen (float): The fibrinogen level.
    albumin (float): The albumin level.

    Returns:
    float: The computed FAR.
    """
    return fibrinogen / albumin
33883935.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS) in bladder cancer patients undergoing radical cystectomy.
33883935.eligibility: Patients with bladder cancer who are undergoing radical cystectomy are eligible.
33883935.interpretation:
- FAR <= 0.08: Lower risk of poor prognosis.
- FAR > 0.08: Higher risk of poor prognosis, indicating worse overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS).
33883935.utility: The FAR-based prognostic calculator has a higher predictive ability than TNM staging. In the multivariate Cox regression analysis, FAR was an independent risk factor for OS [hazard ratio (HR) 3.569, 95% confidence interval (CI): 1.015-12.546, P=0.047] and PFS [HR 5.071, 95% CI: 1.394-18.451, P=0.014].

33889473.title: Pediatric Hand Fracture Complexity Prediction Model
33889473.code:
def compute_risk_score(open_fracture, rotational_deformity, angulation, condylar_involvement, dislocation_subluxation, displacement):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a complex pediatric hand fracture.

    Args:
    open_fracture (bool): True if the fracture is open, False otherwise.
    rotational_deformity (bool): True if there is a rotational deformity, False otherwise.
    angulation (bool): True if there is angulation, False otherwise.
    condylar_involvement (bool): True if there is condylar involvement, False otherwise.
    dislocation_subluxation (bool): True if there is dislocation or subluxation, False otherwise.
    displacement (bool): True if there is displacement, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for a complex pediatric hand fracture.
    """
    return int(open_fracture) + int(rotational_deformity) + int(angulation) + int(condylar_involvement) + int(dislocation_subluxation) + int(displacement)
33889473.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the complexity of pediatric hand fractures, helping to identify those that require the expertise of a hand surgeon.
33889473.eligibility: Patients under 18 years of age with hand fractures are eligible.
33889473.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of complex fracture
- Score ≥1: High risk of complex fracture
33889473.utility: The model has a C-statistic of 0.88, indicating good discriminative ability. It has a sensitivity of 96.4% and a specificity of 45.5%.

33889600.title: Continu-8 Risk Score for Recurrent Venous Thromboembolism
33889600.code:
def compute_risk_score(unprovoked_DVT, male, factor_VIII):
    """
    Compute the Continu-8 risk score for recurrent VTE.

    Parameters:
    unprovoked_DVT (bool): Whether the patient has unprovoked DVT.
    male (bool): Whether the patient is male.
    factor_VIII (float): The patient's factor VIII level.

    Returns:
    int: The Continu-8 risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if unprovoked_DVT:
        score += 2
    if male:
        score += 1
    if factor_VIII > 213:
        score += 2
    return score
33889600.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of recurrent venous thromboembolism (VTE) in patients with deep-vein thrombosis (DVT).
33889600.eligibility: Patients with a history of provoked and unprovoked DVT are eligible.
33889600.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of recurrent VTE
- Scores 1, 2, or 3: Medium risk of recurrent VTE
- Scores 4 and 5: High risk of recurrent VTE
33889600.utility: The concordance statistic (C-statistic) of the Continu-8 risk score was 0.68 (95% CI: 0.61, 0.75), indicating adequate discriminative ability.

33898622.title: Calgary Acute Pulmonary Embolism Score
33898622.code:
def compute_risk_score(ct_ratio, systolic_bp, clot, heart_rate):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the Calgary Acute Pulmonary Embolism Score.

    Parameters:
    ct_ratio (float): CT right-left ventricular diameter ratio.
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure in mmHg.
    clot (bool): Presence of central pulmonary artery clot.
    heart_rate (int): Heart rate in beats per minute.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if ct_ratio >= 1.5:
        score += 3
    if 90 <= systolic_bp <= 100:
        score += 2
    if clot:
        score += 2
    if heart_rate >= 100:
        score += 2
    return score
33898622.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of in-hospital adverse outcomes in patients with acute normotensive pulmonary embolism.
33898622.eligibility: Patients with normotensive acute pulmonary embolism are eligible.
33898622.interpretation:
The risk score is interpreted as follows:

- Score 0-3: Low risk group (73.8% prevalence, 0.34% primary outcome event rate)
- Score 4-6: Intermediate risk group (17.6% prevalence, 5.8% primary outcome event rate)
- Score 7-9: High risk group (8.7% prevalence, 12.8% primary outcome event rate)
33898622.utility: The C-statistic of the risk score is 0.85 (95% CI 0.78-0.91), indicating good predictive performance.

33899279.title: Transfusion Requirement Prediction Score for Cardiac Surgery (TRPS)
33899279.code:
def compute_TRPS(age, gender, bypass_time, haemoglobin, ejection_fraction, warfarin_prophylaxis):
    """
    Compute the Transfusion Requirement Prediction Score for Cardiac Surgery (TRPS).

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient. Score 1 if more than 58 years, 0 otherwise.
    gender (str): Gender of the patient. Score 1 if female, 0 otherwise.
    bypass_time (int): Bypass time in minutes. Score 1 if more than 148 minutes, 0 otherwise.
    haemoglobin (float): Haemoglobin level in g/dL. Score 1 if less than 12.5 g/dL, 0 otherwise.
    ejection_fraction (float): Ejection fraction in %. Score 1 if less than 57%, 0 otherwise.
    warfarin_prophylaxis (bool): History of warfarin prophylaxis. Score 1 if yes, 0 otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The TRPS score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 58:
        score += 1
    if gender == 'female':
        score += 1
    if bypass_time > 148:
        score += 1
    if haemoglobin < 12.5:
        score += 1
    if ejection_fraction < 57:
        score += 1
    if warfarin_prophylaxis:
        score += 1
    return score
33899279.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the transfusion requirement for patients undergoing cardiac surgery.
33899279.eligibility: Patients undergoing cardiac surgery are eligible for this calculator.
33899279.interpretation:
- Score less than 5: Low risk of requiring transfusion
- Score 5 or more: High risk of requiring transfusion
33899279.utility: The TRPS predicted blood utilisation with a sensitivity of 68% and a specificity of 79%. On internal validation, the score was observed to have an accuracy of 70%.

33900502.title: Tumor Marker Score (TMS) Calculator for cHCC-CC Prognosis
33900502.code:
def calculate_tms(afp, cea, ca19_9):
    """
    Calculate the Tumor Marker Score (TMS) based on the levels of AFP, CEA, and CA19-9.

    Parameters:
    afp (float): Level of AFP in ng/mL.
    cea (float): Level of CEA in ng/mL.
    ca19_9 (float): Level of CA19-9 in U/mL.

    Returns:
    int: The TMS, which can be 0, 1, or 2 or more.
    """
    tms = 0
    if afp > 10.76:
        tms += 1
    if cea > 5.24:
        tms += 1
    if ca19_9 > 31.54:
        tms += 1
    return tms
33900502.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with combined hepatocellular carcinoma and cholangiocarcinoma (cHCC-CC) after surgery.
33900502.eligibility: Patients with pathologically confirmed cHCC-CC who have undergone surgery.
33900502.interpretation:
- TMS 0: Lowest risk group.
- TMS 1: Intermediate risk group, with a hazard ratio (HR) of 1.72 compared to TMS 0.
- TMS ≥ 2: Highest risk group, with a HR of 4.15 compared to TMS 0.
33900502.utility: The TMS has a c-index of 0.666 (95% CI 0.619-0.714), indicating good discrimination. The area under the curve (AUC) value of time-dependent receiver operating curves (td-ROC) for TMS is higher than for other clinical stages at any month within 5 years postoperation, showing that TMS has optimal prognostic value.

33906810.title: MR-proADM Guided Hospitalization Risk Calculator
33906810.code:
def compute_risk(mr_proadm):
    """
    Compute the risk of disease progression based on MR-proADM value.

    Args:
    mr_proadm (float): The MR-proADM value in nmol/L.

    Returns:
    str: The risk level ('Low' or 'High').
    """
    if mr_proadm <= 0.87:
        return 'Low'
    else:
        return 'High'
33906810.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk of disease progression in patients with suspicion of infection in the emergency department (ED), and to decide whether hospitalization is required.
33906810.eligibility: Patients who are suspected of having an infection and are in the emergency department are eligible.
33906810.interpretation:
- 'Low': The patient is at low risk of disease progression and can be treated as an outpatient.
- 'High': The patient is at high risk of disease progression and should be hospitalized.
33906810.utility: The MR-proADM guided arm had a 17% lower hospital admission rate in the intention-to-treat population and 20% lower in the per protocol population compared to the control arm. The safety in the MR-proADM guided study arm was similar to the Standard Care arm.

33907284.title: Acute Exacerbation of Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (AE-IPF) Prognostic Calculator
33907284.code:
def compute_score(baseline_FVC, PaO2_FiO2_ratio, FVC_cutoff, ratio_cutoff):
    """
    Compute the risk score for AE-IPF prognosis.

    Parameters:
    baseline_FVC (float): The percent-predicted forced vital capacity within 12 months before AE onset.
    PaO2_FiO2_ratio (float): The PaO2/FiO2 ratio at AE onset.
    FVC_cutoff (float): The cutoff value for baseline %FVC.
    ratio_cutoff (float): The cutoff value for PaO2/FiO2 ratio.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if baseline_FVC < FVC_cutoff:
        score += 1
    if PaO2_FiO2_ratio < ratio_cutoff:
        score += 1
    return score
33907284.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 90-day survival rates of patients with a first episode of AE-IPF, based on the percent-predicted forced vital capacity within 12 months before AE onset (baseline %FVC) and the PaO2/FiO2 ratio at AE onset.
33907284.eligibility: Patients with a first episode of acute exacerbation of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (AE-IPF).
33907284.interpretation:
- Score 0: Mild risk, with a post-AE 90-day cumulative survival rate of 83.3%.
- Score 1: Moderate risk, with a post-AE 90-day cumulative survival rate of 66.2%.
- Score 2: Severe risk, with a post-AE 90-day cumulative survival rate of 22.2%.
33907284.utility: The C-index for this model is 0.702, indicating a good predictive accuracy.

33909004.title: FIB-4 COVID-19 Risk Calculator
33909004.code:
import math

def calculate_fib4(age, ast, platelets, alt):
    """
    Calculate the FIB-4 score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age in years
    ast (float): Aspartate Aminotransferase level in U/L
    platelets (float): Platelet count in 10^9/L
    alt (float): Alanine Aminotransferase level in U/L

    Returns:
    float: FIB-4 score
    """
    return (age * ast) / (platelets * math.sqrt(alt))
33909004.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of adverse outcomes in patients with early-stage COVID-19 based on the Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) score.
33909004.eligibility: Patients with confirmed early-stage COVID-19 are eligible.
33909004.interpretation:
- FIB-4 between 1.45 and 3.25: Greater than 5-fold increased hazard of high-flow oxygen use, greater than 4-fold increased odds of progression to severe disease, and over 3-fold increased odds of death or prolonged hospitalization.
- FIB-4 > 3.25: Greater than 12-fold increased hazard of high-flow oxygen use and over 11-fold increased risk of progression to severe disease.
33909004.utility: The FIB-4 score had an independent and dose-dependent association with adverse outcomes during hospitalization. It provided significant prognostic value for estimating adverse outcomes among COVID-19 patients.

33909236.title: Primary Membranous Nephropathy Progression Nomogram
33909236.code:
import numpy as np

def compute_risk_score(age, sPLA2R_Ab, proteinuria, Uα1m_Cr):
    """
    Compute the risk score for progression in patients with pMN and NS.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient. If the patient is 65 years or older, use 1, otherwise use 0.
    sPLA2R_Ab (float): The level of sPLA2R-Ab in the patient's blood. This should be the natural logarithm of the actual value.
    proteinuria (float): The level of proteinuria in the patient. This should be the natural logarithm of the actual value.
    Uα1m_Cr (float): The level of Uα1m/Cr in the patient. This should be the natural logarithm of the actual value.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score for progression.
    """
    return 7.004*age + 2.150*sPLA2R_Ab + 5.939*proteinuria + 2.808*Uα1m_Cr
33909236.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of progression in patients with primary membranous nephropathy (pMN) who present with nephrotic syndrome (NS).
33909236.eligibility: Patients with biopsy-proven primary membranous nephropathy and nephrotic syndrome are eligible.
33909236.interpretation: The optimal cutoff value of the nomogram score is 117.8. If the computed risk score is less than 117.8, the patient has a low risk of progression. If the computed risk score is equal to or greater than 117.8, the patient has a high risk of progression.
33909236.utility: The nomogram has a C-index of 0.888 and a bootstrap-corrected C-index of 0.869, indicating good predictive ability. The positive predictive value is 44.4% and the negative predictive value is 98.5%.

33934059.title: Drug-Based Prognostic Score Calculator for Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) Patients
33934059.code:
def compute_drug_score(proton_pump_inhibitor, antibiotic, corticosteroid):
    """
    Compute the drug score based on the intake of proton-pump inhibitor, antibiotic, and corticosteroid.

    Parameters:
    proton_pump_inhibitor (bool): True if the patient took a proton-pump inhibitor within a month before therapy, False otherwise.
    antibiotic (bool): True if the patient took an antibiotic within a month before therapy, False otherwise.
    corticosteroid (bool): True if the patient took a corticosteroid within a month before therapy, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The computed drug score.
    """
    score = 0
    if proton_pump_inhibitor:
        score += 1
    if antibiotic:
        score += 1
    if corticosteroid:
        score += 2
    return score
33934059.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the clinical outcome of patients with advanced NSCLC receiving first-line immunotherapy or chemotherapy, based on their concomitant medication intake.
33934059.eligibility: Patients with advanced NSCLC who are about to initiate first-line immunotherapy or chemotherapy.
33934059.interpretation:
- Score 0: Good risk group
- Score 1-2: Intermediate risk group
- Score 3-4: Poor risk group
33934059.utility: The 'drug score' showed a predictive ability with respect to objective response rate (ORR) in the immunotherapy cohort only. The prognostic stratification in terms of progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) was significantly more pronounced among the pembrolizumab-treated patients.

33934199.title: Modified Systemic Inflammation Score (mSIS) Calculator for Gastric Cancer Prognosis
33934199.code:
def calculate_msis(alb, lmr):
    """
    Calculate the Modified Systemic Inflammation Score (mSIS) based on serum albumin level (ALB) and lymphocyte-to-monocyte ratio (LMR).
    
    Parameters:
    alb (float): Serum albumin level in g/dL.
    lmr (float): Lymphocyte-to-monocyte ratio.
    
    Returns:
    int: mSIS score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    if alb >= 4.0 and lmr >= 3.4:
        return 0
    elif alb < 4.0 and lmr < 3.4:
        return 2
    else:
        return 1
33934199.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the long-term outcome of patients undergoing curative resection for gastric cancer.
33934199.eligibility: Patients who have undergone curative resection for gastric cancer are eligible.
33934199.interpretation:
- mSIS 0: Lowest risk of poor prognosis.
- mSIS 1: Moderate risk of poor prognosis.
- mSIS 2: Highest risk of poor prognosis.
33934199.utility: The mSIS was identified as an independent prognostic factor with hazard ratios of 1.35 and 1.63 for mSIS 1 and 2 respectively.

33940542.title: Prognostic Index for Estimating Survival Benefit of Postoperative Radiotherapy in Pathologic N2 Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
33940542.code:
def compute_PI(smoking_history, cN2, num_lymph_nodes):
    """
    Compute the Prognostic Index (PI) for a patient with NSCLC.

    Parameters:
    smoking_history (int): 1 for non-heavy smokers, 2 for heavy smokers
    cN2 (int): 1 for clinical N2 status absent, 2 for clinical N2 status present
    num_lymph_nodes (int): 1 for <=4 positive lymph nodes, 2 for >4 positive lymph nodes

    Returns:
    float: The computed PI score
    """
    PI = 0.8*smoking_history + 0.5*cN2 + 0.7*num_lymph_nodes
    return PI
33940542.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the effect of postoperative radiotherapy (PORT) in patients with completely resected pathologic N2 non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).
33940542.eligibility: Patients with completely resected pathologic stage T1-3N2M0 NSCLC are eligible.
33940542.interpretation:
- PI score <= 3: Low-risk group. PORT shows a trend towards decreased locoregional recurrence (LRR) rates but not significantly improved overall survival (OS).
- PI score > 3: High-risk group. PORT significantly reduces the risk of LRR and improves OS.
33940542.utility: The calculator was validated in a real-world study and showed significant prognostic value in predicting the effect of PORT in patients with completely resected pN2 NSCLC.

33987230.title: Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) and Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) DNA Level Prognostic Calculator for Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma (NPC)
33987230.code:
def calculate_risk_group(GNRI, EBV_DNA):
    """
    Function to calculate risk group based on GNRI and EBV DNA levels.
    Args:
    GNRI (float): Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index score.
    EBV_DNA (str): Epstein-Barr virus DNA level ('high' or 'low').
    Returns:
    str: Risk group ('high-risk', 'medium-risk', 'low-risk').
    """
    if GNRI <= 107.7 and EBV_DNA == 'high':
        return 'high-risk'
    elif GNRI > 107.7 and EBV_DNA == 'low':
        return 'low-risk'
    else:
        return 'medium-risk'
33987230.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with nasopharyngeal carcinoma based on their nutritional status (GNRI) and EBV DNA levels.
33987230.eligibility: Patients with biopsy-proven non-disseminated nasopharyngeal carcinoma are eligible.
33987230.interpretation:
- High-risk: Patients in this group have a significantly lower survival rate compared to those in the low- and medium-risk groups.
- Medium-risk: Patients in this group have a survival rate between the high- and low-risk groups.
- Low-risk: Patients in this group have a significantly higher survival rate compared to those in the high- and medium-risk groups.
33987230.utility: The combined classification of GNRI and EBV DNA levels was demonstrated to be an independent prognostic factor for overall survival and progression-free survival after adjustment using multivariate analysis.

33995602.title: Sjögren's Syndrome Risk Matrix Model
33995602.code:
def compute_risk_score(total_US_score, total_SWE_value):
    """
    Compute the risk score for Sjögren's Syndrome based on total US scores and total SWE values.

    Args:
    total_US_score (int): Total B-mode ultrasonography score of the four glands.
    total_SWE_value (float): Total shear-wave elastography value of the four glands in kPa.

    Returns:
    str: Risk level ('High', 'Moderate', 'Low')
    """
    if total_US_score >= 9 or total_SWE_value >= 33:
        return 'High'
    elif total_US_score < 5 and total_SWE_value < 27:
        return 'Low'
    else:
        return 'Moderate'
33995602.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the probability of a positive labial salivary gland biopsy (LSGB) in patients with suspected Sjögren's syndrome, based on B-mode ultrasonography (US) and shear-wave elastography (SWE) examination results.
33995602.eligibility: Patients with suspected Sjögren's syndrome who have undergone B-mode US and SWE examination on bilateral parotid and submandibular glands before LSGB.
33995602.interpretation:
- 'High': High risk of positive LSGB. The positive predictive value (PPV) was 100% in the derivation cohort and 94% in the validation cohort.
- 'Moderate': Moderate risk of positive LSGB. Specific PPV and NPV are not provided in the article.
- 'Low': Low risk of positive LSGB. The negative predictive value (NPV) was 100% in the derivation cohort and 93% in the validation cohort.
33995602.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for the total US scores and total SWE values were 0.956 and 0.825 respectively, both p < 0.001, indicating high predictive accuracy.

33996854.title: Ectopic Pregnancy Predictive Score Calculator
33996854.code:
def compute_ep_score(history_pid, emergency_pills, cervical_tenderness, serum_hcg, ultrasound_mass):
    """
    Compute the ectopic pregnancy risk score.

    Parameters:
    history_pid (bool): True if the patient has a history of pelvic inflammatory disease, False otherwise.
    emergency_pills (bool): True if the patient is currently using emergency pills, False otherwise.
    cervical_tenderness (bool): True if the patient has cervical motion tenderness, False otherwise.
    serum_hcg (bool): True if the patient's serum hCG is ≥1,000 mIU/ml, False otherwise.
    ultrasound_mass (bool): True if the patient's ultrasound shows an adnexal mass, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The ectopic pregnancy risk score.
    """
    return history_pid + emergency_pills + cervical_tenderness + serum_hcg + ultrasound_mass
33996854.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of ectopic pregnancy in women presenting first-trimester complications such as abdominal pain or vaginal bleeding.
33996854.eligibility: Pregnant women presenting first-trimester complications (abdominal pain or vaginal bleeding) with diagnosis suspected of pregnancy of unknown location are eligible.
33996854.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk of ectopic pregnancy. No patient with a score less than 3 had an ectopic pregnancy in the validation group.
- Score ≥ 3: High risk of ectopic pregnancy. Prompt intervention is recommended.
33996854.utility: The predictive index score was able to determine ectopic pregnancy with an accuracy of 77.8% (95% confidence interval (CI) = 73.1-82.1), specificity of 91.0% (95% CI = 62.1-72.0), sensitivity of 67.0% (95% CI = 88.0-94.0), and area under the curve of 0.906 (95% CI = 0.875-0.937).

33998242.title: Preoperative Absolute Lymphocyte Count (ALC) Risk Score for Prognosis in Children with Tetralogy of Fallot
33998242.code:
def compute_risk_score(ALC):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the preoperative ALC level.

    Parameters:
    ALC (float): The preoperative absolute lymphocyte count in 10^9/L.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score. 1 for ALC < 4.36, 0 otherwise.
    """
    if ALC < 4.36:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
33998242.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the prognostic value of preoperative ALC in children with Tetralogy of Fallot.
33998242.eligibility: Children aged less than 6 years who are about to undergo corrective operations for Tetralogy of Fallot.
33998242.interpretation:
- A risk score of 1 indicates a higher risk of poor prognosis, with an odds ratio of 3.500.
- A risk score of 0 indicates a lower risk of poor prognosis.
33998242.utility: The addition of ALC improved the area under the curve (AUC) from 0.771 to 0.781 (P<0.001), indicating that the ALC risk score has a good predictive performance.

33999252.title: Preoperative Risk-Scoring System for High Malignancy Potential Gastric GISTs
33999252.code:
def compute_risk_score(surface_ulceration, tumor_size, location):
    """
    Compute the risk score for high malignancy potential gastric GISTs.

    Args:
    surface_ulceration (bool): Whether the tumor has surface ulceration.
    tumor_size (float): The size of the tumor in cm.
    location (str): The location of the tumor, either 'cardia' or 'fundus'.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if surface_ulceration:
        score += 1
    if 4 <= tumor_size <= 6:
        score += 2
    elif tumor_size > 6:
        score += 5
    if location in ['cardia', 'fundus']:
        score += 2
    return score
33999252.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the malignancy potential of gastric GISTs preoperatively.
33999252.eligibility: Patients with pathologically confirmed gastric GISTs who are undergoing resection are eligible.
33999252.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk group, with a 19.8% chance of high malignancy potential
- Score >= 3: High risk group, with an 82.7% chance of high malignancy potential
33999252.utility: The area under the receiver-operating characteristic curve for predicting high malignancy potential was 0.81 (95% confidence interval (CI) 0.75-0.86). Discrimination was good after validation (0.75, 95% CI 0.69-0.81).

34010724.title: Fetal Candidacy for Prenatal Intervention Scoring System
34010724.code:
def compute_score(biochemistry, ultrasound, bladder_volume, bladder_refill):
    """
    Compute the risk score for fetal candidacy for prenatal intervention.

    Parameters:
    biochemistry (bool): True if biochemistry is unfavorable, False otherwise.
    ultrasound (bool): True if ultrasound shows evidence of dysplastic kidneys, False otherwise.
    bladder_volume (bool): True if initial bladder volume is inadequate, False otherwise.
    bladder_refill (bool): True if bladder refill is inadequate, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if biochemistry:
        score += 2
    if ultrasound:
        score += 4
    if bladder_volume:
        score += 1
    if bladder_refill:
        score += 1
    return score
34010724.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the candidacy of fetuses for prenatal intervention in cases of severe lower urinary tract obstruction (LUTO), and to predict postnatal survival.
34010724.eligibility: Patients with severe fetal lower urinary tract obstruction (LUTO) who are being evaluated for fetal intervention.
34010724.interpretation:
- Score of 0-3: The fetus is a good candidate for prenatal intervention, with a higher chance of 6-month postnatal survival.
- Score of >3: The fetus is not a good candidate for prenatal intervention, with a 0% chance of 6-month postnatal survival.
34010724.utility: The scoring system has an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.82 (95% CI 0.65-0.99), indicating good performance in predicting postnatal mortality.

34023221.title: COVID-19 Severity and Oxygen Supply Requirement Calculator for Japanese Patients
34023221.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, hemodialysis, crp):
    """
    Compute the risk score for COVID-19 severity and oxygen supply requirement.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    hemodialysis (bool): Whether the patient is on hemodialysis.
    crp (float): C-reactive protein level of the patient in mg/dL.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    age_score = 1 if age >= 50 else 0
    hemodialysis_score = 1 if hemodialysis else 0
    crp_score = 1 if crp >= 1.0 else 0

    return age_score + hemodialysis_score + crp_score
34023221.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the severity of COVID-19 and the need for oxygen supply in Japanese patients based on age, hemodialysis status, and C-reactive protein (CRP) levels.
34023221.eligibility: Japanese patients diagnosed with COVID-19 are eligible for this calculator.
34023221.interpretation:
- Score of 0 or 1: The patient is less likely to require oxygen supply.
- Score of 2 or 3: The patient is more likely to require oxygen supply.
34023221.utility: The sensitivity and specificity of this model for predicting the need for oxygen supply are 68.4% and 79.0%, respectively.

34035053.title: Diabetic Foot Ulceration Clinical Prediction Rule (CPR)
34035053.code:
def compute_cpr_score(monofilament_test, pulse, history):
    """
    Compute the CPR score for diabetic foot ulceration.

    Parameters:
    monofilament_test (bool): True if the monofilament test result is abnormal, False otherwise.
    pulse (bool): True if pulses are absent, False if pulses are present.
    history (bool): True if there is a history of previous ulcer and/or amputation, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The CPR score.
    """
    return int(monofilament_test) + int(pulse) + int(history)
34035053.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of foot ulceration in people with diabetes within 2 years of initial assessment.
34035053.eligibility: Adults over 18 years old with diabetes, who are ulcer-free at the time of recruitment.
34035053.interpretation:
- Score 0: 2.4% risk of ulcer within 2 years
- Score 1: 6.0% risk of ulcer within 2 years
- Score 2: 14.0% risk of ulcer within 2 years
- Score 3: 29.2% risk of ulcer within 2 years
- Score 4: 51.1% risk of ulcer within 2 years
34035053.utility: The CPR's performance was assessed with C-statistics, calibration slopes, calibration-in-the-large, and a net benefit analysis. The C-statistic was 0.829 (95% CI 0.790 to 0.868).

34041659.title: SALT Score for Predicting Postoperative Diabetes Insipidus
34041659.code:
def compute_salt_score(suprasellar_extension, age, craniopharyngioma_histology, kelly_grade_3_csf_leak):
    """
    Compute the SALT score for predicting postoperative diabetes insipidus.

    Parameters:
    suprasellar_extension (bool): True if the patient has suprasellar extension, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    craniopharyngioma_histology (bool): True if the patient has craniopharyngioma histology, False otherwise.
    kelly_grade_3_csf_leak (bool): True if the patient has Kelly grade 3 intraoperative CSF leak, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The SALT score.
    """
    score = 0
    if suprasellar_extension:
        score += 1
    if age < 50:
        score += 1
    if craniopharyngioma_histology:
        score += 1
    if kelly_grade_3_csf_leak:
        score += 1
    return score
34041659.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing postoperative diabetes insipidus (DI) in patients undergoing endoscopic endonasal transsphenoidal surgery.
34041659.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing endoscopic endonasal transsphenoidal surgery for anterior skull base pathology are eligible.
34041659.interpretation:
- SALT score of 0: 4.0% risk of postoperative DI
- SALT score of 1: 6.5% risk of postoperative DI
- SALT score of 2: 15.0% risk of postoperative DI
- SALT score of 3: 36.8% risk of postoperative DI
- SALT score of 4: 85.7% risk of postoperative DI
34041659.utility: The SALT score has a fair to good predictive value for postoperative DI, with an AUROC of 0.735 (95% CI 0.65-0.82).

34045024.title: Urinary DKK3/creatinine Ratio Risk Calculator for Contrast-Associated Kidney Damage
34045024.code:
def compute_risk_score(uDKK3, uCr):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on urinary DKK3 and creatinine levels.

    Parameters:
    uDKK3 (float): Urinary DKK3 level in pg/ml.
    uCr (float): Urinary creatinine level in mg/dl.

    Returns:
    float: Risk score.
    """
    return uDKK3 / uCr
34045024.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of acute kidney injury (AKI) and persistent kidney dysfunction in patients with chronic kidney disease undergoing invasive cardiovascular procedures requiring iodinated contrast medium administration.
34045024.eligibility: Patients with chronic kidney disease scheduled for invasive cardiovascular procedures requiring iodinated contrast medium administration.
34045024.interpretation:
- If the risk score is ≥491 pg/mg, the patient is at risk for acute kidney injury (AKI).
- If the risk score is ≥322 pg/mg, the patient is at risk for persistent kidney dysfunction.
34045024.utility: The addition of the baseline uDKK3/uCr to the Mehran, Gurm, and National Cardiovascular Data Registry (NCDR) scores significantly increased the net reclassification improvement (NRI) and integrated discrimination improvement (IDI), indicating the clinical utility of the risk score in improving the identification of patients at risk for AKI and persistent kidney dysfunction.

34055606.title: Prognostic Clinical Score (PCS) for Recurrent or Metastatic Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Head and Neck
34055606.code:
def compute_pcs(ecog, age, hgb, nlr):
    """
    Compute the Prognostic Clinical Score (PCS) for a patient with r/m SCCHN.

    Parameters:
    ecog (int): ECOG performance status
    age (float): Age in years
    hgb (float): Hemoglobin level
    nlr (float): Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio

    Returns:
    int: PCS
    """
    pcs = 0
    pcs += 1 if ecog >= 2 else 0
    pcs += 1 if age >= 61.8 else 0
    pcs += 1 if hgb < 11.80 else 0
    pcs += 1 if nlr >= 5.73 else 0
    return pcs
34055606.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival of patients with recurrent or metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck (r/m SCCHN) treated with systemic therapy including cetuximab.
34055606.eligibility: Patients with recurrent or metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck who are undergoing systemic therapy including cetuximab.
34055606.interpretation:
The PCS stratifies patients into four risk subgroups:

- Low risk: PCS = 0, median overall survival time = 23.4 months
- Intermediate risk: PCS = 1, median overall survival time = 12.1 months
- Poor risk: PCS = 2, median overall survival time = 7.5 months
- Very poor risk: PCS = 3 or 4, median overall survival time = 4.0 months
34055606.utility: The PCS was found to be strongly associated with overall survival in a cohort of 218 patients with r/m SCCHN. It may guide clinical decision making and serve for stratification of patients in future clinical trials.

34061375.title: ACEF Score for Risk Stratification in MINOCA Patients
34061375.code:
def calculate_acef_score(age, ejection_fraction, creatinine):
    """
    Calculate the ACEF score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    ejection_fraction (float): Ejection fraction in %.
    creatinine (float): Creatinine level in μmol/L.

    Returns:
    float: The ACEF score.
    """
    acef_score = age / ejection_fraction
    if creatinine > 176:
        acef_score += 1
    return acef_score
34061375.purpose: This calculator is used to predict long-term outcomes in patients with myocardial infarction with nonobstructive coronary arteries (MINOCA) using the age, creatinine, and ejection fraction (ACEF) score.
34061375.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with myocardial infarction with nonobstructive coronary arteries (MINOCA).
34061375.interpretation:
- 1st tertile (lowest ACEF scores): Lower risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE)
- 2nd tertile: Moderate risk of MACE (HR 2.70, 95% CI: 1.38-5.29)
- 3rd tertile (highest ACEF scores): High risk of MACE (HR 5.35, 95% CI: 2.72-10.51)
34061375.utility: The ACEF score demonstrated good predictive value for MACE with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.79.

34064988.title: FIB-4 Index for Hepatocellular Carcinoma Risk in Patients with Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease and Chronic Hepatitis B
34064988.code:
import math

def calculate_fib4(age, ast, platelet_count, alt):
    """
    Calculate FIB-4 index.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years
    ast (float): Aspartate transaminase level
    platelet_count (float): Platelet count
    alt (float): Alanine aminotransferase level

    Returns:
    float: FIB-4 index
    """
    return age * ast / (platelet_count * math.sqrt(alt))
34064988.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) in patients with coexisting nonalcoholic fatty liver disease and chronic hepatitis B (NAFLD-CHB).
34064988.eligibility: Patients with coexisting nonalcoholic fatty liver disease and chronic hepatitis B are eligible. Patients with HCC at baseline and those diagnosed with HCC within 6 months from baseline are excluded.
34064988.interpretation:
- FIB-4 index < 1.77: Low risk of developing HCC
- FIB-4 index ≥ 1.77: High risk of developing HCC
34064988.utility: The FIB-4 index has an AUC of 0.70, indicating a good predictive ability. Patients with a high FIB-4 index had a significantly and independently higher risk of HCC than those with a low FIB-4 index (adjusted hazard ratio, 4.35; 95% confidence interval, 1.42-13.24).

34073045.title: Stenosis or Occlusion Prediction Model after Percutaneous Deep Venous Arterialization
34073045.code:
def compute_risk_score(previous_ultrasound_value, current_ultrasound_value):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the decrease in duplex ultrasound values.

    Args:
    previous_ultrasound_value (float): The duplex ultrasound value from the previous month.
    current_ultrasound_value (float): The duplex ultrasound value from the current month.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score, represented as the percentage decrease in duplex ultrasound values.
    """
    decrease = previous_ultrasound_value - current_ultrasound_value
    risk_score = (decrease / previous_ultrasound_value) * 100
    return risk_score
34073045.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of stenosis or occlusion in patients after undergoing percutaneous deep venous arterialization (pDVA).
34073045.eligibility: Patients who have undergone percutaneous deep venous arterialization (pDVA) are eligible.
34073045.interpretation:
- Risk Score < 20%: Low risk of stenosis or occlusion. The patient may require less frequent follow-up visits.
- Risk Score >= 20%: High risk of stenosis or occlusion. The patient may require more frequent follow-up visits.
34073045.utility: The prediction model was able to estimate a patient-specific risk of future stenosis or occlusions. This is promising for the possibility of reducing the frequency of follow-up visits for low-risk patients and increasing the frequency for high-risk patients.

34082249.title: Hodgkin Lymphoma Prognostic Calculator
34082249.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the prognosis of patients with classical Hodgkin lymphoma by integrating leukocyte lymphocyte ratio (LLR) and prognostic nutritional index (PNI) into the international prognostic scoring systems (IPS-4).
34082249.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with classical Hodgkin lymphoma are eligible.
34082249.interpretation:
- High risk: Patients with LLR ≥ 5.8 or PNI ≤ 45.2 are considered at high risk for overall survival. Anemia is considered the highest risk for progression-free survival.
- Low risk: Patients with LLR < 5.8 or PNI > 45.2 are considered at low risk for overall survival.
34082249.utility: The clinical utility of the risk score was evaluated using the Cox regression models for overall survival and progression-free survival. The exact AUC, F-score, PPV values are not provided in the article.

34116468.title: Copeptin and NEWS Survival Prediction Calculator
34116468.code:
def calculate_risk(copeptin, news):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on copeptin and NEWS values.

    Parameters:
    copeptin (float): The serum copeptin level in pg/mL.
    news (float): The National Early Warning Score.

    Returns:
    risk (str): The risk of 30-day mortality.
    """
    if copeptin > 19.78 and news > 8.5:
        return "High risk"
    else:
        return "Low risk"
34116468.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day survival prognosis of elderly patients with critical illness at the emergency department.
34116468.eligibility: Elderly patients with critical illness admitted to the emergency department are eligible.
34116468.interpretation:
- High risk: Patients with both copeptin levels higher than 19.78 pg/mL and NEWS points higher than 8.5 points have the highest risk of death within 30 days.
- Low risk: Patients with either copeptin levels lower than or equal to 19.78 pg/mL or NEWS points lower than or equal to 8.5 points have a lower risk of death within 30 days.
34116468.utility: The AUC values for copeptin, NEWS, and copeptin combined with NEWS are 0.766, 0.797, and 0.854 respectively, indicating a good predictive power. The combined copeptin and NEWS score showed a higher predictive value for 30-day survival (P < 0.05).

34116828.title: Brain Death After Cardiac Arrest (BDCA) Score Calculator
34116828.code:
def compute_bdca_score(is_female, nonshockable_rhythm, cardiac_cause, neurological_cause, natremia_24h, vasoactive_drug_admission, vasoactive_drug_24h):
    """
    Compute the BDCA score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    is_female (bool): True if the patient is female, False otherwise.
    nonshockable_rhythm (bool): True if the patient had a nonshockable rhythm, False otherwise.
    cardiac_cause (bool): True if the OHCA was due to a cardiac cause, False otherwise.
    neurological_cause (bool): True if the OHCA was due to a neurological cause, False otherwise.
    natremia_24h (float): The patient's natremia level 24 hours after OHCA.
    vasoactive_drug_admission (bool): True if the patient was given a vasoactive drug at admission, False otherwise.
    vasoactive_drug_24h (bool): True if the patient was given a vasoactive drug 24 hours after OHCA, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The BDCA score.
    """
    score = 0
    if is_female:
        score += 4
    if nonshockable_rhythm:
        score += 24
    if cardiac_cause:
        score -= 6
    if neurological_cause:
        score += 45
    score += natremia_24h - 140
    if vasoactive_drug_admission:
        score += 4
    if vasoactive_drug_24h:
        score += 6
    return score
34116828.purpose: This calculator is used for early estimation of brain death risk after an out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA).
34116828.eligibility: Patients who have been successfully resuscitated after an out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) and are alive 24 hours after the event.
34116828.interpretation:
- Score < 20: Low risk of brain death (4.0% occurrence in validation cohort)
- Score 20 to 50: Moderate risk of brain death (20.4% occurrence in validation cohort)
- Score > 50: High risk of brain death (67.7% occurrence in validation cohort)
34116828.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) of the BDCA score was 0.82 (95% CI, 0.77-0.86), and the discrimination value in the validation cohort (n = 487) was consistent (AUC, 0.81; 95% CI, 0.76-0.86).

34119504.title: Modified Frailty Index-5 (mFI-5) Calculator
34119504.code:
def compute_mfi_5(chf, copd, diabetes, hypertension, dependence):
    """
    Compute the Modified Frailty Index-5 (mFI-5) score.

    Parameters:
    chf (bool): True if the patient has Congestive Heart Failure, False otherwise.
    copd (bool): True if the patient has Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, False otherwise.
    diabetes (bool): True if the patient has Diabetes Mellitus, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has Hypertension that requires medication, False otherwise.
    dependence (bool): True if the patient has partial or total dependence prior to surgery, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The mFI-5 score.
    """
    return chf + copd + diabetes + hypertension + dependence
34119504.purpose: This calculator is used to predict postoperative outcomes in patients undergoing Minimally Invasive Partial Nephrectomy (MIPN). It helps identify and counsel those at increased risk for morbidity.
34119504.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing Minimally Invasive Partial Nephrectomy (MIPN).
34119504.interpretation:
- mFI-5 score of 0: 5.3% risk of postoperative complications
- mFI-5 score of 1: 7.8% risk of postoperative complications
- mFI-5 score of 2 or more: 11.2% risk of postoperative complications
34119504.utility: The mFI-5 score was found to be a significant predictor of postoperative outcomes, with a nearly 50% increase in the odds of having an overall complication for patients with mFI-5 score ≥2 (OR = 1.48, 95% CI 1.22 - 1.80, P <0.001).

34121230.title: Histopathological Risk Score (HRS) Calculator for Neoadjuvant Therapy Response in Rectal Adenocarcinoma
34121230.code:
def compute_HRS(TP53, CD8, ITB):
    """
    Compute the Histopathological Risk Score (HRS).

    Parameters:
    TP53 (bool): True if TP53 expression is aberrant, False otherwise.
    CD8 (bool): True if CD8+ T cell density is low, False otherwise.
    ITB (bool): True if intratumoral budding (ITB) is high, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The HRS, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(TP53) + int(CD8) + int(ITB)
34121230.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the response to neoadjuvant therapy in patients with rectal adenocarcinoma based on TP53 expression, CD8+ T cell density, and intratumoral budding (ITB).
34121230.eligibility: Patients with locally advanced rectal adenocarcinoma who are undergoing neoadjuvant therapy are eligible.
34121230.interpretation:
- 0-1: Low risk of partial or poor response to neoadjuvant therapy
- 2-3: High risk of partial or poor response to neoadjuvant therapy
34121230.utility: Patients with a high HRS were 3.25-fold more likely to have a partial or poor response to neoadjuvant therapy (95% CI = 1.48-7.11, P = 0.003).

34123853.title: Preoperative Sarcopenia and Lymphopenia Prognosis Calculator for Localized Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma (LPDAC)
34123853.code:
def calculate_risk_score(lymphopenia, sarcopenia):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on preoperative lymphopenia and sarcopenia.
    
    Parameters:
    lymphopenia (bool): True if the patient has lymphopenia, False otherwise.
    sarcopenia (bool): True if the patient has sarcopenia, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score. 2 for high risk, 1 for intermediate risk, 0 for low risk.
    """
    if lymphopenia:
        return 2
    elif sarcopenia:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
34123853.purpose: This calculator is used to predict relapse-free survival (RFS) in patients with LPDAC based on preoperative lymphopenia and sarcopenia.
34123853.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with localized pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (LPDAC) and are scheduled for surgical resection.
34123853.interpretation:
- High risk (score 2): Median RFS of 5.6 months (95% CI = 4.3 to 9.6 months)
- Intermediate risk (score 1): Median RFS of 11.5 months (95% CI = 9.8 to 13.9 months)
- Low risk (score 0): Median RFS of 21.2 months (95% CI = 9.9 to 55.3 months)
34123853.utility: The calculator was able to stratify patients into three distinct risk groups with significantly different median RFS (p < 0.001).

34128086.title: Drainage Or Transiency of Acute Hydrocephalus after Aneurysmal SAH (DOTAHAS) Score
34128086.code:
def compute_DOTAHAS_score(hunt_hess_grade, fisher_grade, vHSS):
    """
    Compute the DOTAHAS score based on Hunt and Hess grade, modified Fisher grade, and vHSS.

    Parameters:
    hunt_hess_grade (int): Hunt and Hess grade of the patient.
    fisher_grade (int): Modified Fisher grade of the patient.
    vHSS (int): Ventricular Hijdra Sum Score of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The DOTAHAS score.
    """
    score = 0
    if hunt_hess_grade >= 3:
        score += 1
    if fisher_grade == 4:
        score += 2
    if vHSS >= 6:
        score += 4
    return score
34128086.purpose: This calculator is used to differentiate patients with transient acute hydrocephalus from those needing surgical interventions after aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH).
34128086.eligibility: Patients with aSAH who underwent endovascular treatment are eligible.
34128086.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Lower risk for needing external ventricular drainage (EVD)
- Score ≥ 3: Significantly higher risk for needing EVD
34128086.utility: The DOTAHAS score was found to be useful in identifying patients with transient acute hydrocephalus. Further evaluation of the score is needed.

34140231.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Predicting Transradial Approach Failure
34140231.code:
def compute_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, stroke_tia_thromboembolism, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Compute the CHA2DS2-VASc score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    stroke_tia_thromboembolism (bool): True if the patient has had a stroke, TIA, or thromboembolism, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): The sex of the patient. 'F' for female, 'M' for male.

    Returns:
    int: The CHA2DS2-VASc score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if stroke_tia_thromboembolism:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'F':
        score += 1
    return score
