34815687.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of sarcopenia in older adults based on their genotype score, which is derived from sarcopenia-related single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs).
34815687.eligibility: Patients aged 60 or above who do not have severe comorbidities, agreed to join the Taiwan Precision Medicine Initiative project, and have sufficient information of required sarcopenic measurements.
34815687.interpretation:
- Genotype score of 0-1: Low risk of sarcopenia
- Genotype score of 2-3: Moderate risk of sarcopenia (OR=408)
- Genotype score of 4 or more: High risk of sarcopenia (OR=630.6)
34815687.utility: The genotype score demonstrated a significant association with the risk of sarcopenia (p-value<0.001). However, the clinical utility of the risk score, such as AUC, F-score, PPV, was not provided in the article.

34821549.title: COVID-19 Mortality Risk Score Calculator
34821549.code:
def compute_risk_score(S_W_ratio, H2O2_concentration):
    """
    Compute the risk score for COVID-19 mortality based on HSA structural damage and oxidative stress markers.

    Parameters:
    S_W_ratio (float): The ratio of strong-binding to weak-binding sites within HSA.
    H2O2_concentration (float): The concentration of hydrogen peroxide in plasma (in μM).

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return S_W_ratio / H2O2_concentration
34821549.purpose: This calculator is used to predict in-hospital mortality in critically ill COVID-19 patients based on the structural damage to Human Serum Albumin (HSA) due to oxidative stress.
34821549.eligibility: Critically ill COVID-19 patients who have undergone blood tests for HSA structural damage and oxidative stress markers are eligible.
34821549.interpretation:
- Risk Score < 0.019: High risk of in-hospital mortality (95.5%)
- Risk Score >= 0.019: Low risk of in-hospital mortality (4.5%)
34821549.utility: The risk score showed high fidelity in predicting mortality, with a log-rank χ2=12.1, p=4.9×10-4.

34826497.title: Early MACE Risk Stratification Score for STEMI Patients
34826497.code:
def compute_risk_score(time_to_reperfusion, grace_score, lvef, mvo):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the four variables.
    
    Parameters:
    time_to_reperfusion (float): Time to reperfusion in hours.
    grace_score (int): GRACE risk score.
    lvef (float): Left ventricular ejection fraction in percentage.
    mvo (float): Microvascular obstruction in segments.
    
    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if time_to_reperfusion > 4.15:
        score += 1
    if grace_score > 155:
        score += 3
    if lvef < 40:
        score += 3
    if mvo > 1.5:
        score += 1
    return score
34826497.purpose: This calculator is used for early risk stratification of major adverse cardiac events (MACE) in patients with ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI).
34826497.eligibility: Patients who have undergone reperfusion for STEMI and have had a cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) performed within one week are eligible.
34826497.interpretation:
The risk score is interpreted into three categories:

- Low-risk: 0-2 points, with MACE per 100 person-years being 1.96
- Intermediate-risk: 3-5 points, with MACE per 100 person-years being 5.44
- High-risk: 6-8 points, with MACE per 100 person-years being 19.7
34826497.utility: The risk score demonstrated significant stratification of MACE risk in multivariable Cox survival analysis (p < 0.001).

34835085.title: COVID-19 Bleeding Risk Score Calculator
34835085.code:
def compute_risk_score(elevated_d_dimer, elevated_ferritin, critical_illness, therapeutic_dose_anticoagulation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for bleeding in COVID-19 patients.

    Parameters:
    elevated_d_dimer (bool): Whether the patient has elevated D-dimer.
    elevated_ferritin (bool): Whether the patient has elevated ferritin.
    critical_illness (bool): Whether the patient is critically ill.
    therapeutic_dose_anticoagulation (bool): Whether the patient is on therapeutic-dose anticoagulation.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for bleeding.
    """
    return elevated_d_dimer + elevated_ferritin + critical_illness + therapeutic_dose_anticoagulation
34835085.purpose: This calculator is used to identify hospitalized patients with COVID-19 at increased risk for major bleeding, non-major bleeding, and bleeding-related death.
34835085.eligibility: Hospitalized patients with COVID-19 receiving standard-, intermediate- or therapeutic doses of VTE prophylaxis are eligible.
34835085.interpretation:
- Score 0: Very low risk for bleeding. Major bleeding occurred in 1.0% of the patients, non-major bleeding in 0.5%.
- Score 1: Low risk for bleeding. Major bleeding occurred in 2.1% of the patients, non-major bleeding in 3.5%.
- Score 2: Intermediate risk for bleeding. Major bleeding occurred in 8.7% of the patients, non-major bleeding in 9.5%.
- Score 3 or 4: High risk for bleeding. Major bleeding occurred in 15.4% of the patients, non-major bleeding in 14.2%.
34835085.utility: The c-statistics was: 0.74 (95% confidence intervals [CI]: 0.68-0.79) for major bleeding, 0.73 (95% CI: 0.67-0.78) for non-major bleeding and 0.82 (95% CI: 0.76-0.87) for bleeding-related death.

34837357.title: Modified CHA2DS2-VASc Score for COVID-19 Patients
34837357.code:
def compute_m_cha2ds2_vasc_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, stroke_tia_thromboembolism, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Compute the Modified CHA2DS2-VASc Score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    stroke_tia_thromboembolism (bool): True if the patient has had a stroke, TIA, or thromboembolism, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): The sex of the patient. 'M' for male, 'F' for female.

    Returns:
    int: The Modified CHA2DS2-VASc Score for the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if stroke_tia_thromboembolism:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'M':
        score += 1
    return score
34837357.purpose: This calculator is used to predict in-hospital mortality in COVID-19 patients admitted to the Emergency Department due to clinical worsening.
34837357.eligibility: COVID-19 patients admitted to the Emergency Department due to clinical worsening are eligible.
34837357.interpretation:
- 0 - 1 points: Low risk, with a survival proportion of 92%
- 2 - 3 points: Intermediate risk, with a survival proportion of 80%
- ≥ 4 points: High risk, with a survival proportion of 63%
34837357.utility: The Modified CHA2DS2-VASc Score was identified as an independent predictor of mortality in a multivariable logistic regression model (OR 1.406, p = 0.007).

34844159.title: Carotid Plaque Stability Calculator
34844159.code:
def compute_nlr(neutrophils, lymphocytes):
    """
    Compute the neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR).

    Parameters:
    neutrophils (float): The number of neutrophils in the blood sample.
    lymphocytes (float): The number of lymphocytes in the blood sample.

    Returns:
    float: The computed NLR.
    """
    return neutrophils / lymphocytes
34844159.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the stability of carotid plaques based on the neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR).
34844159.eligibility: Patients with acute anterior circulation ischemic stroke who are in accordance with extracranial and intracranial large artery atherosclerosis.
34844159.interpretation:
- NLR < 2.55: Low risk of plaque instability.
- NLR >= 2.55: High risk of plaque instability.
34844159.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) of the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve was 0.782, indicating a good predictive performance. The best cut-off value of the evaluation scale was ≥ 4 points with a sensitivity of 0.77, a specificity of 0.75, and an accuracy of 0.76.

34854991.title: AF-SCORE Calculator
34854991.code:
def compute_af_score(age, sex, af_persistent):
    """
    Compute the AF-SCORE based on age, sex, and AF-phenotype.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    sex (str): The sex of the patient. Should be 'male' or 'female'.
    af_persistent (bool): Whether the patient has persistent AF.

    Returns:
    int: The computed AF-SCORE.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 60:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'female':
        score += 1
    if af_persistent:
        score += 2
    return score
34854991.purpose: This calculator is used to predict Fibrotic atrial myopathy (FAM) and ablation-success in patients with atrial fibrillation (AF) based on easily available clinical parameters.
34854991.eligibility: Patients with atrial fibrillation (AF) who are undergoing high-density left-atrial voltage-mapping to quantify FAM.
34854991.interpretation:
- AF-SCORE ≤ 2: Associated with favorable outcomes of PVI-only. Arrhythmia-freedom following PVI is 74.3%.
- AF-SCORE = 3: Moderate risk. Arrhythmia-freedom following PVI is 54.7%. Hazard ratio for arrhythmia is 1.994.
- AF-SCORE = 4: High risk. Arrhythmia-freedom following PVI is 45.5%. Hazard ratio for arrhythmia is 2.866.
34854991.utility: The AF-SCORE showed good discrimination to detect FAM with a c-statistic of 0.792.

34860315.title: Preoperative Prognostic Models for Non-Metastatic Upper Tract Urothelial Carcinoma (UTUC)
34860315.code:
def compute_risk_score(performance_status, cT_stage, SII):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with non-metastatic UTUC.

    Parameters:
    performance_status (int): The performance status of the patient.
    cT_stage (int): The clinical tumor stage of the patient.
    SII (float): The systemic immune-inflammation index of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if performance_status > 0:
        score += 1
    if cT_stage >= 3:
        score += 1
    if SII > 520:
        score += 1
    return score
34860315.purpose: These models are used to predict prognosis and muscle invasion in patients with non-metastatic UTUC before and at radical nephroureterectomy (RNU).
34860315.eligibility: Patients with non-metastatic upper tract urothelial carcinoma (UTUC) undergoing radical nephroureterectomy (RNU).
34860315.interpretation:
- 0 points: Low risk
- 1 point: Medium risk
- 2-3 points: High risk
34860315.utility: The models have high accuracy, with c-indices of 0.843 and 0.820 for cancer-specific survival (CSS) and overall survival (OS), respectively. The model for predicting muscle invasion has an AUC of 0.804.

34862802.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Risk Score for Predicting Successful Endovascular Treatment in Acute Ischemic Stroke
34862802.code:
def compute_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(C, H, A75, D, S, V, A65, SC):
    """
    Compute the CHA2DS2-VASc score.

    Parameters:
    C (bool): Congestive heart failure
    H (bool): Hypertension
    A75 (bool): Age ≥75 years
    D (bool): Diabetes Mellitus
    S (bool): Stroke/TIA/Thromboembolism
    V (bool): Vascular disease
    A65 (bool): Age 65-74 years
    SC (bool): Sex category (Female)

    Returns:
    int: CHA2DS2-VASc score
    """
    return C + H + 2*A75 + D + 2*S + V + A65 + SC
34862802.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the success of endovascular intervention in patients with acute ischemic stroke.
34862802.eligibility: Patients with acute ischemic stroke who are undergoing percutaneous endovascular treatment are eligible.
34862802.interpretation:
- CHA2DS2-VASc score < 3: Higher probability of successful endovascular intervention.
- CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥ 3: Higher probability of unsuccessful endovascular intervention.
34862802.utility: The CHA2DS2-VASc score has a sensitivity of 76.6% and specificity of 83.3% for predicting unsuccessful intervention. The positive predictive value is 50% and the negative predictive value is 84.6%. The area under the curve (AUC) is 0.827.

34866934.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) Risk Score After Hepatitis C Virus (HCV) Elimination
34866934.code:
def compute_risk_score(previous_hcc_history, fib_4_index):
    """
    Compute the risk score for developing HCC after HCV elimination.

    Args:
    previous_hcc_history (bool): Whether the patient has a history of HCC.
    fib_4_index (float): The patient's Fib-4 index.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    if previous_hcc_history:
        return 2
    elif fib_4_index >= 3.07:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
34866934.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing HCC after achieving sustained virological response (SVR) by direct-acting antiviral agent (DAA) treatment for HCV.
34866934.eligibility: Patients with chronic HCV infection who were treated with DAAs and achieved SVR are eligible.
34866934.interpretation:
- 0-point group: The HCC occurrence rate at 5 years is 0.4%.
- 1-point group: The HCC occurrence rate at 5 years is 6.8%.
- 2-point group: The HCC occurrence rate at 5 years is 55.6%.
34866934.utility: The risk score was found to be a significant predictor of HCC development after DAA treatment. The most significant factor was previous HCC history, followed by Fib-4 index.

34870336.title: COP-NLR Risk Calculator for Pharyngocutaneous Fistula After Total Laryngectomy
34870336.code:
def compute_cop_nlr_score(platelet_count, nlr):
    """
    Compute the COP-NLR risk score based on platelet count and NLR.

    Parameters:
    platelet_count (float): The patient's platelet count.
    nlr (float): The patient's neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio.

    Returns:
    int: The COP-NLR risk score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    if platelet_count > 320 and nlr > 3.95:
        return 2
    elif platelet_count > 320 or nlr > 3.95:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
34870336.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing pharyngocutaneous fistula (PCF) in patients who have undergone total laryngectomy, based on the combination of platelet count and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (COP-NLR).
34870336.eligibility: Patients who have undergone total laryngectomy are eligible for this risk calculator.
34870336.interpretation:
- COP-NLR 0: Low risk of developing PCF.
- COP-NLR 1: Moderate risk of developing PCF. The odds ratio compared to COP-NLR 0 is 4.17.
- COP-NLR 2: High risk of developing PCF. The odds ratio compared to COP-NLR 0 is 5.33.
34870336.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for NLR is 0.635 and for platelet count is 0.571. The COP-NLR was found to be an independent risk factor for PCF.

34871376.title: CHADS-P2A2RC Risk Score Calculator
34871376.code:
def compute_CHADS_P2A2RC_score(congestive_heart_failure, hypertension, age, diabetes_mellitus, prior_stroke_or_tia, vascular_disease, sex):
    """
    Compute the CHADS-P2A2RC score.

    Parameters:
    congestive_heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    diabetes_mellitus (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    prior_stroke_or_tia (bool): True if the patient has had a prior stroke or transient ischemic attack, False otherwise.
    vascular_disease (bool): True if the patient has vascular disease, False otherwise.
    sex (str): The sex of the patient, either 'male' or 'female'.

    Returns:
    int: The CHADS-P2A2RC score.
    """
    score = 0
    if congestive_heart_failure:
        score += 1
    if hypertension:
        score += 1
    if age >= 75:
        score += 2
    elif age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if diabetes_mellitus:
        score += 1
    if prior_stroke_or_tia:
        score += 2
    if vascular_disease:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'female':
        score += 1
    return score
34871376.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with chronic coronary syndromes (CCS).
34871376.eligibility: Patients with chronic coronary syndromes undergoing first-time coronary angiography are eligible.
34871376.interpretation:
- High risk: ≥4 points. Major adverse cardiovascular events per 100 person-years were 4.8.
- Moderate risk: 2-3 points. Major adverse cardiovascular events per 100 person-years were 3.8.
- Low risk: ≤1 points. Major adverse cardiovascular events per 100 person-years were 1.5.
34871376.utility: The CHADS-P2A2RC score enabled correct downward risk reclassification of 5161 patients (8%) without events, yielding an improved specificity of 9.7%, a loss of sensitivity of 4.4%, and an overall net reclassification index of 0.053.

34884334.title: Post-Operative Non-Union Risk Score for Subtrochanteric Femur Fractures
34884334.code:
def compute_risk_score(diabetes, deep_wound_infection, comminution, atypical_fracture, gap_size, varus_malreduction):
    """
    Compute the risk score for non-union following a subtrochanteric fracture of the femur.

    Parameters:
    diabetes (bool): Whether the patient has diabetes.
    deep_wound_infection (bool): Whether the patient has a deep wound infection.
    comminution (bool): Whether the patient has simple or severe comminution.
    atypical_fracture (bool): Whether the patient has an atypical fracture.
    gap_size (float): The size of the lateral cortex gap in mm.
    varus_malreduction (float): The degree of varus malreduction.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if diabetes:
        score += 6
    if deep_wound_infection:
        score += 35
    if comminution:
        score += 13
    if atypical_fracture:
        score += 14
    if gap_size >= 5:
        score += 11
    if 5 <= varus_malreduction <= 10:
        score += 9
    elif varus_malreduction > 10:
        score += 20
    return score
34884334.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of non-union following a subtrochanteric fracture of the femur in the immediate post-operative period.
34884334.eligibility: Patients who have undergone surgery for a subtrochanteric fracture of the femur are eligible.
34884334.interpretation:
- Score < 18: Low risk of non-union
- Score >= 18: High risk of non-union
34884334.utility: The area under the ROC curve was 0.790, indicating very good discriminatory capability of the scoring system. The model also showed good calibration (Hosmer-Lemeshow test; p = 0.291) and internal validity (accuracy 0.806; Kappa 0.416).

34895912.title: COVID-19 CT Severity Score (CTSS) Calculator
34895912.code:
def compute_CTSS(CTPA_score):
    """
    Compute the CT Severity Score (CTSS) for COVID-19.

    Parameters:
    CTPA_score (int): The score from the CT pulmonary angiography.

    Returns:
    str: The CTSS, either "high" or "low".
    """
    if CTPA_score >= 14:
        return "high"
    else:
        return "low"
34895912.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the severity of COVID-19 in patients based on CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) and to prognosticate outcomes.
34895912.eligibility: Patients who have undergone both CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) and whole-genome sequencing of SARS-CoV-2 within 14 days of CTPA.
34895912.interpretation:
- "Low" CTSS: Lower risk of clinical deterioration and shorter hospital stay.
- "High" CTSS: Significantly increased risk of clinical deterioration, as defined by subsequent admission to critical care or death, and longer hospital stay (17.4 versus 7.9 days).
34895912.utility: The CTSS strongly correlates with clinical and biochemical severity at the time of CTPA, and with patient outcomes. It has a multivariate hazard ratio [HR] of 2.76 (p<0.001) for predicting clinical deterioration.

34914160.title: Redo-CRA/CAA Success Prediction Calculator
34914160.code:
def compute_risk_score(male, age_over_60, obesity, history_of_radiotherapy):
    """
    Compute the risk score for redo-CRA/CAA success prediction.

    Parameters:
    male (bool): True if the patient is male, False otherwise.
    age_over_60 (bool): True if the patient's age is over 60, False otherwise.
    obesity (bool): True if the patient is obese, False otherwise.
    history_of_radiotherapy (bool): True if the patient has a history of pelvic radiotherapy, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, which is the sum of the four input parameters.
    """
    return int(male) + int(age_over_60) + int(obesity) + int(history_of_radiotherapy)
34914160.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the success of redo colorectal or coloanal anastomosis (CRA/CAA) surgery based on four pre-operative variables.
34914160.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing redo colorectal or coloanal anastomosis (CRA/CAA) surgery are eligible.
34914160.interpretation:
- Score 0: The success of redo-CRA/CAA is 92%
- Score 1: The success of redo-CRA/CAA is 86%
- Score 2: The success of redo-CRA/CAA is 80%
- Score ≥3: The success of redo-CRA/CAA is 62%
34914160.utility: The calculator was found to be statistically significant in predicting the success of redo-CRA/CAA (p = 0.010).

34920962.title: The BALLAR Score Calculator
34920962.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the 30-day mortality in patients requiring left-sided Impella implantation as standalone mechanical circulatory support (MCS).
34920962.eligibility: Patients who require left-sided Impella MCS monotherapy are eligible for this calculator.
34920962.interpretation:
- A score less than 6 predicts a 30-day mortality of under 5%.
- A score over 11 predicts a greater than 95% chance of death within 30 days.
- Scores between 6 and 11 fall into the intermediate probability range.
34920962.utility: The BALLAR score predicted 89% of 30-day deaths in the cohort. All the misclassifications were in the intermediate probability range (scores 5-11).

34944774.title: Glasgow Prognostic Score (GPS) and CRP/Albumin Ratio Calculator
34944774.code:
def calculate_gps(crp, albumin):
    """
    Calculate Glasgow Prognostic Score (GPS)
    
    Parameters:
    crp (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/L
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/L
    
    Returns:
    int: GPS score (0, 1, or 2)
    """
    gps = 0
    if crp > 10:
        gps += 1
    if albumin < 35:
        gps += 1
    return gps

def calculate_crp_albumin_ratio(crp, albumin):
    """
    Calculate CRP/Albumin ratio
    
    Parameters:
    crp (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/L
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/L
    
    Returns:
    float: CRP/Albumin ratio
    """
    return crp / albumin
34944774.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the prognostic value of routine inflammatory biomarkers in older cancer patients.
34944774.eligibility: Cancer patients aged 70 and above are eligible for this calculator.
34944774.interpretation:
- GPS 0: Normal values, lower risk
- GPS 1: One abnormal value, moderate risk
- GPS 2: Two abnormal values, high risk
- CRP/Albumin ratio ≤ 0.215: Normal, lower risk
- CRP/Albumin ratio > 0.215: Abnormal, higher risk
34944774.utility: The discriminative power of the baseline clinical model was increased by adding GPS (C = 0.84 [0.82-0.85]; NRI events (NRI+) = 10% [2-16]) and CRP/albumin ratio (C = 0.83 [0.82-0.85]; NRI+ = 14% [2-17]).

34945259.title: Frailty Syndrome Risk Score Calculator for Heart Failure Patients
34945259.code:
def compute_fs_risk_score(age, systolic_pressure, total_cholesterol, bilirubin, alanine_aminotransferase):
    """
    Compute the Frailty Syndrome (FS) risk score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    systolic_pressure (float): Systolic pressure on admission in mmHg.
    total_cholesterol (float): Total cholesterol level in mmol/L.
    bilirubin (float): Bilirubin level in mmol/L.
    alanine_aminotransferase (float): Alanine aminotransferase level in U/L.

    Returns:
    int: The FS risk score, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 50:
        score += 1
    if systolic_pressure < 110:
        score += 1
    if total_cholesterol < 4.85:
        score += 1
    if bilirubin >= 15.5:
        score += 1
    if alanine_aminotransferase <= 34:
        score += 1
    return score
34945259.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of Frailty Syndrome (FS) in hospitalized patients diagnosed with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF).
34945259.eligibility: Hospitalized patients diagnosed with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF).
34945259.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low risk of FS, with a negative predictive value of 94%.
- Score 3-4: Intermediate risk of FS.
- Score 5: High risk of FS, with positive and negative predictive values of 83% and 72% respectively, and a specificity of 97%.
34945259.utility: The calculator has good positive and negative predictive values, with a specificity of 97% for a score of 5.

34953045.title: Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma Risk Score Model
34953045.code:
def compute_risk_score(total_tumor_volume, ebv_dna, lactate_dehydrogenase):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with stage II nasopharyngeal carcinoma.

    Parameters:
    total_tumor_volume (bool): True if the total tumor volume is a risk factor, False otherwise.
    ebv_dna (bool): True if the Epstein-Barr virus DNA is a risk factor, False otherwise.
    lactate_dehydrogenase (bool): True if the lactate dehydrogenase is a risk factor, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    risk_score (str): 'high' if one or more risk factors are present, 'low' otherwise.
    """
    risk_factors = [total_tumor_volume, ebv_dna, lactate_dehydrogenase]
    if any(risk_factors):
        return 'high'
    else:
        return 'low'
34953045.purpose: This calculator is used to guide individualized concurrent chemoradiotherapy (CCRT) for patients with stage II nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC) in the intensity-modulated radiotherapy (IMRT) era.
34953045.eligibility: Patients with stage II nasopharyngeal carcinoma who are undergoing or considering concurrent chemoradiotherapy (CCRT) or intensity-modulated radiotherapy (IMRT) alone.
34953045.interpretation:
- 'low': Low-risk patients have no risk factors present. There is no significant survival difference between CCRT and IMRT alone for these patients.
- 'high': High-risk patients have one or more risk factors present. These patients have better survival rates with CCRT than with IMRT alone.
34953045.utility: In the high-risk group, CCRT had better survival rates than IMRT alone (5-year FFS: 82.6% vs. 74.0%, p = 0.028). However, there was no survival difference between CCRT and IMRT alone either in the whole training cohort (p = 0.15) or in the low-risk group (p = 0.15).

34955370.title: Kagoshima-DVT Score
34955370.code:
def compute_kagoshima_dvt_score(d_dimer, age, sex, glucocorticoid_therapy, immobility, cancer):
    """
    Compute the Kagoshima-DVT Score based on the given criteria.

    Parameters:
    d_dimer (float): D-dimer level in μg/mL.
    age (int): Age in years.
    sex (str): Sex of the patient. Either 'male' or 'female'.
    glucocorticoid_therapy (bool): Whether the patient is undergoing glucocorticoid therapy.
    immobility (bool): Whether the patient has prolonged immobility.
    cancer (bool): Whether the patient has cancer with high risk of DVT.

    Returns:
    int: The Kagoshima-DVT Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if d_dimer >= 1.5:
        score += 2
    if age >= 60:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'female':
        score += 1
    if glucocorticoid_therapy:
        score += 1
    if immobility:
        score += 1
    if cancer:
        score += 1
    return score
34955370.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of deep vein thrombosis (DVT) before surgery.
34955370.eligibility: Patients who are about to undergo surgery and have a preoperative D-dimer cut-off value of ≥ 1 μg/ml.
34955370.interpretation:
- Score = 0-2: Low probability of DVT (7% in the derivation cohort and 6% in the validation cohort)
- Score = 3-4: Intermediate probability of DVT (23% in the derivation cohort and 22% in the validation cohort)
- Score ≥ 5: High probability of DVT (47% in the derivation cohort and 50% in the validation cohort)
34955370.utility: The area under the curve of the Kagoshima-DVT Score was 0.72 in the derivation cohort and 0.70 in the validation cohort.

34962479.title: Atrial Fibrillation Recurrence Risk Calculator
34962479.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, af_duration, circ_81906_RYR2):
    """
    Compute the risk score for atrial fibrillation recurrence.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    af_duration (int): Duration of atrial fibrillation in months.
    circ_81906_RYR2 (float): Level of circ 81906-RYR2.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return 1.072 * age + 1.007 * af_duration + 2.210 * circ_81906_RYR2
34962479.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of atrial fibrillation (AF) recurrence after surgical ablation in patients with valvular atrial fibrillation.
34962479.eligibility: Patients who have undergone surgical ablation for valvular atrial fibrillation are eligible.
34962479.interpretation:
- Risk score less than 70: Lower risk of AF recurrence.
- Risk score equal to or greater than 70: Higher risk of AF recurrence.
34962479.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for this risk score is 0.77, indicating a good predictive performance. The cut-off value for the risk score is 70 points.

34966666.title: Ferroptosis-Related Gene (FRG) Prognostic Signature Calculator for Advanced Bladder Urothelial Carcinoma
34966666.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expression, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on gene expression and coefficients.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (list): A list of expression levels of the 8 DEGs.
    coefficients (list): A list of coefficients for the 8 DEGs obtained from LASSO regression analysis.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return sum([gene_expression[i] * coefficients[i] for i in range(8)])
34966666.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with advanced Bladder Urothelial Carcinoma (BLCA) based on the expression of Ferroptosis-Related Genes (FRGs).
34966666.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with advanced Bladder Urothelial Carcinoma (BLCA) are eligible.
34966666.interpretation:
- Low risk: Patients with a risk score lower than the median risk score have a higher overall survival (OS) rate.
- High risk: Patients with a risk score higher than the median risk score have a lower overall survival (OS) rate.
34966666.utility: The risk score was determined as an independent predictor of OS by the multivariate Cox regression analysis (Hazard ratio > 1, 95% CI = 1.724-2.943, P < 0.05).

34967427.title: AIMS65 Score for Upper Gastrointestinal Bleeding in Elderly
34967427.code:
def calculate_AIMS65(albumin, INR, mental_status, systolic_BP, age):
    """
    Calculate AIMS65 score.

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/dL. 1 point if <3.0, else 0.
    INR (float): INR level. 1 point if >1.5, else 0.
    mental_status (bool): Altered mental status. 1 point if True, else 0.
    systolic_BP (int): Systolic blood pressure in mm Hg. 1 point if <90, else 0.
    age (int): Age in years. 1 point if >65, else 0.

    Returns:
    int: AIMS65 score.
    """
    score = 0
    if albumin < 3.0: score += 1
    if INR > 1.5: score += 1
    if mental_status: score += 1
    if systolic_BP < 90: score += 1
    if age > 65: score += 1
    return score
34967427.purpose: This calculator is used to predict 30-day mortality in elderly patients with non-variceal upper gastrointestinal bleeding (UGIB).
34967427.eligibility: Patients who are 80 years of age or older and present with non-variceal upper gastrointestinal bleeding.
34967427.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low risk of 30-day mortality
- Score 3-5: High risk of 30-day mortality
34967427.utility: The AIMS65 score showed good performance in predicting 30-day mortality with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.877. The sensitivity and specificity for mortality at a cutoff of 3 were 0.87 and 0.80, respectively. The negative predictive value (NPV) was 0.977 and the positive predictive value (PPV) was 0.392.

34969274.title: After Vertebral Augmentation (AVA) Score Calculator
34969274.code:
def compute_ava_score(intravertebral_instability, focal_kyphosis, symptom_duration, intravertebral_cleft, previous_fracture):
    """
    Compute the AVA score based on preoperative measures.

    Args:
    intravertebral_instability (bool): True if intravertebral instability is >= 5 mm, False otherwise.
    focal_kyphosis (bool): True if focal kyphosis is >= 10°, False otherwise.
    symptom_duration (bool): True if duration of symptoms is >= 30 days, False otherwise.
    intravertebral_cleft (bool): True if there is an intravertebral cleft, False otherwise.
    previous_fracture (bool): True if there is a previous history of vertebral fracture, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The AVA score, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    return int(intravertebral_instability) + int(focal_kyphosis) + int(symptom_duration) + int(intravertebral_cleft) + int(previous_fracture)
34969274.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of acute adjacent vertebral fracture (AVF) after vertebral augmentation surgery.
34969274.eligibility: Patients who are about to undergo vertebral augmentation surgery are eligible for this calculator.
34969274.interpretation:
- AVA score ≤ 1: Low risk of AVF, with only 3% of patients having AVF.
- AVA score ≥ 4: High risk of AVF, with 58% of patients having AVF.
34969274.utility: The AVA score has a corrected AUC of 0.77, indicating a good predictive performance. It has a sensitivity of 92.5% and specificity of 45.6% for low risk, and a sensitivity of 41.5% and specificity of 94.5% for high risk.

34969534.title: Laparoscopic Hysterectomy Readmission Score (LHRS)
34969534.code:
def compute_lhrs(diabetes, copd, cancer, steroid_use, bleeding_disorder, surgery_length, postop_complication):
    """
    Compute the Laparoscopic Hysterectomy Readmission Score (LHRS).

    Parameters:
    diabetes (bool): True if the patient has diabetes, False otherwise.
    copd (bool): True if the patient has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, False otherwise.
    cancer (bool): True if the patient has disseminated cancer, False otherwise.
    steroid_use (bool): True if the patient uses chronic steroids, False otherwise.
    bleeding_disorder (bool): True if the patient has a bleeding disorder, False otherwise.
    surgery_length (bool): True if the surgery length is longer than average, False otherwise.
    postop_complication (bool): True if the patient had any postoperative complication prior to discharge, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The LHRS, ranging from 0 to 7.
    """
    return diabetes + copd + cancer + steroid_use + bleeding_disorder + surgery_length + postop_complication
34969534.purpose: The LHRS is used to identify patients for whom same day discharge (SDD) after minimally invasive hysterectomy (MIH) may not be advisable. It is also used to predict the risk of readmission in a gynecologic oncology population.
34969534.eligibility: Patients undergoing robotic hysterectomy by a gynecologic oncologist are eligible.
34969534.interpretation:
- Score 1: 4.9% risk of readmission
- Score 2: 7.8% risk of readmission
- Score 3: 13.6% risk of readmission
- Score 4: 16.7% risk of readmission
- Score ≥3: Increased risk of readmission (odds ratio 4.20)
34969534.utility: Patients with a LHRS of ≥3 had higher odds of readmission compared to those with a lower score (OR 4.20, p = 0.02).

34979937.title: Composite Risk Index for Postoperative Complications in Older Patients
34979937.code:
def compute_risk_index(modified_frailty_index, malnutrition, hemoglobin, albumin):
    """
    Compute the composite risk index based on four frailty deficits.
    
    Args:
    modified_frailty_index (float): The modified frailty index of the patient. Score 1 if >=0.27, else 0.
    malnutrition (bool): Whether the patient is malnourished. Score 1 if True, else 0.
    hemoglobin (float): The hemoglobin level of the patient. Score 1 if <90 g/L, else 0.
    albumin (float): The albumin level of the patient. Score 1 if <=30 g/L, else 0.
    
    Returns:
    int: The composite risk index.
    """
    mfi_score = 1 if modified_frailty_index >= 0.27 else 0
    malnutrition_score = 1 if malnutrition else 0
    hemoglobin_score = 1 if hemoglobin < 90 else 0
    albumin_score = 1 if albumin <= 30 else 0
    
    return mfi_score + malnutrition_score + hemoglobin_score + albumin_score
34979937.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of postoperative complications in older patients recovering from elective digestive tract surgery based on preoperative frailty deficits.
34979937.eligibility: Patients aged 65 years or older who are undergoing elective digestive tract surgery.
34979937.interpretation:
- Composite Risk Index of 0: Baseline risk
- Composite Risk Index of 1: 2.408 times the baseline risk
- Composite Risk Index of 2: 3.235 times the baseline risk
- Composite Risk Index of 3 or above: 9.227 times the baseline risk
34979937.utility: The area under the receiver-operator characteristic curve (AUC) to predict postoperative complications was 0.653 for the composite risk index, indicating a moderate predictive power.

34981310.title: Aspartate Aminotransferase-to-Platelet Ratio Index (APRI) Calculator
34981310.code:
def calculate_apri(ast_level, platelet_count, ast_upper_limit=40):
    """
    Calculate the APRI score.

    Parameters:
    ast_level (float): Aspartate Aminotransferase Level in IU/L.
    platelet_count (float): Platelet Count in 10^9/L.
    ast_upper_limit (float): Upper Limit of Normal for Aspartate Aminotransferase. Default is 40 IU/L.

    Returns:
    float: APRI score.
    """
    return (ast_level / ast_upper_limit) / platelet_count * 100
34981310.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of postoperative liver failure and perioperative bleeding in patients who have undergone hepatectomy for colorectal metastases and received preoperative chemotherapy.
34981310.eligibility: Patients who have received preoperative chemotherapy and underwent hepatectomy for colorectal metastases.
34981310.interpretation:
- APRI < 0.365: Lower risk of postoperative liver failure and perioperative bleeding.
- APRI ≥ 0.365: Higher risk of postoperative liver failure (Odds Ratio 2.51, 95% CI 1.67-3.77) and perioperative bleeding requiring transfusions (Odds Ratio 1.41, 95% CI 1.13-1.77).
34981310.utility: The APRI score was found to be a better predictor of postoperative liver failure than the MELD score, with a statistically significant larger area under the curve.

34987612.title: Tumor Burden Score (TBS) Calculator for Conversion Outcome in Colorectal Liver Metastases
34987612.code:
def calculate_tbs(tumor_burden):
    """
    Calculate the Tumor Burden Score (TBS) based on the tumor burden.

    Parameters:
    tumor_burden (float): The tumor burden in the patient.

    Returns:
    str: 'High' if TBS > 14.3, 'Low' if TBS <= 14.3
    """
    if tumor_burden > 14.3:
        return 'High'
    else:
        return 'Low'
34987612.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the conversion outcome in patients with initially unresectable colorectal liver metastases (CRLM) after first-line systemic therapy.
34987612.eligibility: Patients with initially unresectable colorectal liver metastases (CRLM) who are undergoing first-line systemic therapy.
34987612.interpretation:
- High TBS: Associated with adverse chemotherapy response and conversion outcome. The conversion rate is 19.0%. High TBS is also associated with worse overall survival.
- Low TBS: Associated with better chemotherapy response and conversion outcome. The conversion rate is 57.3%.
34987612.utility: The TBS showed good discriminatory power with an area under curve (AUC) of 0.726.

34997639.title: Osteosarcoma Prognostic Risk Score Calculator
34997639.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expression, risk_coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on gene expression levels and risk coefficients.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (dict): A dictionary where keys are gene names and values are their expression levels.
    risk_coefficients (dict): A dictionary where keys are gene names and values are their risk coefficients.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = sum(gene_expression[gene] * risk_coefficients[gene] for gene in gene_expression)
    return risk_score
34997639.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival of patients with osteosarcoma based on glycolysis-related genes.
34997639.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with osteosarcoma are eligible.
34997639.interpretation:
- High-risk group: Patients with a risk score higher than the median risk score have a significantly shorter overall survival.
- Low-risk group: Patients with a risk score lower than or equal to the median risk score have a longer overall survival.
34997639.utility: The C indices of the training set and the verification set were 0.882 and 0.828, respectively, indicating a good predictive performance of the risk score.

35002261.title: NABY Score Calculator for Urinary Tract Infections
35002261.code:
def compute_naby_score(positive_nitrite, antibiotic_exposure, numerous_bacteria, yeast_in_urine):
    """
    Compute the NABY score based on the given factors.

    Args:
    positive_nitrite (bool): Whether the patient has positive nitrite.
    antibiotic_exposure (bool): Whether the patient has had antibiotic exposure within 30 days.
    numerous_bacteria (bool): Whether the patient has numerous bacteria in urine.
    yeast_in_urine (str): The amount of yeast in urine, can be 'few', 'moderate', or 'none'.

    Returns:
    int: The NABY score.
    """
    score = 0
    if positive_nitrite:
        score += 1
    if antibiotic_exposure:
        score -= 2
    if numerous_bacteria:
        score += 2
    if yeast_in_urine == 'few':
        score -= 2
    elif yeast_in_urine == 'moderate':
        score -= 5
    return score
35002261.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of bacteriuria, candiduria, and no-growth urine in patients with urinary tract infections (UTIs), based on urinalysis and recent antibiotic exposure.
35002261.eligibility: Patients with urinary tract infections who have undergone urinalysis and urine culture are eligible.
35002261.interpretation:
- A score ≥0 predicts 76% and 54% of bacteriuria in the derivation and validation cohorts, respectively.
- A score ≤-3 predicts 96% of candiduria in both cohorts.
35002261.utility: The concordance index (c-index) was 0.85 in the derivation cohort and 0.82 in the validation cohort, indicating good predictive accuracy.

35002286.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Predicting Contrast-Induced Acute Kidney Injury (CI-AKI) After Primary Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI)
35002286.code:
def calculate_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(C, H, A2, D, S2, V, A, Sc):
    """
    Calculate CHA2DS2-VASc score.

    Parameters:
    C (bool): Congestive heart failure
    H (bool): Hypertension
    A2 (bool): Age ≥75 years
    D (bool): Diabetes Mellitus
    S2 (bool): Stroke/TIA/Thromboembolism
    V (bool): Vascular disease
    A (bool): Age 65-74 years
    Sc (bool): Sex category (female)

    Returns:
    int: CHA2DS2-VASc score
    """
    return C + H + 2*A2 + D + 2*S2 + V + A + Sc
35002286.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of CI-AKI after primary PCI.
35002286.eligibility: Adult patients who have undergone primary PCI are eligible.
35002286.interpretation:
- CHA2DS2-VASc score < 2: Lower risk of CI-AKI after primary PCI
- CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥ 2: Higher risk of CI-AKI after primary PCI
35002286.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for the score was 0.725 [0.662 to 0.788] with a sensitivity and specificity of 66.7% [63.1% to 70.2%] and 66.7% [53.7% to 78.1%], respectively, at a cut-off value of ≥2.

35011894.title: COVID-19 Liver Injury Risk Calculator
35011894.code:
import math

def calculate_fib4(age, ast, alt, platelets):
    """
    Calculate the FIB-4 score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years
    ast (float): Aspartate aminotransferase level in U/L
    alt (float): Alanine aminotransferase level in U/L
    platelets (float): Platelet count in 10^9/L

    Returns:
    float: FIB-4 score
    """
    return (age * ast) / (platelets * math.sqrt(alt))
35011894.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of mortality in COVID-19 patients with abnormal liver biochemistry, particularly focusing on the FIB-4 score.
35011894.eligibility: Patients hospitalized with moderate to severe COVID-19, with abnormal liver biochemistry on admission and no prior history of liver disease.
35011894.interpretation:
- FIB-4 score <= 3.25: Lower risk of mortality
- FIB-4 score > 3.25: Higher risk of mortality
35011894.utility: The FIB-4 score had an area under the receiver operating characteristic (AUROC) of 0.73 for predicting survival, indicating a moderate predictive ability.

35015124.title: TIPS Procedure Risk Score Calculator
35015124.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, child_pugh_score, tips_dep):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient based on their age, Child-Pugh score, and TIPS-DEP.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    child_pugh_score (str): The Child-Pugh score of the patient. Should be 'A', 'B', or 'C'.
    tips_dep (float): The TIPS-DEP of the patient in mm.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 60:
        score += 1
    if child_pugh_score in ['B', 'C']:
        score += 1
    if tips_dep >= 6:
        score += 1
    return score
35015124.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of death or recurrence of portal hypertension symptoms after a transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt (TIPS) procedure.
35015124.eligibility: Patients who are cirrhotic with portal hypertension and have undergone a TIPS procedure are eligible.
35015124.interpretation:
- Score < 2: Low risk of death or recurrence of portal hypertension symptoms after TIPS procedure.
- Score ≥ 2: High risk of death or recurrence of portal hypertension symptoms after TIPS procedure.
35015124.utility: The hazard ratio between patients determined to be low-risk (< 2 points) and high-risk (≥ 2 points) was 2.30 [1.35-3.93] (p = 0.002) in the training set and 2.01 [0.94-4.32] (p = 0.072) in the validation set.

35024923.title: Modified Frailty Index (mFI) for Risk Assessment in Elderly Patients Undergoing Distal Pancreatectomy
35024923.code:
def compute_mFI(frailty_status):
    """
    Compute the Modified Frailty Index (mFI) based on the frailty status.

    Args:
    frailty_status (str): The frailty status of the patient. It should be one of the following: 'non-frail', 'mildly frail', 'severely frail'.

    Returns:
    float: The mFI score.
    """
    if frailty_status == 'non-frail':
        return 0
    elif frailty_status == 'mildly frail':
        return 1/2
    else: # 'severely frail'
        return 3
35024923.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the surgical course and potential complications in elderly patients undergoing distal pancreatectomy.
35024923.eligibility: Patients aged 70 years or older who are undergoing distal pancreatectomy.
35024923.interpretation:
- mFI = 0: The patient is non-frail and has the lowest risk of postoperative complications.
- mFI = 1/2: The patient is mildly frail and has a moderate risk of postoperative complications.
- mFI = 3: The patient is severely frail and has the highest risk of postoperative complications.
35024923.utility: The mFI was found to be a significant predictor of postoperative complications, including abdominal collection, pulmonary complication, postoperative confusion, Clavien-Dindo severity ≥3 events, and length of stay.

35027286.title: Oligometastatic Renal Cancer Survival Risk Calculator
35027286.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the survival of oligometastatic kidney cancer patients treated with metastases-directed Stereotactic Radiotherapy (SRT) based on patient's age, site of disease, and presence of bone disease.
35027286.eligibility: Patients with oligometastatic kidney cancer who are treated with Stereotactic Radiotherapy (SRT) on up to five metastases are eligible.
35027286.interpretation:
- Class 1: Patients aged ≤ 65 years treated on extracranial metastases, with 3-year overall survival of 82.66%.
- Class 2: Patients aged > 65 years, without history of metastatic bone disease, treated on extracranial metastases, with a 3-year overall survival of 67.91%.
- Class 3: Patients aged > 65 years and a history of bone disease, treated on extracranial metastases, with a 3-year overall survival of 37.50%.
- Class 4: Patients treated on brain metastases, with a 3-year overall survival of 9.70%.
35027286.utility: The clinical utility of the risk score is not provided in the article.

35041626.title: Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) Index for Predicting Major Adverse Cardiovascular Events in Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease
35041626.code:
def compute_fib4(age, ast, platelets, alt):
    """
    Compute the FIB-4 index.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age in years
    ast (float): AST level in U/L
    platelets (float): Platelet count in 10^9/L
    alt (float): ALT level in U/L

    Returns:
    float: FIB-4 index
    """
    return age * ast / (platelets * (alt ** 0.5))
35041626.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE) in patients with nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), or at risk of NASH.
35041626.eligibility: Patients with a diagnosis of NAFLD, NASH, or at risk of NASH are eligible.
35041626.interpretation:
- FIB-4 < 2.67: Lower risk of major adverse cardiovascular events
- FIB-4 ≥ 2.67: Higher risk of major adverse cardiovascular events
35041626.utility: The FIB-4 index was found to be the strongest independent predictor of MACE, beyond established cardiovascular risk factors and baseline liver diagnosis. The hazard ratio for MACE overall was 1.80 (95% CI 1.61-2.02, P < 0.001).

35048648.title: Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) Index for COVID-19 Mortality Risk
35048648.code:
import math

def calculate_fib4(age, ast, platelet_count, alt):
    """
    Calculate the FIB-4 index.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    ast (float): AST level in IU/L.
    platelet_count (float): Platelet count in 109/L.
    alt (float): ALT level in U/L.

    Returns:
    float: The calculated FIB-4 index.
    """
    fib4 = (age * ast) / (platelet_count * math.sqrt(alt))
    return fib4
35048648.purpose: This calculator is used to evaluate the mortality risk in COVID-19 patients based on the Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4) index.
35048648.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with COVID-19 are eligible for this calculator.
35048648.interpretation:
- High FIB-4: A high FIB-4 index is associated with an increased mortality risk. The post-test probability of mortality is 54%.
- Low FIB-4: A low FIB-4 index is associated with a decreased mortality risk. The post-test probability of mortality is 19%.
35048648.utility: The FIB-4 index has a sensitivity of 0.56, specificity of 0.80, positive likelihood ratio (PLR) of 2.8, negative likelihood ratio (NLR) of 0.55, diagnostic odds ratio (DOR) of 5, and area under the curve (AUC) of 0.77.

35048758.title: APA Scoring System for Recurrent Spontaneous Abortion (RSA)
35048758.code:
def compute_risk_score(antinuclear_antibody, protein_s_deficiency, antiphospholipid_antibodies):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the presence of three risk factors.
    
    Args:
    antinuclear_antibody (bool): True if the patient has antinuclear antibody spectrum, False otherwise.
    protein_s_deficiency (bool): True if the patient has protein S deficiency, False otherwise.
    antiphospholipid_antibodies (bool): True if the patient has antiphospholipid antibodies, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(antinuclear_antibody) + int(protein_s_deficiency) + int(antiphospholipid_antibodies)
35048758.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of pregnancy loss in patients with recurrent spontaneous abortion (RSA) based on three risk factors: antinuclear antibody spectrum, protein S deficiency, and antiphospholipid antibodies.
35048758.eligibility: Patients with recurrent spontaneous abortion (RSA) are eligible for this calculator.
35048758.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk category with a pregnancy loss rate of 6.3%
- Score 1: Intermediate risk category with a pregnancy loss rate of 24.6%
- Score 2-3: High risk category with a pregnancy loss rate of 50%
35048758.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) for the score of RSA was .733, indicating a good predictive performance.

35063478.title: PRECISE-DAPT Score Calculator
35063478.code:
def compute_precise_dapt_score(score):
    """
    Compute the PRECISE-DAPT score.
    
    Args:
    score (int): The PRECISE-DAPT score of the patient.

    Returns:
    str: The risk level of the patient.
    """
    if score >= 25:
        return "Very High Risk"
    else:
        return "Not Very High Risk"
35063478.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the bleeding risk in patients treated with dual antiplatelet treatment after Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI).
35063478.eligibility: Patients who have undergone PCI and are on dual antiplatelet treatment.
35063478.interpretation:
- Score < 25: Not Very High Risk
- Score >= 25: Very High Risk
35063478.utility: The PRECISE-DAPT score as a continuous variable had low to moderate ability to predict bleeding events BARC 2, 3 or 5 (c-statistics 0.58 [95% CI, 0.52-0.61]); moderate at BARC 3 or 5 (c-statistics 0.72 [95% CI, 0.64-0.78]), and poor for MACE (c-statistics 0.49 [95% CI, 0,45-0.51]).

35064161.title: PCR Index for Predicting 3-Month Mortality in AE-IPF Patients
35064161.code:
def calculate_pcr_index(PaO2_FiO2, CRP, HRCT):
    """
    Calculate the PCR Index for predicting 3-month mortality in AE-IPF patients.

    Parameters:
    PaO2_FiO2 (float): The ratio of partial pressure arterial oxygen and fraction of inspired oxygen.
    CRP (float): The level of C-reactive protein in the blood.
    HRCT (bool): Whether the patient has a diffuse HRCT pattern.

    Returns:
    int: The PCR Index score.
    """
    P = 1 if PaO2_FiO2 < 250 else 0
    C = 1 if CRP >= 5.5 else 0
    R = 1 if HRCT else 0

    return P + C + R
35064161.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 3-month mortality risk in patients with acute exacerbation of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (AE-IPF).
35064161.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with acute exacerbation of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (AE-IPF).
35064161.interpretation:
The PCR Index score corresponds to the following 3-month mortality risks:

- Score 0: 7.7% risk
- Score 1: 29.4% risk
- Score 2: 54.8% risk
- Score 3: 80% risk
35064161.utility: The PCR Index has an area under the ROC curve of 0.7686, indicating good predictive performance. The sensitivity of the scoring system is 78.6% and specificity is 67.8%.

35071119.title: Liver Transplantation Prognosis Calculator for Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) Patients
35071119.code:
def calculate_risk(sPD1, ADV):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on sPD-1 concentration and ADV score.

    Parameters:
    sPD1 (float): Pretransplant serum soluble PD-1 concentration in µg/mL.
    ADV (float): α-FP-des-γ-carboxyprothrombin-tumor volume score in log.

    Returns:
    int: Risk score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    risk_score = 0
    if sPD1 > 177.1:
        risk_score += 1
    if ADV > 5.4:
        risk_score += 1
    return risk_score
35071119.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with untreated HCC who have undergone liver transplantation, based on pretransplant serum soluble PD-1 (sPD-1) concentration and α-FP-des-γ-carboxyprothrombin-tumor volume (ADV) score.
35071119.eligibility: Patients with previously untreated hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) who have undergone living donor liver transplantation.
35071119.interpretation:
- Risk Score 0: Lowest risk of tumor recurrence and highest overall patient survival rate.
- Risk Score 1: Moderate risk of tumor recurrence and moderate overall patient survival rate.
- Risk Score 2: Highest risk of tumor recurrence and lowest overall patient survival rate.
35071119.utility: The combination of sPD-1 concentration and ADV score was found to be significantly associated with the prognosis of tumor recurrence (P < 0.001) and overall patient survival (P = 0.006).

35076058.title: ICU Admission Risk Score Following Lung Resection
35076058.code:
def compute_risk_score(gender, bmi, fev1, dlco, open_access, pneumonectomy):
    """
    Compute the ICU admission risk score following lung resection.

    Parameters:
    gender (str): 'male' or 'female'
    bmi (float): body mass index
    fev1 (float): predicted postoperative forced expiratory volume in 1s
    dlco (float): predicted postoperative carbon monoxide lung diffusion capacity
    open_access (bool): whether the surgery was open access
    pneumonectomy (bool): whether the surgery was a pneumonectomy

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 7.
    """
    score = 0
    if gender == 'male':
        score += 1
    if bmi < 18.5:
        score += 2
    if fev1 < 60:
        score += 1
    if dlco < 50:
        score += 1
    if open_access:
        score += 1
    if pneumonectomy:
        score += 1
    return score
35076058.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of an unplanned admission to the intensive care unit (ICU) following lung resection.
35076058.eligibility: Patients undergoing anatomical lung resections are eligible for this risk score calculation.
35076058.interpretation:
- Score 0 (Class A): 0.7% risk of unplanned ICU admission
- Score 1 (Class B): 1.7% risk
- Score 2 (Class C): 3% risk
- Score 3 (Class D): 7.1% risk
- Score 4 (Class E): 12% risk
- Score >4 (Class F): 13% risk
35076058.utility: The risk score was validated using bootstrap resampling technique and showed a significant association with unplanned ICU admission (P < 0.001).

35078439.title: Tumour Size Ratio (TSR) Risk Calculator for Liver Recurrence-Free Survival
35078439.code:
def compute_tsr(largest_lesion_diameter, smallest_lesion_diameter):
    """
    Compute the Tumour Size Ratio (TSR).
    
    Parameters:
    largest_lesion_diameter (float): The diameter of the largest liver lesion.
    smallest_lesion_diameter (float): The diameter of the smallest liver lesion.
    
    Returns:
    float: The Tumour Size Ratio (TSR).
    """
    return largest_lesion_diameter / smallest_lesion_diameter
35078439.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the impact of size differences of multiple liver metastases on liver recurrence-free survival (RFS) in patients undergoing hepatic resection for colorectal liver metastases (CRLMs).
35078439.eligibility: Patients with colorectal liver metastases (CRLMs) who are undergoing hepatic resection.
35078439.interpretation:
- TSR < 2: High risk of liver recurrence-free survival (RFS). Hazard ratio (HR) is 4.435.
- 2 ≤ TSR < 4: Moderate risk of liver recurrence-free survival (RFS). Hazard ratio (HR) is 2.580.
- TSR ≥ 4: Low risk of liver recurrence-free survival (RFS).
35078439.utility: The predictive ability of the TSR was found to be superior to that of the Fong clinical score in the study. As TSR decreased, liver RFS worsened.

35081257.title: Grade 4 Bleeding Risk Calculator for AML Patients
35081257.code:
def compute_risk_score(platelet_count, pt_inr):
    """
    Compute the risk score for grade 4 bleeding in AML patients.

    Parameters:
    platelet_count (float): Baseline platelet count (in 10^9/L).
    pt_inr (float): Baseline international normalized ratio of prothrombin time.

    Returns:
    risk_score (int): The risk score for grade 4 bleeding.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    if platelet_count <= 40:
        risk_score += 1
    if pt_inr > 1.5 or (pt_inr > 1.3 and pt_inr <= 1.5):
        risk_score += 1
    return risk_score
35081257.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of grade 4 bleeding in patients with acute myeloid leukemia (AML) receiving intensive induction chemotherapy.
35081257.eligibility: Patients with acute myeloid leukemia (AML) who are about to receive or are receiving intensive induction chemotherapy.
35081257.interpretation:
- Risk Score 0: Low risk of grade 4 bleeding. Cumulative incidence at day+60 is 2% in the development cohort and 7% in the validation cohort.
- Risk Score 1 or 2: High risk of grade 4 bleeding. Cumulative incidence at day+60 is 31% in the development cohort and 25% in the validation cohort.
35081257.utility: The risk score was able to stratify patients into low- and high-risk groups for grade 4 bleeding with significant differences in cumulative incidence at day+60 (P < .001 in the development cohort and P = .008 in the validation cohort).

35084429.title: Vertebral Compression Fracture (VCF) Risk Score After Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT) for Spinal Metastases
35084429.code:
def compute_risk_score(epidural_tumor_extension, lumbar_location, gross_tumor_volume, spinal_instability_neoplastic_score):
    """
    Compute the risk score for VCF after SBRT for spinal metastases.

    Parameters:
    epidural_tumor_extension (bool): True if there is epidural tumor extension, False otherwise.
    lumbar_location (bool): True if the tumor is located in the lumbar spine, False otherwise.
    gross_tumor_volume (bool): True if the gross tumor volume is more than 10 cc, False otherwise.
    spinal_instability_neoplastic_score (bool): True if the spinal instability neoplastic score is more than 6, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    return int(epidural_tumor_extension) + int(lumbar_location) + int(gross_tumor_volume) + int(spinal_instability_neoplastic_score)
35084429.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of VCF after SBRT for spinal metastases.
35084429.eligibility: Patients who have undergone SBRT treatments for spinal metastases are eligible.
35084429.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk, with a cumulative incidence of VCF at 2 years of 6.7%.
- Score 2: Intermediate risk, with a cumulative incidence of VCF at 2 years of 17.0%.
- Score 3-4: High risk, with a cumulative incidence of VCF at 2 years of 35.4%.
35084429.utility: The multivariable Cox model had a concordance of 0.760. The hazard ratio per 1-point increase for VCF was 1.93 (95% CI, 1.62-2.30; P < .001).

35086786.title: Early Sigmoid Volvulus Recurrence Risk Score
35086786.code:
def compute_risk_score(left_lateral_section_volume, maximal_colon_distension):
    """
    Compute the risk score for early SV recurrence.

    Args:
    left_lateral_section_volume (float): The volume of the left lateral section in cm3.
    maximal_colon_distension (float): The maximal distension of the colon in cm.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 2.
    """
    score = 0
    if left_lateral_section_volume < 150:
        score += 1
    if maximal_colon_distension > 10:
        score += 1
    return score
35086786.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of early recurrence of sigmoid volvulus (SV) after a first uncomplicated episode based on computed tomography (CT) features.
35086786.eligibility: Patients who have undergone abdominal CT examination for a first uncomplicated SV episode.
35086786.interpretation:
- Score 0: 26% risk of early SV recurrence
- Score 1: 54% risk of early SV recurrence
- Score 2: 89% risk of early SV recurrence
35086786.utility: The risk score was found to be significantly associated with early SV recurrence (P < 0.001).

35093039.title: Flu-IV Score Calculator
35093039.code:
def calculate_flu_iv_score(early_neuraminidase_inhibitor_use, lymphocytes, multi_lobar_infiltrates, systemic_corticosteroid_use, age, pao2_fio2, respiratory_rate, arterial_ph):
    """
    Calculate the Flu-IV score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    early_neuraminidase_inhibitor_use (bool): Whether the patient used early neuraminidase inhibitor.
    lymphocytes (float): The lymphocyte count in x 10^9/L.
    multi_lobar_infiltrates (bool): Whether the patient has multi-lobar infiltrates.
    systemic_corticosteroid_use (bool): Whether the patient used systemic corticosteroids.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    pao2_fio2 (float): The ratio of partial pressure arterial oxygen and fraction of inspired oxygen.
    respiratory_rate (int): The respiratory rate in breaths per minute.
    arterial_ph (float): The arterial PH.

    Returns:
    int: The Flu-IV score.
    """
    score = 0
    if early_neuraminidase_inhibitor_use:
        score -= 3
    if lymphocytes < 0.8:
        score += 1
    if multi_lobar_infiltrates:
        score += 1
    if systemic_corticosteroid_use:
        score += 1
    if age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if pao2_fio2 < 300:
        score += 2
    if respiratory_rate >= 30:
        score += 3
    if arterial_ph < 7.35:
        score += 4
    return score
35093039.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of invasive mechanical ventilation (IMV) in patients with influenza-related pneumonia (Flu-p) within 14 days of admission.
35093039.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with influenza-related pneumonia are eligible.
35093039.interpretation:
- Score < 5: Low risk of requiring IMV
- Score ≥ 5: High risk of requiring IMV
35093039.utility: The Flu-IV score has a sensitivity of 85.5% and a specificity of 88.8%. It has a better predictive performance than the ROX index (AUROC = 0.909 vs. 0.594), modified ROX index (AUROC = 0.909 vs. 0.633), and HACOR scale (AUROC = 0.909 vs. 0.622).

35093068.title: Lung Adenocarcinoma Prognostic Model
35093068.code:
def compute_risk_score(FRLs, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient based on their FRLs and the coefficients from the LASSO algorithm.

    Args:
    FRLs (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the names of the FRLs and the values are their expression levels.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the names of the FRLs and the values are their coefficients from the LASSO algorithm.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score for the patient.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    for FRL, expression_level in FRLs.items():
        risk_score += coefficients[FRL] * expression_level
    return risk_score
35093068.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival (OS) of patients with lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) based on prognostic ferroptosis-related long noncoding RNAs (FRLs).
35093068.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with lung adenocarcinoma are eligible for this calculator.
35093068.interpretation:
The risk score is interpreted as follows:

- Low risk: Risk score < median risk score. These patients have a better overall survival prognosis.
- High risk: Risk score >= median risk score. These patients have a worse overall survival prognosis.
35093068.utility: The area under the ROC curve of the prognostic model for 1-year, 3-year, and 5-year overall survival (OS) was 0.763, 0.745, and 0.778 in the training set and 0.716, 0.724, and 0.736 in the validation set, respectively.

35106968.title: 2022 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for Eosinophilic Granulomatosis With Polyangiitis (EGPA) Risk Score Calculator
35106968.code:
def compute_egpa_score(eosinophil_count, airway_disease, nasal_polyps, anca_positivity, eosinophilic_inflammation, neuropathy, hematuria):
    """
    Compute the EGPA risk score based on the given criteria.

    Args:
    eosinophil_count (bool): True if the maximum eosinophil count is ≥1 × 109 /liter, False otherwise.
    airway_disease (bool): True if obstructive airway disease is present, False otherwise.
    nasal_polyps (bool): True if nasal polyps are present, False otherwise.
    anca_positivity (bool): True if cytoplasmic ANCA or anti-proteinase 3 ANCA positivity is present, False otherwise.
    eosinophilic_inflammation (bool): True if extravascular eosinophilic predominant inflammation is present, False otherwise.
    neuropathy (bool): True if mononeuritis multiplex/motor neuropathy not due to radiculopathy is present, False otherwise.
    hematuria (bool): True if hematuria is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    The EGPA risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if eosinophil_count:
        score += 5
    if airway_disease:
        score += 3
    if nasal_polyps:
        score += 3
    if anca_positivity:
        score -= 3
    if eosinophilic_inflammation:
        score += 2
    if neuropathy:
        score += 1
    if hematuria:
        score -= 1
    return score
35106968.purpose: This calculator is used to classify patients with small- or medium-vessel vasculitis as having EGPA based on a set of criteria.
35106968.eligibility: Patients with a diagnosis of small- or medium-vessel vasculitis are eligible.
35106968.interpretation:
- Score < 6: The patient is not classified as having EGPA.
- Score ≥ 6: The patient is classified as having EGPA.
35106968.utility: The sensitivity of the calculator is 85% (95% confidence interval [95% CI] 77-91%) and the specificity is 99% (95% CI 98-100%).

35106973.title: 2022 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for Microscopic Polyangiitis (MPA) Calculator
35106973.code:
def calculate_mpa_score(perinuclear_ANCA, pauci_immune_glomerulonephritis, lung_fibrosis, sino_nasal_symptoms, cytoplasmic_ANCA, eosinophil_count):
    """
    Calculate the MPA score based on the given criteria.

    Args:
    perinuclear_ANCA (bool): True if perinuclear ANCA or anti-myeloperoxidase-ANCA is positive, False otherwise.
    pauci_immune_glomerulonephritis (bool): True if pauci-immune glomerulonephritis is present, False otherwise.
    lung_fibrosis (bool): True if lung fibrosis or interstitial lung disease is present, False otherwise.
    sino_nasal_symptoms (bool): True if sino-nasal symptoms or signs are present, False otherwise.
    cytoplasmic_ANCA (bool): True if cytoplasmic ANCA or anti-proteinase 3 ANCA is positive, False otherwise.
    eosinophil_count (float): The eosinophil count in 1 × 109 /liter.

    Returns:
    int: The calculated MPA score.
    """
    score = 0
    if perinuclear_ANCA:
        score += 6
    if pauci_immune_glomerulonephritis:
        score += 3
    if lung_fibrosis:
        score += 3
    if sino_nasal_symptoms:
        score -= 3
    if cytoplasmic_ANCA:
        score -= 1
    if eosinophil_count >= 1:
        score -= 4
    return score
35106973.purpose: This calculator is used to classify patients with small- or medium-vessel vasculitis as having MPA based on a points-based risk score.
35106973.eligibility: Patients with a diagnosis of small- or medium-vessel vasculitis are eligible.
35106973.interpretation: - A cumulative score of ≥5 points: The patient can be classified as having MPA.
35106973.utility: When tested in the validation data set, the sensitivity was 91% (95% confidence interval [95% CI] 85-95%) and the specificity was 94% (95% CI 92-96%).

35110332.title: 2022 American College of Rheumatology/European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology Classification Criteria for Microscopic Polyangiitis (MPA)
35110332.code:
def calculate_mpa_score(perinuclear_ANCA, pauci_immune_glomerulonephritis, lung_fibrosis, sino_nasal_symptoms, cytoplasmic_ANCA, eosinophil_count):
    """
    Calculate the MPA score based on the given criteria.

    Args:
    perinuclear_ANCA (bool): True if perinuclear ANCA or anti-myeloperoxidase-ANCA is positive, False otherwise.
    pauci_immune_glomerulonephritis (bool): True if pauci-immune glomerulonephritis is present, False otherwise.
    lung_fibrosis (bool): True if lung fibrosis or interstitial lung disease is present, False otherwise.
    sino_nasal_symptoms (bool): True if sino-nasal symptoms or signs are present, False otherwise.
    cytoplasmic_ANCA (bool): True if cytoplasmic ANCA or anti-proteinase 3 ANCA is positive, False otherwise.
    eosinophil_count (float): The eosinophil count in 1×109/L.

    Returns:
    int: The MPA score.
    """
    score = 0
    if perinuclear_ANCA:
        score += 6
    if pauci_immune_glomerulonephritis:
        score += 3
    if lung_fibrosis:
        score += 3
    if sino_nasal_symptoms:
        score -= 3
    if cytoplasmic_ANCA:
        score -= 1
    if eosinophil_count >= 1:
        score -= 4
    return score
35110332.purpose: This calculator is used to classify patients with small- or medium-vessel vasculitis as having MPA based on a points-based risk score.
35110332.eligibility: Patients with a diagnosis of small- or medium-vessel vasculitis are eligible.
35110332.interpretation:
- A cumulative score of less than 5: The patient is not classified as having MPA.
- A cumulative score of 5 or more: The patient can be classified as having MPA.
35110332.utility: The sensitivity of these criteria was 91% (95% CI 85% to 95%) and the specificity was 94% (95% CI 92% to 96%).

35110333.title: 2022 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis (GPA)
35110333.code:
def calculate_gpa_score(criteria):
    """
    Calculate the GPA score based on the given criteria.
    
    Parameters:
    criteria (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the criteria and the values are boolean indicating whether the criteria is present or not.
    
    Returns:
    int: The GPA score.
    """
    weights = {
        'bloody_nasal_discharge': 3,
        'cartilaginous_involvement': 2,
        'hearing_loss': 1,
        'cytoplasmic_ANCA': 5,
        'pulmonary_nodules': 2,
        'granuloma': 2,
        'sinus_inflammation': 1,
        'glomerulonephritis': 1,
        'perinuclear_ANCA': -1,
        'eosinophil_count': -4
    }
    
    score = 0
    for criterion, present in criteria.items():
        if present:
            score += weights[criterion]
    
    return score
35110333.purpose: This calculator is used to classify patients with small- or medium-vessel vasculitis as having GPA based on a set of criteria.
35110333.eligibility: Patients with a diagnosis of small- or medium-vessel vasculitis are eligible.
35110333.interpretation:
- Score < 5: The patient is not classified as having GPA.
- Score >= 5: The patient is classified as having GPA.
35110333.utility: The sensitivity of the calculator is 93% (95% CI 87% to 96%) and the specificity is 94% (95% CI 89% to 97%).

35110334.title: 2022 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria for Eosinophilic Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis (EGPA) Risk Score Calculator
35110334.code:
def compute_egpa_score(eosinophil_count, obstructive_airway_disease, nasal_polyps, anca_positivity, eosinophilic_inflammation, neuropathy, haematuria):
    """
    Compute the EGPA risk score based on the given criteria.

    Args:
    eosinophil_count (bool): True if the patient's maximum eosinophil count is ≥1×109/L, False otherwise.
    obstructive_airway_disease (bool): True if the patient has obstructive airway disease, False otherwise.
    nasal_polyps (bool): True if the patient has nasal polyps, False otherwise.
    anca_positivity (bool): True if the patient is ANCA positive, False otherwise.
    eosinophilic_inflammation (bool): True if the patient has extravascular eosinophilic predominant inflammation, False otherwise.
    neuropathy (bool): True if the patient has mononeuritis multiplex/motor neuropathy not due to radiculopathy, False otherwise.
    haematuria (bool): True if the patient has haematuria, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    The EGPA risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if eosinophil_count:
        score += 5
    if obstructive_airway_disease:
        score += 3
    if nasal_polyps:
        score += 3
    if anca_positivity:
        score -= 3
    if eosinophilic_inflammation:
        score += 2
    if neuropathy:
        score += 1
    if haematuria:
        score -= 1
    return score
35110334.purpose: This calculator is used to classify patients with small- or medium-vessel vasculitis as having EGPA based on a set of criteria.
35110334.eligibility: Patients with a diagnosis of small- or medium-vessel vasculitis are eligible.
35110334.interpretation:
- Score < 6: The patient is not classified as having EGPA.
- Score ≥ 6: The patient is classified as having EGPA.
35110334.utility: The sensitivity of the calculator is 85% (95% CI 77% to 91%) and the specificity is 99% (95% CI 98% to 100%).

35111392.title: Triglycerides × Total Cholesterol × Body Weight Index (TCBI) Calculator
35111392.code:
def calculate_TCBI(triglycerides, total_cholesterol, body_weight):
    """
    Function to calculate the Triglycerides × Total Cholesterol × Body Weight Index (TCBI).
    
    Parameters:
    triglycerides (float): Triglycerides level of the patient in mg/dL.
    total_cholesterol (float): Total cholesterol level of the patient in mg/dL.
    body_weight (float): Body weight of the patient in kg.

    Returns:
    float: The calculated TCBI.
    """
    TCBI = triglycerides * total_cholesterol * body_weight
    return TCBI
35111392.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality risk in patients with dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM) based on their nutritional status.
35111392.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM) are eligible for this calculator.
35111392.interpretation:
- T1 (lowest tertile): High mortality risk (38.9%)
- T2 (middle tertile): Medium mortality risk (35.1%)
- T3 (highest tertile): Low mortality risk (16.9%)
35111392.utility: The TCBI significantly improved risk stratification over the Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index (GNRI) and N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP).

35113971.title: OUR-ARDs Risk Score Calculator for COVID-19 Mortality
35113971.code:
def compute_risk_score(SaO2, Urea, NLR, Age, Pulse_Rate, Diabetes):
    """
    Compute the OUR-ARDs risk score based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    SaO2 (bool): True if SaO2<95%, False otherwise.
    Urea (bool): True if Urea ≥50 mg/dl, False otherwise.
    NLR (bool): True if Neutrophil-lymphocytic ratio (NLR) >3, False otherwise.
    Age (bool): True if Age ≥50 years, False otherwise.
    Pulse_Rate (bool): True if Pulse Rate ≥100/min, False otherwise.
    Diabetes (bool): True if coexisting Diabetes Mellitus, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if SaO2: score += 11
    if Urea: score += 15
    if NLR: score += 11
    if Age: score += 9
    if Pulse_Rate: score += 7
    if Diabetes: score += 6
    return score
35113971.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality risk among hospitalized COVID-19 patients, aiding in prioritizing admission to tertiary care.
35113971.eligibility: Hospitalized patients with confirmed COVID-19 diagnosis are eligible.
35113971.interpretation:
- Risk Score < 25: Lower risk of mortality
- Risk Score ≥ 25: Higher risk of mortality
