    diameter_ratio (float): The ratio of maximum small bowel diameter over vertical abdominal diameter.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for ASBO surgery.
    """
    score = 0
    if charlson_index >= 4:
        score += 1
    if is_distal_obstruction:
        score += 1
    if diameter_ratio > 0.34:
        score += 1
    return score
35717794.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the need for surgery in patients with uncomplicated Adhesive Small Bowel Obstruction (ASBO).
35717794.eligibility: Patients initially managed conservatively for uncomplicated ASBO are eligible.
35717794.interpretation:
- Score of 0 or 1: Low risk of surgery
- Score of 2: Moderate risk of surgery (51.4% in the retrospective analysis and 62.2% in the validation cohort)
- Score of 3: High risk of surgery (70.3% in the retrospective analysis and 75% in the validation cohort)
35717794.utility: The risk score was found to be predictive of the need for surgery in patients with uncomplicated ASBO. A high score (≥2) was predictive of failure of nonoperative management.

35722489.title: IDO2 Risk Analytic Tool
35722489.code:
def compute_ido2_index(ido2_values):
    """
    Compute the average IDO2 index from a list of IDO2 values.
    
    Args:
    ido2_values (list): A list of IDO2 values measured in the 30-min period prior to each SvO2 measurement.
    
    Returns:
    float: The average IDO2 index.
    """
    return sum(ido2_values) / len(ido2_values)
35722489.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk for SvO2 < 40% in pediatric patients admitted in a cardiac intensive care unit (CICU).
35722489.eligibility: Patients aged 1 day to 12 years, weight >2 kg, who received care in the CICU and had SvO2 lab(s) drawn during CICU course.
35722489.interpretation: - Average IDO2 above 75: Highest absolute risk (42.11, CI: 20.25-66.50) and highest RR (4.63, CI: 2.31-9.28) of SvO2 < 40%.
35722489.utility: The AUC of estimating SvO2 < 40% IDO2 was 0.87 [confidence interval (CI): 0.79-0.94].

35726158.title: Prostate Cancer Risk Classification Calculator
35726158.code:
def compute_risk_score(CCL2_level, PSA_level, Gleason_score):
    """
    Compute the risk score for prostate cancer survival.

    Parameters:
    CCL2_level (float): Chemokine (CC motif) ligand 2 level in pg/mL.
    PSA_level (float): Prostate-specific antigen level in ng/mL.
    Gleason_score (int): Gleason score.

    Returns:
    int: Risk score (0-3).
    """
    score = 0
    if CCL2_level >= 320:
        score += 1
    if PSA_level >= 100:
        score += 1
    if Gleason_score >= 8:
        score += 1
    return score
35726158.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the long-term overall survival in prostate cancer patients based on the combined indications of chemokine (CC motif) ligand 2 level, prostate-specific antigen level, and Gleason score.
35726158.eligibility: Patients who have been histologically diagnosed with prostate cancer are eligible.
35726158.interpretation:
- Score 0: Good risk group
- Score 1: Intermediate risk group
- Score 2 or 3: Poor risk group
35726158.utility: The study demonstrated the utility of this risk classification system as a predictive tool for long-term overall survival in prostate cancer. However, specific evaluation results such as AUC, F-score, PPV were not provided in the article.

35728414.title: Oral Cavity Squamous Cell Carcinoma (OCSCC) Salvage Surgery Risk Score
35728414.code:
def compute_risk_score(interval_last_surgery, depth_invasion_last_surgery, nodal_disease_preop):
    """
    Compute the risk score for OCSCC salvage surgery.

    Parameters:
    interval_last_surgery (bool): True if the interval from the last surgery is <= 12 months, False otherwise.
    depth_invasion_last_surgery (bool): True if the depth of invasion of the last surgery > 1 cm, False otherwise.
    nodal_disease_preop (bool): True if there is clinical evidence of nodal disease at the preoperative evaluation, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(interval_last_surgery) + int(depth_invasion_last_surgery) + int(nodal_disease_preop)
35728414.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the life expectancy after salvage surgery (SS) for patients with recurrent or second primary oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma (OCSCC).
35728414.eligibility: Patients with recurrent or second primary oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma (OCSCC) who are considering salvage surgery (SS).
35728414.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low-risk group with a 1-year postsurgery death rate of 10.3%
- Score 2-3: High-risk group with a 1-year postsurgery death rate of 48.6%
35728414.utility: The scoring prediction model was found to be effective in predicting life expectancy after SS for patients with OCSCC, with a significant difference in 1-year postsurgery death rates between the low-risk and high-risk groups (P < 0.001).

35731907.title: AL Amyloidosis Prognostic Score Calculator
35731907.code:
def compute_risk_score(dFLC, BMPC):
    """
    Compute the risk score for AL Amyloidosis Prognostic Score Calculator.

    Parameters:
    dFLC (float): dFLC level in mg/L.
    BMPC (float): BM plasma cells percentage.

    Returns:
    int: risk score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    score = 0
    if dFLC > 180:
        score += 1
    if BMPC > 10:
        score += 1
    return score
35731907.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the event-free survival (EFS) in patients with AL amyloidosis undergoing high-dose melphalan and stem cell transplantation (HDM/SCT).
35731907.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with AL amyloidosis and undergoing HDM/SCT treatment are eligible.
35731907.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low-risk, with a median EFS of 5.3 years.
- Score 1: Intermediate-risk, with a median EFS of 2.8 years.
- Score 2: High-risk, with a median EFS of 1.0 year.
35731907.utility: The prognostic score was significantly associated with EFS (p < .001), indicating its clinical utility in predicting outcomes in patients with AL amyloidosis undergoing HDM/SCT.

35733172.title: Anterior Cruciate Ligament Deficiency (ACLD) Risk Calculator
35733172.code:
def compute_risk_score(MWPPT, HKA, PTS):
    """
    Compute the risk score for Anterior Cruciate Ligament Deficiency (ACLD).
    
    Parameters:
    MWPPT (float): Maximum wear point of the proximal tibia%.
    HKA (float): Hip-knee-ankle angle.
    PTS (float): Posterior tibial slope.
    
    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = -27.147 + (0.342 * MWPPT) + (0.282 * HKA) + (0.684 * PTS)
    return risk_score
35733172.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of Anterior Cruciate Ligament Deficiency (ACLD) in patients with varus knee and osteoarthritis using radiographic measurements.
35733172.eligibility: Patients with varus knee and osteoarthritis who have undergone radiographic measurements including the maximum wear point of the proximal tibia% (MWPPT%), hip-knee-ankle angle (HKA), and posterior tibial slope (PTS).
35733172.interpretation: - Risk Score > 0.1: High risk of ACLD. The patient is likely to have ACLD with a sensitivity of 96.2% and specificity of 79.2%.
35733172.utility: The AUC of the risk score is 0.949 (95%CI: 0.912-0.986), indicating a high diagnostic accuracy.

35734062.title: Occult Lymph Node Metastasis Risk Calculator for Glottic Squamous Cell Carcinoma
35734062.code:
def compute_risk_score(MTD, differentiation, invasive_depth):
    """
    Compute the risk score for occult LNM in GSCC patients.

    Parameters:
    MTD (float): Maximum tumor diameter in cm.
    differentiation (str): Level of differentiation. Can be 'low' or 'high'.
    invasive_depth (float): Tumor invasive depth in cm.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if MTD > 2.0:
        score += 1
    if differentiation == 'low':
        score += 1
    if invasive_depth >= 1.0:
        score += 1
    return score
35734062.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the probability of occult lymph node metastasis (LNM) in patients with Glottic Squamous Cell Carcinoma (GSCC), particularly those in the T3-T4 stages. It helps in devising individualized postoperative radiotherapy strategies.
35734062.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Glottic Squamous Cell Carcinoma, particularly those in the T3-T4 stages, are eligible for this calculator.
35734062.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of LNM, with a possible rate of 4.7%
- Score 2-3: High risk of LNM, with a possible rate of 25.2%
35734062.utility: The accuracy and validity of this model were verified by 0.716 and remained at 0.717 after 1000 bootstrapping. The calibration curve showed a favorable agreement.

35736524.title: COVID-19 Mortality Risk Score Calculator
35736524.code:
def calculate_risk_score(age, barthel_index, altered_consciousness, oxygen_ratio, breath_sounds, platelet_concentration, c_reactive_protein, glomerular_filtration_rate):
    """
    Calculate the COVID-19 Mortality Risk Score based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (bool): True if age is 50 years or older, False otherwise.
    barthel_index (bool): True if Barthel index is 90 points or less, False otherwise.
    altered_consciousness (bool): True if the patient has an altered level of consciousness, False otherwise.
    oxygen_ratio (bool): True if the ratio of arterial oxygen saturation to fraction of inspired oxygen is 400 or less, False otherwise.
    breath_sounds (bool): True if the patient has abnormal breath sounds, False otherwise.
    platelet_concentration (bool): True if platelet concentration is 100 × 109/L or less, False otherwise.
    c_reactive_protein (bool): True if C reactive protein level is 5 mg/dL or more, False otherwise.
    glomerular_filtration_rate (bool): True if glomerular filtration rate is 45 mL/min or less, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The calculated risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age: score += 2
    if barthel_index: score += 1
    if altered_consciousness: score += 1
    if oxygen_ratio: score += 1
    if breath_sounds: score += 1
    if platelet_concentration: score += 1
    if c_reactive_protein: score += 1
    if glomerular_filtration_rate: score += 1
    return score
35736524.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality risk in patients diagnosed with COVID-19.
35736524.eligibility: Patients aged over 18 years diagnosed with COVID-19.
35736524.interpretation:
- Score 4: Low risk, with a 30-day mortality rate of 2.8%
- Score 5-6: Intermediate risk, with a 30-day mortality rate of 22.5%
- Score 7 or more: High risk, with a 30-day mortality rate of 65.4%
35736524.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) for the score was 0.883; for risk categorization, the AUC was 0.818.

35739315.title: Ferroptosis Score Calculator for Breast Cancer Prognosis
35739315.code:
def compute_ferroptosis_score(gene_expression, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the ferroptosis score based on the expression of ferroptosis-related genes.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the names of the ferroptosis-related genes and the values are their expression levels.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the names of the ferroptosis-related genes and the values are their regression coefficients obtained from the Cox regression analysis.

    Returns:
    float: The ferroptosis score.
    """
    score = 0
    for gene, expression in gene_expression.items():
        score += expression * coefficients[gene]
    return score
35739315.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with breast cancer based on the ferroptosis score, which is derived from the expression of ferroptosis-related genes.
35739315.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with breast cancer are eligible for this calculator.
35739315.interpretation:
- Low ferroptosis score: Poor prognosis, lower overall survival rate.
- High ferroptosis score: Better prognosis, higher overall survival rate.
35739315.utility: The ferroptosis score was found to be significantly associated with the prognosis of breast cancer patients, indicating its potential utility in clinical practice. However, further validation studies are needed to confirm its predictive accuracy and clinical utility.

35742524.title: ABCD-SD Score for Predicting Paroxysmal Atrial Fibrillation in Acute Ischemic Stroke
35742524.code:
def compute_abcd_sd_score(age, systolic_bp, has_cad, has_dyslipidemia, sd_heart_rate):
    """
    Compute the ABCD-SD score for predicting paroxysmal atrial fibrillation in acute ischemic stroke.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure in mmHg.
    has_cad (bool): Whether the patient has coronary artery disease.
    has_dyslipidemia (bool): Whether the patient has dyslipidemia.
    sd_heart_rate (int): Standard deviation of heart rate in beats per minute.

    Returns:
    int: The computed ABCD-SD score.
    """
    score = 0
    score += (age / 10) * 2
    score -= (systolic_bp / 20)
    score += 2 if has_cad else 0
    score -= 2 if has_dyslipidemia else 0
    score += (sd_heart_rate / 3) * 2
    return score
35742524.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (pAF) in patients who have had an acute ischemic stroke (AIS).
35742524.eligibility: Patients who have had an acute ischemic stroke and received 24-hour Holter monitoring are eligible.
35742524.interpretation:
- ABCD-SD score ≤ 7: The risk of pAF is 0.8%
- ABCD-SD score ≥ 15: The risk of pAF is 18.3%
35742524.utility: The ABCD-SD score achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROCC) of 0.767 in the model development group, indicating a good predictive performance.

35750021.title: MAI Score for Shunt-Dependent Hydrocephalus in aSAH Patients
35750021.code:
def compute_MAI_score(modified_fisher_grade, acute_hydrocephalus, intraventricular_hemorrhage):
    """
    Compute the MAI score for predicting the risk of shunt-dependent hydrocephalus in aSAH patients.

    Parameters:
    modified_fisher_grade (bool): True if Modified Fisher grade is ≥ 3, False otherwise.
    acute_hydrocephalus (bool): True if patient has Acute Hydrocephalus, False otherwise.
    intraventricular_hemorrhage (bool): True if patient has Intraventricular hemorrhage, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The MAI score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(modified_fisher_grade) + int(acute_hydrocephalus) + int(intraventricular_hemorrhage)
35750021.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of shunt-dependent hydrocephalus (SDHC) in patients with aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH) after surgery.
35750021.eligibility: Patients who have undergone surgery for aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH) are eligible.
35750021.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of shunt dependency
- Score 2-3: 10-fold higher risk for shunt dependency
35750021.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for the MAI score is 0.773, indicating a good predictive performance. The internal validation AUC was 0.950, further confirming the reliability of the MAI scoring system.

35753018.title: King GBS Outcome Score Calculator
35753018.code:
def calculate_king_gbs_score(age, voice, nlr, albumin):
    """
    Calculate the King GBS Outcome Score.

    Parameters:
    age (float): The age of the child in months.
    voice (str): The strength of the child's voice. Can be 'normal' or 'feeble'.
    nlr (float): The neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio.
    albumin (float): The post-immunotherapy serum albumin in g/L.

    Returns:
    int: The King GBS Outcome Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 69.1:
        score += 1
    if voice == 'feeble':
        score += 1
    if nlr <= 1.65:
        score += 1
    if albumin <= 34.5:
        score += 1
    return score
35753018.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 6-month outcome for children with Guillain-Barre syndrome (GBS) based on clinical and laboratory parameters.
35753018.eligibility: Children diagnosed with Guillain-Barre syndrome are eligible for this calculator.
35753018.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: The child is likely to have a complete recovery (Hughes Disability Score ≤ 1).
- Score 3-4: The child is at risk of poor prognosis and may benefit from additional treatment (Hughes Disability Score > 1).
35753018.utility: The King GBS Outcome Score has an AUC of 0.971 (95% CI 0.921-1.02), demonstrating excellent sensitivity (92.3%) and specificity (96.7%) in determining poor outcome.

35771010.title: Enterococcal Biliary Tract Bloodstream Infections (BT-BSI) Risk Score
35771010.code:
def compute_risk_score(cholangiocarcinoma, hospital_acquisition, previous_carbapenems, biliary_prosthesis, chronic_kidney_disease):
    """
    Compute the risk score for Enterococcal BT-BSI.

    Parameters:
    cholangiocarcinoma (bool): True if the patient has cholangiocarcinoma, False otherwise.
    hospital_acquisition (bool): True if the infection was acquired in the hospital, False otherwise.
    previous_carbapenems (bool): True if the patient used carbapenems in the previous month, False otherwise.
    biliary_prosthesis (bool): True if the patient has a biliary prosthesis, False otherwise.
    chronic_kidney_disease (bool): True if the patient has moderate or severe chronic kidney disease, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for Enterococcal BT-BSI.
    """
    score = 0
    if cholangiocarcinoma:
        score += 7
    if hospital_acquisition:
        score += 6
    if previous_carbapenems:
        score += 5
    if biliary_prosthesis:
        score += 4
    if chronic_kidney_disease:
        score += 2
    return score
35771010.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of developing BT-BSI caused by Enterococcus faecalis and E. faecium in patients with biliary tract infections.
35771010.eligibility: Patients with biliary tract infections who are suspected of bacteremia are eligible.
35771010.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 6: The negative predictive value is 95%, indicating a low risk of Enterococcal BT-BSI.
- Score > 6: The risk of Enterococcal BT-BSI increases with the score.
35771010.utility: The AUC of the model was 0.74 [95% CI 0.67 to 0.80], indicating a moderate prediction ability for enterococcal BT-BSI.

35774514.title: Super-Enhancer Associated Gene Signature Prognostic Calculator for Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL)
35774514.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expression, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression levels of 11 SE-associated genes and their corresponding coefficients.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (dict): A dictionary where the key is the gene name and the value is the expression level of the gene.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the key is the gene name and the value is the corresponding coefficient.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    for gene, expression in gene_expression.items():
        risk_score += expression * coefficients[gene]
    return risk_score
35774514.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) based on a signature of 11 super-enhancer (SE) associated genes.
35774514.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) are eligible for this calculator.
35774514.interpretation:
- Low risk: Patients with a lower risk score have better clinical survival outcomes.
- High risk: Patients with a higher risk score have worse clinical survival outcomes.

The exact thresholds for "low" and "high" risk scores would need to be determined based on further clinical studies.
35774514.utility: The model showed excellent performance in predicting survival outcomes, as determined via time-dependent receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curves. It can effectively classify DLBCL patients into high-risk and low-risk groups in terms of overall survival.

35775673.title: Lactate to Hematocrit Ratio (LHR) Score Calculator
35775673.code:
def compute_lhr(lactate, hematocrit):
    """
    Compute the Lactate to Hematocrit Ratio (LHR) score.

    Parameters:
    lactate (float): Lactate level in the blood.
    hematocrit (float): Hematocrit level in the blood.

    Returns:
    float: LHR score.
    """
    return lactate / hematocrit
35775673.purpose: This calculator is used to predict mortality in patients with severe thoracoabdominal trauma.
35775673.eligibility: Patients with severe thoracoabdominal trauma are eligible for this calculator.
35775673.interpretation:
- LHR score < 0.187: Lower risk of mortality
- LHR score >= 0.187: Higher risk of mortality
35775673.utility: The LHR score has a sensitivity of 77.8% and a specificity of 90.2% in predicting mortality in patients with severe thoracoabdominal trauma.

35778894.title: CHA2DS2-VASc and R2CHA2DS2-VASc Scores
35778894.purpose: These calculators are used to estimate cardioembolic risk in patients with atrial fibrillation (AF) and predict vascular adverse events and death in patients without AF. The R2CHA2DS2-VASc score, which includes renal impairment, allows a better prediction of death and thromboembolism in patients without AF.
35778894.eligibility: Patients at high cardiovascular risk are eligible for these calculators.
35778894.interpretation: For each one-point increase in CHA2DS2-VASc or R2CHA2DS2-VASc scores, mortality increased by almost 1.5-fold.
35778894.utility: The R2CHA2DS2-VASc score (C-statistic = 0.71; 95% CI = 0.65-76) outperformed the CHA2DS2-VASc score (C-statistic = 0.66; 95% CI = 0.61-0.71) in predicting 4-year mortality.

35781089.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma Recurrence Risk Score
35781089.code:
def compute_risk_score(afp, portal_vein_invasion, cirrhosis):
    """
    Compute the risk score for hepatocellular carcinoma recurrence.

    Parameters:
    afp (bool): True if baseline alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) > 7 ng/mL, False otherwise.
    portal_vein_invasion (bool): True if portal veinous invasion is present, False otherwise.
    cirrhosis (bool): True if cirrhosis is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(afp) + int(portal_vein_invasion) + int(cirrhosis)
35781089.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of recurrence in patients with a single large hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) who are undergoing liver resection.
35781089.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing liver resection for a single large hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) are eligible.
35781089.interpretation:
- 0 risk factors: Low risk, with a median disease-free survival of 65 months.
- 1 risk factor: Medium risk, with a median disease-free survival of 36 months.
- 2 or more risk factors: High risk, with a median disease-free survival of 8.9 months.
35781089.utility: The risk score was found to be significantly associated with disease-free survival (p < 0.001) in a multivariate analysis.

35781912.title: NOTCH3-ICH Risk Score Calculator
35781912.code:
def compute_NOTCH3_ICH_score(history_of_stroke_in_siblings, thalamic_haemorrhage, deep_nuclei_lacunae, hippocampal_CMB, thalamic_CMB_gt5):
    """
    Compute the NOTCH3-ICH risk score based on the given criteria.

    Args:
    history_of_stroke_in_siblings (bool): Whether the patient has a history of stroke in siblings.
    thalamic_haemorrhage (bool): Whether the patient has thalamic haemorrhage.
    deep_nuclei_lacunae (bool): Whether the patient has any deep nuclei lacunae.
    hippocampal_CMB (bool): Whether the patient has any hippocampal cerebral microbleed (CMB).
    thalamic_CMB_gt5 (bool): Whether the patient has a thalamic CMB >5.

    Returns:
    int: The NOTCH3-ICH risk score.
    """
    return history_of_stroke_in_siblings + thalamic_haemorrhage + deep_nuclei_lacunae + hippocampal_CMB + thalamic_CMB_gt5
35781912.purpose: This calculator is used to identify the likelihood of a patient having a NOTCH3 mutation, which is a common cause of hereditary cerebral small vessel disease (CSVD) and may be a cause of spontaneous intracerebral haemorrhage (ICH).
35781912.eligibility: Patients with a history of ICH who are part of the CSVD patient cohort are eligible for this calculator.
35781912.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low likelihood of NOTCH3 mutation
- Score 2-5: High likelihood of NOTCH3 mutation. A score ≥2 had a sensitivity of 88.9% and a specificity of 64.2% in identifying the NOTCH3 mutation.
35781912.utility: The NOTCH3-ICH score has a sensitivity of 88.9% and a specificity of 64.2% in identifying the NOTCH3 mutation.

35801324.title: Charlson Comorbidity Index for COVID-19 Mortality Risk
35801324.code:
def calculate_cci_score(patient_conditions):
    """
    Calculate the Charlson Comorbidity Index score for a patient.

    Args:
    patient_conditions (list): A list of the patient's conditions.

    Returns:
    int: The total CCI score.
    """
    condition_scores = {
        'myocardial_infarct': 1,
        'congestive_heart_failure': 1,
        'peripheral_vascular_disease': 1,
        'dementia': 1,
        'cerebrovascular_disease': 1,
        'chronic_lung_disease': 1,
        'connective_tissue_disease': 1,
        'ulcer_disease': 1,
        'mild_liver_disease': 1,
        'hemiplegia': 2,
        'moderate_severe_kidney_disease': 2,
        'diabetes': 2,
        'diabetes_with_end_organ_damage': 2,
        'tumor': 2,
        'leukemia': 2,
        'lymphoma': 2,
        'moderate_severe_liver_disease': 3,
        'metastatic_solid_tumor': 6,
        'aids': 6
    }

    total_score = sum(condition_scores.get(condition, 0) for condition in patient_conditions)
    return total_score
35801324.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the in-hospital mortality risk for COVID-19 patients based on their comorbidity burdens.
35801324.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with COVID-19 and receiving inpatient treatment are eligible.
35801324.interpretation:
- CCI score <= 5.5: Lower risk of in-hospital mortality
- CCI score > 5.5: Higher risk of in-hospital mortality
35801324.utility: The Charlson Comorbidity Index score was found to be an independent predictor of in-hospital mortality with an odds ratio of 1.990 (95% CI: 1.314-3.015, P = .001). The cut-off value for the CCI to predict in-hospital mortality was 5.5, with 81.8% sensitivity and 73.5% specificity.

35804899.title: mRCC Prognostic Risk Calculator in the Immunotherapy Era
35804899.code:
def compute_risk_score(intact_tumor, liver_metastasis, treatment_start, elevated_platelets, karnofsky_status):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient with mRCC based on five risk factors.

    Parameters:
    intact_tumor (bool): Whether the patient has an intact primary kidney tumor.
    liver_metastasis (bool): Whether the patient has liver metastasis.
    treatment_start (bool): Whether the patient started treatment within one year of diagnosis.
    elevated_platelets (bool): Whether the patient has elevated platelets.
    karnofsky_status (bool): Whether the patient's Karnofsky performance status is less than 80%.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score, ranging from 0 to 5.
    """
    return int(intact_tumor) + int(liver_metastasis) + int(treatment_start) + int(elevated_platelets) + int(karnofsky_status)
35804899.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk stratification of metastatic renal cell carcinoma (mRCC) patients treated with checkpoint inhibitors (CPI).
35804899.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with metastatic renal cell carcinoma (mRCC) and are being treated with checkpoint inhibitors (CPI).
35804899.interpretation:
- 0-1 risk factors: Low-risk group
- 2-5 risk factors: High-risk group
35804899.utility: The model received a C-index of 70.7, compared with a score of 62.0 for the Heng's model. When dividing patients into "low-risk" (0-1 risk factors) and "high-risk" (2-5 risk factors), there was good separation between the groups, with a hazard ratio of 5.9 (p < 0.0001).

35813299.title: Lactate Dehydrogenase to Albumin Ratio (LAR) Survival Nomogram for Colorectal Cancer
35813299.code:
def compute_lar(ldh, albumin):
    """
    Compute the Lactate Dehydrogenase to Albumin Ratio (LAR).
    
    Parameters:
    ldh (float): Lactate Dehydrogenase level.
    albumin (float): Albumin level.
    
    Returns:
    float: LAR value.
    """
    return ldh / albumin
35813299.purpose: This calculator is used to predict overall survival (OS) and disease-free survival (DFS) in patients with operable colorectal cancer (CRC) based on the Lactate Dehydrogenase to Albumin Ratio (LAR).
35813299.eligibility: Patients with operable colorectal cancer are eligible for this calculator.
35813299.interpretation:
- LAR < 4.50: The risk of mortality is low and decreases slowly.
- 4.50 <= LAR <= 6.68: The risk of mortality increases rapidly.
- LAR > 6.68: The risk of mortality becomes flat.
35813299.utility: The LAR was found to be superior to the TNM stage for predicting overall survival (OS) as well as disease-free survival (DFS). The survival nomograms based on LAR achieved great predictive ability for OS and DFS in operable CRC patients.

35830440.title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score for Acute Ischemic Stroke Patients
35830440.code:
def compute_CHA2DS2_VASc_score(C, H, A2, D, S2, V, A, Sc):
    """
    Compute the CHA2DS2-VASc score.

    Parameters:
    C (bool): Congestive heart failure
    H (bool): Hypertension
    A2 (bool): Age ≥75 years
    D (bool): Diabetes Mellitus
    S2 (bool): Stroke/TIA/Thromboembolism
    V (bool): Vascular disease
    A (bool): Age 65-74 years
    Sc (bool): Sex category (female)

    Returns:
    int: CHA2DS2-VASc score
    """
    return C + H + 2*A2 + D + 2*S2 + V + A + Sc
35830440.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of major adverse cardiac and cerebrovascular events (MACCEs) at 1 year after an acute ischemic stroke (AIS).
35830440.eligibility: Patients who have experienced an acute ischemic stroke are eligible.
35830440.interpretation:
- Score < 2: Low risk of 1-year MACCEs
- Score ≥ 2: High risk of 1-year MACCEs
35830440.utility: The CHA2DS2-VASc score was found to be an independent predictor of 1-year MACCEs in AIS patients, with a hazard ratio of 1.63 (95% CI = 1.52, 1.76) for high scores compared to low scores.

35837230.title: Screening for Atrial Fibrillation Scale (SAFE)
35837230.code:
def compute_SAFE_score(age, copd_or_osa, thyroid_disease, ntprobnp, la_enlargement, stroke_topography, vessel_occlusion):
    """
    Compute the SAFE score for predicting paroxysmal atrial fibrillation in ischemic stroke patients.

    Parameters:
    age (int): patient's age
    copd_or_osa (bool): whether the patient has a history of COPD or OSA
    thyroid_disease (bool): whether the patient has thyroid disease
    ntprobnp (float): NT-proBNP level in pg/ml
    la_enlargement (bool): whether the patient has left atrial enlargement
    stroke_topography (bool): whether the stroke is in the cortical topography, including hemispheric or cerebellar cortex
    vessel_occlusion (bool): whether the patient has intracranial large vessel occlusion

    Returns:
    int: SAFE score
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 65:
        score += 2
    if copd_or_osa:
        score += 1
    if thyroid_disease:
        score += 1
    if ntprobnp >= 250:
        score += 2
    if la_enlargement:
        score += 2
    if stroke_topography:
        score += 1
    if vessel_occlusion:
        score += 1
    return score
35837230.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation in ischemic stroke patients.
35837230.eligibility: Ischemic stroke patients without documented atrial fibrillation are eligible.
35837230.interpretation: A SAFE score of 5 or above identifies patients with paroxysmal AF with a sensitivity of 83% and a specificity of 80%.
35837230.utility: The SAFE score has a sensitivity of 83% and a specificity of 80% in identifying patients with paroxysmal AF.

35852473.title: EBVCL and TVRR Prognostic Calculator for Locally Advanced Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma
35852473.code:
def calculate_risk(ebvcl, tvrr):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on EBVCL and TVRR.

    Args:
    ebvcl (float): Epstein-Barr virus DNA change level in copies/ml.
    tvrr (float): Tumor volume reduction ratio in percentage.

    Returns:
    str: Risk group ('low' or 'high').
    """
    if ebvcl <= 127 and tvrr <= 32.72:
        return 'low'
    else:
        return 'high'
35852473.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with locally advanced nasopharyngeal carcinoma (LANPC) after inductive chemotherapy (IC) based on Epstein-Barr virus DNA change level (EBVCL) and tumor volume reduction ratio (TVRR).
35852473.eligibility: Patients with locally advanced nasopharyngeal carcinoma who have undergone inductive chemotherapy are eligible.
35852473.interpretation:
- Low-risk group: The three-year OS, PFS, DMFS, and LRFFS are 97.7%, 91.1%, 94.2%, and 97.8%, respectively.
- High-risk group: The three-year OS, PFS, DMFS, and LRFFS are 78.3%, 60.9%, 68.7%, and 77.9%, respectively.
35852473.utility: The area under the ROC curve of EBVCL+TVRR for three-year OS was 0.829, indicating a good predictive ability.

35866083.title: Systemic Inflammation Prognostic Score (SIPS)
35866083.code:
def compute_SIPS(CRP, GNRI, ALI, LCR):
    """
    Compute the Systemic Inflammation Prognostic Score (SIPS)

    Parameters:
    CRP (float): C-reactive protein level
    GNRI (float): Geriatric Nutritional Risk Index
    ALI (float): Advanced Lung Cancer Inflammation Index
    LCR (float): Lymphocyte to C-reactive protein ratio

    Returns:
    int: Systemic Inflammation Prognostic Score (SIPS)
    """
    CRP_score = 1 if CRP > 9.81 else 0
    GNRI_score = 1 if GNRI <= 93.85 else 0
    ALI_score = 1 if ALI <= 23.49 else 0
    LCR_score = 1 if LCR <= 2523.81 else 0

    SIPS = CRP_score + GNRI_score + ALI_score + LCR_score

    return SIPS
35866083.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the survival of elderly patients with cancer using inflammatory indices.
35866083.eligibility: Elderly patients with cancer are eligible for this calculator.
35866083.interpretation:
- 0: Low-risk group
- 1-2: Moderate-risk group
- 3-4: High-risk group
35866083.utility: The SIPS showed better survival prediction than CRP, GNRI, ALI, and LCR alone. The hazard ratios were 2.81 times higher in patients in the high-risk group with malnutrition than in patients in the low-risk group without malnutrition.

35872141.title: VTE Malignancy Risk Score Calculator
35872141.code:
def compute_vte_malignancy_score(age, sex, ivc_thrombus, upper_extremity_thrombus, charlson_index):
    """
    Compute the VTE Malignancy Risk Score.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    sex (str): Sex of the patient. Can be 'male' or 'female'.
    ivc_thrombus (bool): Whether the patient has an IVC thrombus.
    upper_extremity_thrombus (bool): Whether the patient has an upper extremity thrombus.
    charlson_index (int): Charlson Comorbidity Index score of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The VTE Malignancy Risk Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 65:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'female':
        score += 1
    if ivc_thrombus:
        score += 1
    if upper_extremity_thrombus:
        score += 1
    if charlson_index >= 5:
        score += 1
    return score
35872141.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of a newly discovered malignancy within 6 months in patients presenting with venous thromboembolism (VTE).
35872141.eligibility: Patients aged 18 years or older, presenting with a primary diagnosis of deep vein thrombosis (DVT) or a pulmonary embolism (PE), and without known preexisting malignant diseases.
35872141.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low risk of a newly discovered malignancy within 6 months.
- Score 3 or more: High risk of a newly discovered malignancy within 6 months.
35872141.utility: The VTE Malignancy Risk Score has a sensitivity of 86% and a specificity of 89% for predicting the risk of a newly discovered malignancy within 6 months.

35876366.title: Postoperative Pancreatic Fistula (POPF) Risk Calculator
35876366.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of a postoperative pancreatic fistula (POPF) after a pancreatoduodenectomy (PD), taking into account the Fistula Risk Score (FRS) and postoperative serum hyperamylasemia (POH).
35876366.eligibility: Patients who have undergone pancreatoduodenectomies (PD) are eligible for this risk assessment.
35876366.interpretation:
- Low FRS without POH: 3.8% risk of POPF
- Moderate FRS without POH: 22.9% risk of POPF
- High FRS without POH: 48.9% risk of POPF
- Low FRS with POH: 42.9% risk of POPF
- Moderate FRS with POH: 41.7% risk of POPF
- High FRS with POH: 59.2% risk of POPF
35876366.utility: The predictive ability of this model showed progressively and significantly higher accuracy (AUC FRS=0.82, AUC FRS-DFA=0.85, AUC FRS-DFA-POH=0.87, AUC FRS-DFA-POH-CRP=0.90, DeLong always P<0.05).

35879923.title: At-Point Clinical Frailty Scale (CFS) Risk Calculator
35879923.code:
def compute_risk(cfs_score):
    """
    Compute the risk based on the Clinical Frailty Scale (CFS) score.

    Args:
    cfs_score (int): The CFS score of the patient, ranging from 1 to 9.

    Returns:
    str: The risk category based on the CFS score.
    """
    if cfs_score < 5:
        return 'Low Risk'
    else:
        return 'High Risk'
35879923.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of adverse geriatric conditions and in-hospital outcomes in older inpatients.
35879923.eligibility: Patients who are 65 years or older and are admitted to a medical or surgical unit.
35879923.interpretation:
- 'Low Risk': Patients with a CFS score of less than 5 have a 1.7% risk of adverse outcomes.
- 'High Risk': Patients with a CFS score of 5 or more have a 28.5% risk of adverse outcomes.
35879923.utility: The at-point CFS was associated with an increased risk of a fall, pressure ulcers, delirium, 30-day readmission, ED visit, death, and institutionalization after discharge.

35881861.title: Palliative Appropriateness Criteria Score (PACS)
35881861.code:
def compute_PACS(gender, ECOG, diagnosis, PRT_indication, status, treatment_sites):
    """
    Compute the Palliative Appropriateness Criteria Score (PACS).

    Parameters:
    gender (str): 'male' or 'female'
    ECOG (int): ECOG performance status, 0-4
    diagnosis (str): 'lung', 'other', 'breast', or 'prostate'
    PRT_indication (str): 'neurological', 'pain', or 'other'
    status (str): 'inpatient' or 'outpatient'
    treatment_sites (str): 'extraosseous' or 'other'

    Returns:
    int: PACS
    """
    score = 0
    if gender == 'male':
        score += 1
    if ECOG >= 3:
        score += 1
    if diagnosis in ['lung', 'other']:
        score += 1
    if PRT_indication == 'neurological':
        score += 1
    if status == 'inpatient':
        score += 1
    if treatment_sites == 'extraosseous':
        score += 1
    return score
35881861.purpose: This calculator is used to aid clinical decision making between shorter versus longer palliative radiotherapy (PRT) regimens using objective patient factors.
35881861.eligibility: Patients receiving palliative radiotherapy (PRT) are eligible.
35881861.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low risk, a more protracted course of PRT is reasonable.
- Score 3-6: High risk, shorter courses of PRT may be beneficial. High-risk PACS categorization is significantly associated with death within 30 days, regardless of fractionation scheme.
35881861.utility: The PACS is a novel metric for evaluating the utility of PRT regimens to improve clinical decision making. Single fraction is associated with low PRL. When considering multifraction PRT regimens, the PACS identifies patients who may benefit from shorter courses of PRT and alternatively, low-risk patients for whom a more protracted course is reasonable.

35887554.title: TASK Risk Score Calculator for Peri-procedural Cerebrovascular Events in TAVR Patients
35887554.code:
def compute_TASK_score(history_of_stroke, non_balloon_valve, chronic_kidney_disease, peripheral_vascular_disease):
    """
    Compute the TASK risk score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    history_of_stroke (bool): Whether the patient has a history of stroke.
    non_balloon_valve (bool): Whether a non-balloon expandable valve is used.
    chronic_kidney_disease (bool): Whether the patient has chronic kidney disease.
    peripheral_vascular_disease (bool): Whether the patient has peripheral vascular disease.

    Returns:
    int: The TASK risk score.
    """
    return history_of_stroke + non_balloon_valve + chronic_kidney_disease + peripheral_vascular_disease
35887554.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of peri-procedural cerebrovascular events in patients undergoing transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR).
35887554.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) are eligible.
35887554.interpretation:
- 0 points: Very low risk of peri-procedural cerebrovascular events
- 1 point: Low risk of peri-procedural cerebrovascular events
- 2 points: Intermediate risk of peri-procedural cerebrovascular events
- 3-4 points: High risk of peri-procedural cerebrovascular events
35887554.utility: Each one-point increment in the TASK score was associated with a significant increase in peri-procedural cerebrovascular events risk (OR 1.96, 95% CI 1.56-2.45, p < 0.001). The high-risk TASK score group (OR 5.4, 95% CI 2.06-14.16, p = 0.001) was associated with a significantly higher risk of peri-procedural cerebrovascular events compared with the low TASK score group.

35892422.title: Subdural Hematoma in the Elderly (SHE) Score Calculator
35892422.code:
def compute_she_score(age, gcs, sdh_volume):
    """
    Compute the SHE score based on age, GCS, and SDH volume.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    gcs (int): Glasgow Coma Scale score at admission.
    sdh_volume (float): Volume of the subdural hematoma in mL.

    Returns:
    int: SHE score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 80:
        score += 1
    if 5 <= gcs <= 12:
        score += 1
    elif gcs <= 4:
        score += 2
    if sdh_volume > 50:
        score += 1
    return score
35892422.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality in patients older than 65 years with nontraumatic/minor trauma acute subdural hematoma (aSDH).
35892422.eligibility: Patients who are older than 65 years and diagnosed with nontraumatic/minor trauma acute subdural hematoma (aSDH).
35892422.interpretation:
- SHE 0: 4.3% 30-day mortality
- SHE 1: 12.2% 30-day mortality
- SHE 2: 36.6% 30-day mortality
- SHE 3: 97.1% 30-day mortality
- SHE 4: 100% 30-day mortality
35892422.utility: The SHE score has been validated in a study with 131 patients, showing consistent mortality rates with the original study by Alford et al. It is a reliable mortality predictor for minor trauma acute subdural hematoma in elderly patients.

35901363.title: HCC-CTCs Score Calculator
35901363.code:
def calculate_hcc_ctcs_score(afp, albumin, ck18, ck19, platelets_count):
    """
    Calculate the HCC-CTCs score.

    Parameters:
    afp (float): Alpha-fetoprotein level in U/L
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/dl
    ck18 (float): Cytokeratin 18 level in %
    ck19 (float): Cytokeratin 19 level
    platelets_count (float): Platelets count in ×10^9/L

    Returns:
    float: HCC-CTCs score
    """
    return afp * 0.08 - albumin * 84 + ck18 * 2.9 + ck19 * 3.1 - platelets_count * 0.75 - 510
35901363.purpose: This calculator is used for the early detection of Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) in Hepatitis C Virus-High Risk Patients.
35901363.eligibility: Patients with a high risk of Hepatitis C Virus who are being screened for Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC).
35901363.interpretation:
- Score < 0: The patient is likely to have liver cirrhosis.
- Score >= 0: The patient is likely to have Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC).
35901363.utility: The HCC-CTCs score produced an AUC of 1 for differentiating patients with HCC from those with liver cirrhosis, with a sensitivity and specificity of a cut-off 0.

35909159.title: Advanced Lung Cancer Inflammation Index (ALI) Calculator for Right-Sided Colon Cancer (RCC)
35909159.code:
def compute_ALI(BMI, albumin, NLR):
    """
    Compute the Advanced Lung Cancer Inflammation Index (ALI)

    Parameters:
    BMI (float): Body Mass Index in kg/m^2
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/dL
    NLR (float): Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio

    Returns:
    float: ALI score
    """
    return BMI * albumin / NLR
35909159.purpose: This calculator is used to predict short- and long-term outcomes in patients with RCC after complete mesocolic excision (CME).
35909159.eligibility: Patients with right-sided colon cancer who have undergone complete mesocolic excision.
35909159.interpretation:
- ALI > 36.3: Lower risk of postoperative complications and better overall survival (OS) and disease-free survival (DFS).
- ALI <= 36.3: Higher risk of postoperative complications and worse overall survival (OS) and disease-free survival (DFS).
35909159.utility: The ALI was found to be an effective indicator for predicting short- and long-term outcomes in patients with RCC. The nomogram combined with the ALI was consistently superior to a non-ALI nomogram or the pathological tumor-node-metastasis classification based on the net reclassification improvement index (NRI), integrated discrimination improvement index (IDI), and time-dependent receiver operating characteristic (time-ROC) curve analysis after propensity score matching (PSM).

35913526.title: Predicting Success Rate of Re-attempt CTO-PCI
35913526.code:
def compute_risk_score(occlusion_length, non_calcification, distal_landing_zone, rentrop_grade, retrograde_approach):
    """
    Compute the risk score for predicting the success rate of re-attempt CTO-PCI.

    Parameters:
    occlusion_length (bool): True if occlusion length < 20 mm, False otherwise.
    non_calcification (bool): True if non-calcification, False otherwise.
    distal_landing_zone (bool): True if adequate distal landing zone, False otherwise.
    rentrop_grade (bool): True if Rentrop grade ≥ 2, False otherwise.
    retrograde_approach (bool): True if retrograde approach as the initial strategy, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if occlusion_length:
        score += 1
    if non_calcification:
        score += 1
    if distal_landing_zone:
        score += 1
    if rentrop_grade:
        score += 1
    if retrograde_approach:
        score += 2
    return score
35913526.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the success rate of elective percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) after prior failed chronic total occlusion (CTO).
35913526.eligibility: Patients with previously attempted CTO-PCI are eligible for this calculator.
35913526.interpretation:
- Score 0: 0% success rate
- Score 1: 17.9% success rate
- Score 2: 46.2% success rate
- Score 3: 77.8% success rate
- Score 4 or more: 93.3% success rate
35913526.utility: The article does not provide specific evaluation results of the clinical utility of the risk score, such as AUC, F-score, PPV.

35918798.title: I-PET Score Calculator for Thyroid Cancer Progression
35918798.code:
def compute_IPET_score(iodine, FDG, SUVmax):
    """
    Compute the I-PET score based on iodine, FDG, and SUVmax.

    Parameters:
    iodine (bool): True if iodine is positive, False otherwise.
    FDG (bool): True if FDG is positive, False otherwise.
    SUVmax (float): The maximum standard uptake value.

    Returns:
    str: The I-PET score.
    """
    if iodine and FDG:
        return 'I-PET [2]' if SUVmax <= 5 else 'I-PET [2B]'
    elif iodine and not FDG:
        return 'I-PET [1]'
    elif not iodine and FDG:
        return 'I-PET [3]' if SUVmax <= 5 else 'I-PET [3B]'
    else:
        return 'I-PET [0]'
35918798.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the progression in patients with structurally or biochemically incomplete thyroid cancer.
35918798.eligibility: Patients aged over 18 with differentiated thyroid cancer who have undergone F-18 labelled fluoro-2-deoxyglucose (18 F-FDG) positron emission tomography (PET) and whole body scan (WBS) within a 6-month period.
35918798.interpretation:
- I-PET [0]: 22% progression rate
- I-PET [1]: 40% progression rate
- I-PET [2]: 63% progression rate
- I-PET [2B]: 88% progression rate
- I-PET [3]: 74% progression rate
- I-PET [3B]: 78% progression rate
35918798.utility: The I-PET score was found to be significantly associated with progression (p < 0.001). Patients with I-PET [3B] were 9.6 times more likely to commence multikinase inhibitor therapy (p = 0.001) and had 8 times greater mortality (p = 0.003) than patients in other I-PET groups combined.

35932022.title: Systemic Inflammation Response Index (SIRI) Calculator for Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma (NPC) with Negative Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) DNA
35932022.code:
def calculate_siri(neutrophil_count, monocyte_count, lymphocyte_count):
    """
    Calculate the Systemic Inflammation Response Index (SIRI)

    Parameters:
    neutrophil_count (float): Patient's neutrophil count
    monocyte_count (float): Patient's monocyte count
    lymphocyte_count (float): Patient's lymphocyte count

    Returns:
    float: The calculated SIRI
    """
    return neutrophil_count * monocyte_count / lymphocyte_count
35932022.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of NPC patients with negative EBV DNA based on the SIRI.
35932022.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with NPC and have negative EBV DNA are eligible.
35932022.interpretation:
- SIRI <= 1.12: Lower risk of disease progression and better overall survival.
- SIRI > 1.12: Higher risk of disease progression and worse overall survival.
35932022.utility: In the study, SIRI was found to be an independent prognostic factor for progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) in NPC patients with negative EBV DNA. It had higher prognostic value than other indicators.

35932178.title: Non-Transplantable Recurrence Risk Calculator for Hepatocellular Carcinoma Patients
35932178.code:
def compute_risk_score(AFP, prior_HCC):
    """
    Compute the risk score for non-transplantable recurrence (NTR) in HCC patients.

    Parameters:
    AFP (float): Alpha-fetoprotein level in ng/ml.
    prior_HCC (bool): Whether the patient has prior HCC.

    Returns:
    risk_category (str): The risk category of the patient ('low', 'intermediate', 'high').
    """
    if AFP < 100 and not prior_HCC:
        return 'low'
    elif AFP < 100 and prior_HCC:
        return 'intermediate'
    elif AFP >= 100:
        return 'high'
35932178.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of non-transplantable recurrence (NTR) in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) treated by percutaneous thermal ablation (PTA).
35932178.eligibility: Patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) of ≤3-cm who are treated by percutaneous thermal ablation (PTA) are eligible.
35932178.interpretation:
- Low risk: Patients who are HCC-naïve with AFP < 100 ng/ml. They have a 9.3% chance of NTR.
- Intermediate risk: Patients who are non-HCC naïve with AFP < 100 ng/ml. They have a 22.8% chance of NTR.
- High risk: Patients with AFP ≥ 100 ng/ml. They have a 61.5% chance of NTR.
35932178.utility: The risk score has a Harrell's C statistic of 0.76, indicating a good predictive accuracy.

35933369.title: Composite Scoring System for Estimating Lymph Node Metastasis in Submucosal Colorectal Cancer
35933369.code:
def compute_risk_score(histological_differentiation, lymphatic_venous_invasion, tumor_buds):
    """
    Compute the risk score for lymph node metastasis in submucosal colorectal cancer.

    Parameters:
    histological_differentiation (bool): True if unfavorable differentiation is present, False otherwise.
    lymphatic_venous_invasion (bool): True if lymphatic or venous invasion is present, False otherwise.
    tumor_buds (int): The number of tumor buds.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if histological_differentiation:
        score += 1
    if lymphatic_venous_invasion:
        score += 1
    if tumor_buds >= 5:
        score += 1
    return score
35933369.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of lymph node metastasis in patients with submucosal colorectal cancer.
35933369.eligibility: Patients with histologically confirmed T1N0-2M0 submucosal colorectal cancer are eligible.
35933369.interpretation:
- Score 0: The rate of lymph node metastasis is 5.6%.
- Score 1: The rate of lymph node metastasis is 15.5%.
- Score 2: The rate of lymph node metastasis is 31.0%.
- Score 3 or more: The rate of lymph node metastasis is 52.4%.
35933369.utility: The Akaike information criterion (AIC) value was lowest for five tumor buds, indicating that this cut-off number provides the best fit for the data. The odds ratios for the three risk factors were 8.16, 5.91, and 3.01, respectively, indicating that they are all significant predictors of lymph node metastasis.

35958410.title: PREDICT-HF Risk Score Calculator
35958410.code:
def calculate_predict_hf_score(age, systolic_bp, eGFR):
    """
    Calculate the PREDICT-HF risk score based on age, systolic blood pressure, and renal function.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    systolic_bp (int): Systolic blood pressure of the patient in mmHg.
    eGFR (float): Estimated glomerular filtration rate in ml/min/1.73 m2.

    Returns:
    int: The PREDICT-HF risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 80:
        score += 1
    if systolic_bp < 100:
        score += 1
    if eGFR < 30:
        score += 1
    return score
35958410.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of cardiovascular death or first unplanned heart failure hospitalization (HFH) in Asian heart failure patients with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) receiving sacubitril/valsartan.
35958410.eligibility: Patients with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) who are receiving sacubitril/valsartan are eligible.
35958410.interpretation:
- Score 0: Standard-risk group, with an event rate of 10.8 per 100 patient-years.
- Score 1-3: High-risk group, with an event rate of 24.9 per 100 patient-years.
35958410.utility: The C statistics for the PREDICT-HF model in the overall cohort and high-risk group for cardiovascular death or first unplanned HFH at 2 years were 0.73 (95% CI 0.70-0.76) and 0.71 (95% CI 0.65-0.76), respectively.

35968285.title: Female High-Grade Glioma Risk Score (FHGRS)
35968285.code:
def compute_risk_score(miR_4297_level):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the serum miR-4297 level.

    Args:
    miR_4297_level (float): The serum miR-4297 level.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score. 1 for high risk, 0 for low risk.
    """
    if miR_4297_level >= 1.392:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
35968285.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of high-grade glioma in female patients based on serum miR-4297 levels.
35968285.eligibility: This calculator is applicable to female patients who have been newly diagnosed with glioma.
35968285.interpretation:
- Risk Score 0: Low risk of high-grade glioma. The median progression-free survival (PFS) time is 42.89 months.
- Risk Score 1: High risk of high-grade glioma. The median PFS time is 12.3 months.
35968285.utility: The FHGRS exhibited a robust performance in predicting high-grade glioma in females with an AU-ROC of p < 0.001.

35968507.title: MECRG Risk Score Calculator for Colorectal Cancer Prognosis
35968507.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expression, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the MECRG risk score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (dict): A dictionary where keys are gene names and values are their expression levels.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where keys are gene names and values are their corresponding coefficients.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = sum(gene_expression[gene] * coefficients[gene] for gene in gene_expression)
    return risk_score
35968507.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of colorectal cancer (CRC) patients based on the expression of nine prognostic malignant epithelial cell-related genes (MECRGs).
35968507.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with colorectal cancer are eligible.
35968507.interpretation:
- Low risk: Patients with a risk score less than the median risk score. These patients have a longer survival time.
- High risk: Patients with a risk score greater than or equal to the median risk score. These patients have a significantly shorter survival time.
35968507.utility: The MECRG risk score was significantly correlated with the clinical features and overall survival of CRC patients. It can serve as an independent prognostic factor for CRC patients.

35970635.title: Comorbidity Polypharmacy Score (CPS) Calculator for Older Trauma Patients
35970635.code:
def calculate_cps(comorbidities, medications):
    """
    Calculate the Comorbidity Polypharmacy Score (CPS).
    
    Parameters:
    comorbidities (int): The number of comorbidities the patient has.
    medications (int): The number of medications the patient is taking.
    
    Returns:
    int: The CPS score.
    """
    return comorbidities + medications
35970635.purpose: This calculator is used to predict outcomes and mortality in older trauma patients.
35970635.eligibility: Patients aged 45 years and above attending the emergency departments with traumatic injuries (Injury Severity Score [ISS] of 9 and above).
35970635.interpretation:
- CPS < 8: Low risk group
- CPS ≥ 8: Moderate to morbid risk group
35970635.utility: The CPS was found to be associated with increased odds of death and discharge to a rehabilitation or long-term care facility.

35970649.title: Cystic Fibrosis-Specific Frailty Index (CF-FI)
35970649.code:
def compute_cffi(deficits):
    """
    Compute the Cystic Fibrosis-Specific Frailty Index (CF-FI).
    
    Args:
    deficits (list): A list of 66 binary values representing the presence (1) or absence (0) of each deficit.
    
    Returns:
    float: The CF-FI score.
    """
    return sum(deficits) / 66
35970649.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of adverse waitlist and post-lung transplantation (LT) outcomes in cystic fibrosis (CF) patients.
35970649.eligibility: Adult cystic fibrosis patients listed for lung transplantation.
35970649.interpretation:
- CF-FI < 0.25: Not frail
- CF-FI ≥ 0.25: Frail

The higher the CF-FI score, the higher the risk of adverse waitlist and post-LT outcomes.
35970649.utility: The CF-FI was significantly associated with all studied outcomes in the Toronto cohort (FI and post-LT mortality, multivariable HR 1.74 [95%CI:1.24-2.45] per 0.1 point of the CF-FI). In the Swiss cohort, the CF-FI was associated with worsening waitlist status, post-LT mortality, and graft failure.

35972693.title: Bladder Cancer Prognostic Score Calculator
35972693.code:
def compute_prognostic_score(pd1_positive, pdl1_positive, camp_positive):
    """
    Compute the prognostic score for bladder cancer patients.

    Args:
    pd1_positive (bool): Whether PD-1 expression is positive.
    pdl1_positive (bool): Whether PD-L1 expression is positive.
    camp_positive (bool): Whether cAMP expression is positive.

    Returns:
    int: The prognostic score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(pd1_positive) + int(pdl1_positive) + int(camp_positive)
35972693.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the oncological outcomes in patients with bladder cancer who have undergone radical cystectomy.
35972693.eligibility: Patients with bladder cancer who have undergone radical cystectomy are eligible.
35972693.interpretation:
- Score 0: Reference score, no increased risk.
- Score 1: Not significantly associated with cancer-specific mortality (CSM), HR=0.81, p=0.80.
- Score 2: Increased risk of CSM, HR=4.72, p=0.01.
- Score 3: Significantly increased risk of CSM, HR=10.51, p<0.01.
35972693.utility: The prognostic score was found to be a significant predictor of cancer-specific mortality in the study, with higher scores associated with increased risk.

35973177.title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) Risk Calculator for Cirrhosis Patients with Cured Hepatitis C
35973177.code:
def compute_hcc_risk(predicted_probability, gp_probability):
    """
    Compute the incidence ratio and absolute risk difference for HCC.
    
    Args:
    predicted_probability (float): The predicted 3-year probability of HCC for a given patient.
    gp_probability (float): The 3-year probability of HCC in the GP for the equivalent demographic group.
    
    Returns:
    incidence_ratio (float): The incidence ratio for HCC.
    absolute_risk_difference (float): The absolute risk difference for HCC.
    """
    incidence_ratio = predicted_probability / gp_probability
    absolute_risk_difference = predicted_probability - gp_probability
    return incidence_ratio, absolute_risk_difference
35973177.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the 3-year probability of a hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) diagnosis in patients with cirrhosis who have achieved sustained viral response (SVR) from Hepatitis C treatment.
35973177.eligibility: Patients with cirrhosis who have achieved sustained viral response (SVR) from Hepatitis C treatment.
35973177.interpretation:
- If the incidence ratio is greater than 1, the patient is at a higher risk of developing HCC compared to the general population.
- The absolute risk difference indicates how much higher or lower the patient's risk is compared to the general population.
35973177.utility: The calculator helps clinicians communicate risk information to patients and encourage screening uptake. The exact clinical utility metrics such as AUC, F-score, PPV are not provided in the article.

35990213.title: Tumor Marker Score (TMS) Calculator for Single Small Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC)
35990213.code:
def calculate_tms(afp, cea, ca19_9):
    """
    Calculate the Tumor Marker Score (TMS) based on AFP, CEA, and CA19-9 levels.

    Parameters:
    afp (bool): True if AFP level is high, False if low.
    cea (bool): True if CEA level is high, False if low.
    ca19_9 (bool): True if CA19-9 level is high, False if low.

    Returns:
    int: The TMS score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    return int(afp) + int(cea) + int(ca19_9)
35990213.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the postoperative recurrence of single small HCC based on preoperative serum AFP, CEA, and CA19-9 levels.
35990213.eligibility: Patients with one resectable HCC with a diameter of 3 cm or less.
35990213.interpretation:
- TMS 0: Low risk of postoperative recurrence
- TMS 1: Moderate risk of postoperative recurrence
- TMS 2: High risk of postoperative recurrence
35990213.utility: The 1-, 3-, and 5-year AUC values of TMS were 0.698, 0.662, and 0.673, respectively, indicating a good predictive performance. The AUC of TMS was higher than that of other common prognostic models in time-dependent receiver operating curve.

36007987.title: FOURIER Biomarker Risk Score Calculator
36007987.code:
def compute_risk_score(hsCRP, NT_proBNP, hsTnI, GDF_15):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the FOURIER biomarker model.
    
    Parameters:
    hsCRP (float): High-sensitivity C-reactive protein level in mg/L.
    NT_proBNP (float): N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide level in pg/mL.
    hsTnI (float): High-sensitivity troponin I level in ng/L.
    GDF_15 (float): Growth-differentiation factor-15 level in pg/mL.
    
    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    score = 0
    if hsCRP >= 2:
        score += 1
    if NT_proBNP >= 450:
        score += 1
    if hsTnI >= 6:
        score += 1
    if GDF_15 >= 1800:
        score += 1
    return score
36007987.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of major coronary events and complex revascularization procedures in patients with stable atherosclerosis.
36007987.eligibility: Patients with stable atherosclerosis are eligible for this calculator.
36007987.interpretation:
- 0 biomarkers elevated: Low risk
- 1-2 biomarkers elevated: Intermediate risk (HRadj: 1.57 for MCE, 1.33 for complex revascularization)
- ≥3 biomarkers elevated: High risk (HRadj: 2.90 for MCE, 2.07 for complex revascularization)
36007987.utility: The biomarker-based strategy identified stable patients at risk for coronary events, including coronary artery bypass graft surgery and complex percutaneous coronary intervention, and predicted high-risk coronary anatomy at the time of revascularization.

36028979.title: Allo-HCT Prognostic Score Calculator
36028979.code:
def compute_score(age, hla_mismatch):
    """
    Compute the allo-HCT prognostic score based on recipient's age and HLA mismatch status.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the recipient.
    hla_mismatch (bool): Whether there is any HLA mismatch (except HLA-DQ).

    Returns:
    int: The allo-HCT prognostic score.
    """
    if age < 50:
        age_score = 0
    elif age < 65:
        age_score = 1
    else:
        age_score = 2

    hla_score = 1 if hla_mismatch else 0

    return age_score + hla_score
36028979.purpose: This calculator is used to predict overall survival (OS) and non-relapse mortality (NRM) in allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (allo-HCT) recipients.
36028979.eligibility: Patients who have undergone allo-HCT for myeloid and lymphoid malignancies.
36028979.interpretation:
- Score 0: 2-year OS is 62%, NRM is 24%
- Score 1: 2-year OS is 53%, NRM is 34%
- Score 2 or 3: 2-year OS is 38%, NRM is 43%
36028979.utility: The scoring system accurately predicted OS and NRM in two distinct cohorts of allo-HCT patients (p < 0.001).

36029956.title: LNM Pre-Operative Radiological Score Calculator
36029956.code:
def compute_lnm_score(para_aortic_involvement: bool, colon_involvement: bool) -> int:
    """
    Compute the LNM pre-operative radiological score.

    Args:
    para_aortic_involvement (bool): Whether there is para-aortic lymph node involvement.
    colon_involvement (bool): Whether there is colon involvement.

    Returns:
    int: The LNM pre-operative radiological score.
    """
    return int(para_aortic_involvement) + int(colon_involvement)
36029956.purpose: This calculator is used to predict pelvic and/or para-aortic lymph node metastasis (LNM) in patients with advanced epithelial ovarian cancer (AEOC) undergoing cytoreductive surgery.
36029956.eligibility: Patients with advanced epithelial ovarian cancer (AEOC) who are undergoing cytoreductive surgery are eligible.
36029956.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of pelvic and/or para-aortic LNM.
- Score 1 or 2: High risk of pelvic and/or para-aortic LNM. The probability of pelvic and/or para-aortic LNM is 76%.
36029956.utility: The specificity of the score is 85.7% (95CI [67.3-96.0]) and the positive likelihood ratio is 3.6 (95CI [1.4-9.7]) in the construction sample. In the validation sample, the specificity is 78.3% and the positive likelihood ratio is 1.2.

36037228.title: Pediatric COVID-19 Pneumonia Predictive Score
36037228.code:
def compute_score(age, comorbidities, fever, dyspnea):
    """
    Compute the pneumonia risk score for a child with COVID-19.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the child in years. If age < 5, score is 1, else 0.
    comorbidities (int): Number of comorbidities. Each comorbidity adds 1 to the score.
    fever (bool): Presence of fever. If true, score is 1, else 0.
    dyspnea (bool): Presence of dyspnea symptoms. If true, score is 1, else 0.

    Returns:
    int: The total pneumonia risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age < 5:
        score += 1
    score += comorbidities
    if fever:
        score += 1
    if dyspnea:
        score += 1
    return score
36037228.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of pneumonia in children with COVID-19.
36037228.eligibility: Children aged 0-15 years with COVID-19 are eligible.
36037228.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of pneumonia, with a positive likelihood ratio of 0.544 (95% CI: 0.491-0.602)
- Score 2-3: Moderate risk of pneumonia, with a positive likelihood ratio of 1.563 (95% CI: 1.454-1.679)
- Score 4 and above: High risk of pneumonia, with a positive likelihood ratio of 4.339 (95% CI: 2.527-7.449)
36037228.utility: The predictive ability of the pneumonia score was acceptable with the area under the receiver operating characteristics curve of 0.677 and a well-calibrated goodness-of-fit test (p = 0.098).

36043339.title: COVID-19 Severity Predictor Using MELD Score
36043339.code:
import math

def calculate_meld_score(bilirubin, inr, creatinine):
    """
    Calculate MELD score for a patient.

    Parameters:
    bilirubin (float): Bilirubin level in mg/dL.
    inr (float): International Normalized Ratio.
    creatinine (float): Creatinine level in mg/dL.

    Returns:
    float: MELD score.
    """
    meld_score = 3.78*math.log(bilirubin) + 11.2*math.log(inr) + 9.57*math.log(creatinine) + 6.43
    return meld_score
36043339.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the severity of COVID-19 in patients, including the likelihood of ICU admission, intubation, and in-hospital mortality.
36043339.eligibility: Patients over the age of 18 who are hospitalized with a diagnosis of COVID-19.
36043339.interpretation:
- MELD Score < 10: Lower risk of ICU admission, intubation, and in-hospital mortality.
- MELD Score ≥ 10: Higher risk of ICU admission, intubation, and in-hospital mortality.
- MELD Score ≥ 18.5: Predicts ICU admission and in-hospital mortality with 99% sensitivity and 100% specificity.
36043339.utility: The MELD score was found to be an independent predictor of ICU admission, intubation, and in-hospital mortality in COVID-19 patients. The AUC for predicting ICU admission and in-hospital mortality was 0.740 and 0.797 respectively.

36046129.title: SII-PNI Score Calculator for Sarcopenia in Locally Advanced Gastric Cancer Patients
36046129.code:
def compute_sii_pni_score(sii, pni):
    """
    Compute the SII-PNI score based on the given SII and PNI values.

    Args:
    sii (float): Systemic immune-inflammatory index.
    pni (float): Prognostic nutritional index.

    Returns:
    int: The SII-PNI score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    if sii >= 432.9 and pni <= 49.5:
        return 2
    elif sii >= 432.9 or pni <= 49.5:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
36046129.purpose: This calculator is used to screen patients with locally advanced gastric cancer (LAGC) for sarcopenia on admission for early diagnosis.
36046129.eligibility: Patients with locally advanced gastric cancer (LAGC) undergoing radical surgical resection are eligible.
36046129.interpretation:
- Score 2: High risk of sarcopenia.
- Score 1: Moderate risk of sarcopenia.
- Score 0: Low risk of sarcopenia.
36046129.utility: The SII-PNI score was significantly lower in the non-sarcopenic patients than in the sarcopenic patients (p < 0.001). Logistic multivariate analysis showed that the SII-PNI score predicted an independent prognostic factor for sarcopenia (p < 0.001). Patients with high SII-PNI scores had significantly worse prognosis than those with low SII-PNI scores (p < 0.001). The SII-PNI score was an independent prognostic factor for predicting overall survival and disease-free survival (p = 0.016, 0.023).

36048818.title: 17-gene Genomic Prostate Score (GPS) Assay Risk Calculator
36048818.code:
def calculate_risk_score(GPS_result):
    """
    Calculate the risk score based on the GPS result.

    Parameters:
    GPS_result (int): The result of the 17-gene Oncotype DX Genomic Prostate Score® (GPS™) assay.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    return GPS_result
36048818.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of biochemical recurrence in men with intermediate and high-risk prostate cancer post-prostatectomy.
36048818.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with National Comprehensive Cancer Network® (NCCN) intermediate and higher risk prostate cancer, treated with radical prostatectomy.
36048818.interpretation:
- GPS result 0-40: Lower risk of biochemical recurrence.
- GPS result 41-100: Higher risk of biochemical recurrence.
36048818.utility: The GPS assay was a strong predictor of biochemical recurrence after radical prostatectomy in this unfavorable intermediate and higher risk prostate cancer patient population. The hazard ratio per 20 GPS units was 2.14 (95% CI 1.31-3.46, p = 0.003) and for GPS result 41-100 vs 0-40 was 3.00 (95% CI 1.43-6.72, p = 0.003).

36049615.title: Streptococcus Anginosus Group Bacteremia Risk Score Calculator
36049615.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, solid_tumor, c_reactive_protein, wbc_count, diabetes):
    """
    Compute the risk score for SAG bacteremia.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient in years.
    solid_tumor (bool): Whether the patient has a solid tumor.
    c_reactive_protein (float): Level of C-reactive protein in mg/dL.
    wbc_count (int): White blood cell count per μL.
    diabetes (bool): Whether the patient has diabetes.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 63:
        score += 2
    if solid_tumor:
        score += 2
    if c_reactive_protein > 7.61:
        score += 3
    if wbc_count > 8550:
        score += 2
    if diabetes:
        score += 1
    return score
36049615.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of bacteremia caused by the Streptococcus Anginosus Group (SAG) in patients with Viridans group streptococci (VGS) bacteremia.
36049615.eligibility: Patients with VGS bacteremia are eligible for this calculator.
36049615.interpretation:
- Score < 6: Low risk of SAG bacteremia
- Score >= 6: High risk of SAG bacteremia
36049615.utility: The area under the receiver operating curve (AUC) for this risk score is 0.81, indicating good predictive performance. The sensitivity and specificity were 51% and 88%, respectively, at a cut-off of 6 points.

36050367.title: Preoperative Albumin-Bilirubin (ALBI) Score Calculator for Stage III Colon Cancer
36050367.code:
import math

def calculate_albi_score(bilirubin, albumin):
    """
    Calculate the ALBI score for a patient with stage III colon cancer.

    Parameters:
    bilirubin (float): The patient's bilirubin level in μmol/L.
    albumin (float): The patient's albumin level in g/L.

    Returns:
    float: The patient's ALBI score.
    """
    return (math.log10(bilirubin) * 0.66) + (albumin * -0.0852)
36050367.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with stage III colon cancer based on their preoperative albumin and bilirubin levels.
36050367.eligibility: Patients with stage III colon cancer who are preparing for surgery.
36050367.interpretation:
- ALBI ≤ -2.54: Higher 5-year disease-free survival rate (85.5%), cancer-specific survival rate (93.7%), and overall survival rate (90.6%).
- ALBI > -2.54: Lower 5-year disease-free survival rate (75.7%), cancer-specific survival rate (84.4%), and overall survival rate (77.4%).
36050367.utility: High ALBI scores were found to be an independent risk factor for both disease-free survival (HR 1.68, p = 0.048) and cancer-specific survival (HR 2.24, p = 0.028).

36053949.title: Autoimmune Encephalitis Risk Score Calculator
36053949.code:
def compute_risk_score(presentation, charlson_index, complaints, inflammation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for Autoimmune Encephalitis.

    Args:
    presentation (bool): True if the patient has a subacute to chronic presentation, False otherwise.
    charlson_index (float): The Charlson comorbidity index of the patient.
    complaints (bool): True if the patient has psychiatric and/or memory complaints, False otherwise.
    inflammation (bool): True if the patient has absence of robust inflammation in the cerebrospinal fluid, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for Autoimmune Encephalitis.
    """
    score = 0
    if presentation:
        score += 1
    if charlson_index < 2:
        score += 1
    if complaints:
        score += 1
    if inflammation:
        score += 1
    return score
36053949.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the probability of Autoimmune Encephalitis (AE) in patients presenting with encephalitis, assisting with earlier diagnosis and treatment.
36053949.eligibility: Patients presenting with symptoms of encephalitis are eligible for this calculator.
36053949.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk for AE
- Score 2-3: Intermediate risk for AE
- Score 4: High risk for AE
36053949.utility: The performance of the score achieved an area under the curve of 0.918 (95% CI, .871-.966).

36054554.title: Lymphoepithelial Carcinoma of Salivary Gland Distant Metastasis Risk Calculator
36054554.code:
def compute_risk_score(ldh_ratio, n_classification):
    """
    Compute the risk score for distant metastasis in LECSG patients.

    Parameters:
    ldh_ratio (float): The preradiotherapy serum LDH ratio.
    n_classification (str): The N classification of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if ldh_ratio > 0.5:
        score += 1
    if n_classification in ['N2', 'N3']:
        score += 1
    return score
36054554.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of distant metastasis (DM) for patients with lymphoepithelial carcinoma of the salivary gland (LECSG) following postoperative radiotherapy (PORT).
36054554.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with lymphoepithelial carcinoma of the salivary gland (LECSG) who are about to undergo or have undergone postoperative radiotherapy (PORT) are eligible.
36054554.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk group with a 5-year cumulative incidence of DM of 6.0%
- Score 1-2: High risk group with a 5-year cumulative incidence of DM of 30.9%
36054554.utility: The risk score has been shown to significantly stratify patients into high and low risk groups for DM (p < 0.001). It also correlates with overall survival, with high-risk patients having a significantly worse 5-year overall survival rate (83.9% vs. 97.8%, p = 0.006).

36055816.title: Liver Transplantation Difficulty Score
36055816.code:
def calculate_difficulty_score(retransplantation, portal_vein_thrombosis, previous_surgery, peritonitis, variceal_bleeding):
    """
    Calculate the difficulty score for liver transplantation.

    Parameters:
    retransplantation (bool): Whether the patient has had a liver transplant before.
    portal_vein_thrombosis (bool): Whether the patient has preoperative portal vein thrombosis.
    previous_surgery (bool): Whether the patient has had previous upper abdominal surgery.
    peritonitis (bool): Whether the patient has spontaneous bacterial peritonitis.
    variceal_bleeding (bool): Whether the patient has had prior variceal bleeding.

    Returns:
    float: The difficulty score.
    """
    score = 0
    if retransplantation:
        score += 4.34
    if portal_vein_thrombosis:
        score += 3.419
    if previous_surgery:
        score += 2.161
    if peritonitis:
        score += 1.985
    if variceal_bleeding:
        score += 1.401
    return score
36055816.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the difficulty of liver transplantation surgery based on preoperative factors.
36055816.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing deceased donor whole liver transplantation.
36055816.interpretation:
- Score < 4: Low risk of difficult surgery (negative predictive value of 84%)
