36351706.purpose: This calculator is used to classify patients with Giant Cell Arteritis (GCA) based on a points-based risk classification score.
36351706.eligibility: Patients aged 50 years or older at diagnosis who are suspected of having vasculitis or comparator diseases.
36351706.interpretation: A patient could be classified as having GCA with a cumulative score of ≥6 points.
36351706.utility: The model has an area under the curve of 0.91 (95% CI 0.88 to 0.94) with a sensitivity of 87.0% (95% CI 82.0% to 91.0%) and specificity of 94.8% (95% CI 91.0% to 97.4%).

36353420.title: Comorbidity Risk Score (CRS) for Postoperative Complications in Total Shoulder Arthroplasty
36353420.code:
def compute_crs(end_stage_renal, hypertension, copd, functional_status, bleeding_disorder, cancer):
    """
    Compute the Comorbidity Risk Score (CRS).
    
    Parameters:
    end_stage_renal (bool): True if the patient has end-stage renal disease, False otherwise.
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has a history of hypertension, False otherwise.
    copd (bool): True if the patient has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, False otherwise.
    functional_status (bool): True if the patient has functional status, False otherwise.
    bleeding_disorder (bool): True if the patient has a history of bleeding disorder, False otherwise.
    cancer (bool): True if the patient has disseminated cancer, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The computed CRS.
    """
    return end_stage_renal + hypertension + copd + functional_status + bleeding_disorder + cancer
36353420.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of 30-day postoperative complications in patients undergoing total shoulder arthroplasty.
36353420.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing total shoulder arthroplasty are eligible.
36353420.interpretation:
- CRS of 0 or 1: The patient has a lower risk of 30-day postoperative complications, with an average complication rate of approximately 15%.
- CRS of 2 or above: The patient has a higher risk of 30-day postoperative complications, with a complication rate of at least 29.6%.
36353420.utility: The area under the receiver operator curve for the CRS scoring system was 0.595, indicating fair discriminative ability to predict 30-day postoperative complications after total shoulder arthroplasty.

36353554.title: Inflammation-Water Score (IWS) Calculator for Colorectal Cancer Prognosis
36353554.code:
def compute_iws(nlr, icw_tbw_ratio):
    """
    Compute the Inflammation-Water Score (IWS) for colorectal cancer prognosis.

    Parameters:
    nlr (float): Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio
    icw_tbw_ratio (float): Intracellular water/total body water ratio

    Returns:
    int: IWS score
    """
    iws = 0
    if nlr >= 2.42:
        iws += 1
    if icw_tbw_ratio < 0.61:
        iws += 1
    return iws
36353554.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival in patients with colorectal cancer based on the neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR) and intracellular water/total body water (ICW/TBW) ratio.
36353554.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with colorectal cancer are eligible for this calculator.
36353554.interpretation:
- IWS score 0: Lower risk of poor prognosis
- IWS score 1: Moderate risk of poor prognosis (HR: 1.74, 95% CI 1.17-2.57)
- IWS score 2: High risk of poor prognosis (HR: 2.86, 95% CI: 1.77-4.63)
36353554.utility: The IWS score showed better predictive ability for C-index, IDI index, and cNRI compared to its component factors.

36358743.title: Intrahepatic Cholangiocarcinoma Prognostic Score Calculator
36358743.code:
def compute_score(GGT, ECOG, albumin):
    """
    Compute the prognostic score for intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma patients.

    Parameters:
    GGT (float): Gamma-glutamyl transferase level in U/L.
    ECOG (int): Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status.
    albumin (float): Albumin level in g/L.

    Returns:
    int: The prognostic score.
    """
    score = 0
    if GGT >= 750:
        score += 1
    if ECOG > 1:
        score += 1
    if albumin <= 41.1:
        score += 1
    return score
36358743.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival (OS) after Radioembolization (RE) therapy in patients with intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC) using pre-therapeutic parameters.
36358743.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC) and are undergoing or planning to undergo Radioembolization (RE) therapy.
36358743.interpretation:
- 0 risk factors: Median OS of 15.3 months
- 1 risk factor: Median OS of 7.6 months
- 2 or more risk factors: Median OS of 1.8 months
36358743.utility: The prognostic score was found to be significantly associated with overall survival (p < 0.001).

36358880.title: PSA-Only Recurrence Risk Model
36358880.code:
def compute_risk_group(psa_level, positive_sites):
    """
    Compute the risk group based on prescan PSA level and the number of positive sites on PSMA PET/CT.

    Args:
    psa_level (float): The prescan PSA level in ng/mL.
    positive_sites (int): The number of positive sites on PSMA PET/CT.

    Returns:
    str: The risk group ('good', 'intermediate', 'poor').
    """
    if psa_level < 0.5:
        return 'good'
    elif psa_level > 0.5 and 1 <= positive_sites <= 5:
        return 'intermediate'
    elif psa_level > 0.5 and positive_sites > 5:
        return 'poor'
36358880.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 5-year overall survival (OS) for patients with PSA-only recurrence (biochemical recurrence, BCR) who have undergone restaging with [68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11 PET/CT before the salvage treatment.
36358880.eligibility: Patients with PSA-only recurrence (biochemical recurrence, BCR) who have undergone restaging with [68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11 PET/CT before the salvage treatment.
36358880.interpretation:
- 'good': This group has the lowest risk. It includes patients with a prescan PSA < 0.5 ng/mL.
- 'intermediate': This group has an intermediate risk. It includes patients with a prescan PSA > 0.5 ng/mL and a PSMA PET/CT with 1 to 5 positive sites.
- 'poor': This group has the highest risk. It includes patients with a prescan PSA > 0.5 ng/mL and a PSMA PET/CT with > 5 positive sites. The five-year overall survival (OS) for this group is > 60%.
36358880.utility: The risk model significantly predicted the 5-year overall survival (OS) (p < 0.0001, log rank test). Adding a BCR risk score by the European Association of Urology did not significantly improve the prediction of OS (p = 0.64).

36359306.title: T-Lymphocyte Subset Index (TLSI) Calculator
36359306.code:
def calculate_TLSI(CD4_count, CD8_count):
    """
    Calculate the T-Lymphocyte Subset Index (TLSI) based on the number of T-cell subset absolute counts.

    Parameters:
    CD4_count (float): The absolute count of CD4+ T-cells.
    CD8_count (float): The absolute count of CD8+ T-cells.

    Returns:
    int: The TLSI score.
    """
    TLSI = 0
    if CD4_count < 369:
        TLSI += 1
    if CD8_count < 194:
        TLSI += 1
    return TLSI
36359306.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 30-day mortality in COVID-19 hospitalized patients based on the number of T-cell subset (CD4+ and CD8+) absolute counts.
36359306.eligibility: Patients who are hospitalized due to COVID-19 and have their T-cell subset absolute counts measured at ward admission are eligible.
36359306.interpretation:
- TLSI = 0: Lower risk of 30-day mortality.
- TLSI > 0: Increased risk of 30-day mortality.
36359306.utility: The TLSI was found to be an independent predictor of in-hospital 30-day mortality with an odds ratio of 1.893 (p = 0.003). Patients with a TLSI > 0 had an increased risk of death compared to patients with a TLSI = 0 (hazard ratio: 2.83, p < 0.0001).

36387347.title: Difficult Caudal Epidural Blockade Prediction Model (pDCEB)
36387347.code:
def compute_pDCEB_score(unclear_hiatus, unclear_cornua, hiatus_number, history_difficult_CEB):
    """
    Compute the pDCEB score.

    Parameters:
    unclear_hiatus (bool): True if palpation of the sacral hiatus is unclear, False otherwise.
    unclear_cornua (bool): True if palpation of the cornua is unclear, False otherwise.
    hiatus_number (int): Number of the sacral hiatus by palpation.
    history_difficult_CEB (bool): True if there is a history of difficult CEB, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    float: The pDCEB score.
    """
    score = 0
    if unclear_hiatus:
        score += 9.688
    if unclear_cornua:
        score += 4.725
    if hiatus_number >= 1:
        score += 4.451
    if history_difficult_CEB:
        score += 39.282
    return score
36387347.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the difficulty of caudal epidural blockade (CEB) when ultrasound is not available.
36387347.eligibility: Patients scheduled to undergo caudal epidural anesthesia are eligible.
36387347.interpretation:
- pDCEB score < 3: Low risk of difficult CEB
- pDCEB score ≥ 3: High risk of difficult CEB. It is suggested to perform an ultrasound scan.
36387347.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of the pDCEB model was 0.889 (95% CI, 0.827-0.952) in the development cohort and 0.862 (95% CI, 0.747-0.977) in the validation cohort.

36388651.title: Gastric Adenocarcinoma Chemotherapy-Associated Gene Signature Risk Model
36388651.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expression, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on gene expression levels and coefficients.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (dict): A dictionary where the key is the gene name and the value is the gene expression level.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the key is the gene name and the value is the corresponding coefficient.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    for gene, expression in gene_expression.items():
        risk_score += expression * coefficients[gene]
    return risk_score
36388651.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with gastric adenocarcinoma based on a risk model of chemotherapy-associated genes.
36388651.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with gastric adenocarcinoma who are undergoing or have undergone chemotherapy are eligible.
36388651.interpretation:
The risk score is interpreted as follows:
- High risk: Risk score > median risk score. These patients have a shorter overall survival time and are less likely to benefit from immunotherapy and less sensitive to cisplatin.
- Low risk: Risk score <= median risk score. These patients have a longer overall survival time and are more likely to benefit from immunotherapy and more sensitive to cisplatin.
36388651.utility: The model demonstrated good predictability with area under the curve (AUC) for 1-5 years in the training set being 0.78, 0.78, 0.76, 0.79, and 0.81.

36404251.title: Preoperative Controlling Nutritional Status (CONUT) Score Calculator for Biliary Tract Cancer
36404251.code:
def compute_risk_group(conut_score):
    """
    Compute the risk group based on the preoperative CONUT score.

    Args:
    conut_score (int): The preoperative CONUT score of the patient.

    Returns:
    str: The risk group of the patient ('CONUT-low' or 'CONUT-high').
    """
    if conut_score <= 3:
        return 'CONUT-low'
    else:
        return 'CONUT-high'
36404251.purpose: This calculator is used to predict survival outcomes and the risk of systemic disease recurrence in patients with resectable biliary tract cancer (BTC) based on their preoperative CONUT score.
36404251.eligibility: Patients with resectable biliary tract cancer (BTC) who are about to undergo surgery are eligible.
36404251.interpretation:
- 'CONUT-low' (score ≤ 3): Associated with better overall survival, disease-specific survival, and recurrence-free survival. The incidence of distant recurrence at 5 years is 34.2%.
- 'CONUT-high' (score > 3): Associated with worse overall survival, disease-specific survival, and recurrence-free survival. The incidence of distant recurrence at 5 years is 55.4%.
36404251.utility: The preoperative CONUT score was found to be independently associated with overall survival (hazard ratio [HR] 1.906, p = 0.001), disease-specific survival (HR 1.840, p = 0.006), and recurrence-free survival (HR 1.680, p = 0.005). It was also associated with a higher risk of distant recurrence (HR 2.245, p = 0.001).

36411080.title: Serum Neurofilament Light Chain (sNfL) Risk Score Calculator for Multiple Sclerosis
36411080.code:
def compute_risk_score(sNfL):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on sNfL levels.

    Args:
    sNfL (float): The serum neurofilament light chain level in pg/mL.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score.
    """
    if sNfL <= 8.6:
        return 0.244
    else:
        return 1
36411080.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of severe focal axonal damage and disease activity in patients with Multiple Sclerosis (MS) based on serum neurofilament light chain (sNfL) levels.
36411080.eligibility: Patients with relapsing-remitting MS who have undergone Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) assessment within 3 months and MRI within 12 months around blood sampling at baseline and follow-up are eligible.
36411080.interpretation:
- Risk Score ≤ 0.244: 76% risk reduction for EDAT1 at y6.
- Risk Score = 1: No risk reduction for EDAT1 at y6.
36411080.utility: Adding sNfL to the risk score significantly improved NEDA-3T1 prediction (0.697 95% CI 0.616-0.770 vs 0.819 95% CI 0.747-0.878, p < 0.001).

36426167.title: NIPPV Weaning Success Prediction Score
36426167.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, pneumonia, pulse_rate, urinary_output, respiratory_rate, oxygen_saturation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for weaning from NIPPV based on the patient's clinical parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Patient's age in years.
    pneumonia (bool): Whether the patient has pneumonia.
    pulse_rate (int): Patient's pulse rate in bpm before NIPPV.
    urinary_output (float): Patient's urinary output in cc/h while using NIPPV.
    respiratory_rate (int): Patient's respiratory rate in breaths/min before weaning.
    oxygen_saturation (float): Patient's oxygen saturation as assessed by pulse oximetry before weaning from NIPPV.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 75:
        score += 3.1
    if pneumonia:
        score += 2.72
    if pulse_rate > 80:
        score += 1.74
    if urinary_output < 150:
        score += 2.93
    if 60 <= age <= 75:
        score += 0.5
    if respiratory_rate > 26:
        score += 0.5
    if oxygen_saturation < 97:
        score += 0.5
    return score
36426167.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the success of weaning patients with acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema from noninvasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV) in the emergency department.
36426167.eligibility: Patients with acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema who are initiated and weaned off noninvasive positive pressure ventilation (NIPPV) at the emergency department are eligible.
36426167.interpretation:
- Score ≤ 3.5: Low risk of weaning failure
- Score 4-5: Moderate risk of weaning failure
- Score > 5: High risk of weaning failure
36426167.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve for this risk score is 71.3% (95% CI: 66.0-75.7%).

36426570.title: Preoperative Albumin-to-Alkaline Phosphatase Ratio (AAPR) Calculator for Locally Advanced Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma (LAOSCC)
36426570.code:
def calculate_aapr(albumin, alkaline_phosphatase):
    """
    Calculate the Albumin-to-Alkaline Phosphatase Ratio (AAPR)

    Parameters:
    albumin (float): The albumin level of the patient
    alkaline_phosphatase (float): The alkaline phosphatase level of the patient

    Returns:
    float: The AAPR of the patient
    """
    return albumin / alkaline_phosphatase
36426570.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 5-year overall survival (OS), cancer-specific survival (CSS), and disease-free survival (DFS) for patients with LAOSCC based on their preoperative AAPR.
36426570.eligibility: Patients with locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma (LAOSCC) who are about to undergo radical surgery.
36426570.interpretation:
- AAPR ≥ 0.51: The 5-year OS, CSS, and DFS are statistically significantly superior. (OS: 76.1%, CSS: 84.3%, DFS: 68.9%)
- AAPR < 0.51: The 5-year OS, CSS, and DFS are lower. (OS: 48.5%, CSS: 66.4%, DFS: 42.6%)
36426570.utility: The c-index of the predictive nomogram incorporating preoperative AAPR and various clinicopathological factors increases from 0.663 to 0.692 for OS.

36428732.title: Early-VTE Risk Score Calculator for Acute Myeloid Leukemia Patients
36428732.code:
def compute_vte_risk_score(comorbidities: bool, platelets_count: int, history_of_vte: bool) -> int:
    """
    Compute the VTE risk score for a patient with AML.

    Args:
    comorbidities (bool): Whether the patient has comorbidities.
    platelets_count (int): The patient's platelets count.
    history_of_vte (bool): Whether the patient has a previous history of VTE.

    Returns:
    int: The VTE risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if comorbidities:
        score += 1
    if platelets_count > 50 * 10**9:
        score += 1
    if history_of_vte:
        score += 1
    return score
36428732.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of early venous thrombotic events (VTE) in adult patients with acute myeloid leukemia (AML).
36428732.eligibility: Adult patients with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia are eligible.
36428732.interpretation:
- 0-1 points: Low-risk group with a VTE incidence of 6.4%
- 2-3 points: High-risk group with a VTE incidence of 18.4%
36428732.utility: The calculator was able to distinguish between high-risk and low-risk groups for VTE occurrence with a significant difference (log-rank = 0.002).

36436010.title: Older-STEMI-CMR Score Calculator
36436010.code:
def compute_risk_score(GRACE_score, LVEF, MVO):
    """
    Compute the Older-STEMI-CMR Score.

    Parameters:
    GRACE_score (float): The patient's GRACE score.
    LVEF (float): The patient's left ventricular ejection fraction (%).
    MVO (int): The number of segments with microvascular obstruction.

    Returns:
    int: The Older-STEMI-CMR Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if GRACE_score > 155:
        score += 1
    if LVEF < 40:
        score += 1
    if MVO >= 2:
        score += 1
    return score
36436010.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of major adverse cardiac events (MACE) in older patients with ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) who have undergone cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR).
36436010.eligibility: Patients over 70 years of age who have been treated with percutaneous intervention for STEMI and have undergone CMR.
36436010.interpretation:
- Score 0: 0.78 MACE per 100 person-years
- Score 1: 5.53 MACE per 100 person-years
- Score 2: 11.51 MACE per 100 person-years
- Score 3: 78.79 MACE per 100 person-years
36436010.utility: The Older-STEMI-CMR Score significantly improved MACE prediction compared to the model with baseline and echocardiographic characteristics (C-statistic 0.759 vs. 0.685, NRI = 0.6, IDI = 0.08, P < 0.001).

36437550.title: Rectal Cancer Response and Prognosis Prediction Calculator
36437550.code:
def compute_risk_score(MTV, SUV):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on rectal MTV and lymph node highest peak SUV.

    Parameters:
    MTV (float): Rectal metabolic tumor volume in cm^3.
    SUV (float): Lymph node highest peak standard uptake value.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score (0, 1, or 2).
    """
    score = 0
    if SUV > 3.13:
        score += 1
    if MTV > 27:
        score += 1
    return score
36437550.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the response to neoadjuvant therapy and disease-free survival (DFS) in patients with locally advanced rectal cancer.
36437550.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with locally advanced rectal cancer who are about to receive neoadjuvant therapy.
36437550.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk group. Median DFS not reached.
- Score 1: Intermediate risk group. Median DFS not reached.
- Score 2: High risk group. Median DFS of 19 months.
36437550.utility: The risk scoring performed with regard to rectal MTV and lymph node highest peak SUV values determined a median DFS of 19 months in patients with a risk score of 2, whereas the median DFS was not reached in patients with risk scores of 0 and 1 (P < 0.001).

36446430.title: Renal Risk Score (RRS) for Anti-Glomerular Basement Membrane Disease
36446430.code:
def compute_rrs(rrt, normal_glomeruli):
    """
    Compute the Renal Risk Score (RRS) for anti-GBM disease.

    Parameters:
    rrt (bool): Whether the patient needs renal replacement therapy at diagnosis. True for yes, False for no.
    normal_glomeruli (float): The percentage of normal glomeruli in the kidney biopsy.

    Returns:
    str: The risk group of the patient.
    """
    if not rrt and normal_glomeruli >= 10:
        return 'low-risk'
    elif not rrt and normal_glomeruli < 10:
        return 'moderate-risk'
    elif rrt and normal_glomeruli >= 10:
        return 'high-risk'
    else:
        return 'very high-risk'
36446430.purpose: This calculator is used to predict renal survival in patients with anti-glomerular basement membrane (GBM) disease.
36446430.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with anti-glomerular basement membrane (GBM) disease are eligible.
36446430.interpretation:
- 'low-risk': 36-month renal survival rate is 96.4%
- 'moderate-risk': 36-month renal survival rate is 74.0%
- 'high-risk': 36-month renal survival rate is 42.3%
- 'very high-risk': 36-month renal survival rate is 14.1%
36446430.utility: The Renal Risk Score (RRS) for anti-GBM disease was found to be a strong predictor for End-Stage Kidney Disease (ESKD). The need for renal replacement therapy (RRT) at diagnosis and the percentage of normal glomeruli in the biopsy were independent predictors of ESKD.

36447068.title: qSOFAGE Score Calculator
36447068.code:
def compute_qsofage(qsofa_score, temp_at_admission):
    """
    Compute the qSOFAGE score.

    Parameters:
    qsofa_score (int): The qSOFA score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    temp_at_admission (float): The patient's temperature at admission in Celsius.

    Returns:
    int: The qSOFAGE score.
    """
    if temp_at_admission <= 38:
        return qsofa_score + 1
    else:
        return qsofa_score
36447068.purpose: This calculator is used to predict in-hospital mortality in older patients with acute pneumonia (AP) or bacteremic urinary tract infection (UTI) by incorporating temperature at admission into the Quick Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (qSOFA).
36447068.eligibility: Patients aged 75 or older who are hospitalized for acute pneumonia or bacteremic urinary tract infection.
36447068.interpretation:
- qSOFAGE score < 3: Lower risk of in-hospital mortality.
- qSOFAGE score ≥ 3: Higher risk of in-hospital mortality, reaching 37% after acute pneumonia and 55% after bacteremic urinary tract infection.
36447068.utility: The qSOFAGE score showed improved predictive value for in-hospital mortality compared to the qSOFA score alone, with C-statistics of 0.63 vs. 0.56 in the AP cohort and 0.74 vs. 0.69 in the UTI cohort.

36449204.title: Gastric Cancer Post-Gastrectomy Death Risk Calculator
36449204.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, sex, bmi, ecog_status, diabetes, cvd, other_cancer, albumin, total_gastrectomy):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the patient's characteristics.
    
    Parameters:
    age (int): patient's age
    sex (str): patient's sex, 'male' or 'female'
    bmi (float): patient's body mass index
    ecog_status (int): patient's Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status, 0 or 1
    diabetes (bool): whether the patient has diabetes, True or False
    cvd (bool): whether the patient has cardiovascular/cerebrovascular disease, True or False
    other_cancer (bool): whether the patient has other malignant diseases, True or False
    albumin (float): patient's preoperative albumin level
    total_gastrectomy (bool): whether the patient underwent total gastrectomy, True or False
    
    Returns:
    int: risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 75:
        score += 1
    if sex == 'male':
        score += 1
    if bmi < 22:
        score += 1
    if ecog_status >= 1:
        score += 1
    if diabetes:
        score += 1
    if cvd:
        score += 1
    if other_cancer:
        score += 1
    if albumin < 3.5:
        score += 1
    if total_gastrectomy:
        score += 1
    return score
36449204.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of death from causes other than gastric cancer after curative gastrectomy.
36449204.eligibility: Patients who have undergone gastrectomy for gastric cancer are eligible.
36449204.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low-risk group, with a 5-year survival rate of 96.5%
- Score 3-4: Intermediate-risk group, with a 5-year survival rate of 85.3%
- Score 5-9: High-risk group, with a 5-year survival rate of 56.5%
36449204.utility: The hazard ratio for the High-risk group was 16.33 (95% confidence intervals: 10.85-24.58, p < 0.001), indicating a significantly higher risk of death from other causes after gastrectomy.

36450240.title: Electronic Frailty Index (eFI) for COVID-19 Patients
36450240.code:
def compute_efi(deficits):
    """
    Compute the Electronic Frailty Index (eFI) for a patient.

    Args:
    deficits (int): The number of health deficits present in the patient.

    Returns:
    float: The eFI score, calculated as the proportion of deficits present (deficits / 48).
    """
    return deficits / 48
36450240.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of in-hospital mortality, 30-day mortality, 6-month mortality, and length of stay in older COVID-19 patients.
36450240.eligibility: Older patients diagnosed with COVID-19 and admitted to the hospital are eligible.
36450240.interpretation:
The eFI score ranges from 0 to 1. A higher score indicates a higher level of frailty and a higher risk of adverse outcomes. Specifically, a 10% increment in the eFI is associated with:

- 2.95 times higher risk of in-hospital mortality
- 2.39 times higher risk of 30-day mortality
- 2.29 times higher risk of 6-month mortality
- Longer length of stay (β-coefficient = 2.00)
36450240.utility: The eFI demonstrated good predictive accuracy for in-hospital mortality (AUC = 0.780), 30-day mortality (Harrell's C = 0.733), and 6-month mortality (Harrell's C = 0.719).

36450551.title: Heart Failure FAN (HF-FAN) Score Calculator
36450551.code:
def compute_hf_fan_score(fibrosis_4_index, albumin_bilirubin_score, neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio):
    """
    Compute the HF-FAN score based on the fibrosis-4 index, albumin-bilirubin score, and neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio.
    
    Parameters:
    fibrosis_4_index (float): The fibrosis-4 index of the patient.
    albumin_bilirubin_score (float): The albumin-bilirubin score of the patient.
    neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio (float): The neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio of the patient.
    
    Returns:
    int: The HF-FAN score of the patient.
    """
    hf_fan_score = 0
    if fibrosis_4_index > 2.12:
        hf_fan_score += 1
    if albumin_bilirubin_score > -2.25:
        hf_fan_score += 1
    if neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio > 2.41:
        hf_fan_score += 1
    return hf_fan_score
36450551.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of all-cause death and heart failure rehospitalization in patients hospitalized with heart failure.
36450551.eligibility: Patients hospitalized with heart failure are eligible for this calculator.
36450551.interpretation:
- HF-FAN score of 0: Lowest risk group
- HF-FAN score of 1: Low risk group
- HF-FAN score of 2: High risk group
- HF-FAN score of 3: Highest risk group
36450551.utility: The HF-FAN score was significantly associated with a worse prognosis even after adjustment for possible prognostic factors. Changing from the FAN score to HF-FAN score provided significant continuous net reclassification improvement.

36463683.title: Thalassemia International Prognostic Scoring System (TIPSS)
36463683.code:
import numpy as np

def compute_tipss(heart_disease, liver_disease, diabetes, sepsis, alt, hemoglobin, ferritin):
    """
    Compute the Thalassemia International Prognostic Scoring System (TIPSS) score.

    Parameters:
    heart_disease (bool): Whether the patient has heart disease.
    liver_disease (bool): Whether the patient has liver disease.
    diabetes (bool): Whether the patient has diabetes.
    sepsis (bool): Whether the patient has sepsis.
    alt (float): Alanine aminotransferase level in IU/L.
    hemoglobin (float): Hemoglobin level in g/dL.
    ferritin (float): Serum ferritin level in ng/mL.

    Returns:
    float: The TIPSS score.
    """
    score = np.exp(1.4 * heart_disease + 0.9 * liver_disease + 0.9 * diabetes + 0.9 * sepsis + 0.6 * (alt >= 42) + 0.6 * (hemoglobin <= 9) + 0.4 * (ferritin >= 1850))
    return score
36463683.purpose: This calculator is used to differentiate β-thalassemia patients based on mortality risk.
36463683.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with β-thalassemia are eligible for this calculator.
36463683.interpretation:
- TIPSS score < 2.0: Low-risk
- 2.0 ≤ TIPSS score < 5.0: Intermediate-risk
- TIPSS score ≥ 5.0: High-risk
36463683.utility: The TIPSS score was a good predictor for the outcome of death in the validation dataset (AUC: 0.722, 95%CI: 0.641-0.804). Survival was significantly different between patients in the three risk categories (P < 0.001). Compared to low-risk patients, the hazard ratio for death was 2.778 (95%CI: 1.335-5.780) in patients with intermediate-risk and 6.431 (95%CI: 3.151-13.128) in patients with high-risk.

36465816.title: Intracerebral Hemorrhage Risk Calculator in OHCA Patients
36465816.code:
def compute_risk_score(sex, age, rhythm, heart_rate, lateral_st_elevation, inferior_st_depression):
    """
    Compute the risk score for intracerebral hemorrhage in OHCA patients.

    Parameters:
    sex (str): 'male' or 'female'
    age (int): patient's age
    rhythm (str): 'shockable' or 'non-shockable'
    heart_rate (int): heart rate in bpm
    lateral_st_elevation (bool): whether there is lateral ST-segment elevation
    inferior_st_depression (bool): whether there is inferior ST-segment depression

    Returns:
    int: risk score
    """
    score = 0
    if sex == 'female':
        score += 1
    if age < 75:
        score += 1
    if rhythm == 'non-shockable':
        score += 1
    if heart_rate >= 100:
        score += 1
    if lateral_st_elevation:
        score += 1
    if inferior_st_depression:
        score += 1
    return score
36465816.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of intracerebral hemorrhage in patients with out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) who achieved return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC).
36465816.eligibility: Patients with nontraumatic out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) who achieved return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC) and do not have obvious extracardiac causes are eligible.
36465816.interpretation:
- Score 0-3: Low risk of intracerebral hemorrhage
- Score 4-6: High risk of intracerebral hemorrhage
36465816.utility: The odds ratio for intracerebral hemorrhage increased 2.36 for each 1-point increase. A score ≥ 4 had 43.7% sensitivity, 90.8% specificity, 34.1% positive predictive value, and 93.7% negative predictive value.

36465895.title: HTM-Score for Fibrotic Interstitial Lung Disease Progression
36465895.code:
def compute_HTM_score(monocyte_count, lung_fields):
    """
    Compute the HTM score for fibrotic ILD progression risk.

    Parameters:
    monocyte_count (float): Monocyte count in G/L.
    lung_fields (int): Number of lung fields with honeycombing or traction bronchiectasis.

    Returns:
    int: HTM score.
    """
    score = 0
    if monocyte_count >= 0.65:
        score += 1
    if lung_fields >= 5:
        score += 2
    elif lung_fields >= 1:
        score += 1
    return score
36465895.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the 1-year progression risk in patients with fibrotic interstitial lung disease (ILD) based on clinical, imaging, and blood biomarkers.
36465895.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with fibrotic ILD, as defined by the presence of reticulation or honeycombing, are eligible.
36465895.interpretation:
- Score 0: 1-year progression rate of 20%
- Score 1: 1-year progression rate of 25%
- Score 2: 1-year progression rate of 46.9%
- Score 3: 1-year progression rate of 88.9%
36465895.utility: The HTM-Score showed a sensitivity of 70.6% and specificity of 67.9% for predicting 1-year disease progression. The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve was 71.7%.

36478545.title: Chronic Subdural Hematoma Recurrence Risk Calculator
36478545.code:
def compute_risk_score(si_ratio, dementia):
    """
    Compute the risk score for recurrence of Chronic Subdural Hematoma (CSDH).

    Parameters:
    si_ratio (float): The signal intensity (SI) ratio of the middle meningeal artery (MMA).
    dementia (bool): Whether the patient has dementia.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score.
    """
    return 5 * si_ratio + (1 if dementia else 0)
36478545.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of postoperative recurrence in patients with Chronic Subdural Hematoma (CSDH) based on the signal intensity (SI) ratio of the middle meningeal artery (MMA) and the presence of dementia.
36478545.eligibility: Patients who have undergone surgery for Chronic Subdural Hematoma (CSDH) and have had a time-of-flight magnetic resonance angiography (TOF MRA) within 1 month before or after the initial surgery.
36478545.interpretation:
- Score of 5: The predicted probability of recurrence is 2% [95% CI 0.7, 5.7]
- Score of 8: The predicted probability of recurrence is 43.3% [27.0, 61.1]
- Score of 10: The predicted probability of recurrence is 89.5% [65.7, 97.5]
36478545.utility: The risk score was found to be a significant predictor of recurrence of CSDH, with an odds ratio of 1.71 [1.32, 2.22] for the SI ratio and 7.41 [1.83, 30.1] for dementia.

36493579.title: PCI Complication Risk Score Calculator
36493579.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, dialysis, ejection_fraction, multivessel_pci):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    dialysis (bool): Whether the patient is on dialysis.
    ejection_fraction (float): The left ventricular ejection fraction of the patient.
    multivessel_pci (bool): Whether the patient had multivessel PCI.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if age > 80:
        score += 3
    if dialysis:
        score += 6
    if ejection_fraction < 30:
        score += 2
    if multivessel_pci:
        score += 2
    return score
36493579.purpose: This calculator is used to identify patients at higher risk of complications, including all-cause death, major adverse cardiovascular events, and major bleeding, after percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI).
36493579.eligibility: Patients who underwent PCI for stable ischemic heart disease or non-ST-elevation acute coronary syndrome, not presenting with ST-elevation myocardial infarction or cardiogenic shock.
36493579.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk
- Score 2 to 3: Intermediate risk
- Score ≥4: High risk
36493579.utility: The risk score has a good predictive value for all-cause death at 1 year (area under the curve 0.70). Compared with the low-risk patients, those at intermediate and high risk had a fourfold and ninefold higher hazard for all-cause death at 1 year, respectively.

36498630.title: 60-Day Survival Score Calculator for Patients with Malignant Biliary Obstruction
36498630.code:
def calculate_survival_score(PNI, NLR):
    """
    Calculate the 60-day survival score for patients with MBO undergoing PTBD.

    Parameters:
    PNI (float): Prognostic Nutritional Index of the patient.
    NLR (float): Neutrophil to Lymphocyte Ratio of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The 60-day survival score. 0 if PNI < 36.7 or NLR > 3, 1 otherwise.
    """
    if PNI >= 36.7 and NLR <= 3:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
36498630.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the short-term survival of patients with advanced malignant biliary obstruction (MBO) undergoing percutaneous transhepatic biliary drainage (PTBD).
36498630.eligibility: Patients with advanced MBO who are undergoing PTBD are eligible for this calculator.
36498630.interpretation:
- Score 0: The patient has a higher risk of not surviving for 60 days.
- Score 1: The patient has a lower risk and is more likely to survive for 60 days.
36498630.utility: The 60-day survival score was found to be a significant predictor of short-term survival in patients with MBO undergoing PTBD. However, the article does not provide specific values for AUC, F-score, or PPV.

36503406.title: Odontogenic Infection Severity Calculator
36503406.code:
def calculate_sii(platelet_count, neutrophil_count, lymphocyte_count):
    """
    Calculate the Systemic Immune-Inflammation Index (SII)
    Args:
    platelet_count (float): Patient's platelet count
    neutrophil_count (float): Patient's neutrophil count
    lymphocyte_count (float): Patient's lymphocyte count
    Returns:
    float: SII score
    """
    return platelet_count * neutrophil_count / lymphocyte_count

def calculate_crp_nlr(crp, nlr):
    """
    Calculate the sum of C-reactive protein (CRP) and the neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR)
    Args:
    crp (float): Patient's CRP level
    nlr (float): Patient's NLR
    Returns:
    float: CRP + NLR score
    """
    return crp + nlr
36503406.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the severity of odontogenic infections in the head and neck region, particularly for distinguishing necrotizing soft tissue infection (NSTI) and deep neck abscess from cellulitis.
36503406.eligibility: Patients with severe odontogenic infections in the head and neck region that require hospitalization are eligible.
36503406.interpretation:
- SII ≥ 282 or SII < 282 but with a CRP + NLR ≥ 25: The patient likely has a deep neck abscess or NSTI (Group III or IV). 
- SII < 282 and CRP + NLR < 25: The patient likely has cellulitis or cellulitis with shallow abscess formation (Group I or II).
36503406.utility: The classification accuracy of this calculator is 89.3%.

36537652.title: AI-ECG and hs-cTnT Risk Calculator for Major Adverse Cardiovascular Events (MACEs)
36537652.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACEs) in patients undergoing high-sensitivity-cardiac troponin T (hs-cTnT) using artificial intelligence-augmented electrocardiography (AI-ECG).
36537652.eligibility: Patients undergoing high-sensitivity-cardiac troponin T (hs-cTnT) in the emergency department are eligible.
36537652.interpretation:
- 'Low': 11% risk of MACEs
- 'Medium': 26% risk of MACEs
- 'High': 42% risk of MACEs
- 'Very High': 65% risk of MACEs
36537652.utility: The risk calculator has been validated in a retrospective study with 1977 patients. The adjusted hazard ratios for MACEs were 1.54 for 'Medium' risk, 2.12 for 'High' risk, and 2.83 for 'Very High' risk.

36551591.title: Extramedullary Relapse Prediction Score Calculator
36551591.code:
def compute_risk_score(elevated_ldh, osteolytic_lesions, extramedullary_involvement, spleen_enlargement):
    """
    Compute the risk score for extramedullary relapse in NDMM patients.

    Parameters:
    elevated_ldh (bool): True if the patient has elevated LDH, False otherwise.
    osteolytic_lesions (bool): True if the patient has more than two osteolytic lesions, False otherwise.
    extramedullary_involvement (bool): True if the patient has extramedullary involvement, False otherwise.
    spleen_enlargement (bool): True if the patient has spleen enlargement, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for extramedullary relapse.
    """
    return int(elevated_ldh) + int(osteolytic_lesions) + int(extramedullary_involvement) + int(spleen_enlargement)
36551591.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of extramedullary relapse in newly diagnosed multiple myeloma (NDMM) patients.
36551591.eligibility: Patients newly diagnosed with multiple myeloma are eligible for this calculator.
36551591.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: The 3-year extramedullary relapse rate is 9.0%.
- Score 3-4: The 3-year extramedullary relapse rate is 76.7%.
36551591.utility: The extramedullary relapse prediction score model has a better ability to predict extramedullary relapse than the existing ISS and R-ISS stages.

36553270.title: Neonatal Adverse Drug Reactions (ADR) Risk Score Calculator
36553270.code:
def compute_risk_score(systemic_hormones, cardiovascular_drugs, circulatory_system_diseases, nervous_system_drugs, parenteral_nutrition_treatment):
    """
    Compute the risk score for neonatal ADR based on the given factors.
    
    Args:
    systemic_hormones (bool): True if the neonate is receiving systemic hormones, False otherwise.
    cardiovascular_drugs (bool): True if the neonate is receiving cardiovascular drugs, False otherwise.
    circulatory_system_diseases (bool): True if the neonate has diseases of the circulatory system, False otherwise.
    nervous_system_drugs (bool): True if the neonate is receiving nervous system drugs, False otherwise.
    parenteral_nutrition_treatment (bool): True if the neonate is receiving parenteral nutrition treatment, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The total risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if systemic_hormones:
        score += 2
    if cardiovascular_drugs:
        score += 3
    if circulatory_system_diseases:
        score += 1
    if nervous_system_drugs:
        score += 1
    if parenteral_nutrition_treatment:
        score += 1
    return score
36553270.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the presence of Adverse Drug Reactions (ADRs) in neonates, particularly those critically ill, based on several factors including the type of drugs administered and the presence of certain diseases.
36553270.eligibility: Critically ill neonates who are receiving drug treatment are eligible for this calculator.
36553270.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk of ADR
- Score >= 3: High risk of ADR
36553270.utility: The risk score correctly classified 91.1% of the observations in the test set (c-index: 0.914).

36556261.title: HALP Score and NLR Calculator for ICC Prognosis
36556261.code:
def calculate_HALP_score(hemoglobin, albumin, lymphocyte, platelet):
    """
    Calculate the HALP score.
    
    Parameters:
    hemoglobin (float): Hemoglobin count
    albumin (float): Albumin count
    lymphocyte (float): Lymphocyte count
    platelet (float): Platelet count
    
    Returns:
    float: HALP score
    """
    return hemoglobin + albumin + lymphocyte + platelet

def calculate_NLR(neutrophils, lymphocytes):
    """
    Calculate the Neutrophil-to-Lymphocyte Ratio (NLR).
    
    Parameters:
    neutrophils (float): Neutrophil count
    lymphocytes (float): Lymphocyte count
    
    Returns:
    float: NLR
    """
    return neutrophils / lymphocytes
36556261.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the specific site of recurrence or metastasis after surgery in patients with intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC).
36556261.eligibility: Patients with pathologically proven ICC who have undergone curative surgery are eligible.
36556261.interpretation:
- HALP score > 43.63: Higher risk of intrahepatic recurrence
- NLR > 3.73: Higher risk of lymph node metastasis recurrence
36556261.utility: The HALP score exhibited the largest ROC curve area of all in predicting intrahepatic recurrence (AUC = 0.588), and the NLR showed the best predictive value in predicting lymph node metastasis (AUC = 0.703).

36560883.title: Papillary Thyroid Cancer Prognosis Calculator
36560883.code:
def calculate_risk(NLNM, LNR, Tg):
    """
    Function to calculate risk score for PTC prognosis.
    Args:
    NLNM (int): Number of Lymph Node Metastasis
    LNR (float): Lymph Node Ratio
    Tg (float): preoperative Thyroglobulin level

    Returns:
    risk_score (str): Risk score based on the input parameters
    """
    if NLNM < 10 and LNR < 0.295 and Tg < 50.24:
        risk_score = 'Low'
    else:
        risk_score = 'High'
    return risk_score
36560883.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the survival prognosis for patients with papillary thyroid cancer (PTC) and lymph node metastasis.
36560883.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with papillary thyroid cancer (PTC) and lymph node metastasis.
36560883.interpretation:
- 'Low': Patients with a low risk score have a higher disease-free survival rate.
- 'High': Patients with a high risk score have a lower disease-free survival rate.
36560883.utility: The AUC values of the prognostic model at 3 and 5 years were 0.805 and 0.793 respectively, indicating good predictive accuracy.

36561302.title: Hospital-Acquired Pneumonia Risk Calculator for aSAH Patients
36561302.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, bg, rdw_sd, gcs):
    """
    Compute the risk score for hospital-acquired pneumonia in aSAH patients.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The patient's age.
    bg (float): The patient's blood glucose level.
    rdw_sd (float): The standard deviation of the patient's red blood distribution width.
    gcs (int): The patient's Glasgow coma scale score.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    bg = 1 if bg > 7.22 else 0
    return -5.467 + 0.057 * age + 1.023 * bg + 0.111 * rdw_sd - 0.342 * gcs
36561302.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of hospital-acquired pneumonia (HAP) in patients with aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH).
36561302.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (aSAH) are eligible.
36561302.interpretation:
- Total points from -3 to 4: Low risk for HAP
- Total points from 5 to 14: High risk for HAP
36561302.utility: The AUCs values of the Model-Logit, GCS, age, BG (>7.22 mmol/L), and RDW-SD were 0.865, 0.819, 0.634, 0.698, and 0.625, respectively. The AUCs of the Model-Logit were insignificantly higher than the Model-Cat (Delong test, p = 0.157).

36566321.title: Exosome-Related Long Non-Coding RNAs Risk Model for Breast Cancer
36566321.code:
def compute_risk_score(lncRNA_expression, regression_coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the sum of the expression of 15 exosome-related lncRNAs and the regression coefficients.

    Parameters:
    lncRNA_expression (list): A list of 15 lncRNA expression levels.
    regression_coefficients (list): A list of regression coefficients.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    return sum([a*b for a, b in zip(lncRNA_expression, regression_coefficients)])
36566321.purpose: This calculator is used to predict survival outcomes in patients with breast cancer based on the expression of 15 exosome-related long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs).
36566321.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with breast cancer are eligible for this calculator.
36566321.interpretation:
- Low-risk group: Patients with a risk score less than the median risk score. These patients have a higher survival rate.
- High-risk group: Patients with a risk score greater than or equal to the median risk score. These patients have a lower survival rate and are identified with lower scores of ESTIMATE scores.
36566321.utility: The exosome-associated lncRNAs risk model was demonstrated to accurately predict immunotherapy response in patients with breast cancer. The different risk groups exhibited different prognosis, with lower survival rate in the high-risk group.

36567070.title: Triceps Skinfold-Albumin Index (TA) Calculator
36567070.code:
def compute_TA(TSF, albumin):
    """
    Compute the Triceps Skinfold-Albumin Index (TA).

    Parameters:
    TSF (float): Triceps skinfold thickness in mm.
    albumin (float): Serum albumin level in g/L.

    Returns:
    float: The TA index.
    """
    return TSF + albumin
36567070.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with cancer cachexia, based on the triceps skinfold thickness (TSF) and serum albumin level.
36567070.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with cancer cachexia are eligible for this calculator.
36567070.interpretation:
- For women, a TA index less than 49.9 is considered low and associated with increased mortality.
- For men, a TA index less than 45.6 is considered low and associated with increased mortality.
36567070.utility: The TA index showed better discrimination performance (C index = 0.621, 95% confidence interval [CI] = 0.607-0.636) to predict mortality in patients with cancer cachexia than other indices. The 1-, 3- and 5-year time-dependent ROC analyses (AUC = 0.647, 0.625 and 0.630, respectively) showed that the TA had the highest prognostic value among all indices investigated.

36579159.title: 5-factor modified Frailty Index (mFI-5) for Predicting Adverse Outcomes after Elective Anterior Lumbar Interbody Fusion (ALIF)
36579159.code:
def calculate_mfi_5(hypertension, heart_failure, diabetes, copd, dependent_status):
    """
    Calculate the mFI-5 score.

    Parameters:
    hypertension (bool): True if the patient has hypertension, False otherwise.
    heart_failure (bool): True if the patient has congestive heart failure, False otherwise.
    diabetes (bool): True if the patient has comorbid diabetes, False otherwise.
    copd (bool): True if the patient has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, False otherwise.
    dependent_status (bool): True if the patient has partially or fully dependent functional status, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: mFI-5 score
    """
    return hypertension + heart_failure + diabetes + copd + dependent_status
36579159.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of 30-day postoperative adverse events following elective ALIF in adults over the age of 50.
36579159.eligibility: Patients over the age of 50 who are undergoing elective Anterior Lumbar Interbody Fusion (ALIF).
36579159.interpretation:
- mFI-5 score of 0: Lowest risk of postoperative complications.
- mFI-5 score of 1: 2.2 times higher risk of pneumonia.
- mFI-5 score of 2: 3.6 times higher risk of pneumonia and 1.3 times higher risk of related readmissions.
- mFI-5 score of 3+: 7.0 times higher risk of pneumonia, 1.9 times higher risk of related readmissions, 1.5 times higher risk of any complication, 2.4 times higher risk of UTI, and 4.5 times higher risk of unplanned intubation.
36579159.utility: The mFI-5 score is an independent predictor for 30-day postoperative complications, readmissions, UTI, pneumonia, and unplanned intubations following elective ALIF surgery in adults over the age of 50.

36610495.title: Echocardiographic Parameters for Malignancy Prediction in Cardiac Masses
36610495.code:
def compute_risk_score(infiltration, pericardial_effusion, nonleft_localization, sessile_implantation, polylobate_shape, inhomogeneity):
    """
    Compute the risk score for malignancy in cardiac masses based on six echocardiographic parameters.

    Parameters:
    infiltration (bool): True if infiltration is present, False otherwise.
    pericardial_effusion (bool): True if moderate to severe pericardial effusion is present, False otherwise.
    nonleft_localization (bool): True if the mass is not located on the left, False otherwise.
    sessile_implantation (bool): True if sessile implantation is present, False otherwise.
    polylobate_shape (bool): True if the mass has a polylobate shape, False otherwise.
    inhomogeneity (bool): True if the mass is inhomogeneous, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 6.
    """
    return int(infiltration) + int(pericardial_effusion) + int(nonleft_localization) + int(sessile_implantation) + int(polylobate_shape) + int(inhomogeneity)
36610495.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the malignancy of cardiac masses based on six echocardiographic parameters.
36610495.eligibility: Patients who have undergone standard echocardiographic assessment for suspected cardiac masses.
36610495.interpretation:
- Score 0-2: Low risk of malignancy
- Score 3-6: High risk of malignity, with a score of 3 or more having a very high predictive ability (>0.90) for malignancy.
36610495.utility: The predictive ability of the unweighted score of ≥3 was very high (>0.90) and similar to that of the previously published weighted score. The percentage correctly classified by classification tree analysis as malignant was 87.5%. Agreement between observer readings and CM histology ranged between 85.1% and 91.5%.

36650836.title: Bone Metastatic Score (BMS) Risk Model for Prostate Cancer
36650836.code:
def compute_bms(metastasis_sites):
    """
    Compute the Bone Metastatic Score (BMS) based on the presence of metastasis in different bone sites.

    Parameters:
    metastasis_sites (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the bone sites and the values are either 1 (metastasis present) or 0 (metastasis not present).

    Returns:
    int: The total BMS.
    """
    return sum(metastasis_sites.values())
36650836.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of progression to metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) in patients newly diagnosed with de novo bone metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC).
36650836.eligibility: Patients newly diagnosed with de novo bone metastatic prostate cancer are eligible.
36650836.interpretation:
- BMS < 3: Low-risk group (LRG), associated with a later time to CRPC.
- BMS ≥ 3: High-risk group (HRG), associated with an earlier time to CRPC.
36650836.utility: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) for the BMS risk model was 0.620, 0.754, and 0.793 for 1-, 2-, and 3-year, respectively, indicating good predictive performance.

36670007.title: Preoperative Risk Score (PreopScore) for Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC)
36670007.code:
def compute_preopscore(alpha_feto_protein, neutrophil_to_lymphocyte_ratio, albumin, gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase, vascular_involvement, tumor_burden_score):
    """
    Compute the PreopScore based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    alpha_feto_protein (float): Level of α-feto protein.
    neutrophil_to_lymphocyte_ratio (float): Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio.
    albumin (float): Level of albumin.
    gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase (float): Level of gamma-glutamyl transpeptidase.
    vascular_involvement (bool): Whether there is vascular involvement (True or False).
    tumor_burden_score (str): Tumor burden score ('low', 'medium', 'high').
    
    Returns:
    float: The computed PreopScore.
    """
    # Hazard ratios
    hr_alpha_feto_protein = 1.17
    hr_neutrophil_to_lymphocyte_ratio = 2.62
    hr_albumin = 0.49
    hr_gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase = 1.00
    hr_vascular_involvement = 3.52 if vascular_involvement else 1.00
    hr_tumor_burden_score = {'low': 1.00, 'medium': 3.49, 'high': 3.21}[tumor_burden_score]
    
    # Compute the score
    preopscore = (alpha_feto_protein * hr_alpha_feto_protein +
                  neutrophil_to_lymphocyte_ratio * hr_neutrophil_to_lymphocyte_ratio +
                  albumin * hr_albumin +
                  gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase * hr_gamma_glutamyl_transpeptidase +
                  hr_vascular_involvement +
                  hr_tumor_burden_score)
    
    return preopscore
36670007.purpose: This calculator is used to predict overall survival after hepatectomy for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) based on preoperative tumor, liver, nutritional, and inflammatory markers.
36670007.eligibility: Patients who are undergoing curative-intent surgery for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).
36670007.interpretation:
- PreopScore 0-2: 5-year overall survival rate of 85.8%
- PreopScore 2-3.5: 5-year overall survival rate of 70.7%
- PreopScore >3.5: 5-year overall survival rate of 52.4%
36670007.utility: The c-index of the test and validation cohort were 0.75 and 0.71, respectively, indicating a good predictive performance. The PreopScore outperformed individual parameters and previous HCC staging systems.

36672675.title: HCC Risk Calculator for HCV-positive Patients
36672675.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk of Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) occurrence in Hepatitis C Virus (HCV)-positive patients with advanced chronic liver disease (aCLD) who have been successfully treated with Direct Acting Antiviral Agents (DAAs).
36672675.eligibility: Patients who are HCV-positive with aCLD, have achieved Sustained Virologic Response (SVR) after DAAs treatment, and have known values for Liver Stiffness Measurement (LSM), FIB-4, and APRI.
36672675.interpretation:
- Group 1: Negligible risk of HCC occurrence (0.09/100 patients/year)
- Group 2: Low risk of HCC occurrence (1.22/100 patients/year)
- Group 3: Moderate risk of HCC occurrence (1.68/100 patients/year)
- Group 4: High risk of HCC occurrence (4.01/100 patients/year)
36672675.utility: The calculator was able to distinguish between different risk groups with significant differences in HCC occurrence rates. The risk of HCC fell in patients showing a decrease of FIB-4/APRI scores.

36680425.title: PREDICT-crFMF Score Calculator
36680425.code:
def calculate_score(recurrent_arthritis, protracted_febrile_myalgia, erysipelas_like_erythema, exertional_leg_pain, M694V_homozygous_mutation):
    """
    Function to calculate PREDICT-crFMF score.
    
    Parameters:
    recurrent_arthritis (bool): True if the patient has recurrent arthritis, False otherwise.
    protracted_febrile_myalgia (bool): True if the patient has protracted febrile myalgia, False otherwise.
    erysipelas_like_erythema (bool): True if the patient has erysipelas-like erythema, False otherwise.
    exertional_leg_pain (bool): True if the patient has exertional leg pain, False otherwise.
    M694V_homozygous_mutation (bool): True if the patient carries M694V homozygous mutation, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: PREDICT-crFMF score.
    """
    score = 0
    if recurrent_arthritis:
        score += 4
    if protracted_febrile_myalgia:
        score += 8
    if erysipelas_like_erythema:
        score += 2
    if exertional_leg_pain:
        score += 2
    if M694V_homozygous_mutation:
        score += 4
    return score
36680425.purpose: This calculator is used to predict colchicine resistance in children with Familial Mediterranean Fever (FMF) based on initial clinical features and laboratory findings.
36680425.eligibility: Children diagnosed with Familial Mediterranean Fever (FMF) who are about to start colchicine treatment.
36680425.interpretation:
- Score < 9: Low risk of colchicine resistance
- Score >= 9: High risk of colchicine resistance
36680425.utility: The cut-off value (9) was 87% sensitive and 82% specific to foresee the risk of colchicine resistance in the ROC. Validation of the scoring system with an independent group revealed that the cut-off value was 82% sensitive and 79% specific to identify the risk of colchicine resistance.

36683014.title: Right Ventricular Injury (RVI) Score Calculator
36683014.code:
def compute_rvi_score(rv_dilation, rv_systolic_dysfunction, rv_pa_decoupling):
    """
    Compute the Right Ventricular Injury (RVI) Score.

    Parameters:
    rv_dilation (bool): True if RV dilation is present, False otherwise.
    rv_systolic_dysfunction (bool): True if RV systolic dysfunction is present, False otherwise.
    rv_pa_decoupling (bool): True if RV-PA decoupling is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The RVI score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(rv_dilation) + int(rv_systolic_dysfunction) + int(rv_pa_decoupling)
36683014.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the severity of right ventricular dysfunction in septic patients and predict their 30-day mortality risk.
36683014.eligibility: Septic patients admitted to the ICU are eligible for this calculator.
36683014.interpretation:
- RVI score 0: Lowest risk of 30-day mortality.
- RVI score 1: Moderate risk of 30-day mortality (HR: 2.94, 95% CI: 1.20-7.20).
- RVI score 2: High risk of 30-day mortality (HR: 3.20, 95% CI: 1.28-7.98).
- RVI score 3: Highest risk of 30-day mortality (HR: 7.17, 95% CI: 2.65-19.38).
36683014.utility: The RVI score was independently related to 30-day mortality and had the best performance in model goodness of fit, discrimination, and variance explained than the other RV indices.

36685540.title: Second Allogeneic Stem Cell Transplantation Prognostic Score Calculator
36685540.code:
def compute_prognostic_score(disease_status, hct_ci_score, donor_type):
    """
    Compute the prognostic score for a patient undergoing a second allogeneic stem cell transplantation.

    Parameters:
    disease_status (str): The disease status of the patient. Should be 'MRD-negative CR' or other.
    hct_ci_score (int): The HCT-CI score of the patient prior to the second transplantation.
    donor_type (str): The type of the donor for the second transplantation. Should be 'new mismatched haplotype' or other.

    Returns:
    int: The prognostic score of the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if disease_status == 'MRD-negative CR':
        score += 1
    if hct_ci_score == 0:
        score += 1
    if donor_type == 'new mismatched haplotype':
        score += 1
    return score
36685540.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of hematological malignancy patients who are undergoing a second allogeneic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT2) after relapse from the first transplantation.
36685540.eligibility: Patients with hematological malignancies who have relapsed after the first allogeneic stem cell transplantation and are undergoing a second transplantation.
36685540.interpretation:
- Score 0: Lower survival rates and higher relapse incidence.
- Score 1-2: Intermediate survival rates and relapse incidence.
- Score 3: Superior 2-year overall survival (63.3% ± 6.7%) and leukemia-free survival (63.3% ± 6.7%), and a lower cumulative incidence of relapse (5.5% ± 3.1%).
36685540.utility: The prognostic score was found to be a significant predictor of overall survival and leukemia-free survival in the study population.

36690161.title: Radiation Necrosis Risk Calculator for Brain Metastases Patients
36690161.code:
def compute_risk_score(V12_Gy):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the volume receiving 12 Gy (V12 Gy) of radiation.

    Args:
    V12_Gy (float): The volume receiving 12 Gy of radiation in cm3.

    Returns:
    int: The risk group (1, 2, or 3).
    """
    if V12_Gy < 12:
        return 1
    elif 12 <= V12_Gy <= 20:
        return 2
    else:
        return 3
36690161.purpose: This calculator is used to estimate the risk of radiation necrosis (RN) in patients with brain metastases from primary non-small cell lung cancer, renal cell carcinoma, or melanoma treated with Stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) and immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI).
36690161.eligibility: Patients with brain metastases from primary non-small cell lung cancer, renal cell carcinoma, or melanoma who are treated with SRS and ICI.
36690161.interpretation:
1. Risk Group 1 (V12 Gy < 12 cm3): 1-year RN rate is 3.7%, 2-year RN rate is 7.5%
2. Risk Group 2 (20 cm3 ≥ V12 Gy ≥ 12 cm3): 1-year RN rate is 10.3%, 2-year RN rate is 13.8%
3. Risk Group 3 (V12 Gy > 20 cm3): 1-year RN rate is 12.6%, 2-year RN rate is 15.4%
36690161.utility: The risk groups based on V12 Gy were identified with high fidelity and were found to be predictive of RN. The risk is not increased with concurrent administration of SRS and ICI.

36704467.title: Goldenberg Risk Score (GRS) for Non-Ischemic Heart Failure Patients
36704467.code:
def calculate_GRS(atrial_fibrillation, NYHA_class, age, blood_urea_nitrogen, QRS):
    """
    Function to calculate Goldenberg Risk Score (GRS)
    Parameters:
    atrial_fibrillation (bool): Presence of atrial fibrillation
    NYHA_class (int): New York Heat Association class
    age (int): Age of the patient
    blood_urea_nitrogen (float): Blood urea nitrogen level
    QRS (float): QRS duration

    Returns:
    int: Goldenberg Risk Score
    """
    GRS = 0
    if atrial_fibrillation:
        GRS += 1
    if NYHA_class > 2:
        GRS += 1
    if age > 70:
        GRS += 1
    if blood_urea_nitrogen > 26:
        GRS += 1
    if QRS > 120:
        GRS += 1
    return GRS
36704467.purpose: This calculator is used to predict which non-ischemic heart failure patients will benefit from the addition of an implantable cardioverter defibrillator (ICD) to cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT) at long-term.
36704467.eligibility: Patients with non-ischemic heart failure undergoing CRT implantation are eligible.
36704467.interpretation:
- Score < 3: Low risk, patients show a clear long-term mortality benefit by adding ICD to CRT.
- Score ≥ 3: High risk, no further benefit could be observed by adding ICD to CRT.
36704467.utility: The study found that CRT-D patients with low GRS showed a mortality benefit compared to CRT-P (HR 0.68; 95% CI 0.48-0.96; p = 0.03), high-risk patients did not (HR 0.84; 95% CI 0.62-1.13; p = 0.26).

36719281.title: Inflammation-Combined Prognostic Index (ICPI) Calculator for Gastric Cancer
36719281.code:
def calculate_ICPI(LMR, NLR, PLR):
    """
    Calculate the Inflammation-Combined Prognostic Index (ICPI) for gastric cancer patients.

    Parameters:
    LMR (float): Lymphocyte-to-Monocyte Ratio
    NLR (float): Neutrophil-to-Lymphocyte Ratio
    PLR (float): Platelet-to-Lymphocyte Ratio

    Returns:
    float: The calculated ICPI score
    """
    LMR_score = 1 if LMR < 4.315 else 0
    NLR_score = 1 if NLR >= 2.344 else 0
    PLR_score = 1 if PLR >= 212.01 else 0

    ICPI = 2.9 * LMR_score + 2.8 * NLR_score + 2.8 * PLR_score
    return ICPI
36719281.purpose: This calculator is used to predict cancer-specific survival (CSS) outcomes in patients with gastric cancer.
36719281.eligibility: Patients with gastric cancer undergoing curative laparoscopic gastrectomy are eligible.
36719281.interpretation:
- ICPI < 2.9: Low risk group with better cancer-specific survival (CSS) outcomes.
- ICPI ≥ 2.9: High risk group with worse CSS outcomes.
36719281.utility: In the Kaplan-Meier survival analysis, CSS in the high ICPI group was significantly worse than that in the low ICPI group. The ICPI was found to be an independent prognostic factor for CSS.

36733552.title: CEL Score for Advanced Non-small Cell Lung Cancer
36733552.code:
def compute_cel_score(crp, ecog_ps, ldh, ldh_uln):
    """
    Compute the CEL score for a patient with advanced NSCLC.

    Parameters:
    crp (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/dL.
    ecog_ps (int): Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status.
    ldh (float): Lactate dehydrogenase level.
    ldh_uln (float): Upper limit of normal for lactate dehydrogenase level.

    Returns:
    float: The CEL score.
    """
    score = 0
    if crp >= 1.0:
        score += 2
    if ecog_ps >= 2:
        score += 2.5
    if ldh > ldh_uln:
        score += 1.5
    return score
36733552.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the response to Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors (ICIs) in patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).
36733552.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with unresectable/advanced stage NSCLC and treated with at least one cycle of ICIs are eligible.
36733552.interpretation:
The CEL score is interpreted as follows:

- 0-2.5: Good risk, with a median overall survival (OS) of 18.9 months
- 3.5-4.5: Intermediate risk, with a median OS of 7.4 months
- 6: Poor risk, with a median OS of 2.9 months
36733552.utility: The CEL score has a Harrell C-index of 0.73 for predicting overall survival and 0.69 for predicting progression-free survival, indicating significant predictability. The area under the curve (AUC) of the scoring index for predicting the responses was 0.765 (95% CI: 0.685-0.845).

36739357.title: Poor Diuretic Response (DR) Risk Calculator in Acute Decompensated Heart Failure (ADHF)
36739357.code:
def compute_risk_score(discharge_time, loop_diuretics, eGFR):
    """
    Compute the risk score for poor diuretic response in ADHF patients.

    Parameters:
    discharge_time (bool): True if the time since discharge from the hospital for a previous heart failure is less than 3 months, False otherwise.
    loop_diuretics (bool): True if the patient was on loop diuretics at admission, False otherwise.
    eGFR (float): Estimated glomerular filtration rate.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if discharge_time:
        score += 1
    if loop_diuretics:
        score += 1
    if eGFR < 45:
        score += 1
    return score
36739357.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of poor diuretic response in patients with acute decompensated heart failure.
36739357.eligibility: Patients admitted for acute decompensated heart failure and received intravenous furosemide within 1 hour of presentation.
36739357.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of poor DR (18.9%)
- Score 1: Middle risk of poor DR (33.1%)
- Score 2-3: High risk of poor DR (58.0%)
36739357.utility: The risk score was significantly associated with the incidence of poor DR in the study population (p < 0.0001).

36743693.title: Ovarian Cancer Oxidative Stress Response Risk Score Calculator
36743693.code:
def compute_risk_score(expression_levels, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression levels of the genes and their coefficients.

    Args:
    expression_levels (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the gene names and the values are their expression levels.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the keys are the gene names and the values are their coefficients.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    for gene in expression_levels:
        risk_score += expression_levels[gene] * coefficients[gene]
    return risk_score
36743693.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis and immune responses in patients with ovarian cancer based on oxidative stress response-related genes.
36743693.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with ovarian cancer are eligible for this calculator.
36743693.interpretation:
- Low risk: Risk score < median risk score. Patients in this group have a lower risk of poor prognosis and may respond better to immunotherapy.
- High risk: Risk score >= median risk score. Patients in this group have a higher risk of poor prognosis and may respond less effectively to immunotherapy.
36743693.utility: The risk score model was found to be an independent prognostic factor for ovarian cancer. The gene mutation frequency and tumor immune microenvironment in the high- and low-risk score groups were significantly different, confirming the value of the risk score model in predicting immunotherapeutic outcomes.

36748459.title: Coronary Artery Calcium Score (CACS) Risk Calculator for Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma Patients
36748459.code:
def compute_risk(cacs):
    """
    Compute the risk category based on the coronary artery calcium score (CACS).

    Args:
    cacs (int): The coronary artery calcium score.

    Returns:
    risk_category (str): The risk category ('Low', 'Medium', 'High').
    """
    if cacs == 0:
        return 'Low'
    elif 1 <= cacs <= 100:
        return 'Medium'
    elif cacs > 100:
        return 'High'
36748459.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk of cancer therapy-related cardiac dysfunction (CTRCD) and major adverse cardiovascular events (MACEs) in patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma receiving anthracycline-based chemotherapy.
36748459.eligibility: Patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma who are about to receive anthracycline-based chemotherapy.
36748459.interpretation:
- 'Low': CACS of 0, associated with the lowest risk of CTRCD and MACEs.
- 'Medium': CACS between 1 and 100, associated with a moderate risk of CTRCD and MACEs.
- 'High': CACS >100, associated with the highest risk of CTRCD and MACEs.
36748459.utility: The CACS was found to be significantly associated with the risk of CTRCD and MACEs in patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma receiving anthracycline-based chemotherapy. The odds ratio for CTRCD was 2.587 for a CACS between 1 and 100 and 5.239 for a CACS >100. The subdistribution hazard ratio for MACEs was 3.726 for a CACS between 1 and 100 and 7.858 for a CACS >100.

36750772.title: Musculus Psoas Major (MPM) Score Calculator for Mortality in Elderly Polytraumatized Patients
36750772.code:
def compute_mpm_score(short_axis_left, long_axis_left, short_axis_right, long_axis_right):
    """
    Compute the MPM score based on the short and long axes of both MPM.

    Args:
    short_axis_left (float): The length of the short axis of the left MPM.
    long_axis_left (float): The length of the long axis of the left MPM.
    short_axis_right (float): The length of the short axis of the right MPM.
    long_axis_right (float): The length of the long axis of the right MPM.

    Returns:
    float: The MPM score.
    """
    ratio_left = short_axis_left / long_axis_left
    ratio_right = short_axis_right / long_axis_right
    mpm_score = (ratio_left + ratio_right) / 2
    return mpm_score
36750772.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 31-day mortality risk in elderly polytraumatized patients based on the morphology of the Musculus Psoas Major (MPM).
36750772.eligibility: Patients who are at least 65 years old, presented with multiple injuries (≥2 body regions) and an Injury Severity Score (ISS) ≥16, and were diagnosed using a whole-body computed tomography.
36750772.interpretation:
- MPM score < 0.48: High risk of 31-day mortality
- MPM score >= 0.48: Lower risk of 31-day mortality
36750772.utility: The MPM score calculator has an AUC of 0.724, indicating a good predictive performance. The cut-off level of 0.48 has a sensitivity of 80.7% and a specificity of 54.3%.

36761693.title: Microtubule Associated Genes (MAGs) Prognostic Model for Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma (DLBCL)
36761693.code:
def compute_risk_score(gene_expression, coefficients):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the expression of fourteen MAGs and their corresponding coefficients.

    Parameters:
    gene_expression (dict): A dictionary where the key is the gene name and the value is its expression.
    coefficients (dict): A dictionary where the key is the gene name and the value is its coefficient.

    Returns:
    float: The computed risk score.
    """
    risk_score = 0
    for gene, expression in gene_expression.items():
        risk_score += expression * coefficients[gene]
    return risk_score
36761693.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) based on the expression of fourteen microtubule associated genes (MAGs).
36761693.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma (DLBCL).
36761693.interpretation:
- High risk: Patients with a risk score higher than the median risk score have a significantly inferior overall survival time.
- Low risk: Patients with a risk score lower than the median risk score have a better overall survival time.
36761693.utility: The Kaplan-Meier curve analysis showed that the high-risk patients had a significantly inferior overall survival (OS) time compared to low-risk patients in training and validation datasets.

36762716.title: NASH LT Risk-Benefit Calculator
36762716.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, functional_status, TIPSS, hepatic_encephalopathy, creatinine, ventilation, dialysis):
    """
    Compute the risk score for a patient based on the given parameters.

    Parameters:
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    functional_status (bool): Whether the patient needs total assistance.
    TIPSS (bool): Whether the patient has TIPSS.
    hepatic_encephalopathy (bool): Whether the patient has hepatic encephalopathy.
    creatinine (float): The patient's serum creatinine level.
    ventilation (bool): Whether the patient needs mechanical ventilation.
    dialysis (bool): Whether the patient had dialysis within 1 week prior to LT.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score for the patient.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 70:
        score += 5
    if functional_status:
        score += 3
    if TIPSS:
        score += 2
    if hepatic_encephalopathy:
        score += 1
    if creatinine > 1.45:
        score += 5
    if ventilation:
        score += 3
    if dialysis:
        score += 7
    return score
36762716.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 5-year survival post-liver transplantation (LT) in patients with Non-alcoholic Steatohepatitis (NASH) cirrhosis.
36762716.eligibility: Patients with NASH cirrhosis who are candidates for liver transplantation.
36762716.interpretation:
- Scores 0-13: The patient has a survival rate of >65% at 5 years post-LT.
- Scores above 13: The patient has a survival rate of <65% at 5 years post-LT.
36762716.utility: The risk score has good predictive ability as indicated by internal and external validation.

36777555.title: One-Year Colectomy Risk Calculator for Severe Ulcerative Colitis
36777555.code:
def compute_risk_score(single_biologic, multiple_biologic, salvage_therapy, age):
    """
    Compute the risk score for one-year colectomy in severe UC patients.

    Parameters:
    single_biologic (bool): True if the patient had single class biologic exposure prior to admission, False otherwise.
    multiple_biologic (bool): True if the patient had multiple classes of biologic exposure, False otherwise.
    salvage_therapy (bool): True if the patient had inpatient salvage therapy with cyclosporine or a TNF-alpha inhibitor, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The computed risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if single_biologic:
        score += 1
    if multiple_biologic:
        score += 2
    if salvage_therapy:
        score += 1
    if age < 40:
        score += 1
    return score
36777555.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of colectomy within one year in adults hospitalized for severe ulcerative colitis (UC).
36777555.eligibility: Adult patients hospitalized for severe ulcerative colitis are eligible.
36777555.interpretation:
- Score 0: 0% risk of colectomy within one year
- Score 1: 9.4% risk of colectomy within one year
- Score 2: 33.7% risk of colectomy within one year
- Score 3: 58.5% risk of colectomy within one year
- Score 4: 75.0% risk of colectomy within one year
36777555.utility: The article does not provide specific evaluation results of the clinical utility of the risk score, such as AUC, F-score, PPV.

36785947.title: ISAR Score Calculator
36785947.code:
def compute_ISAR_score(non_focal_ISR, time_interval_to_ISR, ISR_of_left_circumflex, ISR_in_calcified_vessel):
    """
    Compute the ISAR score based on four factors.
    
    Parameters:
    non_focal_ISR (bool): True if the ISR pattern is non-focal, False otherwise.
    time_interval_to_ISR (bool): True if the time interval to ISR is less than 6 months, False otherwise.
    ISR_of_left_circumflex (bool): True if the ISR is of the left circumflex artery, False otherwise.
    ISR_in_calcified_vessel (bool): True if the ISR is in a calcified vessel, False otherwise.
    
    Returns:
    int: The ISAR score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    return int(non_focal_ISR) + int(time_interval_to_ISR) + int(ISR_of_left_circumflex) + int(ISR_in_calcified_vessel)
