36785947.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of repeat percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) for recurrent drug-eluting stent (DES) in-stent restenosis (ISR) at 1-year follow-up.
36785947.eligibility: Patients who have undergone PCI for DES-ISR are eligible.
36785947.interpretation:
- ISAR score of 0: Low risk of repeat PCI for recurrent DES-ISR at 1-year follow-up.
- ISAR score of 1-2: Moderate risk of repeat PCI for recurrent DES-ISR at 1-year follow-up.
- ISAR score of 3-4: High risk of repeat PCI for recurrent DES-ISR at 1-year follow-up. The cumulative incidence of repeat PCI for recurrent DES-ISR is over three times higher in DES-ISR lesions with an ISAR score of ≥3 in comparison to lesions with an ISAR score of 0.
36785947.utility: The C-statistic for the model including these four variables was 0.60 (95% confidence interval [CI]: 0.57-0.63), indicating a moderate predictive ability. The model was significantly better than the benchmark ISR classification model, which had a C-statistic of 0.54 (95% CI: 0.52-0.57).

36786471.title: Pediatric DUCT Score Calculator
36786471.code:
def compute_pediatric_duct_score(dilated_bile_duct, ultrasound_choledocholithiasis, total_bilirubin):
    """
    Compute the Pediatric DUCT Score.

    Parameters:
    dilated_bile_duct (bool): True if the common bile duct is dilated ≥6 mm, False otherwise.
    ultrasound_choledocholithiasis (bool): True if choledocholithiasis is seen on ultrasound, False otherwise.
    total_bilirubin (float): The total bilirubin level in mg/dL.

    Returns:
    int: The Pediatric DUCT Score.
    """
    score = 0
    if dilated_bile_duct:
        score += 1
    if ultrasound_choledocholithiasis:
        score += 1
    if total_bilirubin >= 1.8:
        score += 1
    return score
36786471.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the presence of choledocholithiasis (common bile duct stone) in children undergoing cholecystectomy for gallstone disease.
36786471.eligibility: Children (≤18 years of age) who are undergoing cholecystectomy for gallstone disease.
36786471.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk of choledocholithiasis
- Score 1: Intermediate risk of choledocholithiasis
- Score 2: High risk of choledocholithiasis
- Score 3: Very high risk of choledocholithiasis
36786471.utility: The Pediatric DUCT Score demonstrated accuracies of >76%, specificity of >78%, and negative predictive values of >79%.

36794152.title: Late CMV Reactivation Risk Calculator
36794152.code:
def compute_risk_score(t_cell_lymphoma, prior_chemotherapies, cr_after_transplantation, early_cmv_reactivation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for late CMV reactivation.

    Parameters:
    t_cell_lymphoma (bool): Whether the patient has a T-cell lymphoma diagnosis.
    prior_chemotherapies (int): The number of prior chemotherapies the patient has undergone.
    cr_after_transplantation (bool): Whether the patient achieved complete response after transplantation.
    early_cmv_reactivation (bool): Whether the patient had early CMV reactivation.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score for late CMV reactivation.
    """
    score = 0
    if t_cell_lymphoma:
        score += 1.5
    if prior_chemotherapies >= 2:
        score += 1.5
    if not cr_after_transplantation:
        score += 1
    if early_cmv_reactivation:
        score += 1
    return score
36794152.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of late Cytomegalovirus (CMV) reactivation in patients who have undergone autologous stem cell transplantation (auto-SCT).
36794152.eligibility: Patients who have undergone autologous stem cell transplantation (auto-SCT) are eligible.
36794152.interpretation:
- Score < 1.75: Low risk of late CMV reactivation
- Score >= 1.75: High risk of late CMV reactivation
36794152.utility: The predictive risk model showed good discrimination, with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.872.

36795409.title: ONKOTEV Risk Prediction Model
36795409.code:
def calculate_onkotev_score(khorana_score, metastatic_disease, vascular_lymphatic_compression, previous_vte):
    """
    Calculate the ONKOTEV score based on the four variables.
    
    Args:
    khorana_score (int): Khorana score of the patient.
    metastatic_disease (bool): Whether the patient has metastatic disease.
    vascular_lymphatic_compression (bool): Whether the patient has vascular or lymphatic compression.
    previous_vte (bool): Whether the patient has a previous VTE event.
    
    Returns:
    int: The ONKOTEV score.
    """
    onkotev_score = 0
    if khorana_score > 2:
        onkotev_score += 1
    if metastatic_disease:
        onkotev_score += 1
    if vascular_lymphatic_compression:
        onkotev_score += 1
    if previous_vte:
        onkotev_score += 1
    return onkotev_score
36795409.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE) among outpatients with cancer.
36795409.eligibility: Outpatients with a histologically confirmed diagnosis of a solid tumor who are receiving active treatments.
36795409.interpretation:
- Score 0: 2.6% risk of developing VTE at 6 months
- Score 1: 9.1% risk of developing VTE at 6 months
- Score 2: 32.3% risk of developing VTE at 6 months
- Score >2: 19.3% risk of developing VTE at 6 months
36795409.utility: The time-dependent area under the curve at 3, 6, and 12 months was 70.1%, 72.9%, and 72.2%, respectively.

36804310.title: Hypoperfusion-matched Intracranial Venous Scale (hypo-IVS)
36804310.code:
def compute_hypo_ivs(vein_scores):
    """
    Compute the Hypoperfusion-matched Intracranial Venous Scale (hypo-IVS).
    
    Parameters:
    vein_scores (list): A list of 4 scores representing the contrast enhancement degree of each vein. 
                        Score is 1 for attenuated contrast enhancement and 0 for complete contrast enhancement.
    
    Returns:
    int: The hypo-IVS score.
    """
    return sum(vein_scores)
36804310.purpose: This calculator is used to predict clinical outcomes in acute ischemic stroke (AIS) patients who received reperfusion therapy.
36804310.eligibility: Patients with anterior circulation acute ischemic stroke who have undergone pretreatment CTP and received reperfusion therapy.
36804310.interpretation:
- hypo-IVS <= 2: Lower risk of poor outcome
- hypo-IVS > 2: Higher risk of poor outcome, hypo-IVS > 2 was an independent risk factor of poor outcome (75.2% vs 40.8%)
36804310.utility: The hypo-IVS was significantly associated with poor outcome (3-month mRS of >2; OR = 1.194; 95 % CI: 1.014-1.407; p = 0.033). The adjusted ORs for poor outcome and high 24-hour NIHSS were 1.172 (95 %CI: 1.035-1.328; p = 0.012) and 1.176 (95 %CI: 1.030-1.330; p = 0.010) in ordinal regression, respectively.

36814297.title: RDW-CEA Score (RCS) Calculator for Stage I Lung Adenocarcinoma Prognosis
36814297.code:
def calculate_rdw_cea_score(rdw, cea, rdw_cutoff, cea_cutoff):
    """
    Calculate the RDW-CEA score based on the given RDW and CEA values and their respective cutoff points.

    Parameters:
    rdw (float): The RDW value.
    cea (float): The CEA value.
    rdw_cutoff (float): The optimal cutoff point for RDW.
    cea_cutoff (float): The optimal cutoff point for CEA.

    Returns:
    int: The RDW-CEA score (1, 2, or 3).
    """
    if rdw <= rdw_cutoff and cea <= cea_cutoff:
        return 1
    elif rdw > rdw_cutoff and cea > cea_cutoff:
        return 3
    else:
        return 2
36814297.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis of patients with stage I lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) based on the combination of red cell distribution width (RDW) and carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) levels.
36814297.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with stage I lung adenocarcinoma are eligible for this calculator.
36814297.interpretation:
- RCS 1: Patients have the best survival prognosis.
- RCS 2: Patients have an intermediate survival prognosis.
- RCS 3: Patients have the worst survival prognosis.
36814297.utility: The RDW-CEA score has a good area under the curve (AUC) for predicting disease-free survival (DFS) (AUC = 0.81, P < 0.001) and overall survival (OS) (AUC = 0.93, P < 0.001).

36822633.title: HAL Score for Predicting Acute Exacerbation of Idiopathic Interstitial Pneumonias
36822633.code:
def compute_HAL_score(honeycombing, age, ldh):
    """
    Compute the HAL score for predicting AE-IIPs.

    Parameters:
    honeycombing (bool): True if honeycombing is present on high-resolution computed tomography, False otherwise.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    ldh (int): The lactate dehydrogenase level of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The HAL score.
    """
    score = 0
    if honeycombing:
        score += 1
    if age > 75:
        score += 1
    if ldh > 222:
        score += 1
    return score
36822633.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of acute exacerbation of idiopathic interstitial pneumonias (AE-IIPs) in patients with IIPs.
36822633.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with idiopathic interstitial pneumonias (IIPs).
36822633.interpretation:
- Score 0: The estimated cumulative risks for AE-IIPs at 1, 2, 3, 5 and 10 years are 1.9%, 3.5%, 5.1%, 7.7% and 12.9%, respectively.
- Score 1: The estimated cumulative risks for AE-IIPs at 1, 2, 3, 5 and 10 years are 4.7%, 8.3%, 12.0%, 17.7% and 28.4%, respectively.
- Score ≥2: The estimated cumulative risks for AE-IIPs at 1, 2, 3, 5 and 10 years are 8.0%, 14.2%, 19.7%, 28.7% and 43.0%, respectively.
36822633.utility: The HAL score discriminated the risk of AE-IIPs with a C-index of 0.62 (95% CI 0.56-0.67) in the exploratory cohort and 0.67 (95% CI 0.60-0.73) in the validation cohort.

36824664.title: RILT Risk Calculator for Unresectable Stage III NSCLC Patients
36824664.code:
def compute_risk_score(IDO1_activity, FEV1):
    """
    Compute the risk score for RILT in NSCLC patients.

    Parameters:
    IDO1_activity (float): The activity of IDO1 enzyme, reflected by Kyn : Trp ratio.
    FEV1 (float): The FEV1 value, a measure of lung function.

    Returns:
    risk_score (str): The risk score, categorized into two groups.
    """
    if IDO1_activity > 0.069 and FEV1 <= 59.4:
        risk_score = 'High risk'
    else:
        risk_score = 'Low risk'
    return risk_score
36824664.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of radiation-induced lung toxicity (RILT) in stage III nonsmall cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients who are treated with chemoradiotherapy (CRT).
36824664.eligibility: Patients with unresectable stage III NSCLC who are undergoing chemoradiotherapy are eligible.
36824664.interpretation:
- 'High risk': Patients with an IDO1 activity >0.069 and FEV1 ≤ 59.4% have a 50.0% incidence of G3 + RILT.
- 'Low risk': Patients with an IDO1 activity ≤0.069 and FEV1 > 59.4% have a 2.6% incidence of G3 + RILT.
36824664.utility: The predictive model combining both IDO1 activity and FEV1 displayed a moderate predictive value (AUC = 0.83, P < 0.001).

36830136.title: CRE Bacteremia Risk Score in Cancer and HSCT Patients
36830136.code:
def compute_risk_score(days_hospitalized, days_antibiotics, colonization):
    """
    Compute the risk score for CRE bacteremia.

    Parameters:
    days_hospitalized (int): Number of days of hospitalization until bacteremia.
    days_antibiotics (int): Number of days of previous antibiotics.
    colonization (bool): Whether the patient is currently colonized with KPC-carbapenemase-producing Enterobacterales.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if days_hospitalized >= 10:
        score += 2
    if days_antibiotics > 7:
        score += 2
    if colonization:
        score += 5
    return score
36830136.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk for Carbapenem-Resistant Enterobacterales (CRE) bacteremia in patients with cancer and hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT).
36830136.eligibility: Patients with cancer or hematopoietic stem-cell transplant who developed Enterobacterales bacteremia.
36830136.interpretation:
- Score < 7: Low risk, the post-test probability of CRE occurrence is 1.9%, which would virtually rule out the presence of CRE bacteremia.
- Score ≥ 7: High risk, with a sensitivity of 35.59%, specificity of 98.43%, PPV of 77.7%, and NPV of 90.9%.
36830136.utility: The overall performance of the score was satisfactory with an AUROC of 0.85 (95% CI 0.80-0.91).

36835800.title: CRB-65 Risk Stratification Calculator for Pulmonary Embolism
36835800.code:
def compute_crb65_score(confusion, respiratory_rate, blood_pressure, age):
    """
    Compute the CRB-65 score for a patient with Pulmonary Embolism.

    Parameters:
    confusion (bool): True if the patient is confused, False otherwise.
    respiratory_rate (int): The patient's respiratory rate per minute.
    blood_pressure (tuple): The patient's blood pressure as a tuple (systolic, diastolic).
    age (int): The patient's age.

    Returns:
    int: The CRB-65 score.
    """
    score = 0
    if confusion:
        score += 1
    if respiratory_rate > 30:
        score += 1
    if blood_pressure[0] < 90 or blood_pressure[1] <= 60:
        score += 1
    if age >= 65:
        score += 1
    return score
36835800.purpose: This calculator is used to stratify the risk and predict the prognosis of patients with Pulmonary Embolism (PE).
36835800.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Pulmonary Embolism are eligible for this calculator.
36835800.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low-risk group with lower occurrence of in-hospital death and MACCE.
- Score ≥1: High-risk group with a 5.5-fold increased occurrence of in-hospital death and higher occurrence of MACCE.
36835800.utility: The CRB-65 score was found to be independently associated with in-hospital death (OR 5.53 [95%CI 5.40-5.65], p < 0.001) as well as MACCE (OR 4.31 [95%CI 4.23-4.40], p < 0.001).

36857052.title: VALE Scoring System for Hemorrhage Risk in Brain Arteriovenous Malformations
36857052.code:
def calculate_vale_score(ventricular_system_involvement, venous_aneurysm, deep_location, exclusively_deep_drainage):
    """
    Calculate the VALE score for hemorrhage risk in brain arteriovenous malformations.

    Parameters:
    ventricular_system_involvement (bool): Whether the AVM involves the ventricular system.
    venous_aneurysm (bool): Whether a venous aneurysm is present.
    deep_location (bool): Whether the AVM is located deep within the brain.
    exclusively_deep_drainage (bool): Whether the AVM drains exclusively deep.

    Returns:
    int: The VALE score, ranging from 0 to 4.
    """
    return ventricular_system_involvement + venous_aneurysm + deep_location + exclusively_deep_drainage
36857052.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the rupture risk of arteriovenous malformations (AVMs) in the brain.
36857052.eligibility: Patients with unruptured arteriovenous malformations (AVMs) in the brain are eligible.
36857052.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low-risk group with a 10-year hemorrhage-free rate of 95.5%.
- Score 2: Moderate-risk group with a 10-year hemorrhage-free rate of 92.8%.
- Score 3-4: High-risk group with a 10-year hemorrhage-free rate of 75.8%.
36857052.utility: The VALE scoring system performed well in all 3 cohorts, with areas under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.77 in the derivation cohort, 0.85 in the multicenter external validation cohort, and 0.73 in the conservative treatment validation cohort.

36866830.title: Systemic Inflammation Response Index (SIRI) Score for Primary Gastrointestinal Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (PGI-DLBCL)
36866830.code:
def calculate_siri_score(siri):
    """
    Calculate the SIRI score.

    Parameters:
    siri (float): The systemic inflammation response index.

    Returns:
    int: The SIRI score.
    """
    if siri >= 1.34:
        return 1
    else:
        return 0
36866830.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the prognosis and risk of complications in patients with PGI-DLBCL based on the systemic inflammation response index (SIRI).
36866830.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with primary gastrointestinal diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (PGI-DLBCL).
36866830.interpretation:
- Score 0: Lower risk of poor survival and complications.
- Score 1: Higher risk of poor survival and complications.
36866830.utility: The SIRI score showed a higher area under the curve (AUC) (0.916 vs 0.835) and C-index (0.912 vs 0.836) for overall survival in the training cohort, indicating a better performance in predicting prognosis and risk of complications compared to the NCCN-IPI model.

36917313.title: Predictive Model for Positive Video Capsule Endoscopy in Iron Deficiency Anemia
36917313.code:
def compute_risk_score(age, smoking, arrhythmia, hemoglobin):
    """
    Compute the risk score for positive VCE in IDA.

    Parameters:
    age (int): Age of the patient.
    smoking (bool): Whether the patient is an active smoker.
    arrhythmia (bool): Whether the patient has a history of cardiac arrhythmia.
    hemoglobin (float): The patient's preceding hemoglobin level.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if age >= 66:
        score += 1
    if smoking:
        score += 1
    if arrhythmia:
        score += 1
    if hemoglobin >= 8.5:
        score -= 1
    return score
36917313.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of a positive video capsule endoscopy (VCE) in patients with iron deficiency anemia (IDA).
36917313.eligibility: Patients with iron deficiency anemia who are undergoing video capsule endoscopy.
36917313.interpretation:
The risk probabilities for positive VCE-assigned scores are as follows:
- Score -1: 12.3% risk (95% CI 7.3-17.3%)
- Score 0: 20% risk (95% CI 14.9-25.1%)
- Score 1: 34.8% risk (95% CI 28.6-41%)
- Score 2+: 39% risk (95% CI 30-47.8%)
36917313.utility: The study does not provide specific evaluation results of the clinical utility of the risk score, such as AUC, F-score, PPV.

36933287.title: PPCM Risk Score Calculator and PPCM Outcome Predictor
36933287.code:
def compute_ppcm_risk_score(pericardial_effusion, left_ventricular_dilatation, d_dimer_level):
    """
    Compute the PPCM risk score based on the presence of pericardial effusion, left ventricular dilatation, and d-dimer level ≥ 0.5 μg/mL.
    
    Args:
    pericardial_effusion (bool): Whether the patient has pericardial effusion.
    left_ventricular_dilatation (bool): Whether the patient has left ventricular dilatation.
    d_dimer_level (float): The patient's d-dimer level in μg/mL.

    Returns:
    int: The PPCM risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if pericardial_effusion:
        score += 1
    if left_ventricular_dilatation:
        score += 1
    if d_dimer_level >= 0.5:
        score += 1
    return score
36933287.purpose: These calculators are used to predict the risk of Peripartum Cardiomyopathy (PPCM) and to predict poor outcomes in PPCM patients.
36933287.eligibility: These calculators are applicable to pregnant women or women who have recently given birth.
36933287.interpretation: A PPCM risk score of 2 or more predicts delayed recovery with 95.5% sensitivity and 96.1% specificity. The negative predictive value is 97.4% and the positive predictive value is 93.3%.
36933287.utility: The PPCM Risk Score Calculator has a sensitivity of 95.5% and a specificity of 96.1%. The negative predictive value is 97.4% and the positive predictive value is 93.3%.

36942123.title: Quick Sepsis-related Organ Failure Assessment (qSOFA) for Fournier's Gangrene
36942123.code:
def compute_qsofa_score(low_bp: bool, high_rr: bool, altered_mentation: bool) -> int:
    """
    Compute the qSOFA score for a patient with Fournier's gangrene.

    Args:
    low_bp (bool): True if the patient has low blood pressure (SBP ≤ 100 mmHg), False otherwise.
    high_rr (bool): True if the patient has a high respiratory rate (≥ 22 breaths per min), False otherwise.
    altered_mentation (bool): True if the patient has altered mentation (Glasgow coma scale < 15), False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The qSOFA score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    return int(low_bp) + int(high_rr) + int(altered_mentation)
36942123.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the mortality risk in patients with Fournier's gangrene.
36942123.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with Fournier's gangrene are eligible.
36942123.interpretation:
- Score 0-1: Low risk of mortality
- Score 2-3: High risk of mortality
36942123.utility: The sensitivity of the qSOFA score to predict mortality was 88.2%; the specificity was 94.2%; positive predictive value was 83.3%; negative predictive value was 96.1%; positive likelihood ratio was 15.2; negative likelihood ratio was 0.12; and the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of qSOFA was 94.2%.

36944920.title: AST/ALT Ratio Prognostic Calculator for HCC Patients
36944920.code:
def compute_risk_score(ast, alt):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on the AST/ALT ratio.

    Parameters:
    ast (float): The pre-treatment aspartate transaminase level.
    alt (float): The pre-treatment alanine transaminase level.

    Returns:
    float: The risk score.
    """
    return ast / alt
36944920.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the overall survival (OS) of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) patients receiving thermal ablation combined with simultaneous TACE based on the pre-treatment aspartate transaminase (AST)/alanine transaminase (ALT) ratio.
36944920.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) who are receiving thermal ablation combined with simultaneous TACE.
36944920.interpretation:
- If the AST/ALT ratio is less than or equal to 0.89, the patient is considered to have a lower risk. The median overall survival for this group is 48.5 months.
- If the AST/ALT ratio is greater than 0.89, the patient is considered to have a higher risk. The median overall survival for this group is not reached.
36944920.utility: The integrated nomogram showed higher predictive accuracy for OS (C-index 0.674, 95%CI: 0.600-0.748).

36948970.title: Preoperative Model for Predicting Early Recurrence in Hepatocellular Carcinoma Patients
36948970.code:
def compute_risk_score(afp_level, tumor_size, meld_score, cirrhosis):
    """
    Compute the risk score for early recurrence in hepatocellular carcinoma patients.

    Parameters:
    afp_level (float): Alpha-fetoprotein level in ng/mL.
    tumor_size (float): Tumor size in mm.
    meld_score (int): Model for End-Stage Liver Disease score.
    cirrhosis (bool): Presence of cirrhosis.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if afp_level >= 20:
        score += 1
    if tumor_size > 30:
        score += 1
    if meld_score > 8:
        score += 1
    if cirrhosis:
        score += 1
    return score
36948970.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of early recurrence (within 2 years) in patients undergoing liver resection for single hepatocellular carcinoma.
36948970.eligibility: Patients undergoing liver resection for single hepatocellular carcinoma are eligible.
36948970.interpretation:
- Score 0: Low risk, with 2-year recurrence-free survival of 79.8% (95% CI: 75.7-84.2%)
- Score 1-2: Intermediate risk, with 2-year recurrence-free survival of 66.6% (95% CI: 61.1-72.6%)
- Score 3-4: High risk, with 2-year recurrence-free survival of 51.1% (95% CI: 43.0-60.8%)
36948970.utility: The model was developed using a cohort of 773 patients and showed good stratification of risk for early recurrence after liver resection for single hepatocellular carcinoma.

36960119.title: NGAL Sepsis Screening Tool
36960119.code:
def compute_sepsis_score(diabetes, rigors, qsofa, infection, ngal):
    """
    Compute the sepsis score based on the given parameters.
    
    Parameters:
    diabetes (bool): True if the patient has diabetes mellitus, False otherwise.
    rigors (bool): True if the patient has rigors, False otherwise.
    qsofa (int): The patient's qSOFA score.
    infection (bool): True if the patient has a clear focus of infection, False otherwise.
    ngal (float): The patient's plasma NGAL level in ng/mL.

    Returns:
    int: The computed sepsis score.
    """
    score = 0
    if diabetes:
        score += 1
    if rigors:
        score += 1
    if qsofa > 2:
        score += 2
    if infection:
        score += 2
    if ngal > 570:
        score += 3
    return score
36960119.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the likelihood of bacterial sepsis in patients presenting to the emergency department with fever.
36960119.eligibility: Patients presenting to the emergency department with acute febrile episodes fulfilling the Systemic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (SIRS) criteria are eligible.
36960119.interpretation:
- A score of <3 rules out bacterial sepsis.
- A score of >7 is highly suggestive of bacterial sepsis.
36960119.utility: The area under the curve (AUC) for this tool is 0.69. A score >7 has an interval likelihood ratio (LR) of 7.77, indicating a high probability of bacterial sepsis.

36962449.title: MuLB Risk Score Calculator for COVID-19 Mortality
36962449.code:
def calculate_mulb_score(multi_lobe, lymphopenia, bacterial_infection):
    """
    Calculate the MuLB risk score for COVID-19 mortality.

    Parameters:
    multi_lobe (bool): True if multi lobar infiltrates are present, False otherwise.
    lymphopenia (bool): True if lymphocytes are <0.8x109 /L, False otherwise.
    bacterial_infection (bool): True if bacterial co-infection is present, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    float: The MuLB risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    score += 2 if multi_lobe else 0.254
    score += 2 if lymphopenia else 0.18
    score += 3 if bacterial_infection else 0.306
    return score
36962449.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of mortality in patients with severe COVID-19 infection. It can guide further clinical research on risk-based treatment.
36962449.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with severe COVID-19 infection are eligible for this risk score calculation.
36962449.interpretation:
- Score > 8: High risk of mortality
- Score < 5: Mild risk of mortality
36962449.utility: The MuLB risk score model was validated in an independent group of patients from October 2020 to December 2021. It was found to be significantly associated with mortality risk (P < 0.001).

36965411.title: Elective Neck Dissection Risk Calculator for cT1N0M0 Oral Cavity Cancer
36965411.code:
def compute_risk_score(neck_observation, doi, poor_differentiation):
    """
    Compute the risk score for cT1N0M0 OCSCC patients.

    Parameters:
    neck_observation (bool): True if neck observation was performed, False otherwise.
    doi (float): Depth of invasion in mm.
    poor_differentiation (bool): True if the tumor is poorly differentiated, False otherwise.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if neck_observation:
        score += 1
    if doi > 2.5:
        score += 1
    if poor_differentiation:
        score += 1
    return score
36965411.purpose: This calculator is used to determine the risk factors for 5-year outcomes in patients with cT1N0M0 oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma (OCSCC) and to decide whether elective neck dissection (END) should be performed.
36965411.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with cT1N0M0 oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma (OCSCC).
36965411.interpretation:
- Score 0: 98% neck control, 96% disease-specific survival (DSS), 90% overall survival
- Score 1: 95% neck control, 93% DSS, 86% overall survival
- Score 2: 84% neck control, 88% DSS, 79% overall survival
- Score 3: 85% neck control, 85% DSS, 59% overall survival
36965411.utility: The risk score calculator has a significant p-value (< 0.0001) for all outcomes, indicating a strong correlation between the risk score and the 5-year outcomes.

36966235.title: Oesophageal Cancer Prognosis Calculator
36966235.code:
def calculate_risk(histopathologic_regression, nodal_status):
    """
    Calculate the risk group based on histopathologic regression and nodal status.

    Parameters:
    histopathologic_regression (str): Can be 'CR', 'major', or 'minor'.
    nodal_status (str): Can be 'ypN0' or 'ypN+'.

    Returns:
    str: The risk group, can be 'low', 'intermediate', or 'high'.
    """
    if histopathologic_regression == 'CR':
        return 'low'
    elif histopathologic_regression in ['major', 'minor'] and nodal_status == 'ypN0':
        return 'intermediate'
    else:
        return 'high'
36966235.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the 5-year overall survival rate after neoadjuvant treatment of oesophageal cancer based on histopathologic regression and nodal status.
36966235.eligibility: Patients with oesophageal squamous cell (SCC) or adenocarcinoma (AC) undergoing neoadjuvant treatment and esophagectomy are eligible.
36966235.interpretation:
- Low-risk group: 72% 5-year overall survival rate
- Intermediate-risk group: 59% 5-year overall survival rate
- High-risk group: 20% 5-year overall survival rate
36966235.utility: The survival analysis identified three groups with significantly different mortality risks, indicating the clinical utility of the risk score.

36969723.title: Bacterial Arthritis Risk Calculator
36969723.code:
def compute_risk(c_reactive_protein, platelet_count):
    """
    Compute the risk of bacterial arthritis.

    Parameters:
    c_reactive_protein (float): C-reactive protein level in mg/dL.
    platelet_count (float): Platelet count in 104/μL.

    Returns:
    str: Risk group ('low-risk', 'intermediate-risk', 'high-risk')
    """
    if c_reactive_protein > 21.09:
        return 'high-risk'
    elif c_reactive_protein <= 21.09 and 27.70 < platelet_count <= 30.70:
        return 'high-risk'
    else:
        return 'low-risk'
36969723.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the risk of bacterial arthritis in patients based on their C-reactive protein levels and platelet count.
36969723.eligibility: Patients who have undergone arthrocentesis and have available data on C-reactive protein levels and platelet count.
36969723.interpretation:
- 'high-risk': Patients in this group have a high risk of bacterial arthritis. The incidence of septic arthritis is 48.7% for patients with C-reactive protein > 21.09 mg/dL and 36.1% for patients with C-reactive protein ≤ 21.09 mg/dL and 27.70 < platelet count ≤ 30.70 × 104/μL.
- 'low-risk': Patients in this group have a low risk of bacterial arthritis.
36969723.utility: The chi-squared automatic interaction detector decision tree analysis was used to derive this risk calculator. However, the article does not provide specific evaluation results such as AUC, F-score, or PPV.

36972927.title: French Vasculitis Study Group Relapse Score (FRS)
36972927.code:
def compute_frs(pr3_positive, age, egfr):
    """
    Compute the French Vasculitis Study Group Relapse Score (FRS).

    Args:
    pr3_positive (bool): Whether the patient is PR3 positive.
    age (int): The age of the patient.
    egfr (float): The estimated glomerular filtration rate of the patient.

    Returns:
    int: The FRS score, ranging from 0 to 3.
    """
    score = 0
    if pr3_positive:
        score += 1
    if age <= 75:
        score += 1
    if egfr >= 30:
        score += 1
    return score
36972927.purpose: This calculator is used to assess the probability of relapse in patients diagnosed with granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA) and microscopic polyangiitis (MPA).
36972927.eligibility: Patients diagnosed with granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA) and microscopic polyangiitis (MPA).
36972927.interpretation:
- FRS of 0: 5-year relapse risk is 8%
- FRS of 1: 5-year relapse risk is 30%
- FRS of 2: 5-year relapse risk is 48%
- FRS of 3: 5-year relapse risk is 76%
36972927.utility: The FRS was validated in an independent cohort of 209 patients, showing a clear correlation between the score and the 5-year relapse risk.

36991096.title: Elotuzumab Treatment Durability Predictor
36991096.code:
def compute_score(lymphocyte_count, kappa_lambda_ratio, b2mg):
    """
    Compute the risk score based on lymphocyte count, kappa/lambda ratio, and B2MG level.

    Parameters:
    lymphocyte_count (float): The lymphocyte count in μL.
    kappa_lambda_ratio (float): The kappa/lambda ratio.
    b2mg (float): The B2MG level in mg/L.

    Returns:
    int: The risk score.
    """
    score = 0
    if lymphocyte_count < 1400:
        score += 1
    if kappa_lambda_ratio < 0.1 or kappa_lambda_ratio > 10:
        score += 1
    if b2mg >= 5.5:
        score += 1
    return score
36991096.purpose: This calculator is used to predict the durability of the elotuzumab treatment effect in patients with multiple myeloma.
36991096.eligibility: Patients with multiple myeloma who are undergoing or considering elotuzumab treatment.
36991096.interpretation:
- Score 0: Patients with a score of 0 have significantly longer time to next treatment (TTNT) and better survival.
- Score 1 or 2: Patients with a score of 1 or 2 have shorter TTNT and worse survival.
36991096.utility: The scoring system showed significant differences in TTNT (p < 0.001) and survival (p < 0.001) between patients with a score of 0 and those with a score of 1 or 2.
